### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-14T02:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-23774>

Oh, so about today. I had two eggs, 4 bacon, and one turkey burger for BAB. Along with a can of coconut milk with CO. And plenty of butter. I also did a face dunk session. I don't know what my problem is but I always try to breathe when I face dunk. I mean, WTH. I Know how to swim. So, I have to plug my nose which is super annoying. I have been kind of cranky the last two days. Not sure if it is because I ran out of 5 HTP or the blood sugar thing. That's how I got into trouble last night. I was noticing myself getting snippy with my friends and thought, before we are not friends any more, I should eat. Stupid food truck. ANY WHOO...I had a turkey burger, two sausage patties with butter and a swipe of cream cheese. Ok, bad call on my part with the cream cheese, holy bloat. And some CO with butter and cocoa powder. YUM I was gonna skip dinner but felt hungry so I had some CO and an egg with some spinach. Trying to motivate myself to do some push ups and pull downs or something. I cannot stand this fat around my belly. I mean, if I have to re-gain weight, why can't it go back on the boobs! Instead of the love handles

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-14T16:59:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-23895>

GW 125 (or flat stomach)

40 years old, 5’6 & 1/2;

Really not hungry this morning and kind of feeling sick. Last night I had bad stomach ache, like I did when I had gastric erosions. Is is the coconut milk or the bulletproof coffee? I went to be pretty early last night and still could not get up this morning. I feel tired and zony. I'm out of 5HTP, so not sure if it is that or the fact that maybe I pushed it with the coffee. I need to learn that just because I can have something once and feel good, doesn't mean that I can have it every day. I'm super thirsty today too. For my BAB, I had this flank steak (which wasn't very good) and some bacon along with the coconut milk shake (half a can). I went a few days without HCL and at first was ok, but the heartburn got pretty bad. The worse being Saturday night when I ate off the food truck. At least my appetite is down. I will skip the scale tomorrow-going to the dentist today and that's always good for a gain

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-15T15:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24174>

Dr Kruse is right...

You have to put your focus on your health, not on your weight. You have been broken (hormonaly) for years. As long as you won't be fixed, there is no way you can lose weight. Even doing perfects days, it will take long. All you do with focussing about weight is stressing further your adrenals. You'll have to let it go. I know it's hard, I've been there, and it's still difficult at times. All my clothes don't fit anymore (I've gained 20 lbs since Christmas). I know you don't feel good, you don't feel attractive. People around probably don't even noticed that you've gained weight. You are still beautiful, you are still the person they love. They don't love you for your weigth. You should do the same. Don't be so hard on yourself...Please stop define yourself by a number. You are so much more than that. You might not be perfect for the moment, but that's ok. Put your energy at the good place and be patient. I know it's easier said than done, but you have no other option. You have to hang in there. You have to see all that from another angle. You'll probably gain weight again, Then it will stabilize. And after a while you'll lose it.

I hope I don't sound too harsh. That's not my goal. I only don't want you to quit. It's doable, many others have successed, so you will too, even if it takes longer. I hope you feel better and you'll have a good day. I'm sending you a hug. And throw that scale away, please!!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-15T15:45:30+0300

People around probably don't even noticed that you've gained weight. You are still beautiful, you are still the person they love. They don't love you for your weigth. You should do the same. Don't be so hard on yourself...Please stop define yourself by a number. You are so much more than that. You might not be perfect for the moment, but that's ok. Put your energy at the good place and be patient. I know it's easier said than done, but you have no other option. You have to hang in there. You have to see all that from another angle. Thanks ABetterMe, no you don't sound harsh. In fact, you sound very kind. I really just want to feel good and be health and look good! I LOVED how I looked and felt in my body at 127. I hate how I feel now. And I did the whole, I just won't weigh myself and see what happens and that was +10, now I'm at +15. I just can't gain any more. It doesn't really matter if I weigh myself or not. I know when I try to get dressed in the morning and looking in the mirror raises my cortisol more than the scale. I'm just super frustrated because I'm trying to make positive changes in my life. Trying to be happy. Trying to date. Dating is tough enough but dating while eating a crazy diet, no alcohol and an early bedtime. And now I've gained weight to be boot. I just feel really stuck. Caught in a trap.

Sorry, you really did cheer me up. I've always been this way. I want what I want and I think stamping my feet and demanding that reality line up the way I want it will work. Hint, it never has.

**

I gained weight after adding CT. I can't put a number on it as I haven't weighed myself for exactly that reason for ... months now. My doc/nurse has been put under silence and I get on the scale backwards at my doc's office. I know I gained b/c there's a lot of clothes I won't wear right now.

I just had to decide which way I wanted to go. And I decided to give this my 100% since I think I tried all other ways of getting healthy unsuccessfully. In my mind I have decided that the gain is temporary and that I need to be gentle with my body. When I get upset, I remind myself that I am healing. I remind myself of all the things that have improved and that 10 or 15lbs are not a reason to stop. I wear lose clothing that I feel comfortable in.

I think my gain has stopped recently but I do empathize with it being scary. I do think that your day of less hunger/appetite is a good sign. I had that feeling for a while but would still eat the same amount. Now I am noticing that I don't always feel like finishing my meal. Not sure I will ever lose the weight I gained since I was pretty small still from AN before. I will accept those lbs as a sign of my health.**

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-15T20:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24290>

This is probably easier said than done, but still, I think the Key is to focus on doing the right things, rather than reaching your short term goals. In fact, GIVE UP those short term goals since they interfere with your staying on the right path. Love means patience too, right? Then love yourself a little more. Give your body a chance to peacefully go through the learning curve and practice then master the new trick of burning fat, repairing itself, and rejuvenating itself. Throw out the scale, eat fat and protein to satiation, drink a large glass of water/tea with your meal. When you feel tired or moody, go to bed early. Get a body massage. Eat a fatty meal. Do all the things to pamper yourself, or jump into a cold tub, you'll get a "cold high" so you won't need the sugar high. I agree with mamgrok about coconut manna... it gets me bloated too... as soon as I stopped eating a lot of it, my tummy becomes smaller.

Hang in there.

Jennifer Jennifer Jennifer.

I rad the last 3 pages with my eyes watering, blinking very hard so I would not cry here at work. I am in the same boat as you and Paulallynn, Souldanzer, gaining weight and not knowing why, feel like I'm grasping at straws. My ED past is coming up again via my obsession with the scale, and yes I am terrifed. I am up 8 lbs now ( i think I posted to you a few weeks back I was up 5, now its 8)

I hear you on the dating things, that's gotta suck. DH wants us to meet friends for dinner and a movie, and I loathe the idea. I feel like I've become a hermit with a bizarre and addictive lifestyle. WTH.

ABetterMe, your reply made the tears come.

Jennifer, you are not alone. I will post more, and keep you company. (((hugs)))

Thanks so much Souldanzer, Youthfuliz, and Teenie Leek! Thank you for being the voice of reason. I know I have a history of quitting right before the breakthrough. I guess I never really realized how much negative feelings I have toward myself when I gain weight. I get split into two people - the one who wants to be thin and the one who is f-ing it all up. This war we go through with our bodies. If Dr K could only tell me how to move the fat from my belly and love handles to my very flat behind, then I would happily weigh 143.5. I really am not feeling myself today. I've been super tired the past few days and not sure why because I'm alseep before 10pm. Today my eyes are so dry and I feel so moody, like I have PMS but the MS never comes.

It means a lot to get your support! Thanks again

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-15T23:06:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24362>

> **Jennifer;24267 wrote:** Thanks ABetterMe, no you don't sound harsh. In fact, you sound very kind. I really just want to feel good and be health and look good! I LOVED how I looked and felt in my body at 127. I hate how I feel now. And I did the whole, I just won't weigh myself and see what happens and that was +10, now I'm at +15. I just can't gain any more. It doesn't really matter if I weigh myself or not. I know when I try to get dressed in the morning and looking in the mirror raises my cortisol more than the scale. I'm just super frustrated because I'm trying to make positive changes in my life. Trying to be happy. Trying to date. Dating is tough enough but dating while eating a crazy diet, no alcohol and an early bedtime. And now I've gained weight to be boot. I just feel really stuck. Caught in a trap.
>
> Sorry, you really did cheer me up. I've always been this way. I want what I want and I think stamping my feet and demanding that reality line up the way I want it will work. Hint, it never has.

You wouldn't believe how much I understand you. Just like you, I want it HERE and NOW!! It has been easy for me in the past to lose weight. I could lose 10 pounds in 2 weeks restriting and purging myself. Seems that at one point, my body had enough of it. I feel so sad I broke my body for a few miserable pounds. I know what you mean by loving your body ay 127. I loved mine at 126  Now I'm at 156, maybe a little more. But I feel so much better in my head. I can't find the words to tell how grateful I am I discovered this site and the leptin reset. I don't know where I would be right now without it. Some days, I feel really great, and some days, I cry in my bed wondering if I'll ever be normal one day. Every morning when I get dressed it reminds me I don't fit in my favorite clothes, but I've decided I don't want that to ruin my days anymore. I hided them all in an another closet, and once my weight is down again, I'll take them out.

I've decided, even if I'm not perfect and not at MY weight, that I have to date again. They don't know how I looked last year. I know I'm not at my best, but I also know I'm doing everything I can to gain my health back. I'm trying to be confident (I often act ) and I tell myself I have so much more to offer that the size of my thighs. Not always easy, but I can't hide anymore and look at my life pass me by.

Date, have fun, throw that scale away. You are in control more than you think. YOU choose to get healthy, and YOU choose what you put in your mouth. Sometimes you'll make the wrong choice. That is totally normal. You'll be angry at yourself. A therapist once told me that the role of parents are to show their kids they love them even when they do something stupid. They can be angry at the kids, but they still love them. He then suggested me to do the same with myself. I can be angry for some stupid things I do once in a while, but I still have to be kind and love myself. It really touched me when he said that, and I often think of it.

Hugs,

K

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T05:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24509>

What do you take for your thyroid? I am not a fan of T4 only, like Synthroid, so maybe you need a dab of T3 only or my favorite, the natural dessicated thyroid. It helped me with mood and weight loss. Although it didn't help w/all my extra weight but it is better. We are close to the same size (I'm 5'8", small boned and my GW is 124, CW 135) and know what it feels like to be at the higher weight. It's hard to get the weight stabilized when your hormones are messed up and have a wonky thyroid. Hang in there it gets better when things get straightened out. Sounds like you are on your way to figuring some pieces out.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-16T15:07:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24561>

Oh sweet thing, just love thyself and ditch the fricking scale for good! Just think, if you date now and find a good guy who loves you the way you are now (even though you aren't even fat!!!!!!!!) you will be so much better off in the end. You will know he loves you for who you are. I am tired of always thinking I'm fat and not good enough for these men and I'm not fat either. We have to change. We all grew up in a world that said women had to be tall and thin. Blah, blah, blah! I look at the all the young women today who love themselves (something we weren't taught) and they are all pudgy for sure and unhealthy. Somehow, we have to change our mindset and love ourselves no matter what. Yes, we all are messed up.

PaulaLynn brought up a very good point about trusting Jack. I certainly trust him and that is why I have been doing (not perfectly) for almost 10 months. Crazy? Maybe. But I have seen improvements. I know to trust him as most things he has said I have read or been instructed by my doc to do aside from his ground breaking ideas. Yes, not all agree but I do. You really have to put that scale away forever and get the number out of your head. Remember, muscle weighs more than fat. I have a friend who is a size smaller and 10 lbs heavier and I want to look like her and that means the scale would have to go up about 15 lbs. Yikes, but that is my goal. It is about the number but all in context for sure. You really sound like your hormones are messed up. You seem to have mood swings just like I do. Up one day and down the next. Remember when you said recently that the scale was up but you were happy? This is me too. Somedays things that don't matter, just don't matter; and other days things that don't matter, but the piss right out of me!!! So, I am working on the hormones as soon as I talk with Dr. K. and figure out the best route.

Did you read "The Monk Who Sold his Ferrari?" I see both of us needing the benefit of this book. I am working on changing my thoughts and this simple little book has great advice. We have to calm our minds down.

Didn't you have a consult with Jack? If so, how is that going? If not, do it. Let's get this stuff straightened out once and for all. And please keep dating and having fun. If you make a big deal about things you "can't" eat, then it will be a big deal. You can come up with a line to not make it a big deal. Gluten intolerance works just fine. You can always order the crap too and just not eat it and say you aren't hungry and give it to someone else.

Please smile today even if forced and try to have a nice day. Lots of hugs to you!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-16T15:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24567>

Thanks ladies! I feel better today. Skipping the coconut milk and had Optimal Upgraded Bulletproof coffee instead. We’ll see how the adrenals take it.

I agree that I need to change my thoughts – epigenetics and all. It is a larger system at work for me. I have a rigid belief that it's better not to get your hopes up, because thinking things will work out and having them fall apart is way more painful than just being miserable! I seem to have a very strong need not to have the proverbial rug pulled out underneath me. And have history to prove it. When I think things are really gonna work out this time and they don’t, I usually take the biggest hit in the adrenals.

Trust, another theme for me. I do trust Jack, it’s me and my body I don’t trust. There is a fear that “this just won’t work for me” because my body doesn’t DO WHAT I WANT IT TO. (another theme – life would be so much better if everyone just did what I wanted them to!) I mean, seriously. I am eating a diet people use to lose weight and I’m gaining! WTH? At the beginning of this ride, I had a dream that I was in an airplane and Jack was the pilot. So, I have given over to this but obviously there is no cookie cutter approach and something is greatly amiss in Jennifer’s body. I see the functional medicine doctor today. I filled out 20 pages of health questions. So at least I know she will take my symptoms of irritation and low blood sugar seriously (endo told me that those things were annoying but not a disease and to just eat more-thanks, because gaining weight will make it all better. I have belly fat, people!) I am interested in exploring a different med for the thyroid and obviously my hormones. I just hope that I can afford the testing she orders or get my PMD to order it so insurance covers it. Again, I wish I had the money that the stupid MRI cost and could spend that on tests I think I actually need.

But I digress…

The Monk book – got it from the library and had it for a while but didn’t finish it. Focus and follow through are really tough for me right now. One of my huge complaints. I feel like I am all over the place or zoned out. So many books to read – the thyroid book, the hormone book, I never made it through Rob Wolf’s book. I really need to get them on CD and listen to them in my car. Or get them on my iphone to listen to. That’s the only thing that works. I was thinking about getting the Sweet Potato Power book Dr K recommends. But I asked him if it was doable on Leptin RX or for after. He said that it could work on Leptin Rx but he would not recommend for anyone morbidly obese or with ED (in this case, I believe he meant Eating Disorders). The other book Make Shift Happen, would probably be a great read for me. But I hesitate to get it when I haven’t finished the others! I know my library has “Mindset” on audio. I tried to download it but got distracted and of course promptly forgot!

I have been using my emWave at least daily. Sometimes it is a source of great frustration because I get the red light (bad) instead of the green. But I’ll keep at it.

I know none of this drama that I stir up really matters but I stir it up anyway…LOL

God bless us, humans are such funny creatures!

I am thankful I have you all to gently guide me back when I veer off course.

Sometimes I really do think the leptin thing is resetting. I wonder how given my big cheats with nuts and just plain ole eating too much. But I never stopped the BAB. It’s just hard to get my brain to relax about the “you’re gaining weight, something is not working, must be the diet” thing. I eventually re-gain weight on any diet I’ve done. So maybe there is something I am not considering in this…a factor x, perhaps??

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-05-17T01:38:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24728>

Hey girl, saw abt your appt with func med doc. What a dud.

Other than the letdown from that, how are you doing today? I feel better, lots better Maybe coming out of the closet helped LOL

Do you want to try to stop weighing? I am really thinking about it. Feels scary, even thinking about makes me nervous. Do you want to try with me? Maybe when you go away on vacation?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T02:19:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24738>

Scales are for fish. Who would let their self worth be determined by an appliance?

I could never have gotten any AN recovery while weighing myself. My self worth was attached to this appliance and that was one of the biggest mismatches ever in my life!! It was \*structurally\* attached, as in my brain had wired that way over time.

The way out? Create chaos in your brain. Trash the scale and allow your brain to re-wire differently. I still don't love gaining extra weight but my self worth isn't attached to it any longer. I won't do things that hurt me b/c of it anymore.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-17T03:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24751>

I'm right there with you, Jen! I was complaining and asking question after question everywhere I could find today because I've gained a pound each of the last two days. Was getting told how if you're gaining it's because your hormones are wacky, maybe this, maybe that. I just did another round of hcg and I seriously can't put 20 lbs on!! All of a sudden, there's a post on my journal from Jack saying "if you're weighing yourself you're not doing the reset correctly. i said throw out the scale - just looking at it raises your cortisol." And I'm suddenly sitting there like "ok, yes sir, Dr. Kruse. I did read that and forgot it." I believe the man really cares about us all and believes fervently in everything he's teaching. And since I haven't gotten far with mainstream medicine, I've decided to give this a try and trust him. So I made an appointment for next week with THE ONLY doctor in town that works with BHRT. I have been wanting to do that for 15 years and doctors look at you like you've got two heads! Then I went through menopause and figured "what's the point"? But today, it all fell into place. This doctor that I normally couldn't get in with until end of June had JUST told her receptionist she'll be working a couple of days next week that she wasn't going to, and that's when I called for an appointment. My gut says it's all going to fall into place. Well, well, I meant for this to be a quick post and there was my soapbox!! Hang in there! We'll all hang in there together! I want to be in the club too!!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-17T06:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24810>

> **Teenie Leek;24810 wrote:** Hey girl, saw abt your appt with func med doc. What a dud.
>

Hi there! I've been kind of all over the place today but busy so it kept me out of too much trouble. Seeing the func med doc definetly triggered some control things for me. But it also showed me that I am committed to Leptin Reset because when she was suggesting things like 5 meals a day and stopping red meat, I was having a HARD time with it. But she was also bringing up good points. She obviously has her training down and basically was saying, you came here today with these complaints and it's all linked back to leaky gut, so do what you need to do to heal that, and you'll be good. And she was kind of like, are you gonna put your weight above your gut. But I still am not drinking that stuff she wants me to take!  Dang it, I can be really stubborn when I want to be.

Stopping weighing...yes. That is a good idea and it seems crazy to me that I think stepping on the scale = me being in control because it's not. Although, I do like weighing myself to see if certain foods are working for me or not. Although, that could be a total excuse. I will stop when I'm on vacation. I leave next Wed...you in?

And i just love what Souldanzer has written...gotta comment on that next!

And get to bed...I should not be on technology this late at night even with the blueblockers on!

thanks a million for checking on me

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-17T16:36:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24875>

Disclaimer - the ugly truth is what follows here, serious garbage of thoughts purging...please excuse the self-indulgence but I think I need to fess up to some mental crap that is driving my scale obsession...by acknowledging, I am hoping I can move past it instead of continuing. I have a long standing history of avoiding feelings by lots of different means.

I've probably had a leaky gut for most of my life, but the adrenal stress re-started two years ago when my brothers got married. Of course, I am happy for them, but as the oldest and now the only single one in the family, I am feeling a bit like the cheese that stands alone. And that I am not â€œnormal.â€ This is very painful for me. I was in a relationship back then and thought I was finally going to get my happy ending - all I really wanted was a nice little life with a man to love and a kid or two. My hopes were up for a change and I was feeling very optimistic. When it busted, the fall was really great. And as you can see, my body is still dealing with the fall out.

And now all three of my brothers and their wives have had babies in the last nine months and my long time bff is pregnant with her fifth child and now my boss is also pregnant and I'm not only single but also have not had a period since I turned 40. So, the baby train has pretty much left without me. I take full responsibility for this. But the pain is really acute right now. I always look for something to hold on to, to get me by. Since it's not drugs or alcohol, and not food, it was BEING THIN. I figured if I can't have a relationship or a baby, at least I can be thin. And i LOVED it (except for the days I felt like crap and the fact that I wanted to kill people on a regular basis). Now I don't have that so I feel very out of control inside. Iâ€™m looking around for something to tell me that Iâ€™m ok. Iâ€™m normal. Something to say, well yeah, Iâ€™m not married, I donâ€™t have a kid but I have \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_. Right now there is nothing in the blank. I guess this is the truth though. So long as you define yourself by outer things, youâ€™re setting yourself up for pain and suffering.

The Func Med doctor says that my body is in a state of constant war because of this leaky gut situation. I feel like I'm a war with my body and with life in general. It is any wonder that I'm exhausted and cranky as all getout?

I really should just lighten up and say "F-it"...kick it up and have fun and forget about the rest. I mean, sh\*t happens whether you are smiling or crying, right? Focusing on problems doesn't solve them, does it? Of course, I think wine would immensely help me lighten up. That's not on my any of my diet plans.

IDK, Iâ€™ve been eating only protein and fat trying to be a fat burner and kick into keto. I do notice a big relief in sugar crashes, but I still feel my adrenals are stressed out. I seriously need to chill out. (CT?) Whatâ€™s the next step? I think Iâ€™m going to go back to eating vegetables. Iâ€™ll stick to lower carb but I donâ€™t think my body likes this protein and fat only dietâ€¦.

If you read this, I apologize for my bad attitude. I think purging and being honest is a way out for me. AT least I'm not stuffing it down with food or with some other obsessive thing...

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T17:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-24917>

> **Jennifer;24957 wrote:** I always look for something to hold on to, to get me by. Since it's not drugs or alcohol, and not food, it was BEING THIN. I figured if I can't have a relationship or a baby, at least I can be thin. And i LOVED it (except for the days I felt like crap and the fact that I wanted to kill people on a regular basis). Now I don't have that so I feel very out of control inside. Iâ€™m looking around for something to tell me that Iâ€™m ok. Iâ€™m normal. Something to say, well yeah, Iâ€™m not married, I donâ€™t have a kid but I have \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_. Right now there is nothing in the blank. I guess this is the truth though. So long as you define yourself by outer things, youâ€™re setting yourself up for pain and suffering.

That something is something you need to find within yourself. If it is attached to something on the outside, you will always be at the mercy of that thing or person. I replaced thin with healthy when my thinking worked like this. Once that was accomplished, my life started to center around getting as healthy as I could be. This took me on a very different course in my life.

The best way to get to establishing this in yourself is to remove everything else from your life. Remove the scale, remove the looking at other people for self-worth, let it all go and find what is within yourself. Your life won't be all fixed once your self worth is found within yourself... but it is the basis you need to build a life on. I'm in a similar place as you currently with my life, the difference is that I have changed my feelings about it. Create space for the new, empty your cup, allow the things you want into your life. Welcome them by preparing for them and taking action. The monk book is great for working on these things but there's other mindfulness/buddhist approaches you can use.

What actions are you taking to improve the areas in your life that you are not happy with at this point in time? It's helpful to make a plan - in writing or I like collages - so you know which steps are next, what worked or what didn't, and you can see your initiative no matter if it got you the result you wanted. The more often you fail, the more likely you are to succeed. You fall flat on your face? You pick yourself up and try again. Be proud of having failed. It means you tried something new. You can succeed by elimination.

I wouldn't be surprised if having your self-worth attached externally is the source of the reason why you are not in the place in your life you'd like to be (a relationship, etc.). I know for me it is/was.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-05-18T08:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25134>

Jennifer, two unrelated things. But first... I think it's great that you're 'venting' here and working through the truth of how you really feel. It can be so easy to hide behind how we want others to perceive us, but that just means we're treating ourselves badly, acting as if we aren't OK as we are. It's a lot to take on, huge life changes in the face of medical issues and a mostly-unsupportive medical community. Hang in there. We're all in this together, even if (as Quelsen says) everyone's broken is different.

First, I want to say +1000 to scales being only for fish! I gave mine up years ago and when I began the Leptin Rx and all these changes last year I was tempted to get one. I kept seeing everyone talking about 'goal weights' and measuring and weighing and I felt like I couldn't track my progress or know what I was doing if I didn't start weighing myself. But I decided that it would be best - for me - not to have a scale. And I've never regretted it. It's like letting go of a huge burden. You're not stupid and you'll know if you're gaining or losing weight. But the number... who cares. It's not directly tied to health. And I figure if we're going to eat seasonally our weight may fluctuate slightly throughout the year and I don't want to be obsessing about it all. I want to be healthy. And free!

Second, I was listening to Sean Croxton's second interview with TS Wiley today ('Ask TS Wiley' show of April 2011) and she had some interesting things to say about hormones, lack of periods, solutions and even the tie-in to gut health. I have no idea if she is 'right' but I find her very intelligent, saying most of the same stuff I hear Dr Kruse saying, and really giving some great food for thought. Just in case you're up for another podcast and may find it helpful.

Thinking of you....

Lauren

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-18T14:47:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25148>

I've been really touched reading your last post Jennifer. I was there last year, thinking I was having control on my life because I could "control" my weight. My life was miserable, but at least I had that. And I wasn't worth having someone in my life until I reached perfection. What a pressure to live with. I'm so much more in peace with myself since I let it go. Now I want to meet someone who will like me just the way I am. For those who don't, too bad for them. And like vkiernan said, if a good guy likes me the way I am now and support me through this, it can only get better.

Souldanzer is so right. Aesthetic goals are ok, but healthy goals are way better. And by becoming healthy and improving the inside, the outside will follow and get better too.

Continue venting and writing, it is very helpfull and often things appear less difficult once it's out.

Have a good day Jennifer,

Katy

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-18T18:55:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25220>

This might sound funny but one of my biggest lessons in letting go of the weight/scale issue was .... my dog.

Have you ever seen a dog (animal) judge you by your weight or looks? Dogs don't care. My dog lead me along the path of finding myself without a scale. Every morning I would wake up, my patterned reaction would kick in with the need to "check" the body appearance situation... until my dog came around. She just plain didn't care. All that counted for her was that I did get out of the house every day, that I was able to throw that ball further, that she could rough-house harder with me, .... and she gave it all back two and three fold by accepting me just the way I happened to be that day.

Having your self-worth attached to an appliance is a very neolithic human distinct phenomenon. Self-worth does exist outside of this. It's just that it's wired the wrong way for a lot of us.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-18T21:46:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25297>

Souldanzer! I so appreciate your feedback and suggestions, especially from someone who has gone through similar experiences. Thinking that I am SO DIFFERENT from the rest of the world is part of this. I've traveled to India and have a spiritual path that I follow and study, sometimes more ardently than others. So, I know logically what I must do, but emotionally I still rail against these things. And it can be very sneaky. I knew I did these little ego games “to be ok” – if I date the right guy, I’m ok; if I have “cool friends”, I’m ok; etc…I hadn’t realized I was doing this “ok game” with the scale. So, you are pointing to letting go of everything external and see what remains….yeah, kind of terrifying but inevitable. Thank you for these pointers. The funniest thing about myself and humans in general, is often the one thing you are trying to avoid, is usually the thing you’re already living with…in my case, it’s being alone. But that’s a blog for another day

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-18T22:26:22+0300

>
> First, I want to say +1000 to scales being only for fish! I gave mine up years ago and when I began the Leptin Rx and all these changes last year I was tempted to get one. I kept seeing everyone talking about 'goal weights' and measuring and weighing and I felt like I couldn't track my progress or know what I was doing if I didn't start weighing myself. But I decided that it would be best - for me - not to have a scale. And I've never regretted it. It's like letting go of a huge burden. You're not stupid and you'll know if you're gaining or losing weight. But the number... who cares. It's not directly tied to health. And I figure if we're going to eat seasonally our weight may fluctuate slightly throughout the year and I don't want to be obsessing about it all. I want to be healthy. And free!
>
>
>
> Second, I was listening to Sean Croxton's second interview with TS Wiley today ('Ask TS Wiley' show of April 2011) and she had some interesting things to say about hormones, lack of periods, solutions and even the tie-in to gut health. I have no idea if she is 'right' but I find her very intelligent, saying most of the same stuff I hear Dr Kruse saying, and really giving some great food for thought. Just in case you're up for another podcast and may find it helpful.
>
>
>
> Thinking of you....
>
> Lauren

Thank you Lauren. That means so much. It actually was a really hard thing to put out there. I felt very vulnerable and weak-minded for it! I almost deleted it. I walk a fine line with myself between venting and perpetuating. But I kind of had no idea I was holding on to that so tightly and no wonder I was freaking out so badâ€¦.I didnâ€™t weigh myself today but I wanted to. Till my brain kicked in and said, what are you going to do about it if you did gain?

Thanks for the tip on the podcast. I downloaded both of hers to listen to! I need the hormone help bad. My func med doc feels it will straighten itself out once I stop the leaky gut, which triggers the adrenal fatigue, which gives me pregnenolone steal and zero sex hormones. Which I totally get, but in the meantime, I have such terrible mood swings! OMG, this morning my tolerance was zilch! I seriously almost got in a â€œfightâ€￾ with Siri â€“ my iphoneâ€™s personal assistant. You know your hormones are bad when you are yelling at your phoneâ€¦

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-18T22:31:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25308>

> **AbetterME;25230 wrote:** I've been really touched reading your last post Jennifer. I was there last year, thinking I was having control on my life because I could "control" my weight. My life was miserable, but at least I had that. And I wasn't worth having someone in my life until I reached perfection. What a pressure to live with. I'm so much more in peace with myself since I let it go. Now I want to meet someone who will like me just the way I am. For those who don't, too bad for them. And like vkiernan said, if a good guy likes me the way I am now and support me through this, it can only get better.
>
>
>
> Souldanzer is so right. Aesthetic goals are ok, but healthy goals are way better. And by becoming healthy and improving the inside, the outside will follow and get better too.
>
>
>
> Continue venting and writing, it is very helpfull and often things appear less difficult once it's out.
>
>
>
> Have a good day Jennifer,
>
>
>
> Katy

Thank you, Katy! You mean, I'm not the only one? I really thought this was some secret behavior only I did! LOL

I am glad you're past it - because I'm not sure there ever is a "good enough," You know?

I hear myself saying, well if I was just under 140, I'd be ok with it. I'd feel better. But I know if I got to 138, I'd want 133. IDK, I'm not giving up my desire to banish the belly fat. I refuse to believe that I can't both feel good and be thin. But I am willing to get there the right way, the way that I can actually sustain it. I've lost and gained these same 15 pounds at least three times in the last two years and more than that if you go back to high school. I'm so sick of the war...

Gotta be happy where I am at to get to where I am going

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-18T22:53:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25315>

So, today has been a rollercoaster day for me. I chose to have coffee the last two days and was feeling kind of edgy, so today I figured Iâ€™d better cut it out. Itâ€™s not exactly on the list for leaky gut, AF, etc. But after my BAB (pork tenderloin, an egg made with unsweetened cocoa powder, and CO with cocoa powder and tea) I was feeling crappy. IDK, maybe pork and I donâ€™t get along or maybe it is left over from yesterday? At any rate, when I got to work, the first person I encountered I could feel myself getting annoyed. And it continued from there. I was in a seriously angry mood. It was like anger was just coursing through my body with no real reason. I knew it was bad when I got into a huge fight with Siri (my iPhone personal assistant). I was screaming at her to clean her ears out because she was getting my talk to text wrong. Something not right there. But then, as the morning went on, I started to feel better. IDK Iâ€™m kind of all over the place lately. Oh, I totally forgot that I had run out of 5 Htp, so I was off that and the L-tyrosine for more than a few days. I finally bought some last night and today was the first day I took them again. So maybe they have kicked in.

I also went bathing suit shopping last night. UGH Believe it or not, I didnâ€™t beat myself up about it too much but Iâ€™m not happy with current state of my body. I bought a few other things that were on sale but I gotta take some others back because one pair of shorts gives me bad muffin top and the other stuff just isnâ€™t working for me right now.

OH, I almost forgot â€“ I skipped the scale this morning. Partly because I knew if I did have a gain I would totally have had a meltdown. Meltdowns are not good for body. Oh, and I read this excellent article on stress and happiness and the ability to deal with stress â€“ when people who are happy in a deeper way (have more long-term, eudaimonic happiness), are faced with negative stimuli, they have more activity in the prefrontal cortex of the brain, which governs executive planning and higher-level thinking. They also have reduced activity in "lower" brain regions, like the amygdala, the seat of our stress and fear responses. People with eudaimonic happiness are also slower to evaluate negative events as being such, which could mean that they are also less likely to "freak out" over real upsets in life.

[http://www.theatlantic.com/health/archive/2012/05/what-we-know-now-about-how-to-be-happy/257310/](http://http://www.theatlantic.com/health/archive/2012/05/what-we-know-now-about-how-to-be-happy/257310/)

Kind of something that sounds worth cultivatingâ€¦being happy in a deeper way will help my adrenal glands stop â€œfreaking outâ€! Oh my, what would I do with that kind of freedom!

Oh no, you're not alone!! Like you, it was never good enough...I weighted 122 lbs (at 5'5'') and was still thinking I was fat and had to lose more...So, so sad. I spent so much time ruining my health instead of being happy for what I had. I'm glad it's behind me, and I know it's not easy to let go...

Your belly fat is a symptom your hormones are a mess. You'll lose it once you'll be LS and all hormones are balanced. Instead of having a goal linked to your belly fat, foccus on your hormones and what you can do to have them in the good place. It will be much easier to stay on the plan and way less frustrating. You'll be thin and happy one day, that's for sure. I'm confident I will be too even if it takes long.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-20T05:48:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25647>

Epigenetics is all in my head?

So, I read the blog and I am a pondering many things...I do believe in the axiom "If you wonder what you really want in life, take a look at what you got". I have wondered why I did not make some of my goals come true because I do know that I can put my mind to somethings and "whoosh" they happen. But other times, no so much...So what are my thoughts selecting for I wonder. I say I want to be married, have a family, be thin --- but I'm 40 and single without children and with some belly flub. So, how can i see this - the actual real life circumstances of my life - as something positive that I've selected for as some means of survival?

Putting the love life and kid thing aside, gaining weight made me focus on my health and now I have to heal my leaky gut. I have a strong motivation to avoid the fates of my grandmother (Alzheimer's) and my grandpie (Parkinson's Disease). My mom has been thin for most of her adult life. She never had to worry about her diet and she didn't exercise. Now she is paying the price. She's got pain in her body and no muscle mass, osteoporosis, migraines, and this horrible eye condition - pterygium eye, sounds horrible and her doctor told her she has to wear sunglasses all the time or it will get worse. She tries to make healthy changes, but has trouble sticking with it and I'm afraid she might think that she's too far gone. I hope not for her sake.

So, turning away from that philosophical musing....I have decided that I have to be nicer to my self and to my body. And that asking my body to heal itself and lose weight might be unrealistic. Kind of like if you're sick, you shouldn't try to run on the treadmill. I really have no choice here. I have to get my gut healthy and I really don't want it to take a year. I also have to find a diet I can live with forever. I still go in with the mindset of "making" myself eat a certain way to get certain results which have an end date. I think I'll be super strict, lose the weight and then eat "normally" and the weight will stay off. Well that just hasn't happened yet. I think most of us that have been on the diet wagon know that losing weight is easier than keeping the weight off. I've been eating keto diet but I am missing veggies. I'm going to start eating more. And eating more seafood. I've been taking the probiotics like the Fx Med doc said and I've got major bloat. I hope that's a good thing. I also feel like I have ADHA lately...I'm just all over the place with thoughts and changing topics and can't remember what I am saying in the middle of saying it and mood all over the place. IDK what the hell to think. Still taking the estradiol pill and using the progesterone cream I bought on my own. Thinking maybe I should get some 7keto DHEA. I was taking pregnenelone for awhile and was feeling more energy but than i started with some heart palps, so I cut it out. I honestly wish I could just like move back into a dorm or something. You know how when chicks live together they start cycling together? LOL You have no idea...I just dream about posting "OMG, it's a miracle, TOM is back"

nite!

Oh yeah. One more thing I wanted to make note of - I've been having some belly aches. Can't figure if it's from eating foods that don't agree w me, coffee, or if I'm having gastric erosions again. It's prob the coffee.

I got a slip from Fx Med doc for bloodwork...there are fasting labs involved, so I'm going to go on Wed.

I think I'll call her on Monday and ask for a few to be added. I want to be tested for candida. I think that can be done with bloodwork, right?

She ordered:

ESR

Folate

Magnesium

Selenium

Zinc

Vit C

Vit B6

Vit B12

Gliadin AB (IGG)

Thyroid AB (ATA, TPO)

Thyroid Panel

Free T3

Free T4

TSH

rT3

Thyroid antibodies

IgG4 food sensitivies for wheat, eggs (white and yolk), Salmon, chicken and casein

She asked if I had a Vit D level checked and I said maybe. She also asked about comp metabolic panel. I gotta check and see if they were drawn recently but I think I'll have her add the Vit D and mabye Insulin since I'll be fasting. I think I'm afraid to ask for the Cardio CRP...afraid to know...

The endo said that when I was diagnosed with Hashimoto's that my thyroid antibodies were negative, but my ultrasound showed IDK something about "diffuse infiltrates" or something, so he gave me Ha\*\*\*\*omotos...so it will be interesting to see if I have antibodies now.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-20T18:44:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-25725>

> **Jennifer;25794 wrote:** I got a slip from Fx Med doc for bloodwork...there are fasting labs involved, so I'm going to go on Wed.
>
> I think I'll call her on Monday and ask for a few to be added. I want to be tested for candida. I think that can be done with bloodwork, right?

Unfortunately candida is diagnosed through a stool test. I call it the "poop test". I did the Metametrix GI Effects test and waiting for results right now. So it's separate from the blood tests but I would ask you doc if you can get a stool test for candida anyway.

I would definitely get the Vit D and the hs CRP. Mine was 2.8 down from 3.5 last year. Mine is high and I'm not entirely sure why unless it's the Hashis. So don't worry about it.....better to know the number so you can work toward getting it lower if need be. Good luck on the tests!

Oh and I am finally starting to drop. I dropped a lb yesterday and haven't seen this number on the scale since I started CTing and gained 8# (over 3 months now). It will happen just keep it up. Also my appetite subsided about two weeks ago and I think that helped. I'm on the no lunch plan (maybe BP coffee or some fat) and then a full fatty dinner and it seems to be working. You will start to lose the belly, just give it time. You sould like you are built like me. I'm 5'8" 134# (today!), thin legs/arms, small bones, and a BELLY full of fat. I'm good at 125# but would like to be 122#.  I feel ya sistah!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-22T01:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-26128>

SO...I hadn't heard any results from my MRI. I usually go on the old adage, "no news is good news" but I figured let's hear it. So, I called for my results and to my shock, the endo says I actually do have a spot on my pituitary which he believes is a Rathke cleft cyst. He says it is really small and that he actually does not think it is causing my symptoms. But it needs to be watched and if it gets bigger then it has to be drained. YUCK

Guess this gives me serious motivation to get on board with the CT, especially icing the caratoids. Let's see if I can give an n=1 of no more pituitary cyst after CT. But first, labwork and vacation.

So, I still have no real reason for no TOM except leaky gut and pregnenelone steal and I have a cyst, but as the doctor said "It's not a tumor"

Well, I guess it doesn't matter because it doesn't change the game plan of diet and CT.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-31T17:07:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-28586>

> **vkiernan;27683 wrote:** Let me know how that works out with your adrenal fatigue. I want to do something and I was told no exercise with AF. I am assuming that means no weights either correct? What do you think?

Hi V!

I've been off grid for awhile. I have mixed feelings on the weights and AF. On the one hand, I know I can't stress my adrenals. And I know that I am not LS yet. I was in a hot spring (I know - don't kill me Dr K - I LOVE hot springs. I did go in the cold water for a 5 min and the air was cold up there in the mountains) and the next day I was sore. Realized it's from hoisting myself out of the pool. SAD But I need my pituitary working and if Dr K says lifting weights will tell my pituitary to work. I gotta try. I'll let you know how it goes.

I just spent 7 glorious days in Colorado. In Denver and Salida. I loved it. I did get up and eat my BAB every day but I def was not strict with the diet. I started out strong but eventually caved. My brother eats gluten free, and he is super fit so he eats what he wants and doesn't have to worry. That meant lots of GF treats around. We also celebrated his birthday. Lots of peer pressure to eat and "be on vacation." I know it's lame but I'm human!

So, now I'm back. Elimination diet starts Saturday. Gotta get back into CT and HIIT.

and Sleep I need that too...

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-02T22:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29093>

Just noticed that I'm filling out my bras once again. I am peeved with myself for gaining weight but happy that at least some of it is going back to "the girls." I think that means that my estrogen level is getting higher? No one has told me which hormone gives you a behind, but I do believe I remember reading the estrogen puts fat in your chest. The rest of my labwork came in the mail today. I posted them here : [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1424-Labs-up-for-hacking](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1424-Labs-up-for-hacking)

The func med doc ordered some food sensitivity tests and I came up high on wheat, eggs, dairy. Chicken and salmon were ok. Total bummer. I went to an acupuncture guy for NAET treatments and spent $$ on clearing egg sensitivity. I guess I really should have waited until after the elimination diet and really worked on leaky gut RX before going. I didn’t realize the whole mechanism behind food allergy being the stuff leaking out of your gut and into the body. Makes sense to stop the leaky before training your body not to attack it.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-05T18:09:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29556>

Day three elimination diet. So far, I am eating chicken and salmon primarily. I am also eating homemade sauerkraut, bone broth, kefir water, and kombucha. Sat and Sun I had no appetite. Monday I was STARVING. Today I feel pretty full. I did weigh myself Monday because I knew I had to get on the scale at the doctor and I figured better to see the naked pre-eating number than the full dressed and post BAB number. 145.5

Let me just focus on where I am going and not where I am, I guess.

Jack said on fb today “Are you living at the end of your history......or starting to live at the beginning of your time? Think about it some today then adapt if you need too.

My thinking is kind of stuck on the present as being the future. Which is not what I want to create. I have been thinking that when I gain weight, I “won’t be able to lose it.” I have been thinking that I’ll never see TOM again. I was also reading the thread by tweety “Eating Disorder Tendency” and that has got me thinking as well. Some great posts by Souldanzer and others over there. My choice is to keep my eye on the prize and live today as if I have already achieved my objective instead of focusing on what I don’t have right now. I’m gonna stop asking when TOM is going to return and just assume that each and every time I make a healthy choice, I am that much closer. Which means, elimination diet, leaky gut RX, adrenal RX, Leptin RX, sleep, CT, and most importantly keeping reign on my thoughts. I was late for work and totally working up my adrenals over this when I said to myself “Jennifer, is it worth stressing your adrenals because you’ll be 15 minute late?” NO – nothing is worth stressing my adrenals out for. That is my new mantra.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-07T16:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29893>

Day four elimination diet....feeling better every day...sticking with Leptin Rx but I still haven't gotten CT in. And I am unhappy with how late I have been staying up but living life keeps getting in the way! LOL

I'm still no TOM and still up in weight. Although several people have recently told me how good I look. My friend at work who knows all about my weight ups and downs told me she thinks I look better with some weight on (especially in the face). Well, I'm not happy with the belly fat. So I've decided I don't need to lose weight but I need to transform my body comp! I also decided that while doing Leptin Rx and elimination diet that I will also be following the Bulletprof Exec/Better Baby Diet (as close as I can with elminiation diet). I know Dr K and Dave are not fans of fowl, but besides fish and lamb, that's about all that's on the elimination diet meat wise. I also bought a T-Tapp DVD. It's supposed to be good exercise and good for lymph drainage or something. I'll let you know if I like it.

I'm pretty worried about my mom (and dad). She is stuck in poor eating and literally falling apart.  She has migranes, some crazy eye disease that mostly surfers and people who live at the equator get (which her doctor has told her she has to wear sunglasses all the time for), osteoporosis, and now body parts are falling out (prolapsed bladder last year and now her uterus). She is coming to visit for a few days next week and I am going to get her trying some of my foods. She was trying to do BAB but says it makes her want to throw up. Certain foods she can't eat in the morning - like eggs. So she's been eating Speacial K!  I told her, at least please buy a gluten free ceral, so she got Rice Chex. I suggested maybe she could do sweet potatoes or crock pot for breakfast, since I know she likes those. I also suggested she get the book "Sweet Potato Power" and a collagen supplement. You think that would help?

HI Katy! How ya doing? Loving your binge-free-ness!

Elimination diet is for food allergies. It's 28 days of no beef, no pork, no eggs, no dairy, no alcohol, no caffienne, no fun, no gluten, no dating...LOL It has some allowed foods that I won't eat like nuts, nut butter, non-gluten grains. And then some veggies, some fruit. Here is a link to the actual hand out she gave me.

[http://www.functionalmedicine.org/content\_management/files/ifm\_Comp\_Elim\_Diet\_091503.pdf](http://http://www.functionalmedicine.org/content_management/files/ifm_Comp_Elim_Diet_091503.pdf)

Then after 28 days, you add one food in at a time for three days. (one new food eaten for three days before moving to next new food) See if the food gives you a reaction. So, it is pretty much AI and then some.

Thanks for the care and concern for my mom. We'll see how she does when she's here. If I could only get my dad on board, then she would do it. My dad has opposite problems. He has lymphocytic colitis and is allergic to things like lettuce. They did paleo a la Nora Gedgaudas and both did well and lost weight. But my dad didn't feel better because of his weird colitis thing. SO, they fell off  I told them about the 23andme sale, and my mom sounded interested, so I pray they do that and start working on changing their future. My dad's dad is currently on Hospice care with Parkinson's Disease and my mom's mom passed with Alzheimers. Dad's mom has diabetes and mom's dad had MI. Well, at the very least I hope to help my mom work on healing leaky gut and giving up gluten and diet pop! AGHHHH

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-07T22:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29961>

Hmm...stumbled on some articles about Hypothalamic amenorrhea. My brain fog seems to be kind of bad today. I had that issue with feeling full physically but starting to feel hungry chemically (as in mood getting short). I ate a little canned salmon with avocado, sauer kraut and bone broth but now I feel out of it. Can't focus. I tried reading a few article on her website here. They are very interesting to me in regards to my situation of losing weight and not getting TOM. She talks about how your body gets used to your fat cells making estrogen and then your ovaries don't produce as much. When you lose weight, your ovaries need to step up but sometimes they don't. This is where I start getting lost.

She kind of goes both ways with it...she is a safe starch advocate and suggests at least 100g but she also says if you correct leptin signaling, you may regain your hormone balance. Any way, I need to go back and reread some of her stuff when my head is feeling less like it is stuffed with cotton.

[http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/](http://http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/)

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-08T05:47:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-30062>

From the Eating Disorders blog:

> Ghrelin and Agouti both stimulate hunger but in AN, they are uncoupled from their master controller leptin, **while IL-6 levels are quite elevated and fuel the conversion of T4 and T3 to make high levels of reverse T3. This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.** It also activates the alpha MSH program that allows them to live long periods on low calorie diets until they die. The protectant mechanisms afforded to calorie restriction are limited because of the uncoupling of IL-6 and leptin to the normal circadian cycles of light and temperature that normally control the genetic expression of these programs.

No idea if this is what's going on with you but that was probably it for me. Do you have high rT3? I don't think you need to have had AN for this "uncoupling maneuver" to occur necessarily. I'd say any source of inflammation could probably do it.

According to the article you posted you could be leptin insensitive/resistant and your brain would interpret that as very low leptin levels (just like AN) despite leptin being high and therefore, no menses.

I'm not sure I know what I'm talking about here  That's how I put it together for myself.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-10T17:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-30407>

One week in the elimination diet, although I slipped up yesterday. I was at a festival with friends and kind of forgot and ate read meat. I don't really get the whole avoid red meat part of the diet any way. I don't react to red meat. I think I figured out why Dr K is down on chicken, it has more O6 than grass fed beef. My doctor wanted me to buy this medical food supplement Metagenix Ultra Clear Sustain but it is made with rice flour and rice syrup, so I said no. But I am seriously considering this [http://www.betterbabybook.com/coconut-protein-formula-with-colostrum/](http://http://www.betterbabybook.com/coconut-protein-formula-with-colostrum/)

I just don't want to waste time. If I am doing the elimination diet, I want to get the most healing done

I feel better every day. More happy and I am so glad to be out of that hell of irritation and wanting to kill people. OMG that sucked.

We got into a bit of a discussion on nutrition yesterday and this guy, who is very fit and very athletic was telling me that he is following this Thrive diet. It's basically a vegan diet for athletes. He says he feels better and it sounds like he is doing it gluten free with his largest meal at breakfast. I did notice that he looked thin for him (he's never heavy). He likes this amaranth for breakfast. I guess it's not as bad as some of the other vegan choices out there but ....Well, it seems like he is not eating a lot of food and still doing his running, cycling, racing stuff. He said he is interested in eating optimally and wants to know "why everyone is suddenly gluten intolerant when we've eaten it for centuries." So, I sent him a few links. I'm sure we'll discuss it more. I have no desire to eat like a bird, but well people have to make their own choices I guess.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-30947>

> **vkiernan;31048 wrote:** What happened with the maca, I am taking it right now. Want to know if maybe it is affecting me and I don't know it.

I would have low blood sugar after taking it - as in, I would feel extremely irritable and angry and sometimes hungry. Symptoms always got better with eating. My theory is that the maca is a stimulant which encouraged my adrenals to work. So the adrenals ask the liver for energy and liver makes sugar. I was much worse when I was still primarily a sugar burner. Seem to be better since I did the super high fat thing for a week (a la Quelsen shakes) and is better now that I just don't get fired up over silly stuff (like being late for work or someone being a jerk).

I read your journal. Sounds like a big week for you. Good luck! I hope you can get of the HC and feel awesome!

I'm on some new supplements as per JanSz recommendations from Dr Kane's work. Taking Phosphatidylcholine, L Carnitine

and upped my fish oils and Vit D. My multi has B6 in it, so I am torn about taking it. Don't really feel like buying separate vits. Also upped the preg and add 7 Keto. Haven't picked up the Butyrate yet because I can't find it local. I am really interested in some T3. My T3 is low at t3, free 1.8 l (2.3-4.2) and rT3 is a high end rt3 29 (11-32). I think that gives me a ratio of 6% if I'm doing the math right and it should be 20% +.

Then again, maybe changing your dna with your thoughts is way easier than all this

speaking of strawberries, PaleoCowgirl...I've been eating them as well. But I mix them with stuff or eat them with CO, which is really good. For example frozen strawberries with coconut milk and avocado for ice cream and I made a salad dressing with balsamic vinegar and olive oil - YUM. Although, I do wonder if they are the source of bloating. I did read that CT blog Dr K pointed us to and she said she could not tolerate fruit while on TOM. Things that make you go hmmmmm. And I saw somewhere a post about sugar content of fruit. And something about certain types of sugar in fruit is better than others...Oh, I can't remember! Maybe raspberries are a better choice. Or Rhubarb is in season now!

Fruits Lowest in Sugar

•Small Amounts of Lemon or Lime •Rhubarb •Raspberries •Blackberries •Cranberries

Fruits Low to Medium in Sugar

•Strawberries •Casaba Melon •Papaya •Watermelon•Peaches•Nectarines•Blueberries

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-21T19:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-32756>

OMG, the bacteria in my gut knows when I'm stressed out? IF this doesn't convince me to avoid stressing out about things, I don't know what will! STESS causes a MESS in my body. No more stress:

Is Your Stress Causing Leaky Gut and Candida?

Did you know what goes on in your gut can dictate your mood, your perspective, and your sense of optimism?

Having earned the title of the second brain, the gut is in constant dialogue with the:

**Everyday stress could cause harmless microbes in your body to become pathogenic! These bacteria will rapidly multiply and mutate, leaving you at risk for an infection.

■Immune system

■Brain and neurological system

■Hormonal system

■Our inner ecology

The connection between the gut and our psychology is a two-way street. On the one hand, gastrointestinal inflammation and infection can contribute to things like depression and brain fog. (1) On the other, mental stress or trauma can cause intestinal permeability or â€œleaky gut.â€￾ (2) (3)As it turns out, psychological stress can do more than influence the integrity of our own cells. Scientists have found that the bacteria living inside of us can actually detect whether or not we feel stress. (4)

When we experience mental, emotional, or physical stress, we release stress hormones like cortisol and norepinephrine.These stress hormones are meant to protect us during potentially dangerous events. They move energy stores into the muscle, increasing our heart rate and our breath. And in the process, cortisol and norepinephrine shut down our digestive system and our immune system.

Stress hormones move our body from digestion to a fight-or-flight response.The bacteria that are normally present in the digestive system can read stress in the body and detect the presence of stress hormones. Researchers have found that usually harmless microbes will suddenly become pathogenic in response to the stress hormones that we release. (5) (6)

When bacteria become pathogenic, they multiply rapidly or mutate, and this often leads to infection. Once this happens, the inner ecology of the gut is thrown out of balance.

Keep in mind that stress not only signals bacteria to multiply and mutate, it also shuts down the digestive system and the immune system. These systems usually protect us from disease. This means that when we experience stress, we are more vulnerable than ever to bacterial overgrowth and infection.**

Once the inner ecology of the gut becomes imbalanced, the door opens for a wide range of health conditions to develop and manifest.

Anytime you change the diet, you change the bacteria.In addition to the unnoticed stressors of daily living, many of us spend a great deal of time feeling stress. Whether this stress involves global catastrophes, family drama, or personal struggles, the body releases the same stress hormones.

These stress hormones can ultimately contribute to a long list of health disorders, including infection from opportunistic bacteria.

As it turns out, the health of the digestive system is central to how good we feel on a daily basis. And our mental wellbeing influences whether or not the bacteria normally present in the digestive tract are able to cause disease.

This is why we have found that one of the best ways to consistently generate health is to eat fermented foods on a daily basis and, if possible, at every meal!

Diet can help to modulate the effects of stress in the body. Especially when the diet: />

1\. Contains plenty of fermented foods. Truly fermented foods have several beneficial strains of bacteria that keep overgrowth and infection in check when the bodyâ€™s defenses are down.

Beneficial bacteria found in fermented foods and probiotic beverages do things like soothe intestinal cells that have become inflamed and gently stimulate the movement of food through the intestinal tract. The multiple functions of beneficial gut bacteria are so important that some scientists refer to our inner ecology as a â€œvirtual organ.â€￾ (7)

2\. Contains foods that support the growth of beneficial bacteria. This means choosing foods that keep the body in a slightly alkaline state.

Foods that create an acidic environment are foods that contribute to inflammation and that trigger an immune response in the body. When the body is already in a state of high alert from outsides stressors, the last thing that you want to do is eat something that creates more work - or more physiological stress.While everyone is different in their response to food, there are some foods that are notoriously pro-inflammatory and best avoided. These are:

■Foods with added sugar or refined sugar.

■Processed foods.

■Foods containing industry byproduct oils, such as canola, safflower, vegetable, soy, and corn oil.

What to Remember Most About This Article:The health of your gut can influence your mood, which is why itâ€™s often referred to as the second brain. Suffering from gastrointestinal inflammation can contribute to issues like depression, and conversely, mental stress can cause a leaky gut. In fact, scientists have discovered that the bacteria in your body can detect when you feel stress!

Stress hormones can trigger your digestion to go into a fight-or-flight response. This can cause normally harmless microbes to become pathogenic in reaction to the stress hormones released by your body - leading to infection. This vicious cycle throws the inner ecology of your digestive system quickly out of balance.

The good news is that you can change your diet to change the bacteria in your body. Supporting your digestive health will support your mood on a day-to-day basis. Start out with:

1.A diet rich in fermented foods and probiotic beverages to support your gut with beneficial bacteria to keep infection at bay.

2.A diet that will keep your body in a slightly alkaline state. Acidic foods are known to cause inflammation and trigger an immune response, creating further physiological stress.

[http://bodyecology.com/articles/is-your-stress-causing-leaky-gut-and-candida](http://http://bodyecology.com/articles/is-your-stress-causing-leaky-gut-and-candida)

She also gave a very good explanation of short-waisted vs long-waisted and how each gains weight. I am short-waisted and pack it in my belly. So, pretty much I am never gonna have the teeny tiny waist even if I was rail thin. Add in the fact that I have scoliosis with a pretty significant curve in my Tspine (55+) and another in the Lspine (35), my waist is short. If I could get my spine straight, I'd be at least two inches taller and probably this weight would be fine on me. SIGH, don't think Dr K knows how to straighten spines (without surgery) but you never know.

Here's her claim:

Get the Most Exercise In the Least Time with T-Tapp

The T-Tapp Basic Plus Workout is one of our favorites for fertility–it’s a premier preconception detox workout because the movements are designed to maximize lymphatic circulation. The lymphatic system is one of the body’s primary ways of eliminating toxins. It’s made of lymph nodes and pathways that run throughout the body like blood vessels. These channels are used to eliminating toxins. The lymph system is not pumped by the heart, however, it’s pumped when we move, and that’s where T-Tapp comes in.

Teresa Tapp is a renowned fitness expert who designs her own workouts and has worked with professional models in the fashion industry.

The effects of T-Tapp are outstanding:

Detoxifies the body

Increases insulin sensitivity, which can promote loss of excess weight

Reduces stress

Skyrockets energy levels and mental clarity

Boosts metabolism

Improves cardiovascular function

Helps the brain gain better control over the body (builds neurokinetic function)

Improves your physique

Takes just 15 minutes and can be done anywhere, so it’s convenient and easier to keep up

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-27T20:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-33997>

not much to report. Elimination diet is almost over, then I'm supposed to add things in one at a time for three days at a time but honestly, i have no idea how to tell if I am sensitive to a food or not. I get a lot of bloating and it seems really random (as in my diet doesn't change hugely and some days I am and some days I'm not). So...kind of like I don't know what the point is but I keep going.

I have been super tired lately. I think the days working on the house with my mom were probably too much (for both of us). I've been a little worried that I messed up my adrenals because I've been SO TIRED all day and then catching a second wind at night. BUT, today I decided to skip my matcha green tea and see how I felt and low and behold, I feel like I have more energy without it! IDK, green tea and I still don't get along. I got nothing else really. Been kind of hungry past few days. Keep eating a BAB that I 'think' is really BIG but am hungry at lunch. So I eat. Been hoping to cut back for money reasons. Between working on the house, my family vacations, supplements and co-pays, the expendable income is a little small. Speaking of copays, I was supposed to see new gyn who does BHRT tomorrow but they called with a conflict. I am disappointed. Might look at compounding pharmacy website for a different doc. Also kind of peaved that the functional med doc has not responded to my phone call at all...frustration. I wanna get where I'm going and NOW...LOL

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-28T19:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34193>

I also notice changes in skin and energy, too.
