### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-19T22:46:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-39475>

Soul,

It's a good thing no other Krusers (they I know if) live in Buffalo - rite aide has lindt on sale for 1.99 and I'm driving all over town buying up the 90% bars.

In other news, I went to dds today to get the first 3 of my NINE cavities filed (dam# gluten). I opted out of the novocaine but he put some kind of gel on my gums before I opted out. Idk if that was smart or not. It was like 5 min of drilling. It felt hot and I kept telling my body I knew what was happening and it didn't need to signal pain. Lol. Now I am so tired, my jaw hurts and I have a bit if a headache. Prob nothing fish dinner and so fluids won't fix.

Trying to read BG5 on the sly at work. I've very interested in the raw fish E3 biz. But confused too. Good things I got no plans tonight.

Cooking of these marine food sources impedes their optimal usefulness to the optimal system functioning but it can easily be overcome by increasing those nutrients from other less dense sources or increasing the volume of cooked seafood eaten. One small example of the effect of cooking is the formation of estrogen in humans. When there is a large volume of undercooked or raw seafood eaten consistently it favors the formation of a special estrogen called estriol (E3). During rapid growth of a fetus a big risk to the system is uncontrolled growth in a high growth environment. This is commonly known as cancer. Estriol (E3) is the weakest of the three major estrogens. In fact, it is 1,000 times weaker in its effect on breast tissue. Vitamin D is found in great concentrations in many seafoods. Weston A Price mentioned it many times in his writings. Vitamin D was found to be exceedingly important in breast cancer prevention in 2009 and the link further cemented recently when the JAMA linked artifical light to breat cancer risk as well. Estriol is the estrogen that is made in large quantities during pregnancy and has potential protective properties against the production of cancerous cell transformation. This is the evolutionary protection of fetal structure by estriol in a highly powered growth state. This is yet another reason why maternal dietary seafood consumption correlates with brain power of children at 8 years old postnatally as I mentioned above.

Raw seafood will not limit the production of estradiol and estrone by the gut flora, as well that confers major advantages to humans as they age as well. These are commonly found in humans who eat large amounts of undercooked seafood. Both of these estrogens are been shown to have links to many human diseases when other systems are out of their allosteric hormonal balance.

Yes it helps constipation big time. You aren't taking very much. Have you tried taking more? Take 2 with BAB and and 1 with lunch, see how you do, then take 2 with BAB and perhaps 2 with lunch and see how you do and keep titrating it up until you find the good spot. Might also help with the bloating. I really use to take 7 with BAB and 3 or 4 with lunch and some at dinner if I remembered too. I remember when it all turned the other way and I couldn't take anymore. That was a good day although in the process it sucked. Bad reflux. No more though. I still think you need to add pancreatic enzymes to your diet too. That helps with the proten digestion.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-21T01:14:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-39839>

Ok, V, I'll give it a try and look for enzymes.

I kind of screwed up. I tried this scallop ceviche recipe that called for plums and tarragon. Two things I don't normally eat but I thought its summer what the hey. Had it for lunch and had a bad sugar crash. My stomach hurt real bad, I was hot and short tempered and hungry. I got nauseous, which happens when my blood sugar goes low but I was kind of scared my belly was unhappy with the raw scallops. Any way. I had a big dinner and i feel better now. I had haddock, avocado w spicy mustard, cooked a few scallops and gone sweet potato. I asked dr k about butyric avid and he said eat it. Lol. Eggs and butter are a good source but I tread lightly there. Gonna need to get some ghee. Of course nuts also have butyric acid which I wonder If that has anything to do with my craving them. I must say i did notice colon works better with them. But I'll stick w what dr k recommends. Actually oddly enough the things he suggested to eat are all thing I am eating. I seems like with the new challenge of eating seafood raw, I've been naturally eating the right things. I've been eating seafood for two meals a day for over a month now (started when I was doing elimination diet). I've been drinking homemade booch and eating homemade kraut. Moving away from the bloaty veggies but I'm eating berries and dark chocolate. Gave up the coffee for the most part. Some ghee but mostly CO. I NEED yo get my carbs down, I know but overall doing ok I think!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-27T05:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-41451>

Saw the new gyn today. I loved her personality and she is very easy to talk to but she is pretty CW. suggested cardio when I said my weight is higher than I like. But any way, she said quit taking the estradiol and get repeat labs. She rechecking my TSH which I'm happy about because I've been on the "pig" as my endo puts it and I wonder where I'm at.

Other than that I don't have much go say. Well I am kind of wondering if I'll ever get LS. I still eat three meals and have only ever been able to skip on weekends. Should I be eating more at the BAB? I eat a lot of CO, believe me. I've decided not to buy any more chocolate although I change my mind about that a lot. I need to give it up. I've been noticing the sugar crashes. It's probably triggering my adrenals. I also am going to stop the FODMAPS. God help me. I love avocado and coconut milk. Def stopping the fruit. I gotta do it! Might keep the sweet potato. Still need to get the book Sweet Potato Power. Half listened to a podcast with the author and Abel James (fat burning man). I like her. She does crazy experiments. I should get a glucometer. Put it on the list. Nite kiddos!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-27T23:26:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-41588>

Ok! Breakup time  U know toilet, it's just not working out anymore. I thought we were going to walk the same path in this life but now that I've looked deeper and peeled away some more layers on the search for \*me\* I am discovering that might not be true after all. No, there is nobody else, I promise. No I have not cheated on you. Well, maybe I'm cheating a bit.... leptin rx, epi-paleo, and some frosties.....not of the sugar-glazed kind... nothing serious, though, I swear. So yeah, I hope we can be friends

Well, add on a lb of shrimp (wild, no heads or shells and cooked) to the earlier. I have a space issue with my tummy. If I eat til my brain shuts off the hunger my tummy is distended and uncomfortable. But since I finished my earlier affair, I chose to lay down in bed for a while instead.

I was just going to add in the avo's. Sorry they're not working for you. You do nuts despite AI?

Squid is the one seafood my children actually eat and love. It's pretty tasteless... sort of the tofu of the seafood world... and will absorb the sauce it's in. I do caution against buying a whole one and cleaning it yourself... it was, um, interesting. This 2-foot long creature on my cutting board, all wobbly and weird. i suggest small ones or ones already cleaned (I read the ink can stain your fingers for weeks and your clothes forever. Perhaps we could use it to print optimal t-shirts)

Lmao...and I have no arse to laugh off!!

Nuts. Well there is another one. I love them. Total binge Fest though. They Are not optimal for me because of the O6 : O3 deal. But I do buy the brazil nuts for selenium. At least that's what I say.

Well today I was out of chocolate and out of sweet potatoes so what does Jen do? Eats an apple. I baked it and ate it w ghee and CO. Kind of counter productive.

The squid was interesting. I pan grilled it b/c I don't have a grill. A little rubbery but they taste good w evoo and balsamic vinegar. Then I was still hungry so I tried oven baking some. Pretty much the same. I like the tentacle parts the best.

What shall be the next adventure in seafood eating? Mussels?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-28T03:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-41609>

> **Jennifer;41826 wrote:** Squid ink tie dye. The way grok would do it. Lol. I think I'll have to get more creative but seems like you don't cook them long or they would be super chewy? I didn't mind them. And it was cheap. But not too filling. Almost ate whole package

Trust me, if you eat the WHOLE squid it's super filling. And cheap (whole thing cost me $6, fresh caught, from the fishmonger.) However, it took me a month to want to face any part of the squid again. And, yes, flash cooked is typically best (or cooked in some sort of sauce). I've also heard NZ chefs talk about marinating it in milk first... but I'm guessing that's not really epi-paleo and I never have milk on hand. (They don't cook with the milk, it's tenderising somehow.)

LOVE the bathroom-breakup saga. (Your cartoon, Jen, and Soul's hilarious post follows that lovely line in BG 6 about a smile lighting your heart and cooling your brain and reducing your cortisol. You guys rock!)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-29T21:05:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-41835>

> **Souldanzer;39547 wrote: Jen I noticed a huge change in myself after getting out into the sun every day for extended periods of time. I think not cooping up inside is one of the more important things to get your hormones back to normal.
>
>
>
> We're all so focused on avoiding artificial light after dark.... let's start focusing on getting \*daylight\* during the day as well and see what happens. I'm pretty sure this was one major important part for me feeling a bit better lately - getting real daylight and sun for as many hours as I can during the day. Now it's like my body flips the switch at night. Sun down and I want to go to bed.
>
>
>
> > **Jennifer;39570 wrote:** brain is super active today - lol - fishy food?
> >
> > JK quote : "Never fight your body........you have your life and your unlived life. In between is your life's resistance. This is where you are. When you move to either pole and immerse yourself in you........you will find the missing link."
> >
> >
> >
> > NEVER FIGHT YOUR BODY - yes, body always wins somehow better not to stress the system.
> >
> > But the rest of the quote confuses me. I gues the life and unlived life part and moving to either pole...
> >
> >
> >
> > JK QUOTE Here is the key point. It is about mtDNA. This is why they went extinct. My thoughts are tied to leptin because it controls the human placenta. Although modern humans share some nuclear DNA with the extinct Neanderthals, the two species do not share any mitochondrial DNA, which in primates is always maternally transmitted. This observation has prompted the hypothesis that whereas female humans interbreeding with male Neanderthals were able to generate fertile offspring, the progeny of female Neanderthals who mated with male humans were either rare, absent or sterile (in line with Haldane's rule). If mtDNA is not present then you eventually die out.........I think interbreeding is what killed them and the fact that their DNA allowed for impulsivity......so they would have been quite unstable deep into life because of some interesting features unique to them.
> >
> >
> >
> > If I have any prayer at all at reproducing, I need to be LS and it's taking me a L O N G time to get there (totally on me).
>
>
>
> Jennifer;41669 wrote:** Saw the new gyn today. I loved her personality and she is very easy to talk to but she is pretty CW. suggested cardio when I said my weight is higher than I like. But any way, she said quit taking the estradiol and get repeat labs. She rechecking my TSH which I'm happy about because I've been on the "pig" as my endo puts it and I wonder where I'm at.
>
> Other than that I don't have much go say. Well I am kind of wondering if I'll ever get LS. I still eat three meals and have only ever been able to skip on weekends. Should I be eating more at the BAB? I eat a lot of CO, believe me. I've decided not to buy any more chocolate although I change my mind about that a lot. I need to give it up. I've been noticing the sugar crashes. It's probably triggering my adrenals. I also am going to stop the FODMAPS. God help me. I love avocado and coconut milk. Def stopping the fruit. I gotta do it! Might keep the sweet potato. Still need to get the book Sweet Potato Power. Half listened to a podcast with the author and Abel James (fat burning man). I like her. She does crazy experiments. I should get a glucometer. Put it on the list. Nite kiddos!

Sweet Potato Power is a great read. It convinced me that maybe a few carbs in that form aren't so bad. It also talks a lot of about reading labs and of course reaffirms why we eat the way we do.

I can relate to fruit and chocolate being trigger foods that need to be avoided. I've been on a fruit kick so much lately; tis the season right? But that doesn't mean I should be eating it! Good luck with fodmaps. I've been trying, too...never been too much into coconut milk, but I do like avocados. Hello guac! Or, I guess, goodbye!

Glad to hear you like your doctor. Hopefully the gyn can get you on the right track.

Thanks Lauren. I read your post on Souls page and got a lot out of it. Especially the hypervigilence part. I'm experiencing that in my dating life right now. And giving myself a hard time over it yet I should be thankful that I'm concerned with self preservation. Lol. The last one wrecked me! Of course I'm hypervigilent. But where is the space for a different outcome? A new paradigm. I'm telling myself stop trying to predict the future. I always want to know the end result before I make a choice and I guess that's really just not possible for all situations in life.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-06T16:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-43197>

So I have a complaint and some observations.

First the complaint. My elbow is KILLING me. It is the wierdest thing. If I lean on it the wrong way, I get a sharp shooting pain like I leaned on broken glass. I keep looking at it and it's not swollen. I don't think it's red. It's my left one, so I def lean on it more than the right. Any ideas? I saw that Dr K said mag oil was good for tendon.

Some observations. I seem to care less about food. I think I'm eating less but its more because I just don't care. I go to bed hungry some nights because my dinner was light. I've cut fruit except a few cherries a day (i have huge bag, should probably just give them away). I still eat like 1/3 a sweet potato a day but I'm getting sick of them so I probably won't buy any more. I don't think I'm losing belly fat. My clothes are still tight and I had to put some shorts away that no longer fit. I seem to have thicker thighs. Clothes are tight in places I'm not used to. Am I growing a butt? I'm still bloating and not eating FODMAPs so I am guessing it's something else going on. My sleep kind of took a dump with some emotional things going on. Lots of things seem to be coming to a head, but not the cystic acne on my face! DOH My gramps is in a Hospice bed on his last days with Parkinson's Disease. This is really rocking my dad's world. Does that mean it's rocking mine? If it's affecting my dad's cells, does it affect mine? I fell really uneasy all the time. Like something big is about to let loose. Dating is rocking my foundation on a regular basis. Constantly questioning the things I think. Thinking is overrated

I'm not using as many supplements - taking the probiotic, drinking my homemade bouch, (out of sauerkraut but have a cabbage head, just need the time to do it), 7 keto, pregnenelone, Vit d, Mag, red clover, aloe, hcl when i need it, my pig hormone.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-43227>

Also of note really short tempered today. Interesting since I'm off my estrogen and OTC p cream

Don't underestimate that "taking care of the parents" stuff. It is supremely impactful on one's mental state of being. Don't fight it, you SHOULD have lots of uncomfortable feelings, that is appropriate reaction to that kind of stress. One of the hardest things about parents passing is the "I'm an orphan feeling". Ask someone who's parents have passed - it's very strange and takes a while to deal with and move past.

Thanks Cavemam. I know my dad is having a hard time watching his dad pass and he tends to stuff things down like I do. The first area to get hit for me w stress is usually my sleep and stomach. So like him. So I wonder how to turn off those genetic switches. If my dad mellows out and becomes more accepting and expressive, will I? And vice versa? I'm doing a lot of asking questions about how I'm feeling and why and using some healing techniques. Staying calm. Centered accepting. Saying yes to life.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-09T18:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-43907>

I saw a new PMD today. My func med doc recommended her. At first I was disappointed because its a teaching practice and I got stuck talking to another Doogie Howzer about my history. But I liked her. She is interested in my HDL which was 145. I guess I win the prize because she says it's the highest she's ever seen. She says she is researching HDL so I guess that makes me interesting. She ordered a VAP. She asked about a mammo and I said no, I don't believe in them. She laughed which I really didn't know what to make of. Later she repeated that and said "you're my kind of person." so I'm glad about that. My most recent TSH was 2 something which makes me happier. Last one was 4. But my free T4 is only 0.7. Touch low. She says she could give a little more Armour but she'll wait two months. Only thing is after all that I forgot to have her look at my elbow! Doh and after Doogie Nd I talked about it for a good 5 minutes.

I told the nurse not to tell me my weight. But nowadays they give you that print out of your visit and I saw the number. I give myself 5 pounds for clothing and BAB. I'm not thrilled about the number but it's not horrible. I still would like to mobilize 10 pounds of fat into muscle and/or energy.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-09T18:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-43912>

> I told the nurse not to tell me my weight. But nowadays they give you that print out of your visit and I saw the number.

I step on the scale backwards, while I clearly announce that I do not want to know my weight b/c I'm recovering from AN. I then make sure to not look at the sheet that they are writing on, even if it is left with me in the exam room while I wait for the doc.

Important if there's print-outs involved: Bring a black permanent marker and before they give you the sheet, ask the nurse to use the marker to erase it.

For me, I've decided I don't need to be weighed a few times a week..... not necessary. I allow them to weigh me where it's important - anesthesiologist needs to know my weight, I get that. But most times, I now decline.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-09T20:17:09+0300

>
>
> Important if there's print-outs involved: Bring a black permanent marker and before they give you the sheet, ask the nurse to use the marker to erase it.
>
>
>
> For me, I've decided I don't need to be weighed a few times a week..... not necessary. I allow them to weigh me where it's important - anesthesiologist needs to know my weight, I get that. But most times, I now decline.

Good tip on the marker. Thanks Soul. I just need to build a butt. Prob squats and LHT. I know someone in my neighborhood who does figure competitions and trains at a locale gym. I had a thought about joining (not to do figure competitions though!). We'll see what I do with that thought.

Of course after my work drama from yesterday, I could care less about that number. Seems insignificant in comparison. Although thankfully I think the hub bub is dying down.

And even more than that is sitting in a gown telling ppl you feel really good!  ur next for that one, Soul!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-12T16:12:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-44520>

So I have been noticing that I care less about food and eat less in general. Yesterday I went to a funeral for my great uncle and a lunch with family afterward at an Italian place. Can u say gluten-Fest? Luckily they had Italian sausage although it was in tomato sauce. I had salad, sausage and the chicken meat I dug out of chicken parm. And butter. Sorry. I was scared I'd be starving and feel sick w low blood sugar but I was surprisingly satisfied. Hmm

I'll take it.

Been really not doing the CO and notice a lot less bloating.

I read with interest what Katy wrote about her consult and Hashimotos and bingeing and inflammation.

Even stress doesn't provoke an eating response in me right now. Hmm.

Although I will admit that I am drinking wine more than once a week. And it does crap out my sleep when I do. I remember dr k saying that's why he doesn't drink wine any more.

I find myself getting annoyed with people who don't feel good but won't change their eating habits because "I don't like that". Lol. It seems so stupid now. Like how do you think you'll like getting Alzheimer's or Parkinson's disease? Seems to me that if you asked someone fighting one of those, they'd say the f-ing bread and pasta wasn't worth it.

Sorry family stuff.

Gramps has Lewy body dementia. His sister had it too, so my dad and his brothers are a bit freaked.

On another note, dad takes nexium to suppress stomach acid b/c w/o it he'll have constant diarrhea. Any known substitutes for that? Or I guess in his case is it ok? Idk. My dad has the most backward gut I know. Lettuce makes him sick (lymphocytic colitis).

Hi Jen!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-15T17:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-45025>

> **AbetterME;45337 wrote:** Hi Jen!
>
>
>
> Got a question for you...I know you did the Elimination Diet...How did it go? Did it help? My doctor wants me to do it, and I'm not super exited about it, but if it can help...Can I have your input on it?? Thank you

Hi Katy. Well I felt like you that it was a stupid exercise. However after I did it, I got TOM so maybe it's worth it. At least you can eat fish. Dr K feels fowl has no nutritional value and chicken is one of the approved meats. I went ahead and ate some grass fed beef b/c I don't feel that I have an issue w it. The adding things back in wasn't very insightful for me. I don't have patience for that. The only take away was that cheap chocolate makes me break out. So now I eat chocolate, wine, ghee, red meat and pork. Otherwise everything else is the same as elimination diet so no biggie really.

Hope that helps. I would say do it. It's 3 weeks.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-25T15:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-46985>

Been away at gramp's funeral. Observing how family members deal with stress grief and loss. Pretty sure my dad has blown out his adrenals. At least he rests instead of drinking coffee or exercising more.

I fell off wagon day of funeral. Had four meals (late lobster dinner) lots of blueberry goat cheese and two cookies that I know dang well were not gluten free (or sugar free). I also drank wine which totally wrecks my sleep now.  so sad for me because I love my wine but I also love my sleep.

I brought a big supply of beef w me for in the car and BAB. But I went thru it all. In the 9 hr car ride home, we ate at Boston market. I had just chicken. Oh boy my stomach was so unhappy. I really thought I was gonna lose that "lunch" and prob would have felt better if I did but I hate to vomit do I take writhing in pain instead.

This morning was a hard BAB to swallow. I like beef in the morning as fish turns my tummy. But the fridge us pretty bare. I tried to choke down salmon and eggs. Knew I was short so I pulled out some protein powder and had that with eggs. Not the best but it got me through.

As soon as my parents hit the road, I'm going to farmers market and restocking on pork side, beef, eggs and whatever veggies look good.

I'm sitting outside trying to get some sun. I have a lot of trees in my yard.

Life is weird

And that's all I got

More thoughts on k2. Thought I needed it for my teeth but now I think I need it for more

> **Shijin13;46928 wrote:** I follow my intuition when eating... seafood has become a priority in how I eat, with skeletal meats and organs interspersed throughout the week. Veggies of choice have always been broccoli, kale, collards, cabbage, cauliflower, Brussel sprouts etc... they are my favorite veggies - but then again I'm E-dominant... I can use all the help I can get.
>
>
>
> I have discovered in the past 3days - that IF I DONT TAKE THE MAX DOSE OF K2 my FBG SPIKES! I knew I was running out and thought I ordered in plenty of time - but haven't received the order yet...grrr - 2 days w/o 45mg K2 - and my FBG this morning spiked to 109mg/dl!
>
>
>
> I've also discovered since really pushing the seafood - that chicken is crap. I DO NOT LIKE IT. Who BRIAN WASHED me into thinking it actually tasted good. blech! I told DH I will not eat chicken any more. yucky. it doesn't satisfy me AT. ALL! And when I do eat it - I have to load it w/Ghee to even make it palatable and cover it in horseradish - which I dislike!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-26T18:14:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-47261>

Listening to Lynne MacTaggart very interesting. You can trick your brain into thinking everything is ok and turning off pain receptors By keeping your mouth full of salvia and repeating a positive intention. A dry mouth is the first signal to your body that something is wrong. Now If only I could have my dental work done w/o getting all my salvia sucked out. Lol

She also says that you can get benefit of exercise just by sitting on a chair and thinking about exercising - lifting the weight, flexing your muscles etc. gonna try it w abs!

It MUST work this way. You have to be confident AND womanly. There is no need to be a bitch at all, but do not feel bad about rejecting them if they don't behave in a way that you want in a mate. They can smell that a mile away, and will know you don't have to be a priority.

I bet you want someone who values you enough to chase, no?

The other thing, get out there and do activities around men. Actually - this is where I lose my madam powers. . . I've met all my closest people on the internets. Interestingly enough, the "chemistry" was there with the ones I had a real connection with. That is a perfect gel with my personality. Figure out who you are, and who you want to be with - then go BE where they are, but be doing something that YOU want.

There is zero wrong with you going on lots of dates. It would be fun (for me!  ) if you started down that path then you could tell us all about it.. .

Instead of asking yourself what you could do to have the guys you meet like you, ask yourself if they are good enough to share your like and if they deverse YOU. Be yourself, go there to have fun and to meet a new interesting person, no pressure, and you'll see.

I'm sure when hormones are balanced you feel so much better about yourself and you feel sexier. That is attrative. And you are probably more confident too, so you might attract all types  Then it's you to choose

Have a great time tonight. Think about the moment, and enjoy it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-49077>

> **Souldanzer;49545 wrote:** Dr k commented somewhere that vinegar and the acetaldehydes (sp) in ferments do not mix so well with hashi's.

He's commented several places about ferments being not-so-great for Hashi's and for gut issues, actually. Ferment less and you get more sugar. Ferment more and you get more _acetaldehydes_. There was a blog comment or two about this, too. I think the consensus was that a bit was OK, a lot not. And he was definitely asked on Ask Jack if he'd eat them with Hashi's and pretty sure he said no. I'm trying to make sense of it, too, as I'm operating as if I have Hashi's until I get 3 consecutive tests that prove otherwise. I think this is 'context' again. Context makes my head hurt.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-09-03T05:10:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-49112>

So, for those of you dating vicariously, had date with B tonight. He's a nice guy but after talking on the phone, I could sort of tell that we probably weren't gonna be a match. But as everyone says to date outside of your type, I met him. Very nice guy and we had a lovely chat but no bells or whistles.

Here's the interesting thing as we women were talking on another thread about hormones and taking notice of the opposite sex and attraction. So, this guy obviously works out (he had guns) and says he is mostly Italian but blonde haired, blue eyed and he complained that he doesn't have a lot of hair, like on his face and chest (but full hair on head). He says he only needs to shave once a month and has three chest hairs. Now, of course, this immediately makes me starting thinking about his hormone panel and what he would have to tweak (or ice) to grow hair on his chin - formulating the Ask Jack in my mind "Dear Jack, men without facial/body hair, good swimmers or no?"

But any way, although he def probably has a nice body, as much as I can tell as he was fully clothed, the ovary wasn't flipping. On the other hand, the younger gentleman who goes by T, shaves his head (pretty sure it because he has to) but has nice facial hair and nice body (fit but not the guns) makes the ovary flip pretty darn well, so....

keep on keeping on, I guess.

Oh almost forgot...so I did make the liver meatballs. I hope they are gonna taste good because they smelled really bad! I def dumped way too much turmeric and then too much cinnamon (I put it in everything, I weird like that) in them, so I'm praying that is what the smell was and it wasn't the liver. I didn't notice the smell until after I added the spices. Any way, I also threw in some zucchini and basil. IDK, could be a weird combo. And an egg. My liver meatballs are always so runny...any advice on that?

It is really cheap where I go and I can get a monthly pass for unlimited - twice a day 6 days a week is their rx for osteoporosis patients - so it's actually quite a bit. ### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-09-07T05:11:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-49922>

So func med doc. I wanted to leave there w a script for T3 but instead I left with a slip for more bloodwork. Sigh. I've had so many TSH's drawn this year and they've been all over the place.

So what's the take on the "your Armour thyroid has T3 in it"? Should I be ok w an increase in Armour or ask for T3 directly. My ratio is low. I'll have to insert it here after I look it up.

So ft3 is 1.8 and rt3 is 29 so I think that gives me a ratio of 6.2. Which sucks. Right?

I complained about the weight gain, acne and bloating. I told her don't tell me weight but it's on old fashion scale and I could hear her move the big one. Yikes. Not happy.

She says it's my gut making me fat and giving me acne  no ship Sherlock but fix it! Make it stop!

But overall I'm 100x better than the first appointment. She has a review of systems complaints sheet and she said my score I gave myself today was half the score from last time. I believe it.

She was gonna recommend a fiber supplement but I told her I'd rather go after the cause of the chronic constipation than take fiber. I also told her I never took the medical food because it had rice flour and rice sugar in it. I don't eat that. And I told her I stopped taking the fish oil supplement and eat fish twice a day. She said my skin is dry (I agree-I had SCALES on my knees- or is it the fish- HA). She ordered BW to test omega 3 & 6 fatty acid. Idk

And so I bought myself a stool analysis. But her kits were expired so not yet.

Had long convo w dad about this and about my suspicion of sbio (thanks soul for the info). I started gaining weight after taking the double dose Prilosec for the gastric erosions (thanks J2). So the low stomach acid probably lead to Undigested food getting into small bowel, large bowel And then the mess really got messier. Any way, my dad says small particle colloidal silver for a colon reset. Any thoughts?

I do anything to stop the bloating, weight gain and acne. My brain is finally changing its mind about Food choices and "cheats" (as in a cheat only messes u up one day).

This is serious folks

Some random thoughts based on Dr K's consults with me about healing my gut.... I'm assuming you're on HCl but perhaps you need to up the dose? Add digestive enzymes? NAC and tumeric supp? ### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-09-10T03:43:29+0300

note taking (like soul)

> **Jack Kruse;51151 wrote: The evolutionary tale of fertility is quite simple. We are designed to change our LDL to pregnenolone using free T3 and Vitamin A as co factors from our diet. When the cell get pregnenolone it looks to the cytosol and assesses whether the environment is more oxidized (inflamed) or more reduced chemically(not inflamed). Once that decision is made pregnenolone is transfered down one of two paths depending upon the reduction/oxidation surroundings of the cell. If oxidized pregnenolone is shunted to cortisol to help us survive. If the cells are reduced pregnenolone is shunted to the pro- gestation hormone called progesterone. When proegesterone is high so is estrogen, testosterone, DHEA and vitamin D. That is why the distal hormone chain tells you how inflamed or reduced you really are. The giant circle of life. If you are not fertile your goal is to become reduced. It means you need to be come boring, cold embrace the dark and sleep and then begin to have mad sex once you adapt to this reduced life. The more you stress about not getting knocked up the more you will shunt your pregnenolone to cortisol for survival. This is the basic biochemistry of fertility. Inflamed means LR. LS = reduced. The giant circle of life all from the Hormone 101 blog. It is critical to all our biology and our species.
>
>
> Jack Kruse;51152 wrote:** If you dont have free T3 (hypothyroidism of any cause) or Vitamin A (bad diet and is pretty rare even on a SAD) your cells will be constantly oxidized and over time chronic oxidation destroys cellular signaling. When signaling is broken nothing works.......never mind having a kid. This is why the obese and the anorexic have fertility issues. It is why the old and the sick also have trouble being fertile. This applies to both sexes. It is more critical in women.......but it is as deadly in men. Oxidation kills slowly and reduction increases longevity and fertility.

Hormone 101 blog post key point:

Dr. Mitch Moore asksJack so I re=read the H101 post. I read it to say high stress causes HIGH CORTISOL which, due to pregnenolone steal, causes decreased T3, sex hormones, sarcopenia and osteopenia.

But you said my cortisol was LOW..and you wanted it higher

So low cortisol could be a symptom also of pregnenelone steal – right? And increasing T3 and vit D is the key to making more pregnenelone and other hormones as well – right?

09-09-2012, 05:54 PM #2

3,837

Answer: @Mitch when the effect is CHRONIC the decision in the cell is always on…..meaning all the pregnenolone shunts to cortisol to hep you survive. When we measure it in the plasma it is low…..that is a sign the PVN nucelus is working over time and this is sign your oxidizing your cells. The result is all the hormones going the other way in the hormone synthesis chain are very low……..that is the reduction path. Re Read BG 11. Low cortisol= low melatonin = epithelial cancers= LR. Oxidation =LR. Low cortisol is not a good thing. When the process first begins……ACUTELY you will have hyper cortisolism for a time until you fatigue your PVN nucleus output chemicals in the hypothalamus. Do it long enough and you oxidizes (age) your body, your sleep declines and your body fails with your body composition.

The critical equation of life = LDL + T3 = pregnenolone. If free T3 is low you cant make pregnenolone. If you live a life in the modern world you are in survival mode (living in the cortisol pathway of oxidation) shunting the already low amount of pregnenolone your cells can make to cortisol at the sake of making progesterone. Progesterone is the base hormone for the testosterone, estrogen, DHEA, etc (the hormones of fertility)……..The more we shunt to survival mode hormones the less fertility ones we make. More survival path activation of cortisol makes the cell more oxidized the faster the cell ages…….Moreover, the more oxidized the cell becomes the more Vitamin D has to be used to offset this increased oxidation by the immune system so Vitamin D levels also falls with oxidation. I hope this clarifies it. This is why modern humans are infertile (1 in 7 couples) because to have a child you must be more reduced than oxidized because it favors a higher level of the pro- GESTATION hormone called progesterone. Most modern humans are starved for progesterone because modern life keeps them in the survival pathway of cortisol. The cell always has to chose between survival or reproduction and inflammation is the traffic cop for the decision. Hence why we see upside down PG/E2 ratios in all modern neolithic diseases. when this happens the lab panel show increased HS CRP, increased E2, and LR, with LOW free T3 levels

wellllll, there is a box in Rochester that I'd vouch for, I don't know anything about the Buffalo area. I can look into it if you are serious. I know the questions to ask (as well as the answers I want to hear.) At my place, we have a Strength & Power class 2 x week, those classes are only olympic weightlifting and powerlifting, that is what you want. (That is basically all I do) Sometimes it is called a barbell club or oly club. Separate $ from the CF membership. You will not be doing crazy conditioning that JK is always referring to as CF madness. CF can be a worthwhil endeavor, but one has to do their research first with the owners.

OK, little bit of freakiness with the messages for today. Getting a strong vibe that there is a lot of confusion in the air and you gotta go inward to figure it out. JK's facebook is also saying this;

Today realize when you're in the midst of that struggle its hard to see what you really are. Finding something more important than you are and dedicate your life to it is where happiness resides.

my favorite astrological lady says for today:

September 14th

Pluto Squaring Uranus, exactly on September 19th

Sometimes we a...sk ourselves why the world doesn't see who we are? Hello Earthlings, I am here, don't you see my heart? My facade has been striped.........don't you see me?

There is so much shifting going on that people cannot always see much beyond their own fog and dust around personal demolition, leaving us to a very individual process.

Even a bright light in the fog usually focuses within a short range. Chiron continues to be in a 5 degree orb of Neptune (in Pieces). Meaning the task of becoming something fresh requires wound work. There is something profoundly exquisite about becoming, but it also most exhausting.

As we are in the in-between stages, answers can land, and they can float away.

Let the fog serve its purpose, wrapping you in a mystical blanket of self-awareness. Look to your own heart for responses. Communication and relationships with others may prove to be difficult, but only because the Universe is pointing you back toward your own process of becoming.

Let the mystical fog hold your grace. The magic can happen with ingredients already within; this is the music that fed Rumi his words.

Namaste ~

### chocolate — 2012-09-23T21:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-53679>

Jennifer, I am completely confounded by copper and zinc. In hyper thyroid, you have too little copper. In autoimmune diseases, you have too little copper. Copper is a huge antiviral. But it is also very bad in the wrong balance with zinc or without the right cofactors. I know molybdenum, boron, and phosphorus are all players and I'm very intimidated by the metals. We were taking copper for awhile, but I have to admit, the thoughts of being wrong give me the cold sweats. We were taking the copper for the phenalynine that was in the kelp giving us the jitters.

I remember something about magnesium supplements making it difficult to supplement with zinc. We must have crossed that bridge because I'm ok with it. My kid was textbook barfing on the zinc supps. She could only get zinc with meat. Which is appropriate...but she was very sick and on awful prescription meds. She takes it every now and then, she can tell by the way she feels if she'll throw up or not, and doesn't take it if she gets that hunch. Copper is really important for hormones.

It seems like it could be really good to keep the zinc in balance for someone hypothyroid.

### chocolate — 2012-09-24T01:38:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-53710>

[http://www.health-science-spirit.com/copper.html](http://http://www.health-science-spirit.com/copper.html)

COPPER SALICYLATE

A Potent Inflammation Fighter and Rejuvenator

By Walter Last

> A deficiency of transport and storage proteins can lead to copper deficiency in the same way as low dietary intake. Copper deficiency is widespread. I regard copper deficiency as the main cause of grey or white hair. For adults a daily intake of 2-3 mg is generally recommended but intakes of less than 1 mg are very common. Unfortunately there is no reliable test to see if we have enough copper. Blood tests are totally unreliable, and also hair tests are uncertain.
>
>
>
> Supplementation with high amounts of vitamin C (e.g. 1000 mg) or zinc (e.g. 30 mg) during a meal tends to prevent the absorption of copper. Therefore supply additional copper with meals when these are not being used. However copper salicylate may not to be affected by vitamin C and zinc.
>
>
>
> Prolonged cortisone treatment has been shown to increase the body's excretion of copper and may lead to copper deficiency. This may cause various health problems, including the development or acceleration of osteoporosis, scoliosis, skeletal abnormalities and susceptibility to fractures.
>
>
>
> Copper and Diseases
>
>
>
> Adequate copper intake is essential for normal functioning of the immune system. The immune system is significantly weakened at marginal copper levels that do not even affect tissue copper levels or the activity of copper enzymes. A weakening of the immune system could be detected as soon as one week after starting a diet low in copper. Conversely, the addition of sufficient copper improved the suppressed immune system within one week (1). All forms of copper compounds are also effective fungicides.
>
>
>
> Copper deficiency has been shown to have a strong role in anemia, arthritis, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, epilepsy, fat metabolism, free radical damage, immune functions, inflammatory diseases, osteoporosis, and thyroid function (underactive). Wilson's disease is a rare genetic condition with a lack of ceruloplasmin, low serum copper levels, and high copper stores in the liver.
>
>
>
> Deficiency symptoms include anemia, bone disorders, defective spinal cord, hair greying and hair becoming fine and straight, losing its curl, infertility; weak connective tissue as evidenced by heart problems such as enlarged heart, weak aorta with holes and ruptures, aneurisms, varicose veins, wrinkling skin, and hernias. Copper deficiency greatly increases the lipid peroxidation of lipoproteins and cardiovascular tissue. It causes high cholesterol levels, and heart attack victims have low copper levels in the heart.

It's a pretty good article. I guess it gets to be tough call because zinc and copper need to be in balance. but you can't tell how much you have.

They are just getting bigger and more painful.

Right now I'm day 13, so it can't be my cycle. I get no reprieve.

But what kind of acne are you getting Lauren? I'm getting the cysts.

I want to try the Gymnema. ANYONE??

Cavemam if you are reading check out the blurb on Tribulus. Talks about increasing iron absorption. Chocolate were you looking for that too?

I was googling around antiandrogens and found some herbs to try. But honestly, I am really close to going back on an oral med. I can't do this.

[http://www.soulcysters.net/showthread.php/129222-Natural-Plant-Therapies-for-Healing-PCOS](http://http://www.soulcysters.net/showthread.php/129222-Natural-Plant-Therapies-for-Healing-PCOS)

Primary Herbs

Paeonia lactiflora (White peony)

Paeonia lactiflora has been used for gynecological conditions by both Chinese and Western herbalists, and is used by Western herbalists for PCOS, hyperprolactinemia, endometriosis, ovarian failure and androgen excess. Paeonia has been shown to positively influence low progesterone, reduce elevated androgens (testosterone) and acts to modulate estrogen and prolactin. (7) In vitro, the active constituent paeoniflorin has been shown to affect the ovarian follicle by its action on the aromatase enzyme. (8) Aromatase is important for follicle maturation, ovulation and corpus luteum function, steroid hormone synthesis and the regulation of the conversion of androgens to estrogens. The biofeedback in the pituitary and hypothalamus rely on aromatase to regulate prolactin and GnRH. The daily dose for Paeonia is 4.5 to 9 mL of a 1:2 dried plant extract. (9)

The traditional Chinese/Kanpo formula known as Shakuyaku-Kanzo-To or TJ-68, which is a decoction of Glycyrrhiza glabra and Paeonia lactiflora, has been the subject of a number of clinical trials, all of which demonstrate activity in the hormonal regulation of androgens. In one trial involving eight women with hyperandrogenism and oligomenorrhea, the formula was given for 2 to 8 weeks. This combination regulated the LH to FSH ratio. Over this period of time, serum testosterone levels decreased to less than 50 ng/dL and this resulted in seven of the eight women ovulating regularly. (10)

Another trial involved 20 women diagnosed with PCOS. The formula was successful in lowering testosterone in 90% of the women, of which 25% went on to conceive. (11) It is suggested that it acts directly on the ovary, increasing the activity of aromatase, which promotes the synthesis of estradiol from testosterone, thus lowering serum testosterone levels. It also seems to regulate the LH to FSH ratio. (12)

Gymnema sylvestre (Gymnema)

Gymnema is a traditional Ayurvedic herb used as an antidiabetic, hypoglycemic, lipid lowering agent and to support weight reduction. Gymnema possibly has a trophorestorative action of the beta cells of the pancreas. (13) The plant part used as medicine is the leaf. Gymnema is well indicated for PCOS, due to its insulin modulating activity and the added benefits of reducing the elevated triglycerides associated with PCOS. Key constituents of Gymnema include saponins, especially the gymnemic acids. Gymnemic acid suppresses the sweet taste on the taste buds, so if taken before food masks the sweet sensation. Gymnema has demonstrated hypoglycemic activity in experimental models of diabetes and regulated blood sugar in hyperglycemia. The mechanism of action also includes the inhibition of glucose absorption in the intestine. The daily dose of Gymnema is 3.5 to 11 mL of 1:1 liquid extract. (9,14) Since conventional medical models are focussing on pharmaceutical agents such as metformin to control PCOS, Gymnema may prove to be one of the most significant herbs in the treatment of the underlying factor of insulin resistance.

Tribulus terrestris (Tribulus)

Tribulus is an endemic weed to many regions of the world, such as the Mediterranean, India, China, South Africa and Australia and is commonly known as puncture vine. The aerial parts, particularly the leaf, are used for medicinal purposes in the Western tradition. As a result of Bulgarian research, Tribulus has become a popular herb for the treatment of female and male endocrine disorders. (15) It acts as a general tonic, aphrodisiac, estrogen modulator and androgen modulator and is used to restore vitality, libido and reduce the physiological effects of stress. (9,11)

The Bulgarian research has identified a unique steroidal saponin class known as furostanol saponins, and extracts are standardized to contain at least 45% of these saponins, calculated as protodioscin. The leaf is noted to be higher in these unique saponins rather than the fruit or root. Other active constituents include phytosterols and spirostanol glycosides.

The tonic activities of Tribulus have been shown to act by intensifying protein synthesis and enhancing the activity of enzymes associated with energy metabolism. It increased iron absorption from the small intestines and inhibited lipid peroxidation during stress. This leads to more muscle strength and improved endurance and stamina. (9)

To ensure the desired clinical results, it is recommended to use only the Bulgarian grown Tribulus standardized to 40% furostanol saponins. It is not interchangeable with the Chinese or Indian Tribulus. The daily dose of Tribulus corresponds to extracts containing furostanol saponins as protodioscin at 300 mg to 400 mg per day. In PCOS it is best used on days 5 to 14 of the menstrual cycle to restore menstrual regularity. For more information on Tribulus, see the forthcoming review which will be published in next months Townsend Letter.

Vitex agnus-castus (Chaste tree)

Vitex beneficial for ovulatory factors associated with PCOS, in particular it has been shown to downregulate the production of excess prolactin, a condition known as hyperprolactinemia. (16) Vitex is also postulated as having antiandrogenic properties. (16) Hyperprolactinemia is related to adrenal stress and hyperinsulinemia in PCOS.

Vitex reduced the thyroxin releasing hormone (TRH)-induced prolactin release (essentially a pituitary-thyroid axis problem), normalized shortened luteal phases, corrected luteal phase progesterone deficiencies and reduced PMS symptoms in women with luteal phase defects due to latent hyperprolactinemia. (9)

Vitex should be considered first line botanical therapy for hyperprolactinemia and given for the duration of at least 3 to 6 months.

Caulophyllum thalictroides is known by the common name of blue cohosh and is native to North America. Within traditional use among the Native North Americans it was used by women as a remedy for amenorrhea and profuse menstruation, both of which are common features of PCOS. It is particularly useful to being on the menses in PCOS. It has action as a uterine and ovarian tonic and pelvic anti-inflammatory. The known constituents of Caulophyllum root include glycosides, caulosaponin and caulophyllosaponin, which are known to stimulate the uterus. Other identified constituents include N-methylcystine, taspine and thalictroidine. (9) The daily dose is 1.5 to 3 mL of 1:2 dried plant extract. (9)

Sample PCOS Formula

Vitex agnus-castus 1:2 12.5 mL

Glycyrrhiza glabra 1:1 12.5 mL

Paeonia lactiflora 1:2 25 mL

Gymnema sylvestre 1:1 25 mL

Schisandra chinensis 1:2 25 mL

Dose: 15 mL per day or 5 mL three times daily 100 mL

In a case of a PCOS patient with amenorrhea, include Caulophyllum thalictroides at a dose of 2 mL per day to help induce the menses.

When a cycle has been initiated, change to Tribulus concentrated extract, equivalent to furostanol saponins (as protodioscin) 300 to 400 mg per day on days 5 to 14 of the cycle to ensure cyclic regularity.

Dietary Modification

A review of the extensive literature specific to lifestyle factors in PCOS demonstrates that an essential treatment strategy for ameliorating the symptoms of PCOS and resolving the underlying metabolic derangements is the implementation of a low carbohydrate diet. This will tightly control blood sugar levels and resultant insulin production. High levels of insulin result in high levels of triglycerides and low levels of high density lipoproteins, which puts these patients into a high cardiovascular disease risk category. Modulating the diet not only helps the female endocrine cycle, but also serves as preventative medicine against these cardiovascular risk factors. As the insulin levels normalize, this will also improve circulating levels of SHBG, therefore limiting the problematic effects of free androgens on the menstrual cycle. (2,6)

My T was just over the range - new doc isn't concerned. Who knows. I'm getting the cysts, too (painful and larger, not the normal tiny pimples) and only on my chin and upper neck. I asked in Ask Jack (mine seems to be perfectly timed with ovulation) and he said it was hormone surges. Which makes me wonder for those of us on BHRT... since we know it's a hormone chain, when we take one we will naturally effect all the others. So it's really hard to work out what is happening. I also wonder about the liver. But sort of like you, I'm not sure what I can do... been eating keto epi paleo, drinking heaps of green tea and dandelion tea, taking lots of tumeric, AI, circadian, etc. I should probably read the Leptin Part Deux blog again. I try to remember healing is non-linear. But it's awful bumpy, too  I do however have a history of zinc deficiency and I've added back in supplementation (and also been dabbing it directly on my face). It seems to have helped. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-58074>

Jennifer. I am sorry about your acne.. it sucks. My skin likes no dairy.. except of maybe raw organic goats milk from old brand goats. I can buy it here and drink it occasionally. Butter, cream, cheese.. makes me not pretty. I wonder if you would suffer less if you went HARD CORE with your food and light and dropped all hormones? That your body get a chance to regulate itself slowly? I am thinking of yucky foods like MHS, raw hearts and stuff. Rotten meat. Candles only after dark, triple protection when using computer evenings.. dark sunglasses when going to parties or shops or stuff after sunset. Yeah.. I know but I bet you would be happier with your outlook fast! Look, I can tell you it works for me that way at least. I kinda need to choose.. to please my palate.. or looking good. I cannot have both. Sure I eat delicious stuff too like salmon tartar and oysters and sashimi but they are well choosen and on purpose. Now I am used to stuff like raw lambs hearts carpaccio and like it a lot but it might be you need to get used to it first.

I am thinking when doing it the natural way it goes smoother... without too much turmoil because in the foods are all the stuff you need for detox and healing.. Now, it takes longer maybe? But the trip is funnier..

For skin I have been smearing bone marrow from reindeer (I bet any other would do as well) on my face and then some hawthorn berries on my face in the evening..lol Very natural, very cheap and I can tell you, I like it! I bet you could use any wild berries!I just had no money to buy the argan oil I was using so I thought.. eating the reindeer for breakfast, why don't use the marrow for my face instead? I sometimes make a peeling with some epsom salt, honey and olive oil too. I leave it on for a while after too, before rinsing. Makes the skin feel really good!

About fruit.. I would not eat any. I cannot, I can eat only wild grown berries and feel real good. Strawberries, any cultivated berries are not so good for me. The apples from our garden too do me no good. I will not eat these before I am very healthy.. maybe.

Just some thoughts.. might not be the right for you but I thought I could as well share what works for me...

### indigogirl — 2012-10-14T17:28:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-58108>

Jennifer, you might want to look at spironolactone to counter the cystic acne effects of T.

Spironolactone is used to treat many different disorders, from high blood pressure to fluid retention. Although the U.S. Food and Drug Administration doesn't recognize spironolactone as an acne treatment, it is often prescribed off-label to treat hormonally influenced breakout in women.

This medication isn't a first line of defense against acne breakouts. It is only effective against acne caused by hormonal fluctuations. But it can be especially helpful for women with hormonal disorders that trigger acne or who suffer with other problems, like unwanted facial hair.

I get cystic facial acne when I take T or DHEA. I took spironolactone while on T and DHEA and it prevented me from breaking out. I stopped taking all 3 because I felt like why was I taking a drug to counteract the side effect of 2 other drugs? I guess for me the T and DHEA benefits didn't outweigh taking a drug to counteract their effects.

You need a prescription for spironolactone.

### laertes — 2012-03-20T18:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3317>

I just wanted to ask if anyone knows if showers or baths are more effective in CT?

As far as I understand, it's more about making the body think it's freaking cold than to actually cooling.

I, for one, can lie in the bathtube for 40 mins at 50Ã‚Â° F, no problemo. But when I try to shower at 50Ã‚Â°F, I'm a-screaming, raving, cursing the inventor of showers, I can hardly go on for 10 mins - besides, I'm out of curses at that point of time. Is that just a psychological thing?

>
>
> I, for one, can lie in the bathtube for 40 mins at 50° F, no problemo. But when I try to shower at 50°F, I'm a-screaming, raving, cursing the inventor of showers, I can hardly go on for 10 mins - besides, I'm out of curses at that point of time. Is that just a psychological thing?

I wonder if the shower is harder to do because of the air...even though it's not necessarily windy in a bathroom the air is hitting your cold wet skin and air could contribute to your discomfort, like the wind chill factor. I have not done a bath yet, just face dunks and cool showers, but I can't bring myself to make the shower cold. I think baths will be easier...

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-20T18:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3328>

I find the opposite to be true. I can take a really cold shower, and be in there for however long I need. But the Bath immersion, I struggle with.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-20T18:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3333>

I think showers are way tougher.....just give me immersion

### laertes — 2012-03-20T19:11:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3347>

Ok, seems like I'm not the only one who can't stand them, but are they only tougher or are they tougher and more effective?^^

### colleencoble — 2012-03-20T19:17:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3352>

I can't do the showers. Submersion is way easier for me. My skin goes numb very quickly. LOL

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-20T19:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3356>

I find showers much easier than submersion. But the submersion does, for me at least, seem to be more effective than the showers. I reckon that 8 days in, it's too early to tell other than that I feel energised by the showers in the morning, and the baths leave me feeling tireder in the evening ....

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T19:21:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3357>

I've found showers to be the most difficult! I'm ice packing and going to try to go to bath . I've been forcing myself to cold shower - and I seriously scream and jump and can't make it 2 a moment past 2 mins.

I'm still going with goal of keeping skin at 50-55 for as long as possible however that works.

Once spot icing starts, is there any benefit to continuing face dunks? Is there a topical effect directly on the skin?

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T19:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3377>

Spot icing doesn't bother me, immersion doesn't bother me. Luke-warm showers don't bother me (and are, in fact, my norm). COLD showers SUUUUCK, unless it's 90 degrees out...and even then, I have to be pretty frickin' desperate to turn the shower knob all the way to "cold".

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-20T19:41:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3382>

I have gotten use to putting the shower all the way cold. I feel by body tense up a bit and and get a similar feeling to my pool immersion. Bally's in my area has a cold pool. I may just have to join again for the convenience of taking a dip a few days a week. Now that was hard when I did it. My breathing choked up, and I could not do it for more than a few long minutes. I need to find out what the temperature is.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T20:16:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3394>

I do both currently. Start off with warm shower to clean off, then start turning it cold and plug the tub. I letting it fill the tub turning colder every few minutes (to adjust) until finally at full cold (roughly 15 min). Then soak with blue ice in the tub for another 30 min or so depending on how long the magazines last.  That way I'm not wasting my water.

The shower stings more (and I feel like a roaster, turning around every 30 secs to a minute to get the cold shower on another bodypart), but neither's really been bad for me personally. I've noticed that as I adjust, I can turn down the cold faster, but haven't gotten to the "full cold OMG!! blast from the start" stage yet. I imagine I could, but I don't really see the need for the pain yet.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T20:26:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3400>

I find the baths to be much more relaxing, so I use the cold showers in the morning, which give me a great burst of energy (adrenaline?), and the baths at night, which prepare me for sleep.

Both the cold baths and showers are getting easier. If I had to pick one to do all the time, it would be bathing.

I venture to guess that baths might be more efficient because your whole body is submerged and therefore a greater surface area of skin (and thus more receptors) are experiencing 50 degree temps from the water, however this doesn't take into account how cool air on wet skin affects those receptors. Since I'm not sure, I'm just going to continue to do both.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T20:33:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3404>

I bow to anyone who's able to do a cold shower. I can sit in a tub of floating ice for an hour or more, but can't seem to stand 60 seconds of cold water coming out of that shower head. I don't understand why, I only know it's like some kind of torture device that makes me want to scream.

This is one of those times when I think a poll function on the forum would be interested - we could see what percentage of readers here use a bath v. a shower, and then discuss how we've come to that.

### shilohman — 2012-03-20T20:41:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3411>

>
>
>
> This is one of those times when I think a poll function on the forum would be interested - we could see what percentage of readers here use a bath v. a shower, and then discuss how we've come to that.

Clabbergirl, do you take bitter melon? My guess is as you develop more BAT, then a cold shower will become a piece of cake. Since there isn't a way to test for how much BAT we have, the best test that I have found (may be wrong on this) is the reduction of shivering = increased BAT.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T20:48:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3417>

> **shilohman;3435 wrote:** Clabbergirl, do you take bitter melon? My guess is as you develop more BAT, then a cold shower will become a piece of cake. Since there isn't a way to test for how much BAT we have, the best test that I have found (may be wrong on this) is the reduction of shivering = increased BAT.

I don't take bitter melon (but I was in the process of ordering some as I got this response). I hope I get a lot of perks from more BAT. I still haven't had shivering no matter how cold or how long I stay in. I've gotten close to shivering, and had goosebumps and cherry red skin, but no shivers at all. I've got a lot of weight to lose though, has to be throwing that process off somehow.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T20:51:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3420>

The test they use for BAT is a PET scan to check metabolism/areas (brown fat has higher metabolism than white fat). $3k plus for a whole body scan, so pretty much no way to test for most of us.

### Laney — 2012-03-20T20:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3422>

After about 4 weeks, I can go from bed to straight tap water without much of a shiver. I'm good in 1-2 minutes or so. Not sure about the water temp, but must be lower than 10 degrees Celsius or so at this time of the year. I can comfortably go on but I stop around 20 min so as not to waste too much water. Immersion-wise, I typically feel like getting out in 50-60 minutes although I could press on if needed. Immersion feels generally harder because I keep hands in all the time and feet most of the time; in the shower, as I twist around, the skin at parts takes some rest until it adjusts, after which it's just... water. I likely feel better in the shower because I have to twist around. But as I mentioned, the water waste for long durations gets too much, so I opt for the full tub instead (actually shower for a while and when the level is good I lay me in)

### David — 2012-03-20T22:27:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3496>

Cold showers were quite a challenge at first. The "OMG! This is a SHOCK!" moment still happens, and I image one can never get past that. But, I find that a slow process works for me: taking a cool shower and scrub first and then standing aside to turn the water to cold, followed by something like a vertical face dunk (water stream hitting only the top of my head and face) \[to get the 'dive response'?\], for a few moments before turning and letting the first shock of the cold water hit the back of my neck and my back. Water temp varies 63-64F, so it isn't terribly cold and I can easily stay under the stream for a half hour.

I tried icing my chest and midsection 30 minutes yesterday, noticed no problems.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T22:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3504>

As a side note, I find humming through chattering teeth in the shower part is entertaining. Not sure it's 30 minutes plus entertaining though.  Magazines make the soaking part go by faster too. The whole idea being to distract yourself from the cold vs concentrating on it.

### Sharon — 2012-03-21T01:09:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-3615>

No can do cold showers. Words come out of me that I didn't even know I knew.

### freezin4areason — 2012-03-21T22:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-4060>

> **eva;3850 wrote:** i havent got a tub so ive had to do just cold showers. usually 15-20 minutes sometimes 2 x a day. ive started to love the initial shock actually.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-21T23:10:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-4066>

stand next to your AC vent naked and set temp at 50 degrees......i do this twice a week. I am doing it now as we speak in my speedos.

### DaveJF — 2012-03-22T18:10:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-4478>

> **MJ\*;3349 wrote:** I wonder if the shower is harder to do because of the air...even though it's not necessarily windy in a bathroom the air is hitting your cold wet skin and air could contribute to your discomfort, like the wind chill factor. I have not done a bath yet, just face dunks and cool showers, but I can't bring myself to make the shower cold. I think baths will be easier...

I also find showers "feel" a lot colder than the baths at the same water temp. I believe there is solid physics behind the reason: a thin insulating film of water is built up in the tub, where movement is limited, and also the moving water of a shower carries away heat faster. With a shower the insulating film can't be sustained, as it would be in a tub. I've confirmed this as best I could with the thermometer I have - my skin temp matches the water temp in the shower but not in the tub.

I've read elsewhere (Ray Conise I believe) that hypothermia will be delayed, for someone falling into cold water, if they limit movement.. There have been studies carried out on that. The ultimate CT may be swimming in a large body of cold water - I believe also that Ray Cronise mentioned he has been experimenting with that concept recently.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-23T22:41:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-5149>

My water outa the tap right now is 49 F. I guess I should be grateful, right?

I've been doing cold water immersion in the tub for almost 2 weeks now. I did my 1st attempt at a cold shower today - wow, different experience! The 1st 3 minutes were tough. I shivered immediately, my feet and hands were icy, and I wasn't a happy camper.

At about 8 minutes, I thought, I'm done...then the discomfort eased and I ended up stayed in for 20 minutes. Eventually, I got bored (normally I read in the tub), so I started doing some isometric flexing, squats, and that yoga pose where you balance on one leg. When I got out I felt...well, pretty darn awesome, sleepy, but calm and strong. I liked being able to move around in the shower and pump my arms up and down, etc.

Another shower plus is you can alternate face and body. That's harder to do in the tub and still keep your hands and feet out (hands and feet "in" ends my session too fast).

### Adria — 2012-03-24T00:21:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-5189>

I can only do cold at the end of warm shower. Especially since I take showers in the morning, cold early in the morning just freaks me out. I do face dunks and cool off after the shower.

Baths and ice packs in the evening are best way for me.

### Powder — 2012-03-24T02:34:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-5251>

I take 2 showers a day as cold as I can get them, and fit in baths with ice as I am able. The showers seem to get the skin cold much quicker, but it is more topical, as I don't shiver after like I do with a bath.

### differentstory — 2012-03-24T19:26:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-5483>

hysterical!

### differentstory — 2012-03-24T19:32:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-better-showers-or-baths.324/post-5487>

> **Powder;5284 wrote:** I take 2 showers a day as cold as I can get them, and fit in baths with ice as I am able. The showers seem to get the skin cold much quicker, but it is more topical, as I don't shiver after like I do with a bath.
