# Thread: Anyone else get hives (cold urticaria) with CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2012-03-21T04:44:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3768>

I had a hives a week or so ago and they were pretty bad, only on the areas where I used ice while in the cold bath (on my abdomen, chest, and neck and chin area). I stopped CTing and waited until the hives were gone about 5 days. I started over with face dunks and showers and then, baths again and I am up to 25 minutes in a cold bath, with water about 53 degrees, and able to tolerate it ok (not easy but ok). Scared to use ice again while in there so no ice. But I did buy some of those bubble wrap ice packs thingies and used one very briefly on my tummy yesterday just to try it out, I had 2 shirts between me and the pack. My skin seemed fine yesterday (and I did not do my cold bath yesterday) until today where I noticed it was very sore and thought it had to do with some core exercises I had done a few days ago. Then, I looked, and it looks like the hives are back on my tummy, very red and sore. Very weird stuff. From only about 5 minutes on my tummy

Back to just cold baths for me. Curious if I'm the only one who has had this in this forum. I've been paleo for 2 years, before that I had a pretty decent diet for about 7 years (WAP-style).

### dinglebutt — 2012-03-21T06:18:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3794>

According to Kruse: [http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

" If you develop **[cold urticaria](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cold_urticaria)** at this time, this is a sign you have high levels of tissue and serum omega six content. Stop the experiment and adjust your diet until you have a blood omega six to three ratio that is below 10 to 1 using a ketogenic paleolithic diet. "

### JC2K — 2012-03-21T07:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3800>

No problems here as of yet.

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T15:05:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3851>

> **dinglebutt;3819 wrote: According to Kruse: [http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)
>
>
>
> " If you develop [cold urticaria](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cold_urticaria)** at this time, this is a sign you have high levels of tissue and serum omega six content. Stop the experiment and adjust your diet until you have a blood omega six to three ratio that is below 10 to 1 using a ketogenic paleolithic diet. "

Thanks, I read that and I will try to get it tested if I doctor will go for it, but I've been paleo/ketogenic for 2 years...very strict low carb all of the time, no sugar. I have been using krill oil way before it became popular, for years and years, and fish oil and cod liver oil. I eat salmon every week, also for years. It just seems very unlikely that this could be the problem. I don't eat anything high in omega 6. I haven't touched processed foods in years, don't use vegetable oils (only coconut oil, butter). I know something is probably off with me. I could have some omega 6 from previous years stored in the fat I need to lose, but I only need to lose 8 lbs. or so. I've added even more fish and extra krill oil in the last couple of weeks.

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T15:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3858>

> **JC2K;3825 wrote:** No problems here as of yet.

Good for you!

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-21T15:21:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3859>

I had hives after trying my first cold shower. That was about 5 days ago. Since then I've been doing cold baths ( 52 or 53 degrees). I haven't had the hives again. Do you have a history of getting hives? I had horrible allergies for years where I would get hives often, and I would get them very often in cold weather. (at its worst, I couldn't stay outside in the cold, even while bundled, even for a couple min.) I always thought the cold weather hives were related to the allergies I had. I'm so thankful that I'm able to do the cold baths, I could have never imagined doing them just a couple years ago!

The hives I had after the cold shower were gone in the same day (not sure exactly how long it took, but they were gone pretty fast). But I wonder if that's because my body has so much 'experience' with hives in the past!

I'm glad that you have found something that seems to work for you .... Maybe you just need to take it slow?

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T15:34:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3865>

> **joyfuljoyful;3884 wrote:** I had hives after trying my first cold shower. That was about 5 days ago. Since then I've been doing cold baths ( 52 or 53 degrees). I haven't had the hives again. Do you have a history of getting hives? I had horrible allergies for years where I would get hives often, and I would get them very often in cold weather. (at its worst, I couldn't stay outside in the cold, even while bundled, even for a couple min.) I always thought the cold weather hives were related to the allergies I had. I'm so thankful that I'm able to do the cold baths, I could have never imagined doing them just a couple years ago!
>
> The hives I had after the cold shower were gone in the same day (not sure exactly how long it took, but they were gone pretty fast). But I wonder if that's because my body has so much 'experience' with hives in the past!
>
> I'm glad that you have found something that seems to work for you .... Maybe you just need to take it slow?

Thanks. I'm sorry you have had that problem. No history of hives, never had them my whole life. So weird to have them now. I have it bad on my tummy again, sore this morning still, and that's from just few minutes with a gel back on my tummy and clothes in between, not a compression shirt either. Are you adding ice to get the water to that temp? Does your skin get to that temp also? The cold baths seem to be ok for me, but the tap warming is starting to get warmer here in NY. It was around 52 and now it's closer to 55. I didn't take the bath last night because of the hives on my tummy again. This is such a bummer. I guess I do have to take it slow, which is fine, but will I still cold adapt without ice?

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-21T16:42:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3893>

> **Claudia;3890 wrote:** Thanks. I'm sorry you have had that problem. No history of hives, never had them my whole life. So weird to have them now. I have it bad on my tummy again, sore this morning still, and that's from just few minutes with a gel back on my tummy and clothes in between, not a compression shirt either. Are you adding ice to get the water to that temp? Does your skin get to that temp also? The cold baths seem to be ok for me, but the tap warming is starting to get warmer here in NY. It was around 52 and now it's closer to 55. I didn't take the bath last night because of the hives on my tummy again. This is such a bummer. I guess I do have to take it slow, which is fine, but will I still cold adapt without ice?

I just started to add ice packs (just the freezer packs that I put in lunches) to keep the bath water a bit colder. Out of the tap it is about 52 degrees and with the ice packs after 30 minutes it is 53 degrees. I'm going to stock up on some more ice packs to progressively cool the water. It seems cheap and easier than doing ice.

I haven't been able to check my skin temp's, since I use my digital meat themometer to test the bath water and it wont work on skin (I tried  )

Dr. Kruse said that cold adaptation is 'cumulative'....so any time you expose yourself to any kind of cold, you are doing a good thing! Even if it feels like 'something small', you are headed in the right direction. I try to look at it like any little bit helps. Try to look at the big picture, of where you are headed, and try not to fret about how quick (or slow) you will get there. Just do what you can each day, and try to keep perspective. I think one thing that really helped me in CT is that I already had the signs of LS. Are you Leptin Sensitive now, or have signs of it? Dr. Kruse says that the more Leptin Sensitive you are, the easier you'll cold adapt. I started with the Leptin Reset at the end of December, had the signs of LS in early February, and just last week started CT. I have had set-backs since starting the Reset (discovering a pork sensitivity, which caused me to feel like garbage for weeks before I realized it.....and then gaining 5 lbs and I'm still trying to figure out exactly what that was....and now I'm trying to heal my gut too....still am up 3 lbs, hoping to lose that and another couple lbs) but I try to think of the big picture: I have embraced this as a lifestyle, and I really have no plans of going back to what Conventional Wisdom says is 'healthy'. So I had to tell myself that if look me longer than I hoped, that in the long run it's ok! Since every day that we are making the truly healthy choices we are doing a great thing! And I'm thankful to have started it when I did, as opposed to waiting another 5 years.

You are doing a good job. Try not to be stressed or discouraged. You are headed in the right direction, just take it slow and remind yourself that every little bit helps!

### dinglebutt — 2012-03-21T19:02:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-3942>

Are pink areas the same thing? I've been using ice packs on my stomach and noticed this morning that there are two pinkish areas on my stomach where I've been placing the packs. They don't hurt or itch or anything, though. They're just... there, haha.

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T22:01:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-4028>

When did you put on the packs? Yesterday? I find it odd that I did mine on Monday for such a brief period of time and then, nothing appeared until later on Tuesday. Are your pink spots still there? Mine don't go away and they are raised and sore. It feels like I did ab work, hundreds of crunches. Hopefully yours are just spots. This isn't fun.

### Julie — 2012-03-22T15:15:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-4342>

Hi! I posted this on another thread... I started CT and followed the protocol exactly. Worked my way up to baths and had taken six with no problem. My last one was really cold. And the next morning I woke up with white bumps/hives on the tops of my legs. Now three days later the are itching. Ughhhh! I'm afraid I have really hurt myself. I think it's so weird that this happened three weeks in to the protocol. What are you doing to control symptoms? Any ideas? Thanks!

### Claudia — 2012-03-22T18:00:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-4466>

> **Julie;4370 wrote:** Hi! I posted this on another thread... I started CT and followed the protocol exactly. Worked my way up to baths and had taken six with no problem. My last one was really cold. And the next morning I woke up with white bumps/hives on the tops of my legs. Now three days later the are itching. Ughhhh! I'm afraid I have really hurt myself. I think it's so weird that this happened three weeks in to the protocol. What are you doing to control symptoms? Any ideas? Thanks!

Do you know the temp of the water? It was probably too cold. I think we are moving too fast. Were you fine when you did the ice only part of the protocol? The only thing I am doing is avoiding cold for right now until my hives are gone. I may take a cold bath tonight but keep my tummy out, which isn't so hard to do because my tub is so shallow anyway. My hives are better today but still sore to the touch and mine are raised and red.

### Marsha — 2012-03-27T00:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-6638>

Just a warning so you don't make the same mistake I did!!!

While watching Frozen Planet last night, I decided to ice my belly with a gel pad and compression belt. Really enjoyed the show and when it was over, removed my 'getup' - belly was very numb - then noticed some stinging high up on my belly. The gel pad had pressed directly against my skin, while I thought I was protected by the tights I had pulled up high - they must have inched down during the show and because I was numb in that area, I didn't realize it.

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/6674=52-Ice burn .jpg>)

It burns like crazy  even today - even after applying CO and an aloe leaf!

I'll certainly _be more careful from now on_!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-27T02:20:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-6691>

>
>
>
> It burns like crazy  even today - even after applying CO and an aloe leaf!
>
> I'll certainly _be more careful from now on_!

Ok Marsha! Picture was worth it, I did the EXACT same thing. . compression shirt had moved and I didn't realize I burned my belly. A big swath all across. It's been over 1 week now, still somewhat numbish - and the burning lasted several days. Just like a sunburn. it's still slightly off color too.

### Kathy — 2012-03-27T03:48:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-6735>

I got a tummy burn too, ice pack was over my shirt and only on for 8 minutes! and here I thought I was going super slow...  Once skin returns to normal, think I'll ditch the compression shirt & try something a little thicker to see how that goes.

### Claudia — 2012-03-27T04:46:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-6763>

> **Kathy;6771 wrote:** I got a tummy burn too, ice pack was over my shirt and only on for 8 minutes! and here I thought I was going super slow...  Once skin returns to normal, think I'll ditch the compression shirt & try something a little thicker to see how that goes.

Yep, that's what my hives looked like...maybe the second time it was an ice burn and not hives.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-31T21:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-9328>

I'm starting to wonder if mine might actually be cold urticaria, but is it possible for it to appear on my head/neck, the only two parts NEVER immersed?

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-04-02T04:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-10136>

Ok, so it's looking like a weird cold urticaria: "Localized cold reflex urticaria leads to symptoms of hives and swelling away from the area of direct exposure to cold. An ice-cube test on the arm may cause hives to form a few inches away from the site of ice-cube application, for example."

So how long will it last?? This most recent re-flare has been going on several days  Aside from antihistimines (which I'm taking) and quitting CT, how do you treat it?

### MingP — 2019-04-17T05:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-268274>

V late to the party, but I thought u might wanna check this post out:

[https://allergylosangeles.com/allergy-blog/altered-gut-microbes-chronic-hives/](https://allergylosangeles.com/allergy-blog/altered-gut-microbes-chronic-hives/)

Disclaimer: Not a doctor. Bio-hacking, like everyone else.

So I reason, that if hives is a function of microbiome, and from Vermont 2018, the sun is a strong builder of the microbiome, then we should get our backsides out into the sun alot more.
Perhaps things like kim chi, saurkraut, naato and other fermented foods might help too.

My daughter had an infection from a bite, the doc gave augmentin, which destroyed her gut flora, resulting in acute stomach pain and also hives.
I'm really fucking pissed off.
There's certainly a better way to treat this shit.

### robnotbob — 2024-05-28T07:24:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-get-hives-cold-urticaria-with-ct.338/post-335218>

> [Claudia said:](/goto/post?id=3768)
>
> I had a hives a week or so ago and they were pretty bad, only on the areas where I used ice while in the cold bath (on my abdomen, chest, and neck and chin area). I stopped CTing and waited until the hives were gone about 5 days. I started over with face dunks and showers and then, baths again and I am up to 25 minutes in a cold bath, with water about 53 degrees, and able to tolerate it ok (not easy but ok). Scared to use ice again while in there so no ice. But I did buy some of those bubble wrap ice packs thingies and used one very briefly on my tummy yesterday just to try it out, I had 2 shirts between me and the pack. My skin seemed fine yesterday (and I did not do my cold bath yesterday) until today where I noticed it was very sore and thought it had to do with some core exercises I had done a few days ago. Then, I looked, and it looks like the hives are back on my tummy, very red and sore. Very weird stuff. From only about 5 minutes on my tummy
>
>
>
> Back to just cold baths for me. Curious if I'm the only one who has had this in this forum. I've been paleo for 2 years, before that I had a pretty decent diet for about 7 years (WAP-style).

How did this resolve? I'm just starting CT and I got some red, sore patches after several days of using cold packs on my abdomen and chest through a compression shirt.

### nhmlw — 2012-03-21T20:58:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-7-is-live.339/post-3998>

I have recently come off a round of HCG and I have been doing CT therapy for about a week and a half, pretty much falling asleep with 10 ice packs on me using compression wear. I fall asleep wonderfully BUT every night I am waking up at 1:30AM . I am am in bed by 9-10 and asleep fairly quickly. I just do not understand why every night it is that time, and I do not wake up with the need pee. I am at a loss on how to fix. I am 43 and female. I have about another 45 more pounds to loss and I have had amenorrhea for the last year except one cycle which my acupuncturist brought on. I am on my second day of the leptin RX. Anyone any ideas, doctors have been a very big disappointment for me. On a positive note, my round of HCG helped ALOT and I am feeling human once again and I am excited to do the Leptin reset. I just need to resolve what is walking me up each night at 1:30 and how to get a normal cycle back. Seeing OBGYN on 4/4 and requesting/demanding BHRT.

I would like to hear everyone who has tried [Bulletproof Coffee](http://http://www.bulletproofexec.com/?a_aid=4f7b0d43e59bb) on the Leptin Rx to give me their opinion. I cannot handle the extra caffeine or I would have another cup. However, my thoughts are: Have you used it while on the reset? If so, what have been your results? Do you think it has helped or hindered your progress?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-21T15:23:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-3861>

I didn't use it on the LR - but I have it in the morning now w/HAB on the post Leptin RX...

now that said - when I was doing the LR I was using the following: [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread18090.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread18090.html) in my coffee daily...

In all honesty - I'll never go back from BP coffee...right now I'm trying to finish up the coffee I have and plan to order some BP beans from BPExec.... I use ghee- I make from kerry gold unsalted butter...If I can't have bp coffee I'll just have it black from now on....

that said if you don't have adrenal problems - there shouldn't be a reason why you can't have a cup of BP coffee - also if you read up on what makes good coffee beans - on BPExec's site: [http://www.bulletproofexec.com/what-dr-mercola-didnt-say-about-coffee-brains-and-muscle-video/](http://http://www.bulletproofexec.com/what-dr-mercola-didnt-say-about-coffee-brains-and-muscle-video/)

good beans won't give you the shakes or anything of the sort...what gives you that is the mold and associated toxins in low grade beans - its one reason why stabucks coffee effin sucks - they buy the lowest grade beans, and burn the crap out of it...

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-24T17:43:46+0200

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-24T17:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-5446>

> **donkjellberg;5121 wrote:** I tried it yesterday and it tasted good! Going to add cocoa and cinnamon to the mix today. Everything can be optimized, lol.

I add the 99% dark chocolate, cinnamon AND tumeric. It's slightly yellow, but tastes mostly of chocolate and cinnamon.

I also add a lump of the grassfed raw heavy cream instead of butter. I figure it's practically butter anyway since I have to have a sturdy spoon to get it out of the jar. It does make me wonder how they get it out of the cow!

### Cody — 2012-03-24T18:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-5470>

Bulletproof coffee is the recipe, good coffee, blended with pastured butter and coconut oil.

The coffee product that is sold on the bulletproof exec's website is called [Upgraded Coffee](http://http://www.bulletproofexec.com/?a_aid=4f7b0d43e59bb). And my ten pounds showed up this morning. Too late for my morning coffee, but I'm going to hit Trader Joe's and World market and see what kind of cinnamon I can find to grind up with the beans...

### Valorie33 — 2012-03-24T19:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-5478>

If you don't want to do the coffee it is excellent using Green Tea. I used to have coffee every morning, after tasting the bulletproof way with Green Tea I ended up converting to tea every morning very easily. Now coffee is a treat for once or twice a week.

### Lexi — 2012-03-24T19:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-5485>

anyone try this without egg?

### Valorie33 — 2012-03-24T19:49:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-5494>

I just use the butter, [coconut oil](http://http://store.nutiva.com/aff/7563933154C0D4013687DE48F5051500/index.html), cinnamon and stevia. I make butter from heavy cream, usually about 7 oz, and add 7 oz of coconut oil, lots of cinnamon and some stevia to the mixer and made a tub full that lasts about a week if I am the only one using it. I use two tablespoons in my morning coffee.

It goes much faster when my bf and son get in on it. My bf has had to cut down as it makes him too full to actually eat and he is trying to gain weight.

### Cody — 2012-03-24T19:58:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-5504>

I ended up making a batch of coffee. Just had to try it. And it's still early in the afternoon yet.

I mixed a half a pot of black coffee with 2 TB coconut oil and 2 TB butter. Delicious!!!

I thought that maybe the effects that this guy listed in his blog were marketing drivel and placebo, but thought it was worth a shot. But this coffee really does impact you the way he says.

Well worth the money. My 10 lbs should last me the rest of the year.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T22:42:57+0300

> **Continuum Fitness;7377 wrote:** I was going to do that in the morning. I had CO in my morning coffee. I saw the BP coffee and wanted to give it a try. Its been a couple of hours and I am feeling good...... I am way past the reset..... Its all about pure leanness and putting on muscle now. I will break into single digit BF% by mid April.

jealous! I've got a ways to go but changes are happening... took me a while to get where I'm at.... I'll be there, its just a matter of when...

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-27T23:12:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee.340/post-7361>

> **Shijin13;7382 wrote:** jealous! I've got a ways to go but changes are happening... took me a while to get where I'm at.... I'll be there, its just a matter of when...

Don't be jealous, you will get there. Trust me it has not been a cake walk. I am in the health and fitness field. I was a heavy kid, I yo-yo ed my whole life I have been super lean and super heavy (for me) I have used all the tricks and knowledge that I have but the weight would always come back. This last time I bloated up to 210 lbs from 180 that I maintained for years. I hold my weight in the middle so with a little clothing magic most people can not tell when I gain or lose until it is over/under 30 pounds.

In October 2011 I was 210 at 27% BF I am now 175 at 11.5% I started paleo(again) in Oct but this time I found the Dr. and implemented the Leptin Reset. I knew about leptin and hormones but never really applied the theories to myself. This time I did and BOOM!!!! The other day I ate a couple of cookies from fresh market. In the past they were my favorites and I would eat a box in a sitting. I bought two out of nostalgia and ended throwing one away because They just didn't taste good to me anymore. eating to me now is just something I have to do. I much rather chew on a chunk of meat......

Now I am LS and IFing moderately and find it easy and I do not think about food anymore. I just have to master CT which I am getting benefits from. I have one bathroom on my condo and it is a shallow tub. So I do what I can with packs and I bought a cold water circulating machine and large pad. which works pretty well. I live in Fl so the weather is already hot so no rivers or cold lakes around lol.

### ealachan — 2012-04-06T21:27:09+0300

> **MartiD;12294 wrote:**
>
> Question, does it have to be blended? Does this change the texture or flavor some how or just better than mixing with a spoon?

I don't have a blender, but I dump mine into a bowl and give it a good thorough beating with a wire whisk. It gives it a nice head of froth, although the froth turns back into oil-slick if the coffee sits / cools off too long. I only do CO in mine currently, no butter. Not sure if butter would help keep the froth emulsified longer....hmmm.

# Thread: P2, Paleo style?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-21T17:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3911>

Hi all, I'll be starting HCG soonish, probably next month giving shipping times, etc. So I'd love suggestions for a paleo-style P2. I've already decided to nix the "toast" and fruit, and make up the calories with more protein. Any menu recommendations or other ideas are more than welcome!

Oh also, I'm tentatively planning on breakfast w/in 30 mins. of waking, then dinner probably around 5pm, so incorporating a bit of the Leptin Rx along with it.

Thanks!

\-- Jennifer

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T19:07:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3945>

There are several on the big forum doing that, jeepifer. I'd try to load as much of the protein for breakfast as you can so you can go through until dinner. Eager to hear how you do!

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-21T19:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3947>

Thanks, I found a P3 group but no one seems to be in P2. Although I'm sure I'll end up coinciding with some people eventually.

Oh, does the tomato count as a fruit? I know in P&I, it's listed as a veggie. I was thinking ground beef and some tomato (aka P2 "chili") but now I'm second guessing. I don't have any allergen issues w/ eggs or nightshades as far as I know.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T19:52:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-3959>

It counts as a veggie.  Check this link, Jeepifer. There are several threads relating to it: [http://hcgdietinfo.com/hcgdietforums/f42/](http://http://hcgdietinfo.com/hcgdietforums/f42/)

### thomasina838@gmail.com — 2012-03-30T17:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-8775>

> **jeepifer;3936 wrote:** Hi all, I'll be starting HCG soonish, probably next month giving shipping times, etc. So I'd love suggestions for a paleo-style P2. I've already decided to nix the "toast" and fruit, and make up the calories with more protein. Any menu recommendations or other ideas are more than welcome!
>
>
>
> Oh also, I'm tentatively planning on breakfast w/in 30 mins. of waking, then dinner probably around 5pm, so incorporating a bit of the Leptin Rx along with it.
>
>
>
> Thanks!
>
> \-- Jennifer

im sorry i just noticed your post. but i tried this paleo/hcg and thought it didnt work. at least not for weight loss.. i had been doing the reset since nov 10. the end of jan i started an hcg round. eating mostly protein/some veggies. had no trouble with appetite. losses were painfully slow. but then they had been painfully slow the 3 rounds i tried last year that i did in a more conventional style. this was even slower. and by day 12 i had terrible protein cravings. i never had that before on hcg. but then my body may have been used to a lot more protein in recent months. i was down load weight and 5 more lbs. and i was due for dental surgery. so i thought best to eat all the protein i wanted. had the surgery. overate for a total of 8 days. it was a snap. gained some weight and rather than going back to hcg. i just called it quits as of day 12. turned to JUDDD to take off the weight. did it the first week. and of course i only had to diet every other day. so i am now sitting at almost 2 lbs below LIW. you know all that stuff we tell people on the hcg boards? that if you stop before 20 days you regain the weight and more? not so. i think it depends on what you do after. so i am now doing paleo/reset style eating on JUDDD and loving it. i think my hcg days are over.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T04:53:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-9090>

People who lose weight slowly on hcg tend to keep it off, thomasina. That probably helped you. There are always exceptions but it's very rare to keep the weight you've lost off if you stop before 23 days. Glad you did though. What is JUDD?

### Xena — 2012-03-31T05:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-9096>

I loaded protein in the a.m., sometimes with an allotted veggie, sometimes not. I lost 29.4 lbs in 30 days and ate grass-fed 90-10 hamburger nearly every day. I was thrilled at my losses. Didn't have dinner much because I ate all my food earlier but it worked great. I also took all of Dr. K's recommended supplements for HCG users. I am gearing up for another round in a few weeks and have been doing the reset since I came off HCG Feb. 9 (stabilized like a dream). I hope the next round works well but my losses will likely slow as I near where my body wants to be. I usually do about 30 day rounds--less is not that appealing and more makes me bored and likely to cheat.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T05:24:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/p2-paleo-style.342/post-9097>

That's fabulous, Xena! What did you lose on the previous round?

# Thread: Weight Loss on Leptin Reset -- for men
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BenG — 2012-03-21T17:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-3913>

I noticed the following in the original Leptin Rx that I wanted to clarify from any guys who have been doing this for a while:

"A. Men will notice quick weight loss.

B. Women will notice mood changes first (calmer/sleepy) and their sleep will improve. Their clothes will fit differently but weight may not change drastically initially because of effects on the pituitary. This will change too if they continue moving forward. "

My question for the guys...did you weight barely move for the 6-8 weeks and then drop off quickly around the time LS came up? I'm noticing some of the symptoms, (and I'm CTing), but the initial weight loss I had I think was just because I happened to be eating less via a reduced appetite. OTOH, I don't think I'm fully sensitive yet (I'm at week 7 now).

I'm thinking that once full LS is acquired in the brain, liver, and muscles, then fat loss occurs rapidly. But during the first 6-8 weeks (on average) the fat loss is little to insignificant, then like a switch, it drops off quick?

Anyone who has been on the reset a while..could you tell what really happened?

Thanks

### Croak — 2012-03-21T20:13:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-3971>

I was stable for weeks (early December through end of January), Then it just started dropping, and coinciding with several signs (sleep, temps, etc). After a couple weeks of that I started doing daily CT, and it's still dropping at about the same rate, roughly 2 pounds per week on average.

I wouldn't call that rapid, but it's not far off what Mark Sisson calls "effortless". I do stay below 25g carbs (often WELL below that), but don't avoid dairy (make my own whole milk kefir, eat hard cheeses). I also ate a helluva lot larger BAB the first several weeks, and took awhile to actually get the fat intake up, I suspect I was getting too much protein early on and not enough fat.

### kc_boxerman@yahoo.com — 2012-03-21T21:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4004>

I'm at about the same # of weeks as you. I have been fairly strict with the exception of dairy. Big change was cutting all rice and white potato. Also the high protein breakfast. I have snacked nearly zero but did not do a lot of that before. Protein may be a little low as my usual breakfast is 4 quality eggs and 3-4 good sized pieces of thick bacon.

I have lost about about 12lbs in a fairly consistent manner. Weight loss wasn't really a goal although I have some stomach fat I'm now motivated to shred. I'm considering moving into post reset but this isn't torture and I don't want to be "wrong" that I'm sensitive. For that matter I may have been sensitive to start with. However, I had bad rT3 and hs-CRP labs in October that indicate inflammation. Just had my blood drawn last week again so we'll see where I'm at...

Hang in there.

### AKMan — 2012-03-22T02:29:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4161>

Hey Ben - I started on Paleo/Primal Blueprint before i knew about the Leptin Reset, but I pretty much did the same thing. I stopped snacking, started eating a big, low-carb breakfast when I got up and skipped lunch most days.

It took a month or two, but soon enough I quit being hungry all the time. I was losing weight right from the start even without counting calories or carbs. The only carbs I was eating were fruit and veggies--no potatoes or rice.

When Jack started posting about the LR in late summer, I was like--'hey, that's what I did!' I saw all the signs he talked about (no cravings, losing weight, better sleep, not sweating like a pig). I started following his post-reset plan as soon as he came out with it and it has worked like a charm.

Back in 2010, I weighed 250 lbs. I now weigh 168lbs and have a 6-pack and feel awesome. My weight came off slowly but surely and I never had 2 months in a row where I gained back.

Not sure if this helps, but that's my story and I'm sticking to it!

### David — 2012-03-22T16:24:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4398>

Like AKMan, I was eating a Primal Diet for some time before encountering this site and the Leptin RX. Eliminating grain, sugar, dairy and legumes had a definate result: I lost 35 pounds fairly rapidly. Since starting Leptin Rx I have noticed the lack of cravings, better sleep and change in sweating, but my weight loss is not so noteworthy. CT has made for a bit more energy, I wake at 6AM or so every day and I am never tired enough to hit the sack before 10PM. Oh, and I never feel the urge any longer for an afternoon nap. Last week, for the first time in 14 years, I cleaned out the garage. (Found my favorite pocket knife!)

### Cody — 2012-03-22T19:29:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4534>

This is a great question.

I've lost no weight from the Leptin Reset. However, keep in mind that my starting weight was 456 pounds and I've lost 140 pounds so far on low carb and PSMF (100 grams of nothing but protein per day).

So my hormones and metabolism are probably FAR from optimal.

My most recent round of PSMF, I lost 20 more pounds. I got down to 310 lbs (I'm 6'4") and I was using ephedrine along with PSMF.

I felt crappy and constantly cold. My skin was extremely dry and flaky at all times. Thyroid issues anyone???

I decided to do CT and use the Leptin Reset at the same time.

I gained 10 pounds in about 2 weeks. It's ALL MUSCLE!!! Seriously awesome.

My weight is starting to come back down now, I'm at 318.

The first time I tried the Leptin Reset was many, many months ago, and I gained weight then as well, but didn't know about CT (none of us did) and wasn't sticking out 2 months of weight gain (or more) to see myself healed.

Leptin Reset combined with cold showers, sleeping in 40 degree temps at night, and many hours of home-made ice vest wearing a day has SIGNIFICANTLY helped here. See that Avatar photo up there in the left hand corner? I'm thinking that CT and Leptin Reset diet can get you to his level of muscle WITHOUT THE STEROIDS!!!

### tweety — 2012-03-22T20:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4590>

Woman here, but asking for my guy. Are hormones and stress enough to stop someone completely from seeing success? He has about 60lbs to lose, definitely low in testosterone, 42 yrs old... ton of stress and bad sleep. His appetite is fairly nonexistent, but we are thinking its due more to stress than true appetite because on the weekends he gets very hungry. However, he is really responding positively to CT....

stress reduction is a work in progress, and we're hoping the CT straightens out hormones and thyroid.

thoughts?

### David — 2012-03-22T20:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4596>

From all I have been able to absorb on this site, tweety, the answer is yes. Sleep problems, overweight, hormones and stress seem to be a very difficult to handle set of circumstances, but it seems that you have come to the right place!

### AKMan — 2012-03-23T02:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4773>

> **tweety;4618 wrote:** Woman here, but asking for my guy. Are hormones and stress enough to stop someone completely from seeing success? He has about 60lbs to lose, definitely low in testosterone, 42 yrs old... ton of stress and bad sleep. His appetite is fairly nonexistent, but we are thinking its due more to stress than true appetite because on the weekends he gets very hungry. However, he is really responding positively to CT....
>
> stress reduction is a work in progress, and we're hoping the CT straightens out hormones and thyroid.
>
>
>
> thoughts?

Get him to straighten out his diet and sleep before worrying about anything else. A strict primal diet, no snacking, and plenty of sleep in a dark room. Get a couple weeks of this under his belt, then start exercising some (pushups, pullups, easy stuff to do). See where this gets him in 2-3 months, my bet is he will be a new man, then if he wants to get even better in shape try cold and exercise more. If he doesn't respond in a few weeks, the leptin reset might be in order.

### BenG — 2012-03-23T07:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-4871>

> **David;4624 wrote:** From all I have been able to absorb on this site, tweety, the answer is yes. Sleep problems, overweight, hormones and stress seem to be a very difficult to handle set of circumstances, but it seems that you have come to the right place!

I asked Dr. K about weight loss rate, and he said it depends on Vitamin D and Testosterone level. In my case, in 1 month of CT, my T went from 224 to 336. Dr. K said it takes about 3-6 months of CT for T to really go up, so I figure fat loss will accelerate as CT continues to improve hormonal profile.

Regarding Vitamin D. I was 7 ng/dl in December. Been Supping 15k-20k a day, so it's 83.3. At least something has improved. Onward!

In Dr K's case, he said his T went from 300's to 800's in 3 months in his radio interview.

### Croak — 2012-03-25T18:58:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-on-leptin-reset-for-men.343/post-5867>

I'm seeing the scale drop steadily (did my weekly weigh-in this morning, -4.6lbs for the week!). On Monday I started doing a combination of two-a-days or double length soaks, for a total of at least an hour daily in 40f-ish water, which is what I attribute to the more than doubling of my average loss rate this week.

Seeing the numbers going down faster is sweet, but I'm becoming a bit awestruck at the rapid body comp and capability changes. I can see my ribcage again. I can see my special purpose _without sucking in my gut right after getting out of my soak_! Pushup, pullup and setup gains (returns?) have been rapid, so much so that I think I'd actually pass a Marine PFT (though the ability to do 18-20 minute 3 mile runs like I did when in the Corps 25 years ago is probably not coming back). Tone and definition are visibly returning.

Pitting edema on shins is minor now, and there is no pain at all when walking...it's just gone.

Took my motorcycle out for a total of 8 hours yesterday, in a combination of heavy traffic, twisty mountain roads, and high-speed highway blasts. It's a "naked" sport bike (Aprilia Tuono for those that care) and that much saddle time in those conditions (and SAD diet) would usually wipe me out and leave me feeling it the next day, even in late summer when I'd been acclimated to it for months.

Yesterday, got off the bike, stretched a little and was fine, with energy to spare. Did dinner, an hour CT, my evening calisthenics, got up this morning and feel great, so great that I'm shortly going back out on the bike again. Oh, and of course, I didn't need to turn on the heated gear even when blasting 70+ MPH down the highway for an hour (to get to and from the good roads), even though outside temps were around 45F.

# Thread: Are you doing chronic exercise that will cause you fail?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail.344/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2012-03-21T20:07:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail.344/post-3968>

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2PdJFbjWHEU\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;2PdJFbjWHEU\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2PdJFbjWHEU\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;2PdJFbjWHEU)

Dr Doug McGuff, an ER doctor and owner of a gym in South Carolina talks about the reasons why people fail in their exercise and body composition goals. He also wrote the book _Body by Science_

He also talks about how one can do less work in the gym and make huge gains by resting 7 days between workouts.

For the med students and doctors on this board, there is a good section of this presentation regarding medical school biochemistry.

### Cody — 2012-03-22T19:13:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail.344/post-4523>

Yes, but does he talk about using CT after working out to speed healing and lower inflammation??? If one uses CT, especially Cold Showers after strength training, I'm betting the frequency of workouts can go way up (they have for me).

### Dextery — 2012-03-22T23:23:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-doing-chronic-exercise-that-will-cause-you-fail.344/post-4654>

> **Cody;4551 wrote:** Yes, but does he talk about using CT after working out to speed healing and lower inflammation??? If one uses CT, especially Cold Showers after strength training, I'm betting the frequency of workouts can go way up (they have for me).

The video was taped a couple years ago and CT was not being discussed. But Dr McGuff commented that he now has his gym at 60 degrees F and Dr Kruse replied to him that he could go even lower and increase the benefits.

If you use the markers that Dr McGuff uses as well as Drew Baye uses, [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8nIZ4Yih\_88&feature=relmfu\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;8nIZ4Yih\_88\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8nIZ4Yih\_88&feature=relmfu\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;8nIZ4Yih_88)

you can test yourself to see if you can decrease the time between workouts. There is a sweet spot for everyone...but the take away is

do not overtrain...as that results in frustration in not making any headway....the muscles must be allowed to recalibrate to handle higher and higher weight stress in the epigenetic pathway.

Many people are so locked into more lifting/exercising is better conventional wisdom that they set themselves up for failure.

As a side note, you are in Des Moines where Dr Terry Wahls does her work. She has licked her mutiple sclorosis and gives talks around your area. Here is her first on mitochondria [http://www.bing.com/search?q=dr+terry+wahls&form=HPNTDF&pc=HPNTDF&src=IE-SearchBox](http://http://www.bing.com/search?q=dr+terry+wahls&form=HPNTDF&pc=HPNTDF&src=IE-SearchBox) Minding your mitochondria.

Wow. I've been training like a total idiot for years. Cycling, running, and swimming at the lower end of my exercise heart rate. Chronic-cardio moron, that was me. Hitting it everyday or every other day. All the while, I was never really increasing my "physiologic headroom" as discussed in this video. I blamed my lack of progress on age and "not going long enough."

Okay, exercise for me now is going to be climbing, crawling, balancing, throwing rocks, and logs and then once a week I'm going to give my mitochondria all the pyruvate they can handle with maximum effort sprints or body weights exercise...I'm too cheap to join a gym, plus I hate driving anywhere I don't have to. # Thread: Hello from Dogtor J in the deep South
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-dogtor-j-in-the-deep-south.345/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DogtorJ — 2012-03-21T20:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-dogtor-j-in-the-deep-south.345/post-3987>

Hi Dr. Kruse and fellow forum members,

My name is John B. Symes, DVM otherwise known as "Dogtor J" on the Internet. I was just referred to your site by an Internet acquaintance who felt strongly that I would enjoy reading about your work. She was right! We are definitely on the same path.

I have been a veterinarian since 1979. But..as I like to tell people, I've only been 'awake" for the last 12 years. The life-changing event in my life was my personal diagnosis of celiac disease (gluten intolerance). EVERYTHING changed after this. I went from a 45 year-old on 5 different medications to bullet-proof in less than 6 months, the latter status being reached after finding that dairy (cow's milk), corn and soy were also affecting my health very negatively. More research into these four foods led to thhem being called "the big 4", or..."the four horsemen of our medical apocalypse" as I like to write when being melodramatic (of which I am frequently - and accurately - accused). How can you not get fired up about this when medical miracles (including your own) keep happening around you?

As you will see on my site, I have developed a diet (The GARD - The Glutamate & Aspartate Restricted Diet; The Gut Absorption Recovery Diet) that has been particularly effective in the management of idiopathic epilepsy, ADHD, fibromyalgia and other neurological conditions. But it has also been very effective in the treatment of allergies, asthma, sinusitis, psoriasis, and numerous "autoimmune" disorders. A fundamental understanding of food intolerance is really all that is required to understand how this is so.

However, I have dug quite deeply into the workings of the body - both four and two legged - and I can honestly say that medicine now makes sense...complete sense...except for how far the medical professions have strayed from the path of rooting out the true causes of what we like to call "disease", which are rarely more than chronic, worsening symptoms that occur because we never got to the core of why they occurred in the first place.

I look forward to sharing with you and hearing the concerns of those on the forum. I hope you will have the opportunity to visit my site. I am always looking for forward-thinking neurologists who can help bring this critically important dietary message to mainstream neurology. Epilepsy is an easy starting point. I call it "the hook" because of its dramatic presentation (what is more frightening and potentially debilitating than seizures?) but its equally dramatic response to dietary intervention.

Your comrade in arms,

John (Dogtor J)

### Jillm — 2012-03-21T23:27:05+0200

# Thread: If I were to invest in one test ...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T20:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-3989>

What would you recommend?

DHEA? rT3? HS-CRP?

I can't afford a full panel of tests at this time, so what might you suggest will provide me with the most information for the least amount of $.

In case some background info is helpful: I'm on the reset and doing CT. I'm about 10-15 pounds of fat from ideal body comp. After 2 weeks on both protocols, I am seeing signs of LS, like improved energy, mood, libido, hair, skin, and body comp. Prior to starting the reset, I was LC Primal for nearly 2 years, and lost nearly 60 pounds that way, until hitting a major plateau this fall/winter.

Thanks!

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T20:46:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-3990>

My vote would be HS-CRP. It's non-specific, but predictive. It's a good sign-post for multiple issues. Get it when you feel well. It will go up with a cold, etc.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-21T20:52:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-3993>

save your $$ if one test is all you can afford

follow the Leptin RX and merry it with any others you might need (leaky gut rx etc.) do CT

if you want to invest the money get a few good supplements... b vitamin complex, multi, d vitamin ... from the top ten supplement list

Or even better, get some high quality food grass-fed meat, wild fish...

### Garfield — 2012-03-21T21:07:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-4002>

Depends how much money you have available for your 1 test.

Personally I would recommend getting the March monthly special tests from [Direct Labs](http://https://www.directlabs.com/MonthlySpecials/tabid/2492/language/en-US/Default.aspx). For about $140 you get a gender specific health check and D3. That is a whole raft of tests and includes the hs-crp as well as a number of other good things. I just wish it had the reverse T3 and ferritin as well. (Adding those would cost about $110 from [I Need Labs](http://http://www.ineedlabs.com/) with their 15% discount.)

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T21:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-4025>

> **Souldanzer;4018 wrote:** save your $$ if one test is all you can afford
>
>
>
> follow the Leptin RX and merry it with any others you might need (leaky gut rx etc.) do CT
>
>
>
> if you want to invest the money get a few good supplements... b vitamin complex, multi, d vitamin ... from the top ten supplement list
>
>
>
> Or even better, get some high quality food grass-fed meat, wild fish...

THis is a good point. If you're really strapped, just spend money on the the very best food you can afford. Cold showers are pretty cheap, too, thankfully. One test isn't really going to give you a clear picture. I don't think you can go to far wrong with good food, good sleep, low stress, and some cold water.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T22:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-i-were-to-invest-in-one-test.346/post-4037>

Thanks, everyone!

I'm already eating a high quality diet and taking most of the supplements that were recommended.

I might go for the direct labs special (Thanks for the link, Garfield!). That would give me HS-CRP, CWP, Urinalysis, Estradiol for $99. I already had my d levels checked last year (37 ng/mL), so I'm working on getting that up.

# Thread: Competition! Need Measurable Goals!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/competition-need-measurable-goals.347/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-21T20:58:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/competition-need-measurable-goals.347/post-3997>

Here's the deal. My husband joined me in LRx and CT a few weeks ago. The bum only has about 20ish pounds to lose and he's already lost at least 5 of those (he hasn't weighed in about a week). Me, I've lost 0. No surprise.

Anyhow, at his place of employment they're having a "Healthy Living Contest" Below are the rules:

We are encouraging all of our employees to team up and win with Healthy Living Teams. Each team of two to four employees will choose the measurable goal or goals that they wish to achieve by December of 2012. After a deliberation by outside professionals at the end of the year, the winning team will get a prize of their choice valued at up to $1000.

Criteria

Teams must register by Wednesday, March 28.

Teams must consist of two to four employees.

When registering, teams must specify what category their goal or goals fall into. All goals must have documented measurables at the close of competition which is on Friday, December 7, 2012.

Teams can determine what their prize will be, which cannot exceed a $1000 value total. Prizes will be reviewed by the exec team for feasibility.

Team results will be confidentially reviewed and judged by outside professionals.

There are a few more details, but that is the gist of it. The categories are medical, physical, and spiritual. He is currently trying to convince at least one more person who will join his team. He's had several people laugh at him and tell him he is crazy for doing CT. But our big question is... _**what should be the measurable goals??? Ideas???**_

# Thread: CT + keto -- concerns
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 117
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### anonymouse — 2012-03-21T21:17:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4010>

I've been digging through this website and others for about a week now to try and figure out why Dr. K recommends the ketogenic diet. I understand the concept -- all starch/sugar causes some level of damage and it's best to avoid them. But I'm hung up over the side effects that long-term ketosis can have....I posted this here because I wonder if CT is what fixes keto's problems, though I can't sift through the articles here :/

I've heard stories of long-term keto adherents ending up with very high fasting blood glucose levels, something about the body conserving glucose...does this cause harm? Also, temporary insulin resistance from long-term keto?

Also been reading about poor T4 -> T3 conversion leading to low metabolism on keto -- while this may not be a problem, it _does_ lead to crazy cholesterol numbers, apparently.

I need someone more knowledgeable than me to look at this and help alleviate my fears... I'm 2 weeks into CT and 3 days into

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-21T21:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4014>

Long story short with CT, keto is needed for the "ancient pathway" to work. SO if you are not ketogenic, the pathway has a roadblock.

This is a therapy to reverse things wrong. Once "fixed" you can then choose to live by natural cycles such as more carbs in summer, more keto in winter.

### kc_boxerman@yahoo.com — 2012-03-21T21:49:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4016>

> **Cavemam;4040 wrote:** This is a therapy to reverse things wrong. Once "fixed" you can then choose to live by natural cycles such as more carbs in summer, more keto in winter.

And Dr. K thinks you should check telomere length ($290 blood test) to know how quickly your cells have aged relative to your chronological age. CT while keto will grow you telomeres, which he think almost everyone needs. Check for yourself...

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-21T21:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4026>

It's not long-term. It's temporary. It may be a few months for some, 2 years for others, but I've been VLC for close to 6 months and fasting blood glucose has only gone down. My LDL has gone up, but as you mentioned, it's a symptom of a thyroid problem that was already there. Thyroid stuff is getting better with the addition of dietary thyroid, and I expect it will continue to improve (and reverse the LDL trend) as my adrenals turn around.

BTW, my HDL has gone up, triglycerides have gone down, and percentage of LDL that is large & fluffy has increased. My cholesterol situation is undoubtedly improving, even if the LDL number is temporarily on the rise.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T22:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4027>

Keto is the only way to do CT because there were/are no carbs growing in cold climates or during winter. So, if you eat carbs and do CT, you are causing a mismatch, leading to inflammation. You confuse the body that thinks it is winter by feeding it food that is only available in the summer.

Keto and CT work in harmony, since our bodies expect and know how to respond to a high fat, moderate protein diet in winter/cold environments. For the biochemistry, you'll have to dig into the CT series directly on the blog. I'm a "non-geek," so I can't help with that.

### anonymouse — 2012-03-21T22:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4050>

Thanks for the replies...

MamaGrok, it's reassuring that your cholesterol is doing good. I'm going to keep reading into this, particularly on the whole thyroid thing, but I'll keep at it.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-22T03:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4200>

He covered this today pretty well in CT7

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-22T15:20:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4346>

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T15:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4358>

I have to get down to pretty much just meat, fat, & greens (all I want of them), and then the ketostix show purple. Just keep in mind that if they show purple, then stop, that doesn't necessarily mean you've gone out of ketosis, if diet is the same. It just could mean you're using all the ketones you're producing, instead of wasting them in your urine.

### primat — 2012-03-22T16:45:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4422>

I have to stay below 15-20 to get some pink on the stick. But it's unlikely you're not making ketones with such low carb intake - you just don't make enough to make it spill out into the urine. I guess that's what an efficient fat burner would do; make as little ketones as possible while utilizing free fatty acids directly.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-22T16:47:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4424>

Can someone advise me on a good ketone test product?

Thanks MamaGrok and Primat. Hadn't considered that. Just looking to shred this belly blubber but I'm only 5 pounds from my goal. I'll just keep going!

### Snowyky — 2012-03-22T23:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4647>

Not sure where to stick this but barely above trace on a ketostix has me wondering what the heck. I'm feeling pretty down about all this, why can't I lose weight? I'm over 300 lbs, I did lose when I first started but nothing since. 2 1/2 months on the leptin reset and over a month doing CT with ice packs, face dunks and cold baths and am now having ice baths for 60mins with the temp being 50\* - 55\* everyday now for 2 weeks. I take all the supplements Dr K mentions, I've been using the yellow glasses at night and am usually in bed by 9:30 with lights out by 10, I still wake up quite a bit at night and am getting hungrier by the day  I did better it seemed when I first started, I thought it was because I was going so very very low carb with no veggies, just fat and meat, so I added back a salad for lunch, add mushrooms to my BAB omelet. I've tried cutting out CO and it didn't make any difference. I notice fat disappearing in some areas ie: weird spots in my arms and wrists, husband says my legs are getting smaller but when I check my other measurements, they haven't moved and neither is the scale. When I'm in the tub I don't shiver and I'm warming up quicker once I get out and I'm starving when I get out  and can't eat because I do the bath after my last meal at night. I drink water which is probably what's making me get up during the night.

I've been looking at what labs I should get done as I read that Dr K told someone with the same kind of problems that something else is wrong if CT isn't stopping the hunger, but I'm lost when it comes to what labs, I have gone through menopause, but don't have any of the typical symptoms ie: hot flashes and dryness below, if you know what I mean. I read somewhere that Dr K suggested 3 labs if we can't afford all, and they were Alpha MSH, Vit D and HS CRP. Since I don't have much money left to spend as I've been spending well over $200 a month on supplements, does anyone have any suggestions, I had the typical labs done at my doctor's but she wouldn't order the rest as she didnt' think I needed them, and they included all the hormone ones which I thought was kind of strange, but she says I have no symptoms so why worry. Here are my labs for what I did get done,

Tryglycerides 106 mg/dl Range or =46

LDL Cholesteral 109 Rangeg weight, I've tried lowering the calories for lunch and dinner to less then 500, but no difference except more hungry. The BAB is usually around 51gm of protein, fitday says a typical day is Fat 75%, Protein 23%, Carbs 2%. avg 1100 to 1900 calories depending what I have. Exercise, I try to walk every day or use my gravity glider or tubes, my job is very sedentary so I try to move around at least once an hour via the gravity glider or walk outside for a bit.

If anyone has any ideas for labs or other tips that would get me on the weight loss road I would really appreciate it, I will stick to the reset and CT because i feel better when I'm doing it, my blood sugar is lower via CT so that's a definite plus. Oh yes the ketostix show very little even though I eat very little carbs, just slightly above a trace. Help, ~Eleanor

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T23:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4655>

Have you added the CO back in? I know I get very, very hungry when I don't have enough fat.

How were you eating before the LR? It looks like your low HDL & high Trigs, Insulin, Blood Glucose, are likely relics from your former way of eating? Are you having \*any\* wheat at all? How about dairy?

I would personally increase the protein at breakfast, make sure I had plenty of fat for breakfast & dinner, and \*not\* reduce dinner calories until I had a natural reduction in hunger (it will likely take a long while for this). Remember the LR range for BAB protein is 50-75gm ... some of usneed more to trigger the healing that leads to hunger reduction & craving reduction ... OTOH, stomach acid could also be a problem. Have you supplemented hcl at any point? Lacking the HCl or digestive enzymes neeeded to digest food can lead to hunger, since you're not getting the nutrients you need, even when eating a lot..

Remember, weight is a symptom of underlying problems - your weight will likely not budge until some other things turn around.

sorry about odd typos; my arms are still shaking from my cold bath!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-22T23:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4668>

Snowyky...have you read the Oprah blog? I re-read it today along with comments and it helped. Sorry, I don't know anything about labs but I am wondering why you are exercising. If you are still Leptin Resistant, exercise will not help, and may only make things worse. If you want, list out the foods you are eating...could be a food sensitivity or something.

Nice tip on the Hcl, MamaGrok - I think I need some.

Hang in Eleanor!

### Snowyky — 2012-03-23T00:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4690>

Thanks for your input MamaGrok,

Actually I had the same labs done a couple of years ago before I started the paleo way of eating and things got a bit higher on some things but i figured that would happen from what I read happened to other people,

2009

Trigs 126 now at 106

Cholesteral, Total 123 now 181

HDL Chol 45 now at 51

LDL Chol 53 now at 109

Glucose was 185 now at 100

HA1c was 7.9 now is 5.7

After I found out I was diabetic I followed weight watchers and eventually found Dr Berstein who is mainly low carb, then switched to Dr K's protocol when the weight wasn't moving.

I'm having absolutely no wheat, I make my own dressing using walnut oil and red wine vinegar when have salad, I've always eaten alot of eggs, we do eat out quite a bit but I have salad and meat or seafood, I can taste right away if there is any sugar in any of the meals and stop if I find any.

I will check out the HCL and keep on trucking, will also add extra protein, funny thing was I used be able to go all day until dinner with just a BAB, not quite sure when I noticed this changed, I think when I started the CT, which should've made it better eh?

Yeah I think they is something underlying, but what is the question.

Jennifer, I will go and read it again, thanks, also the exercise isn't really exercise like I was doing, it's only something to get my blood working since sitting at the computer for my job tends to make me feel sore and it's like my muscles are atrophying.

Here is what I eat on a typical day,

BAB

3 omega 3 eggs, 4 slices bacon, 4 links of sausage, sometimes 1/4 cup of mushrooms.

chocolate bark made with 1T coconut oil, 1t 100% Cacao, 5 drops of liquid splenda. Sometimes will have cheese but have stopped that along with other dairy.

Lunch

2 cups raw salad mix, mainly lettuce

2 T walnut oil with 2 T red wine vinegar

3 oz hard salami (less then 1 carb per oz)

chocolate bark

Dinner

shrimp scampi (5 oz shrimp, 3 T butter, garlic powder)

asparagus spears wrapped in bacon, 2 bundles of 5 with 1 bacon slice

chocolate bark

only change is for dinner I will have 4 - 5 oz of chicken or omega 3 beef (steak) tried 2 oz when i was watching calories, veggies sometimes will be 1 cup cabbage, cauliflower or broccoli with butter/coconut oil

I was having 1/2 sugar free jello with 1/4 cup homemade whipped cream using liquid splenda or divinci's syrups,

I sometimes have pork rinds for a snack along with a meal, usually with tuna salad at lunch.

I never go above 20 carbs usually stick below 10 until I have veggies with both meals.'

Been trying to eat more seafood, if we have fish I usually raise the size to 6oz, tilapia, cod or salmon.

thanks for you help ~Eleanor

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-23T00:55:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4697>

I also thought I'd mention that it's possible to get ketostix that are duds! I got a whole big package on them that were duds, I guess that's why they were so cheap. I've been showing the darkest level of ketones on my current ketostix for several weeks now but haven't lost even a pound! ARGH.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-23T01:07:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4710>

> **Snowyky;4718 wrote:**
>
> only change is for dinner I will have 4 - 5 oz of chicken or omega 3 beef (steak) tried 2 oz when i was watching calories, veggies sometimes will be 1 cup cabbage, cauliflower or broccoli with butter/coconut oil
>
>
>
> I was having 1/2 sugar free jello with 1/4 cup homemade whipped cream using liquid splenda or divinci's syrups,
>

>
> I never go above 20 carbs usually stick below 10 until I have veggies with both meals.'
>
> Been trying to eat more seafood, if we have fish I usually raise the size to 6oz, tilapia, cod or salmon.
>
> thanks for you help ~Eleanor

My hack for you is eat more. That's really not that much for someone at your weight. I think those that get down to 2 meals, and no hunger are less fat to be blunt. I can say that! I'm a woman and I have been that fat!. Perhaps the sheer number of fat cells are still sending out there little "i'm hungry!" signals. What realllllly helped me change my tastes was dropping diet drinks and any and all sweet tasting things. One of his posts allude to the taste receptors doing signalling, and I think for some of us, even the taste of sweet might have an impact. It was a good 6 months after cutting all sweets until I stopped being hungry for them - then BOOM, one day I realized my tastes had changed.

Also, I know we are not supposed to exercise until reset - but I found daily leisure walks (along with jumping and screaming during CT) to help bust a plateau. Plus it gets you out, gets the real vit d, and generally makes you happier.

Good Luck!

Keep us posted!

I don't know for sure what my percentage of fat is, but I do know that so far, anything over 30g carbs a day brings me out of ketosis.

I'd ditch the Splenda, use stevia if you need sweetness. Grassfed beef is a better option to chicken. What's in your salami and sausages? Upping your seafood is a good idea. Increasing grassfed beef would be good too. Better to spend money in good food than lots of Supps. Homemade bone broths and fermented vegetables are healthy and cheap. Organs from grassfed animals are also really nutritious. I'd also increase your fats. Protein + fats is what every meal should consist of, then if you want some veg add some leafy greens or fermented veg. I drink green tea with coconut oil to add extra fat. All the best.

### Snowyky — 2012-03-23T04:00:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4797>

I'll get rid of the liquid splenda and just have coconut oil and the 100%cacao powder, hmmm I just remembered another thing I'm having that probably has carbs in it, lemon juice, I was drinking 'True lemon' with splenda and ended up buying a box of just the 'true lemon' crystallized lemon and have been using that in my water, it says there are 0carbs but it has evaporated cane juice and soy in the ingredients. Probably have about 9 of them a day, still only 9 carbs if we go with at least 1 per packet. But I'll get rid of it, and try to stick with as pure food as I can get, the hard salami is from walmart, the package says carbs 0 gm per 28gm, but I count 1 carb per oz, sausage had no fillers but has 1 carb per 2 links.

I only eat grass fed beef and have for months now, the chicken is no hormone, no antibiotic from kroger, more expensive too, but really I don't eat much of that anymore, sticking with the seafood and grassfed beef. Can't eat organ meats, sorry, just can't make myself eat it, the smell and the taste makes me gag.

I'll add more fat and eat more protein, I guess it's the SAD heritage that is making me think .. eat less and exercise more even though I know what is best is the opposite. It's a hard habit to get rid of when you are working hard at eating right and the fat is just sitting there hanging on for dear life :|

Fitday says I had 103gm of protein today with 201 grms of fat, so I could up it a bit and see how I feel, will start to add the coconut oil to some tea as well.

Y'all are a great help and I thank you, while I was in the tub tonight I read some of the "Leptin Reset Experiment - the optimized living edition" and was inspired again, sounds like I'm not the only one getting hungry from CT but it looks like things will eventually even itself out, I'll just keep working the plan.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-23T04:32:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4819>

Snowyky, just wanted to second what others have said about the importance of a clean diet. Use more CO to up your fats and cut out the substandard stuff like jello and pork rinds. Even bits of sugar slipping in have the effect of making me go bananas (or, well, chocolate!) and make me feel that awful craving feeling all day long. CT does definitely increase hunger, at least after you warm up. Increasing the fat in the meal prior is helpful. And, like others have said, as soon as I start feeling hungry and deprived I end up with other problems.

As far as organ meats go, get some bruschweier (that's probably not spelled correctly) liverwurst. It's easy to get down (and I was a vegetarian for 25 years so that's a big statement!).

Only other thought that comes to mind based on my similar experiences... work on the gut! If your gut is leaky all those supplements are lost. Make yourself some sauerkraut (EASY to do and very cheap - all you need is a head of organic cabbage, sea salt and a container) and maybe think about other probiotics, digestive enzymes and anti-parasite things. It helped me a lot. I re-read the leaky gut Rx posts often. My budget for supplements is almost non-existent, so I've tried to focus on the gut and the liver and deal to them most directly via a very clean paleo keto diet and CT.

Good luck!! And hang in there.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-23T04:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4824>

I'd definitely swap the splenda for stevia (or nothing), and chuck the pork rinds \*if\* they're storebought (usually fried in some junky oil).

Lauren, how do you prepare the bruschwhatever. I have two different packagess in my freezer, one uncooked from US Wellness meats, and another uncooked from a local farm.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-23T05:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-4837>

MamaGrok, I just slice and pan fry (usually in CO). It's very mild (at least to me) and doesn't taste at all of liver in any other form that I've tried it. Sometimes, if I'm having it in the morning, I'll pan fry and cut those slices into bite-sized bits and add eggs.

### Cody — 2012-03-24T17:24:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-5431>

Fried Braunschweiger, eggs, and sauerkraut is a traditional German breakfast and very good for you. Especially fried in butter or coconut oil. Good stuff!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-24T17:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-5436>

I have to second the idea of spending the $ on food before supps. Read some of the paleo books/info on bad oils. It makes you realize how bad the oils are that are not "clean".

So you mission, should you choose to accept it - eat more real food. Stop putting everything in Fit day! The only thing to worry about on the reset is enough protien at BAB. 8oz of a clean protein source should do it. Don't worry about only two meals till the weight starts to move and you don't want them.

I bet this helps!

### Birdy — 2012-03-24T18:52:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-keto-concerns.348/post-5469>

OP, I think you have been spending too much time reading stuff at the banana websites. Please go back and read Dr. K's earlier posts on leptin and other hormones. For information about low carbs and insulin resistance, read up on Hyperlipid. Kresser believes that low carb diets cause high rT3, but if the problem was rampant, it would have been noted by the other low carb people.
