# Thread: PaleoSue's weight loss journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoSue — 2012-03-23T01:05:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-4709>

Hello, all. I'm a 30-something struggling with sugar addiction, leaky gut and obesity. I learned about Dr. Kruse from Jimmy Moore's podcast, and has been following his blog and Facebook page ever since. I thought I'd join here and see if I can use this journal to help keep myself on track.

SW: 259

GW: 180 or less

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-23T01:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-4718>

Best of luck to you!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-23T01:22:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-4722>

Homemade shrimp/mushroom curry, no rice. This meal was completely spontaneous, and is my first successful attempt at curry. It was unbelievably delicious.

(I've had zero carbs all day, but this is more carbs than I would have liked.)

And on another note, there \*has\* to be an easier way to post pictures. This was crazy. Tim Ferriss recommended photographing all the food you eat in his book, and I'd like to give it a go.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/4750=26-6860937278_43e48c0aae.jpg)

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-23T15:17:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-4936>

(Oops, I got the date wrong in the posts above.)

Breakfast: Leftover curry, and some tuna. The tuna tasted TERRIBLE. It was very tough. I am guessing I overcooked it. I am not used to cooking fish and have been trying to increase seafood intake in my diet per Dr. Kruse's recommendation that about half of your food come from seafood.

I put the tuna in the curry, and I could only eat half of it, it was so filling.

Lunch: Chicken soup and egg frittata

Dinner: Leftover curry, sans tuna

Attached files [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/4968=31-7008132733_d81bb38b93.jpg)

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-24T13:54:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-5365>

The curry has not been agreement with my stomach. I'm not sure if the coconut milk wasn't diluted enough, if it was just too high in fat, or if there is another food intolerance I don't know about. It's frustrating because the curry was soooo yummy.

But moving on.

I really want to be posting photos of my meals in this thread, but I've not found a simple way to get them from my phone to the computer, so that's a bit annoying also.

Breakfast was chicken sausage fried in coconut oil.

Lunch was smoked turkey and homemade mushroom soup.

I went HIKING this afternoon in Vibrams, which I've been wearing for a little over a year. Woot. I had a wonderful time. I have struggled with Achilles pain for something like 15 years, and it is 85% gone following the leptin RX. I will see whether my Achilles hurts tomorrow, but I am crossing my fingers no. The muscles in my feet did get tired by the end of the hike, but I had a good time.

[http://www.flickr.com/photos/76409524@N07/6864611816/](http://http://www.flickr.com/photos/76409524@N07/6864611816/)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/5399=33-7011673299_50faf11b1d_m.jpg)

### ealachan — 2012-03-24T15:10:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-5382>

> **PaleoSue;4968 wrote:** (Oops, I got the date wrong in the posts above.)
>
>
>
> Breakfast: Leftover curry, and some tuna. The tuna tasted TERRIBLE. It was very tough. I am guessing I overcooked it. I am not used to cooking fish and have been trying to increase seafood intake in my diet per Dr. Kruse's recommendation that about half of your food come from seafood.
>
>
>
> I put the tuna in the curry, and I could only eat half of it, it was so filling.
>
>
>
> Lunch: Chicken soup and egg frittata
>
>
>
> Dinner: Leftover curry, sans tuna

Tuna is often better the less it's cooked. I like mine just seared on the outside but still red in the middle.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-25T04:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-5663>

Good luck. I ended up finding out about Dr. Kruse through Jimmy Moore as well. I'm thankful to these folks for getting the word out to use about the things that will help us!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-26T01:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-6088>

@ealachan, thanks so much for the tip on cooking tuna! I think I'm going to pick up some more paleo cookbooks soon. I can cook well enough to keep myself alive, but maybe not-so-well to keep me extremely happy with food selection. Unfortunately my stomach is so sensitive, that I often cannot tolerate a lot of paleo adaptations, like mashed cauliflower and probably butter. So maybe I just really need a cookbook for making fish. Heh.

@paulalynn, hello! Yes, I'm so thankful for the Jimmy Moore podcasts, but I have to admit I have not listened as much as I once did. Love that guy.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-26T01:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-6089>

I'm back from a weekend away, and I am SOOOOOOO PROUD of myself. There was temptation everywhere, and I dodged all of it.

I at an event where there was unlimited food and soda included in the admission. And of course none of the food that I saw was paleo. There were sweet delights, tons of candy, and of course any variety of soda you could want. (And soda is one of my biggest weaknesses.)

The worst part was one of the people I went with decided to go get McDonald's, one of my previous binge places, and this person even ordered some of the very food I would binge on. He offered me food, and I said, "no thanks," and fortunately he didn't keep pushing so I didn't have to keep saying no.

I brought my own food for the weekend, and stuck with water. I felt satisfied and full, and I feel so proud of the way I handled things.

Update: My Achilles both felt tight this morning from yesterday's hike, but the pain went away quickly and they don't hurt now at all. I'm not limping around all day like I would have before starting the leptin RX and giving up soda. However, it's a sign I'm not completely healed yet, either.

Also my tummy got quick sick this evening. I'm not sure what is causing this. Again, possibly the sausage I ate this morning had too much fat. (It is gluten-, grain- and soy-free.)

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-26T14:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-6290>

Still had diarrhea this morning. I had an opposite thought: maybe it's too much protein that could be causing it. I know I have fat malabsorption, and protein as well because my digestive tract is so damaged. I think I might add some easily digestible vegetables a la the SCD diet and see if that improves anything.

Good news is neither of my Achilles hurt this morning. YES!!!! I can't wait to go hiking again.

Started watching the documentary "Hungry for Change." I really like this quote:

"Sugar is without question the cocaine of the food world, but we kind of get away with hiding that drug with 'food,' " -- Hungry for Change.

Breakfast - smoked turkey, homemade pork "meatloaf," and egg fritata

Lunch - smoked turkey, pork meatloaf

No dinner. Got home at 7:30 p.m. from work and not particularly hungry.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-26T14:31:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-6291>

Another quote - loving this documentary so far.

"For me, the biggest cause of obesity bar none is addiction. But to understand the level of addiction that we've got might be challenging for some because of course people can understand the addiction to cigarettes, because that has now been proven, and they can understand the addition to alcohol. ... The way that cigarettes are addictive is much in the same the way that certain food is addictive. They know why they shouldn't do it, but they have no idea why they are doing it."

"The food companies engineer addictions, I believe, into many of the foods."

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T03:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-7474>

Didn't sleep great last night - I think that's a leftover from being up so late on Saturday and taking a nap on Sunday. Hopefully I'll sleep better tonight.

6:30 a.m. - I had pork meatloaf and a small portion of boiled/mashed carrots. I didn't finish the carrots.

2:30 p.m. - Tried to eat new tuna I cooked, and it \*still\* tasted TERRIBLE. I'm so disappointed. That's $20 down the drain in wild tuna. I also brought smoked turkey and ate some carrots.

8 p.m. - Small amount of coconut water, kombucha and 3 olives.

Today's big news is I went for another hike. I hiked for an hour at a pretty good pace. It was so amazing and just downright exhilarating to be able to hike without pain. If you've always been able to walk without pain, then I don't think you could really comprehend what a joy it was. I almost broke into a run at some points, but I didn't want to overdo it.

When I got back to the house, I expected the usual stiffness in my calves/achilles when I got out of the car, but it just wasn't there. We'll see how I feel tomorrow morning.

I JUST CANNOT BELIEVE I CAN GO HIKING WITHOUT PAIN.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T14:03:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-7657>

I did wake up with some stiffness in the Achilles. It is going away. I'll need to rest a few days.

Also, I had another dream last night. I've been having/remembering a good number of dreams in the last week or two. And they are really cool dreams. It's like going to a movie theater every night. I'm wondering if this is also a "side effect" of going cleaner paleo/leaky gut RX.

6 a.m. - Bristol type 2. Finally not diarrhea. Maybe adding the carrots is working.

6:30 - Small piece of leftover fish, some boiled/mashed carrots, pork and sauerkraut and coconut oil.

7:20 - spoke too soon. Bristol type 6.

12:30 - pork and sauerkraut, carrots, pork meatloaf

2:30 - Bristol type 6. :/

6 p.m. - sashimi!

9 p.m. - 2 pcs. 100% chocolate. It just arrived tonight, and I went to a movie with a friend. I told myself if I didn't have any popcorn or candy or soda, I could have chocolate when I got home.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/7697=55-6877428968_0cd52a921d_m.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/7697=60-6879006782_8be4351935_m.jpg)

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T13:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-8186>

I'm in my fourth week, and I'm nearly down 10 pounds. Woot woot.

I had another really vivid dream last night. I dreamed I was a helicopter pilot in an interstellar war. I have had these vivid dreams nearly every night for perhaps a week. Someone theorized this might be a sign our body is making more melotonin. I'd love to post this to Dr. K.

6:15 a.m. - Pork and kraut.

12 p.m. - Pork n kraut, a bit of salmon.

5 p.m. - Bristol Type 6. What?

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8231=64-6880251226_5eb70d1fbf_m.jpg)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-29T19:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-8347>

10 pounds! Awesome! You're doing great!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-30T13:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-8691>

It's official:

I've lost 10 pounds!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-30T13:55:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-8694>

So yesterday was super duper awesome. I went to a gathering at my Mom's house, and it has always been near impossible not to binge at her house. It's where I started binge eating, and there is always guaranteed to be tons of junk food of all kinds, even gluten-free junk food.

She made pork barbecue (with sugar, obviously). I passed down dinner at first because I was so full. FOUR different adults tried to get me to eat dinner. (WTF people?!) I just said I was so stuffed because I had a late lunch, and maybe I'd have some later. Later my stomach did start growling, so I had a little bit of pork barbecue, and I got an instant headache, probably from the sugar.

They pulled out cupcakes, ice cream, potato chips, pretzels, all manner of junk food. And I wasn't even tempted. I WASN'T EVEN TEMPTED!!!

I was so full and so satisfied, that it didn't even bother me.

Awesome.

6 a.m. - Bristol Type 2.

6:30 a.m. - Leftover pork n kraut. Getting sick of it, but planning to make something special tonight.

1:30 p.m. - pork n kraut. So sick of it. And 1 square 86% dark chocolate.

3:30 p.m. - Bristol Type 2.

8 p.m. - Portabello mushroom with meatballs and organic tomato sauce without sugar

So, bad news. The worse of my Achilles is hurting me a bit today. Not a lot, not as much as before I started the RX, but enough for me to notice it. I'm wondering if the tiny bit of sugar I had last night -- less than 1/2 cup of pork barbecue -- has made that much of a difference. In any case no sugar is passing my lips any more.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8744=71-6884613008_790689040a_m.jpg)

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-03-30T14:10:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-8699>

Woooow!! That was the real test and you did awesome!!  You must be so proud!

Funny (I'll stay polite) how people want to force others to eat!! I said I'm stuff, why would I want to eat some more?? It's also hard for people to see others changing for better while they are stuck in their old habits and would love to change too, less the compromises.

Keep going girl, you are a rock start!!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-30T19:33:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-8851>

Well I think we're all going to finish off March on a positive note! Yeah for us!!! Here's to a fabulous April

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-31T17:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-9240>

Interesting thing happened last night. I \*wanted\* a cold bath. I was a bit tired, but I really craved it, so in I went. I had the coldest water I had to date, and it didn't bother me that much. Cool. I'm reading "Game of Thrones," and I enjoy sitting in the tub, reading by candlelight and really relaxing.

So work called me in the middle of the night to do something, so my sleep was completely interrupted. (I at least knew this was coming, and in the past it caused massive insomnia. I didn't fall asleep immediately last night, but fairly quickly compared with the past.) I woke up not feeling so refreshed though.

I'm down exactly 11 pounds this morning since I started March 2, although I didn't weigh myself until March 7, so I'm not entirely sure what my "true" starting weight was.

Plan for the day is to take my bicycle in to the shop to be repaired, and possibly go kayaking. The weather doesn't look great, though.

10:30 a.m. - Leftover meatballs and sauce. So yummy.

11 a.m. - Bristol Type 4. Woot!!!!

6:30 p.m. - Last of the leftover meatballs and sauce. 2 squares 100% dark chocolate. Kombucha.

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-02T17:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-10307>

Didn't sleep great last night because of the interruption from work, but what can you do?

Breakfast was a grass-fed hamburger wrapped in bacon, and lunch/dinner was the same. Yum.

I also ate a peeled apple and 2 squares of 86% dark chocolate. Yummy.

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-02T17:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-10308>

Yikes, I stayed awake way too long tonight, and I'm going to be suffering today. Being awoken in the middle of the night for work has really challenged me the last couple of days -- I don't know how parents do it on a regular basis.

Breakfast was a leftover grass-fed burger wrapped in bacon, and lunch will be the same.

My weight is falling at a rate of about 0.6 pound per day, so that's awesome.

The weather this weekend was pretty cold, and I didn't go kayaking like I had hoped.

Tonight I need to run some errands, so no opportunity to go hiking in the near future, either. Perhaps on Thursday.

I am thinking my leptin is reset or nearly reset, so I will be wanting to incorporate weight lifting in the next couple of weeks, and I can't wait!

### Birdy — 2012-04-02T18:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-10344>

Great!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-03T13:47:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-10724>

Wow, last night ended up being really stressful. I am giving a big presentation Wednesday, and went to five stores looking for a business suit. NONE of the first four had a single suit in my size. It was a sobering reality of how far I have to go yet. At the fifth store, they were too big. But seeing all the size 8 and size 10 suits that looked so nice was like teasing me. "You're too fat to have a flattering business suit." Bah.

I need to have a good cry for emotional healing, but didn't get to it last night.

Also when I got home, I was so hungry. I ate half of a giant bone-in split chicken breast with 1.5 slices bacon. Now it's the next morning, and I'm not particularly hungry. Don't think the BAB is going down well this a.m.

In good news, I've joined the CT "big leagues." I needed to buy ice for the first time from the convenience store. (I've been just using what I can make in the freezer.)

And still no signs that I'm going to get the cold every family member of mine had, just the general feeling that I "might" be getting sick. Sweet.

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-10T04:10:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-13212>

Didn't check in for a while - yikes!

I followed the diet without problem over Easter weekend, but I will say I fought some vicious cravings for candy. BUT I didn't give in.

I'm a bit sad today because I gained 3.5 pounds overnight even though I didn't cheat. I know it's not "real" weight, but I'm going to have to take a break from the scale for a few days so I don't freak out. Also, my Achilles are still bothering me sometimes but not all the time.

Been having super awesome dreams nearly every night. They are so vivid. Last night's dream might even turn into a book.

One other change I've noticed: My ankle used to always crack. All the time when I walked, or just when I moved it around in a circle. My ankle doesn't do that any more and will only crack once in a great while. No idea what that actually means, but I'll claim it as another victory.

### janagram — 2012-04-13T00:21:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleosues-weight-loss-journey.369/post-14434>

you are inspiring...! I'd love to get to the vivid dreams! And I'm sure I will, but I'm not CTing yet.....(too chicken)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Post-Meno Estrogen Confusion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-meno-estrogen-confusion.370/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### blindpig — 2012-03-23T01:24:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-meno-estrogen-confusion.370/post-4724>

I keep reading that CT is detoxing estrogen from all the ladies (and guys?), and there is something I'm having a hard time getting my head around. I'm hoping someone can explain. I am many years post meno, have too low cholesterol, low pregnenolone, low DHEA, low everything downstream including estrogen...I'm making basically no estrogen on my own. I have been on estradiol patches for about a year but am still having hot flashes/night sweats. Because I will have no insurance after the end of this month, I will be switching to estradiol pills with the intention of being off of everything when CT miraculously straightens all my hormones out. I do realize this is not ideal and would love to do BHRT but I can't afford it right now. My primary care doc doesn't know how to prescribe it at any rate.

So I'm not getting what is going to happen when CT starts detoxing estrogen from my body, while I am trying desperately to hold on to some?? I feel like I am missing something simple here. I want estrogen. I need estrogen. Don't take my estrogen away from me!

Can someone give me the Dummy version of this so I understand what is going to happen? (I am doing some spot icing but will not be starting any serious CT until probably the end of April as I need my sanity while I support my niece through a difficult experience, but I am trying to get my ducks in a row. I have an already compromised liver due to Hep C so I have no idea how efficient my liver is going to be at clearing toxins.)

Anyone brave enough to take a stab at it?

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-23T01:58:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-meno-estrogen-confusion.370/post-4742>

CT detoxes excess of anything - because excess is toxic. So if you need it, you keep it!

### Birdy — 2012-03-23T02:21:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-meno-estrogen-confusion.370/post-4754>

Read Dr K's latest blog post. I suspect that when you do lots of CT, your estrogen will go up, and this may give you symptoms of estrogen dominance. I have had good results from CT, probably because my estrogen is up a bit. After a day of skin breakouts, my skin is wonderful, my fat distribution is changing rapidly, and my sex drive is up.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-23T02:32:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-meno-estrogen-confusion.370/post-4761>

I'm basically in the same boat so at least you aren't alone. I don't fully comprehend the effects of detoxing, but as others say, I trust that as things level out, it will all be much better. Hang in there!

### blindpig — 2012-03-23T18:22:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-meno-estrogen-confusion.370/post-5033>

> **MamaGrok;4770 wrote: CT detoxes excess of anything - because excess is toxic. So if you need it, you keep it!
>
>
> Thanks, MamaGrok, even this dummy can understand that! I knew I was making it more complicated than it needed to be and I appreciate you breaking it down for me. (I am glad to see you made it over here. You were one of my daily inspirations from the monster thread at MDA.)
>
>
>
>
> > **Birdy;4782 wrote:** Read Dr K's latest blog post. I suspect that when you do lots of CT, your estrogen will go up, and this may give you symptoms of estrogen dominance. I have had good results from CT, probably because my estrogen is up a bit. After a day of skin breakouts, my skin is wonderful, my fat distribution is changing rapidly, and my sex drive is up.
>
>
>
> Oh, Birdy, thank you for giving me some much needed hope. I haven't gotten through the latest blog yet but hope to this weekend. At this point even estrogen dominance sounds good to me. Estrogen anything sounds good to me. Hope your good results continue!
>
>
>
> Lyndra;4789 wrote:** I'm basically in the same boat so at least you aren't alone. I don't fully comprehend the effects of detoxing, but as others say, I trust that as things level out, it will all be much better. Hang in there!

Lyndra, sorry you to have to share this particular boat with me. I do absolutely trust the process, though, so I think we can both trust there is dry land ahead! I will be watching for you to post about your progress. I can't wait to really get started!

# Thread: Fun little side benefits of eating Paleo/Primal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-23T02:53:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-4771>

Just a thread for fun. We get so much into the science of eating, sometimes you just need to laugh. So here's my current fave benefit

Eating Paleo answers the question "What to do with the other half of the avocado to keep it from going brown?" No more trying to find someone to share it, no avocado saver (does. not. work), no lemon juice to preserve it, no make up salsa right now.

Just eat it. The whole thing. By yourself. Spinkle some good salt on it if you must. But just eat it.

Paleo solution to neolithic problem

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-23T20:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5091>

I still don't get the idea of eating only half an avocado. I eat half if sharing, otherwise its all mine. I think the idea goes back to how we have been socially engineered by CW. Funny how that works. I don't even know where eating only half came from. Any ideas?

### Sherry — 2012-03-23T22:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5137>

The only reason I could see would be concern over the price. Trying to make it last longer. Our Florida avocados were/are in season and I pass them up for a much smaller and more delicious Haas any time - no matter the cost.

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-24T03:28:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5271>

Benefit- softer elbows!!

I used to be on a low carb forum 10 yes ago and we all remarked how this happened to us, plus skin in general became soft. None of us knew why, just that it happened.

Dr K is the first to actually EXPLAIN why this happens. Oh yes, it's a good sign!!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-24T03:42:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5281>

Fat! It's the fat. Don't you remember when you weren't allowed to have the fat even in olives, almonds, & avocados?

Krusing -- when did he explain the softness? I admit I haven't read all the blogs.

### PrimalPam — 2012-03-24T04:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5300>

This is so funny because yes, these days, I do eat the whole thing...all for me!!!

One of my favorite dinners is what I call a "Fat Salad". Huge serving-sized bowl filled with greens, one whole avocado, 1-2 HB eggs, maybe 1-2 slices turkey, 2 tbsp. CO oil, balsamic vinegar. Tons of good fat.

And I, too, used to try all the tricks to save the other half. Funny that's such a non-issue now!

### Lexi — 2012-03-24T04:30:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5304>

slathering butter on my steak as my BF stares incredulously.... then showing him how much my triglycerides fell and my HDL rose....

yeah, babe, eat up!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5323>

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-24T17:45:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5444>

### TerrierMom — 2012-03-25T21:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-5969>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;5474 wrote:** I remember seeing it and the light going on. I think it's on the blog where he talks about how you know when your lepton is reset... Less dead skin on elbows palms and feet, that your pedicurist would notice.. I'll find and quote it here.

I found this in the Leptin Rest FAQ comments section (cannot BELIEVE I found it!)

Dan H Says:

September 2nd, 2011 at 5:41 am

This is the best post so far! I am bookmarking this and sending it to everyone I know on a Paleo diet.

Wait so why would LR cause you to have dead skin on your feet?

@Dan when your LR you have higher levels of inflammation and it leads to increased O6 oils in the dermis that causes increased stem cell recruitment and more layers of dead skin to be placed on your heels, palms, and elbows. You will also notice more skin tags and red splotchy skin areas.

Dr. K

### janagram — 2012-03-26T20:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-6481>

hmm, my heels and toes have all that....(yuck) and my ears have exzema....wow, where'd I get all the omega 6? (Oh, could it be by being vegetarian for 25 years?.....but have eaten meat for the last 7 yrs. and been primal for a year, not perfectly, tho.) Really staying away from veggie oils except olive....trying to get the omegas right.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-26T20:53:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-6493>

Omega 6 = grains, soy, "veg" oils, factory-farmed meat & eggs & fish, nuts.

### nyika — 2012-03-27T01:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-6669>

I no longer can eat a whole avocado (that is if that is a decent size. The little ones I can eat of course.)

I find I eat WAY LESS now.

So the other half GETS SPRAYED WITH OLIVE OIL and goes in the avocado saver (sprayed with oil it actually works) TILL THE NEXT MEAL.

I guess I would spray it with butter if there was a butter spray.

### Marsha — 2012-03-27T01:57:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-6677>

I eat fresh fried bacon with melted 99% World Market Chocholate mixed with a little butter - then a few nuts on top! Wonderful dessert!

My dd thought I was crazy!!!

### vlynnb — 2012-03-27T18:08:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-7050>

Elbows good, heels have a way to go yet. Skin tag, fairly new, under one eye gone, one under other eye that has been there forever, is shrinking. Also a bump on my nose (that my dermatologist assures me no one else notices) is shrinking. Yea!

Great from a vanity standpoint but as my late husband, a physician, was worried about the cholesterol implications of the skin tags around my eyes also, that is what I should be ecstatic about, maybe my body is not depositing cholesterol in inappropriate locations anymore.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-27T21:26:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-7294>

I'm only about 4 weeks in. But once I got through the horrible withdrawal, I have so much more energy. I'm sleeping soundly and the insomnia has disappeared. Chronic pain I had in my Achilles for over 15 years is 90% gone. I wake up before the alarm and feel refreshed. And my eyes have become a brighter blue.

### Vicster — 2012-03-27T21:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-7303>

### Ane — 2012-03-27T23:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-7370>

>
>
>

That sounds amazing!!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T13:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-7656>

Has anyone noticed they are dreaming/remembering their dreams more? I have/remember a dream now almost every night now, when before I might remember 2-3 dreams a month.

### HS4 — 2012-03-30T01:19:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-8504>

After having tried every method of saving avocado halves, I've found the best way is to basically do nothing. The leftover half must be the one with the pit; put it in the fridge preferrably on a door shelf. A very thin harder layer will form on the avocado meat which can easily be cut off the next day or two when you want the rest of it. Keeping ripe avocades, unopened or opened, works best on the door of the refrigerator which is the 'warmest' part of the appliance.

### ealachan — 2012-03-30T20:37:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-8883>

> **PaleoSue;7696 wrote:** Has anyone noticed they are dreaming/remembering their dreams more? I have/remember a dream now almost every night now, when before I might remember 2-3 dreams a month.

Absolutely! I've been able to remember dreams fairly well all my life, but my dreams now are almost always super vivid, and I can definitely recall far more detail from them than before. They also seem to be more crazy and yet make more sense at the same time, which I know sounds bonkers. I used to have fairly mundane dreams where something totally out of left field would happen now and then. Now, I seem to have dreams where there's a TON going on, lots of people I haven't seen in ages or places I haven't been in years, stuff like that...and I seem to be able to process them better the next morning when I'm recalling them and thinking about what they mean.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-30T22:05:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-8932>

> **HS4;8552 wrote: After having tried every method of saving avocado halves, I've found the best way is to basically do nothing. The leftover half must be the one with the pit; put it in the fridge preferrably on a door shelf. A very thin harder layer will form on the avocado meat which can easily be cut off the next day or two when you want the rest of it. Keeping ripe avocades, unopened or opened, works best on the door of the refrigerator which is the 'warmest' part of the appliance.
>
>
> Running very cold water over the cut portion before refrigerating helps them to stay fresher and not get brown too.
>
>
>
> ealachan;8935 wrote:** Absolutely! I've been able to remember dreams fairly well all my life, but my dreams now are almost always super vivid, and I can definitely recall far more detail from them than before. They also seem to be more crazy and yet make more sense at the same time, which I know sounds bonkers. I used to have fairly mundane dreams where something totally out of left field would happen now and then. Now, I seem to have dreams where there's a TON going on, lots of people I haven't seen in ages or places I haven't been in years, stuff like that...and I seem to be able to process them better the next morning when I'm recalling them and thinking about what they mean.

I haven't really been able to recall dreams for the last several years. At one time I was told it was a B vitamin deficiency and it did seem to get a bit better when I added nutritional yeast to my diet, but it was only temporary. Since starting the leptin Rx 9 weeks ago I've had a few dreams I could recall, but that's it. Anyone have any other ideas on why some have such poor dream recall?

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-04-10T21:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-13435>

> **Gladina;9587 wrote:** That's also a good trick if you \*gasp\* can't always get a hold of the grass fed meat. You know...for when you're eating out or at a friend's place. lol Slather EXTRA for those occasions. People in public look horrified! It's nice.

Ya, it's tough if you have to get factory meat. What you do if you're DESPERATE is get the lean cuts, and then supplement your own fat with butter from grass fed (b/c I find at least that's easy to find etc.). For me personally I get wild fish, pastured eggs, and butter from grass fed cows. I also get Nutiva CO. I also get sauerkraut that is made with cabbage, salt and bacterial culture. I feel good about the things I can do! As soon as I get more income, then grass fed meat will be UP! I obviously do CT too. I'm happy.

Love to hear everyone's good stories of progress and success.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-10T21:12:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-13436>

Fun side benefit -- excellent night vision now. And the optometrist tells me that I have "young" eyes, whatever that means.

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-13T20:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-14784>

Still having wildly vivid dreams, nearly every night, minutes before I wake up. But this won't happen if I go to bed late. I am loving it. A couple of them could make a good book.

### shilohman — 2012-04-13T20:36:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-14790>

Don't get the dreams, but my fine focus is coming back. Currently I have to use reading glasses to read, have just added face dips in cold water to help accelerate this improvement. When I don't have to use reading glasses anymore, I will be shouting from the roof tops, yippe yay.

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-14T18:16:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-little-side-benefits-of-eating-paleo-primal.371/post-15071>

I have also noticed not needing reading glasses so much, hardly reach for them at all now. I was just at the point in life pre leptin rx that I felt like I needed them daily!

### Llanabanana — 2012-03-23T05:21:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-4846>

Ok got my test results back...I hope someone can shed some light on them..I haven't a clue as to the numbers and what they all mean.

Personal info......45 yr old female, good health slightly overweight working on it with HhCG at the moment R2

did LR for 6 weeks, noticed some small changes.

LIPASE 1070 range 23-300

CALCIUM 2.32 range 2.10-2.60

ALT 25 range 13.-48

ALKALINE PHOSPHATASE 49 range 39-128

GLUCOSE FASTING 3.9 range 3.6-6.1

CREATININE SERUM 59 range 35-100

ELECTROLYTES

SODIUM 143 range 135-145

POTASSIUM 4.2 range 3.6-5.0

CHLORIDE 107 range 96-110

CO2 CONTENT 23 range 20-30

C-REACTIVE PROTEIN NEGATIVE

THYROID STIMULATING HORMONE 1.50 range .20-6.00

VIT B12 238 range 133-375

FERRITIN 23 range 13-375

FREE T4 13.4 range 7.4-20.0

LIPID

CHOLESTEROL 3.67 range 3.80-5.20

TRIG .90 range .60-2.30

HDL 1.14 range >.91

TOTAL HDL 3.2

LDL CALCULATED 2.12 range 2.0-3.4

PHOSPHATE 1.39 range .80-1.50

MAGNESIUM .08 range 0.7-1.0

GGT

### Glamorama — 2012-03-23T05:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-4859>

I think it would help if you can include the ranges for all of these?

### L8F — 2012-03-23T15:59:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-4956>

Agree with ranges, also your ferritin and B12 are low as I scanned quickly. I would get a methyl B12 supp lozenge, cause likely you are not able to get enough B12 which can lead to low iron (ferritin) stores. A1C is most excellent!

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-23T17:40:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5000>

Agree that B12 is very low and so I believe is that FT4

those are from a Canadian lab and chances are the top end of the FT4 range is 20 and that Iron is below range I think

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T19:26:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5060>

Additionally - what cycle day did you test???? & are you menopausal?

this is critical when looking at hormones... Initial look - not knowing when you tested - your P is low and your E is HIGH....

### Llanabanana — 2012-03-23T19:33:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5063>

Thanks so much for pointing out a few things, I did add the ranges.

I don't believe I am menopausal as of yet...phew and as for cycle, I was about a week out of my last period. Had to hold off because of that

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-23T20:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5083>

> **Llanabanana;4877 wrote:**
>
> THYROID STIMULATING HORMONE 1.50 range .20-6.00
>
> FREE T3 3.7 range 2.7-5.7 33% in range, should be 75% or higher
>
> FREE T4 13.4 range 7.4-20.0 48% in range, not as bad as feared (but that is a low bottom end of range)
>
> VIT B12 238 range 133-375 LOW but that is also a super low range
>
> MAGNESIUM .08 range 0.7-1.0 LOW
>
> VITA D 52.7 (ON 10000 UI AT THE MOMENT) range 80.0-200.0 LOW
>
> FERRITIN 23 range 13-375
>
>
>
> IRON 11 (LOW) range 12-31
>
> TIBC 50 range 40-77
>
> TRANFERRIN .22 range .20-.55

your FT4 is okay but your FT3 is low, you want it to be 5 or higher. with the low D, Iron and B12 it looks like you are unable to convert the T4 into T3 and if you were able to get an RT3 test in our country (you can't without going through a naturopath and paying $300 for a full testing spectrum from Quest) you would likely find it is high.

I would not try for thyroid supplementation yet but would look at getting your iron in particular in order. you cannot convert T4 to T3 without iron/ferritin. the good news is you do not appear to have much in the way of inflammation (other wise your ferritin would be high despite the low iron levels (guess how i know that one )) but the maybe not so great news is with the low B12 and low iron I would suspect pernicious anemia.

I think if you can get that iron and D3 up then you will begin to convert T4 to T3

your total cholesterol is also low so you may not be creating enough for cortisol and repro hormones but shijin13 is more the expert on that one than I so would defer to her on that call.

### Llanabanana — 2012-03-24T00:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5172>

Wow, you got all that out of my labs...I am amazed...Thanks you so much for helping me out...

I have struggled with low iron for years now...I don't know what to take that won't offset other things if you know what I mean?

Any suggestions?

Thanks again

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-24T00:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5175>

I have problems with iron as well. Dr K suggests eating liver buuut that's not working out so well for me so I found a dessicated liver supplement at a compounding pharmacy that seems to be helping, will know when I get my next set of labs.

I tried chelated iron once for 6 weeks, made me constipated and my iron dropped 2 points, it was lovely

### Llanabanana — 2012-03-25T00:07:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-all-hackers-please.372/post-5578>

Thanks so much Colleen...I hear you on the liver part. I can't eat it..I would gag and then some.

I did a little digging on the pernicious anemia thing. I believe you may have hit it bang on. Some of the symptoms that are listed are high homocystine levels, which I have, Arrhythmia, which I have that shows its ugly head every now and then as well as lately I have had numbness in my middle toes of both feet. Thats another symptom. I did pick up some iron and b12 from the health store. The iron is made from beets, plant source. I also got b12 in 5000 mcg I believe. Will start taking them. I will be testing in another month or so. Hopefully this helps. Thank you so much for your input! Greatly appreciated!

# Thread: Itching
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LisaAPB — 2012-03-23T06:01:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4860>

Just curious who else is experiencing itching since starting CT, and what do you think is the cause?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-23T06:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4862>

Yes, I have, especially on the legs. I also had pimples in the first 2 weeks. I put it down to detox. Crap seem to come out left, right and centre. The skin is a major organ for detox, no wonder we all seem to get itchy, pimply, greasy and dry all at the same time.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-23T09:18:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4878>

Yes - I've been very itchy, particularly on the soles of my feet and the small of my back. Spots/pimples are beginning to appear now, too.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-23T09:31:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4881>

Its happening to the guys too. I noticed three pimples on my left upper chest. Never had them there before. I get a little itchy but not much. This does have a notable cleansing effect.

I started CT 2 weeks ago.

### Linz — 2012-03-23T10:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4886>

Yes my shins are itching again. I have eaten grain-free scd diet for 6 years and itching shins and soles of my feet is always a sign that I have not been careful enough with my diet.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-23T15:47:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4953>

My shins are itching too, and in the past it meant gluten exposure or liver issues. So I'm confound because my diet is clean and my liver enzymes were taken last week and were at a fantastic low. I also have itching and rash in the small of my back. A few pimples here and there too.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-23T16:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-4960>

Itchy an Scratchy an Rashy here, too.

I've been going on the idea that our fat is storing all kinds of things, so no matter what is showing in your labs when you are stable and not burning fat - once you start to cold adapt, and make the cells more fluid AND clear out that fat, that the cooties are leeching out too.

I've had symptoms before when burning fat, but never this severe or skin-obvious. I've chalked that up to the change of cell membrane.

I have zero science to back up this theory, but it's what I have been telling myself (and husband!).

Is this your understanding on what's going on?

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-23T21:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-5114>

It's weird. I itch when I get out of the tub, but if I use the swimming pool I don't. I have attributed it to the water ph. Now you all have me scratching my head.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-23T21:29:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-5120>

I get really itchy on my back and sides. The part that gets the most dunk time in the CT. I also just broke out with a few pimples on my hip, of all places. It's detoxing and I actually like to feel/see these things so I know the CT is doing some amazing things to get that crap out of my body!

### nonchalant — 2012-04-04T21:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-11424>

I got what the doctor once diagnosed as "contact dermatitis", a few small, weepy blisters on my fingers. Like a blast from the past! It's been a while since I had those. So I doubt that, long ago, they were from contact with anything, except probably rancid vegetable oil.

The blisters are now healing up, though I still have some raised itchy areas elsewhere on my hand.

### ealachan — 2012-04-04T22:15:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itching.374/post-11436>

I'm itchy, and my skin is quite dry and a little flakey in places, but it also feels a whole lot smoother than before and my keratosis pilaris on my upper arms has improved quite a lot. It's an interesting paradox.

# Thread: Fighting for Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-for-optimal.376/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### adam_ — 2012-03-23T15:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-for-optimal.376/post-4946>

Long overdue that I took responsibility. Shit's about to get real.

I've been dabbling in paleo for about 12 months, but have struggled with making it an all or nothing pursuit - as soon as I'm falling short of perfection I nose-dive through self-sabotage and undo any progress I've made.

I don't know if I'm leptin resistant, although given that I'm carrying a lot of fat in my stomach, as well as being 50+ pounds overweight, I think it's more than likely. In addition, years of thermogenics I suspect have fatigued my adrenals.

I'm done with treading water, and ready to make some long-term changes, to fight for the optimal health I deserve.

The best I've ever felt was when I was following the Leptin reset protocol a few months ago - by having a BAB I wasn't thinking about food until the evening. This was great - I don't want my day revolving around food.

So, I'm aiming to lose 50lbs, improve energy, get rid of carb/sweet cravings, repair hormone profile.

I'm planning on doing this by starting over with the leptin reset, starting CT, and getting at least 8 hours of sleep per night.

### sooperb — 2013-11-09T14:42:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-for-optimal.376/post-100249>

Wonders how Adam got on. Such a shame that so many have started journals and left them in mid air. I won't be committing to one until I feel ready to commit to the reset which won't be until I've finished Jack's book and tried to read as many of the blogs as I can.

### sooperb — 2013-11-09T15:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-for-optimal.376/post-100256>

Fair enough  It's interesting comparing journeys/journals. Of those I have read through so far, those with the most problems seem to be the ones who are keeping journals up to date. It's a shame that if others are succeeding they aren't documenting it so that others would be encouraged.

### caroline — 2013-11-10T00:32:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-for-optimal.376/post-100295>

There has been a couple of people that don't post at all. I remember one woman came on a year later and she wrote the most amazing post and she had lost 100lbs and had improved her health tremendously. Everyone does it in different ways.
Everyone's n=1 is totally different ....

JK always says "when you become sick and tired of being sick and tired"

We all have to turn our own titanic around ....we have the tools here....we just need to be fully committed .... our head in the right space....

I think it is so great that we can take a crack at learning all this stuff in Dr. K's blogs. It gives us power to do what we need to do.....but we can only help ourself .....and then be a positive role model for others.

I see that my friends watch me - but I also see that they don't have the necessary commitment ..and they intuitively seem to know that. They are not ready \[by a long shot\] to give up their pasta and cake and wine etc.

That is their choice...... and this is mine. I love being here and trying to figure this all out ......and I love feeling so much better ...and best of all .... I love being in control of my journey to Optimal health and well being ...

I look forward to seeing how far we can take this ..... I can't wait to see what Dr. K. has in store for us.... I suspect we can't even imagine where he is taking us.....


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: skin color
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-23T16:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-4974>

Background: I put together a spreadsheet charting times, weight, etc etc.

Anyway, I started the entire process with face dunking 2/20.

Currently, i am up to 25 minutes cold immersion bath (50 F). I also take 10 cold shower each morning.

Okay, now my question - my skin never turns cherry red. I do get a slight pink - I never get white.

Is this a problem? Does everyone doing CT turn a dark cherry red?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-23T21:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-5123>

After rereading the protocol, what is cherry red? Is it a dark color or just a pink color? I eat all kinds of cherries! Even yellow ones. And I am red/green color blind - lol!

### kc_boxerman@yahoo.com — 2012-03-23T22:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-5139>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;5006 wrote:** Okay, now my question - my skin never turns cherry red. I do get a slight pink - I never get white. Is this a problem? Does everyone doing CT turn a dark cherry red?

So did you ever do the bags of ice on a compression shirt followed by bags of ice on skin up to 60 minutes? No pink from that?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-24T17:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-5433>

yes, I got up to 60 minutes of ice with the compression shirt. slight patches of pink, but I guess my confusion is what is "cherry red".

Is it just pink? or is it a darker color?

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-24T19:13:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-5479>

For the first time ever today I was a consistent, bright reddish/pinkish colour all over from my bath, and that was at the lowest temperature I've managed so far (other than my first, over-the-top bath!). 25 minutes did me! Although the water was at 52F, my skin temp. on the infrared thermometer measured 57F, so I don't think I'm at optimum temperature yet. I have been noticing that the skin around my collarbone and on the back of the neck down to the shoulderblades goes a much deeper colour, so maybe that is a good sign?

### Melinda — 2012-03-25T00:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-5579>

I think cherry red is like the color of a face cheek that has been slapped hard, it's pretty well charged with "red". I personally have only been getting pinkish after 20 minutes at 59F. My DH's color is deeper pink.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-25T17:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-5824>

Ok, I'm not getting anything dark yet. it is more like the color of a blush wine.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-30T20:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-8874>

Well, I think I know what cherry red is now. I started taking the bitter melon, and after about 60 minutes all of my skin turns cherry red....Anyone know what is going on? What is happening at the skin level for it to be turning red?

### kc_boxerman@yahoo.com — 2012-03-30T20:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-color.377/post-8884>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;8926 wrote:** Well, I think I know what cherry red is now. I started taking the bitter melon, and after about 60 minutes all of my skin turns cherry red....Anyone know what is going on? What is happening at the skin level for it to be turning red?

Can't say that I do, but I'm not sure you need to worry about it too much. I'd be more interested in what your skin temps are, as you need to get them down without damage. I think the color talk is just to help you understand the red is generally not going to hurt you whereas white is probably will.

# Thread: Another weight loss surgery? Carnie Wilson

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T16:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-weight-loss-surgery-carnie-wilson.378/post-4979>

Everyone here has heard by now about Carnie Wilson's [2nd weight loss surgery](http://http://todayhealth.today.msnbc.msn.com/_news/2012/03/21/10799250-two-weight-loss-surgeries-why-carnie-wilson-tried-again)? Sounds like it was lap band this time to finish off the bypass surgery she had 12 years ago.

Here's some video of it. \[h=1\][Carnie WilsonÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s Second Weight-Loss Surgery Probably WonÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t Be Her Last (VIDEO)](http://http://thestir.cafemom.com/healthy_living/134971/carnie_wilsons_second_weightloss_surgery)\[/h\]

I didn't see any mention of her appearance on the Celebrity Fit Club (which suggests she has tried losing with CW for a long time). This saddens me. # Thread: Where is the best place to start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-23T16:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-4982>

I've been reading as much as I can here on the blog & at MDA. This amount of info is massive; which, don't get me wrong, is much appreciated! Any suggestions where I should start? I've understood the value of eating low-carb for years & always feel the best when I do but I've always fallen off the wagon several times over the years (for instance...gained about 15 lbs since Christmas...crazy). Anyway, I've read the leptin Rx and FAQ but where should I go from there? I'd really like to start incorporating "all things optimal" into mylife now & would like to do it the right way. On the other hand I think it would take a long, long time to read & understand everything first.

Thanks In Advance. I appreciate it!

Paula

### quelsen — 2012-03-23T17:32:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-4995>

Start with How you Feel and LABS

Basic Labs will give any Kruse Veteran a joygasm to assist you. Full Labs tend to catch the masters eye.

Your Goals matter as well

Here the initial answer will always be Ketogenic + CT + Body By Science. but your mileage may vary and you may have special needs

Leaky GUT

Heavy Metal Detox

Liver Flush

but there are tons of vets here to help

Good Hunting

Basic Labs

1

Metabolic Panel (14), Comprehensive CPT Code: 80053

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-23T19:37:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-5068>

**1\. Sleep- turn down lights 3-4 hours before bed, reduce/eliminate computer & TV after sunset (alternatively...wear sunglasses). Ideal bedtime no later than 10pm.

**2\. 50g of protein for breakfast. - A typical breakfast for me is 4 eggs, 4 slices (no sugar) bacon, 2oz Turkey breast, 1-2 oz raw green vegetable (spinach or baby romaine lettuce usually). This will wipe out afternoon cravings.

**3\. Eliminate sugar/sweeteners/starches - fruit included. (_liquid_ stevia ok if you must).

**4\. Eliminate Dairy, except for grass-fed, clarified butter.** - I use Kerrygold brand (Silver package is unsalted).

5\. Eliminate Omega-six fats/increase Omega-three.** - Most folks have way too much Omega-six stored in their body. Proper ratio should be 4:1. No olive oil/canola oil. Use coconut/palm/Macadamia oil instead.

Constipation?** An issue for many. Dr. Kruse recommends [Magnesium Malate](http://http://www.amazon.com/Source-Naturals-Magnesium-Malate-Capsules/dp/B000EQ6KGS/ref=sr_1_4?ie=UTF8&qid=1332523145&sr=8-4) two hours before bed. I have also had good luck with [Chiro Klenz tea](http://http://www.amazon.com/Chiro-Klenz-Original-Made-Edom-Labs/dp/B0058RHY18/ref=sr_1_1?s=grocery&ie=UTF8&qid=1332522106&sr=1-1) from time to time.

Your goal is less than 50g/carbs per day** (25g if you have more than 30 lbs to lose).

If you have an iPhone, use an app like livestrong to keep track of what you're eating. GREAT app!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-23T23:05:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-5158>

...and no snacking whatsoever!

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-23T23:48:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-5169>

If I were doing this from scratch again, I'd hack myself in the following order: 1. eliminate excessive stress 2. sleep all I want 3. eat a paleodiet 4. initiate cold therapy 5. optimize exercise.

Maybe you could decide which of these areas was the biggest mismatch keeping you from optimal and start there. Get some baseline labs at the beginning and take your "before" picture, because you're on the path now. Good luck.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-24T00:56:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-5209>

Thanks Guys! I'm glad I asked.

quelsen...thanks for mentioning Body By Science. I've read the Primal Blueprint and some of MDA but hadn't heard of it. After a quick google search it's something I'm going to look into.

Dan...I've got lots of "Before" pics from lots of attempts at different trys! This time is going to be the time that produces a great "After" pic!!

Chris...that's a nice summary of how to start. It's been overwhelming reading about CT, HCG, etc and wondering where and what to start with!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T02:00:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-5620>

great summary Chris!

### cljcj04@gmail.com — 2012-03-26T06:01:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6232>

All the advice seems perfect, except I thought Dr. K said we were not to excercise when we started the LRx until we were LS? I am getting off HCG and starting LRx and I didn't think I was supposed to start excercise. Can someone please clarify?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T06:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6239>

I'd recommend you start by sorting out your sleep (get it, and get it well, for starters), then introduce the Leptin-rx eating-plan, and I guess introduce CT shortly after that. Things will realign and fix themselves (many issues, anyway) just by doing these things.

### optimeyes — 2012-03-26T06:36:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6243>

I started GF and almost grain free, preservative-free before I started (found) leptin reset. I did not have the paleo detox blues that other people sometimes have. So I would probably cut out gluten and then a couple of things more etc and meanwhile do the other things like sleep and de-stress. Also work on where you are going to get your good sources of meat (grass fed beef, pasture raised chicken etc) Oh and the labs are a great idea so you can see where you were

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-26T16:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6355>

Thanks everyone. I'm a week into the Leptin Rx & don't plan on exercising yet; apart from the occasional walk. I thought I'd start CT yesterday with some facebaths; although just going around the house all day in a t-shirt instead of sweater was tough (I live in Nova Scotia so the inside temp was 65 degrees without turning on heat). I found out quickly I have issues with putting my face in a sink full of water. It was so strange. I almost felt like I was going to panic. I think I need a snorkle.

The BEST thing is the food cravings are almost gone & since I have been known to binge, especially on a weekend it was wonderful not to be thinking about food constantly. I felt great yesterday. Not as good this morning but I think that is because I ate too late last night.

Anyway, thanks again for your words of wisdom!

### David — 2012-03-26T16:57:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6369>

> **Glamorama;6274 wrote: ... start by sorting out your sleep (get it, and get it well, for starters), then introduce the Leptin-rx eating-plan, and I guess introduce CT shortly after that. Things will realign and fix themselves (many issues, anyway) just by doing these things.**

**I really think this is sound advice. In trying to get the best sleep possible, ala Robb Wolf I have installed blackout curtains and removed ALL light sources from my bedroom. It seems to have worked out well. A sleep mask is supposedly NOT an effective substitute, (although, a completely darkened room is a real challenge when getting up to use the bathroom during the night!) And, it was pointed out by AKMan** in another thread, a snorkel may be less effective that holding your breath in sink dunks.

Best of luck in getting started.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-26T17:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6382>

> **David;6405 wrote: I really think this is sound advice. In trying to get the best sleep possible, ala Robb Wolf I have installed blackout curtains and removed ALL light sources from my bedroom. It seems to have worked out well. A sleep mask is supposedly NOT an effective substitute, (although, a completely darkened room is a real challenge when getting up to use the bathroom during the night!) And, it was pointed out by AKMan** in another thread, a snorkel may be less effective that holding your breath in sink dunks.
>
> Best of luck in getting started.

I hear you on the sleep thing that's for sure! I don't feel as great today as I did on the weekend, I always sleep longer on the weekends but do get to bed most weekdays by 10-10:30; last night was 11:30 so I suspect that is playing a part today. I can't wait to go to bed tonight, after another attempt at the facebath (which I really thought wouldn't be an issue for me, oh well!).

### shilohman — 2012-03-26T19:16:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6438>

Paulalynn, Go to Walmart to the sporting goods section and pick up a pair of the Winchester shooting glasses. They need to be the yellow or amber ones. They cost $8 something. Once the sun goes down wear these and try to avoid rooms with bright lights. The first night I did this my sleep was deep and restful, something I hadn't had in years. The amber lenses block blue light from hitting your eyes. Blue light tells your brain NOT to produce melatonin. So, blocking the blue with amber lenses tells your brain its time to start producing melatonin. I also replaced several of the lights that I use at night with the yellow bug lights. Give it a try and see if it effects your sleep.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-03-27T02:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-best-place-to-start.379/post-6685>

They cost $8 something. Once the sun goes down wear these and try to avoid rooms with bright lights. The first night I did this my sleep was deep and restful, something I hadn't had in years. The amber lenses block blue light from hitting your eyes. Blue light tells your brain NOT to produce melatonin. So, blocking the blue with amber lenses tells your brain its time to start producing melatonin. I also replaced several of the lights that I use at night with the yellow bug lights. Give it a try and see if it effects your sleep.

Great advise! I was wondering how I was going to be able to read at night. Now, any advise on how to get this behind in a cold bath...makes me shiver just thinking about it...lol!

# Thread: What is going to happen in the summer heat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-23T17:16:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-4989>

So, as I keep tossing around the idea of buying a small room a/c and setting up some kind of sleeping chamber in the basement for the summer months, it got me to wondering. . .

What is the heat going to feel like? Jack, do you feel hot when it's hot?

As I was enjoying my shivering/feverish feeling (skin hot, inside feels cold) I was wondering if we might actually be more heat tolerant as well.

I know some of you are already in hotter locations - how do you feel in the heat?

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-23T17:26:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-4992>

I don't have anything to back this up, but I think they are somewhat separate things. You can do things to make yourself adapt to the cold and the heat. I have a feeling that if you were constantly being exposed to both you would just be increasing your comfort zone. People these days have like a 10 deg. range of temps they are comfortable in. By doing cold and heat training I would imagine you could expand that range 50 deg. or perhaps even more. However, I also think that if you are only doing CT that then heat may become more uncomfortable if your not exposing yourself to extreme heat. I notice that my tolerance for warm buildings in winter is terrible because I keep my own home on the lower end, and in the summer I have no AC and find buildings with AC to be freezing.

### quelsen — 2012-03-23T17:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-5002>

over 70f is HOT to me now. I need the CT to keep me comfortable. when the true humidity starts i fear the results

As an aside i have a programmable thermostat and i alter the pattern to follow the bodies temps and at least for last night the girl slept better than she had in a long while. AFM my sleep is int he tank again... sigh

### nonchalant — 2012-03-23T21:20:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-5109>

Cavemam, I suspect we will be more heat tolerant. It was in the 80's last week, and people all around me at work were complaining about the heat. I'd stop, look around, and agree that it was warm. It sure wasn't bothering me, though. I hope that will continue!

\-Sally

### MartiD — 2012-03-23T21:23:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-5112>

My poor body is going to be so confused after two full weeks of two cold baths a day plus spot icing and on this Sunday we are headed to Mexico and I plan on parking my a$$ on the beach for 5 days to soak up Vit D! It will be interesting when I get back and start up the CB again to see how easy it is to get going again!

### LinD — 2012-03-23T21:27:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-5115>

> **Nonchalant;5141 wrote:** Cavemam, I suspect we will be more heat tolerant. It was in the 80's last week, and people all around me at work were complaining about the heat. I'd stop, look around, and agree that it was warm. It sure wasn't bothering me, though. I hope that will continue!
>
>
>
> \-Sally

That is a good report, Sally! I hope the same is true for me in Texas.

Sent from my Sprint Samsung Galaxy S II Epic.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-23T21:48:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-5127>

I'm starting to feel hot all the time. So I'm not sure I'll feel any hotter when it gets warm outside.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T21:53:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-5128>

> **Cavemam;5021 wrote:** So, as I keep tossing around the idea of buying a small room a/c and setting up some kind of sleeping chamber in the basement for the summer months, it got me to wondering. . .
>
>
>
> What is the heat going to feel like? Jack, do you feel hot when it's hot?
>
>
>
> As I was enjoying my shivering/feverish feeling (skin hot, inside feels cold) I was wondering if we might actually be more heat tolerant as well.
>
>
>
> I know some of you are already in hotter locations - how do you feel in the heat?

Basic laws of thermodynamics... as you tune your engine to cold adaptation - it will become more efficient - thus making your body more efficient in cooling when its hot out side...

what I've found - is that I'm hotter indoors than out. I anticipate that as summer hits here in the DC metro area - I'll find I'm more tolerant to the high heat/humidity - especially if I continue to use CT... I figure by summer time I'll relish an even relaxing in some cold water before going to bed...

especially considering last night I was a freaking furnace! I woke up I was so hot and had to change clothes

### nonchalant — 2012-07-26T00:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-41096>

So what's the verdict? Does CT make the summer feel worse? Or does being cold adapted actually make the summer heat more tolerable?

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-26T00:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-41102>

> **Nonchalant;41311 wrote:** So what's the verdict? Does CT make the summer feel worse? Or does being cold adapted actually make the summer heat more tolerable?

more tolerable for me ...but I summer is still not my favorite season....

### Birdy — 2012-07-26T04:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-41152>

too early to tell. This has been the coolest summer I ever remember.

### tweety — 2012-07-26T15:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-41182>

Its been hard to tell for me too. At the beginning, we seemed to be handling it much better. We have no AC and didn't put any window units in until July. But once summer really hit, it came with humidity. So the heat has not been the issue so much as the mugginess. I have been sweating all summer!

But the past few years we put our window units in anytime the temps were in the 80s. This time we just opened windows. The kids haven't been turning their's on at night. Only when its been in the 90s during the day and not cooling at night do they want the AC. I would do the same but DH likes the AC blasting him.

I am still CTing, but summer CTing. In other words, whatever the water is at is the temp I am using. I may throw in some ice blocks, but its really not budging the temps. So like water in the 70s. I am taking the natural approach- this is what is available to me, this is what I would be using if I didn't have modern conveniences to change things. As the fall comes I will just gradually get colder and colder.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-26T17:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-to-happen-in-the-summer-heat.380/post-41198>

I spent 10 days in hot Orlando in July - and it didn't seem as bad as I was dreading.

However, not enough data for me to draw any conclusion from this.

Overall, I seem to be about the same as before. I will say that if I'm out side working - I crave a cold shower.

# Thread: Building a proper sleep cave
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-proper-sleep-cave.381/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-23T17:21:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-proper-sleep-cave.381/post-4990>

We are pretty darn neolithic. Our large 2 story house with all beds up, run the whole house a/c in the summer, etc.

I'm keep thinking about how much I'm enjoying my "dead as a rock" sleep where the whole fam is snuggled under the blankets in a freezing room with fresh air (colorado's spring, cold night - warm days). It just gets HOT up in the bedrooms in summer. A/C cools it down to high 60's. . .but it never feels the same cold as a icy breeze chillin the nose.

So we have a basement, that is cooler. It's unfinished, but I just keep thinking of setting up a "sleep room" down there somehow. Has anybody done that? Any suggestions? I don't want to undergo a basement remodel, it's unfinished. . but I was thinking perhaps a tent with a room a/c or?!?! Any ideas?

### Lyndra — 2012-03-23T18:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-proper-sleep-cave.381/post-5022>

Sounds like a good idea. I'm in the same boat, though the upstairs rarely gets cooler than 75-78 when we go to bed and we have no basement :-(. I'm not looking forward to summer at this point.

# Thread: Doggcrapp Training

### Swagger — 2012-03-23T17:46:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doggcrapp-training.382/post-5005>

Dont let the name throw you off. Doggcrapp is the screenname of the guy who came up with it. Doggcrapp or DC Training is amazing and I feel falls into the guidelines Doc K has outlined in the Rx. It is a lot of info, so please google it. Intense heavy powerlifting for short intervals. Prior to doing the Rx, I made incredible gains on it. I'm anxious to see how well an "optimal" human can do.

### Jim — 2012-03-25T01:58:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doggcrapp-training.382/post-5619>

Thanks for posting Swagger. I have recently started started something that is functionally similar called the max pyramid protocol ([http://www.bodybyscience.net/home.html/?page\_id=798](http://http://www.bodybyscience.net/home.html/?page_id=798)). I like it because I feel it will protect my joints. It's too early to say how well it is working for me yet. After I give it a good go I may give Doggcrapp a shot. I have always been a HIT guy having been trained by an NFL strength coach.
