### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T09:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70291>

First time I measured blood ketones in the morning was today. They were 1.8. From what Iâ€™ve been reading ideal is between 0.5 â€“ 3.0. I can usually tell when Iâ€™m in ketosis so why am I testing??? Maybe because itâ€™s something that I â€œcanâ€ actually test. Many things I want to test I canâ€™t get from the Dr and with no private labs here Iâ€™m sh\*t out of luck. So Iâ€™m testing ketones, at least thatâ€™s one part of the puzzle I know I have right. Iâ€™m in ketosis.

But, holy sweet hanna I feel like crap today. Tired, tired, tired. Headachy, kinda stiff and sore. I donâ€™t feel like Iâ€™m getting sick or anything.

That was yesterdayâ€™s post which I didnâ€™t get to post because I had no luck getting a window to open in order to post it!

Besides being tired yesterday I became cranky as the day progressed and by the evening I was a real â€œyou know what!â€ I do not like this. That negative, cranky, critical mindset has been out of the picture for months and I donâ€™t want it back. Go away!!!

I ate scallops andâ€¦thatâ€™s rightâ€¦andâ€¦shrimp. Hubby had a delicious concoction made when I got home. Not sure how protocol-ish they were because they tasted sweet. Apparently he cooked them in some wine, which turned out to be a sweet wine.
Didn't sleep great last night, I remember waking at least twice. No problem getting back to sleep but from the look of my eyes I definitely need uninterupted sleep! Maybe it was sugar from the wine?!!! Sigh...of well...it's Friday!!!

### endless — 2013-01-11T11:48:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70296>

Coudn't have been too much sugar in the dinner, if you are in ketosis this morning...so that's good! Wish I had a hubby to make me yummy seafood dishes!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-11T13:40:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70308>

Paulalynn, you need salt. Ketosis can make you feel this way at first, so grab a cup of bone broth and add extra salt. Some seaweed maybe and you feel better soon. Thats what I am having right now.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T15:52:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70325>

Yup, I know about the salt thing. No lack of it in my diet. It wasn't like the carb-flu, I was just feeling like I could rip someone's eyes out. Sounded kinda harsh! I think my negativity yesterday and still some today is a combination of things. I'm doing what I can to improve my body, mindset, etc, you know...the whole package and it's starting to work..but I think the past few days I've let the negative energy & mood from a particular person suck the life out of me. Even though I am determined not to let myself be affected by that person I find myself having negative conversations (in my head...the pretend kind...I'm losing it!) to that person and I end up feeling bitter...and...enter the cranky irritable me! Deep breath...deep breath...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T16:56:00+0200

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T22:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70365>

Well tonight was an exercise in futility!!! Went to the mall, which I hate, but hey, every few years one needs new dress pants! It is next to impossible to get pants that fit my thighs and waist. My thighs are quite large so IF I can get pants that aren't skin tight in the legs they will HAVE to be altered in the waist. Anyway...nope...nadda...not one pair of pants were fitting these thighs tonight! So, since I could use a pair of boots I thought i'd give that a go. Never had a problem with boots before, except of couse I could never consider a pair of those riding type boots because of the thick legs again, they'd never fit. That's okay because there are some decent short boots. Yup...nope!!! Couldn't even get boots tonight that would zip up past my ankle. I must have the thickest legs ever! Big sigh! Whatever! I'm going to bed. New day when I wake up.

I buy slacks from Lands End .com. They are (wait for it!) Starfish pants. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

They are the most comfortable, best looking slacks I've EVER owned. They fit like a dream, they are fully stretch but do NOT look it. Wash up easily, throw in the dryer (but do NOT over do it, I take mine out when they're still just a tad damp), press them and they look like top dollar duds.

When we went to Ireland I bought 4 pair to take with me. The wear like iron because I just ditched them a few months ago and bought new ones. They still looked really good but I got a tear in one pair, another I over dried so they're like 2 sizes smaller than originally and one pair was getting a bit thin. They still fit my fat behind even though I'd gained 30 pounds since I bought them. I did go up to my current size with the new ones though.

Oh and they do NOT look like stretch pants! I'm serious. They fit so well I still cannot believe it.

### diane — 2013-01-12T12:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70399>

Paulalynn - have been there for those frustrating shopping days. Then I realize that I spend my time trying to fit into what I think I should be able to fit into, and when I accept what I actually fit into, it's much more relaxing LOL.

Someone at work showed me a trick for comfortable "dress pants". Lululemon yoga pants. Oh my god - changed my life immediately. Just pair with a dressy top or sweater and shoes/boots - and they really do look like dress pants. I didn't even know that's what she was wearing until she pointed it out. I've done it since then - amazing! I know - everyone says that Lululemon is so expensive, but I have my first pair that I bought 10 years ago. 10 years! They're still black, still in shape. How can you beat that!

And the boots? Just consider how much money you saved yourself!

Although I confess, I love boots too. My favourite pair right now are my Blundstones that I bought last year. Oh my god. So comfortable and I wear them everywhere.

Darleen - I'm going to check out those Lands End pants too!

So I am typing this from the tub. Before you pat me on the back, it's not as cold as I intended. I have one of those darn 1 tap thingy and it's obviously hard to get just cold water. ### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-14T19:04:56+0200

Other than that, everything is fine. MAybe time to go try the cold tub thing again.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-14T19:24:23+0200

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-14T20:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70760>

Ok fine

thanks for the inspiration!
I am typing in between curses and loving thoughts of the good JK from a bath tub which elicited gasps as well as "how the heck does Miss Darleen just 'do it'!!!!
I have to come up with a better system because the water from our sink faucets is freezing cold (eastern canada and we have a well, it's cold friggin water) but this darn one tap adjustment thing in the tub produces barely cool water. I was literally filling a container from the sink and dumping into the tub. Anyway...job done for now and the cursing has stopped, maybe it's because I can't feel my legs lol!!!
Time to re-listen to jan webinar, that'll help pass some time.
Have a good night you brave CT souls!!!

### caroline — 2013-01-14T20:46:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70765>

Great job Paula - I don't know as it gets easier - sometimes - but you do feet better and you are accomplishing so much! My tap water comes out at 75 degrees. It is impossible to get it below 60. So now I just lay in the tub and cover myself with the bags of ice. I don't know if it is the best but I do wind up shivering quite a bit.
I think when you extend yourself like this - the food and other parts of the R/X becomes easier because you don't want to wreck all the good work you are doing - a snowball effect I guess.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-14T23:45:49+0200

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-15T21:06:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-70958>

Didn't sleep well last night. It was the first time in months that I woke to go pee. Can't really be related to my first cold bath? Woke again at 5:30 with terrible back pain. The kind that made me wonder if I was going to be able to get out of bed. That hasn't happened in many years. I took a robaxacet and managed to get to work. It's still not right and boy do I NOT have patience for a bad back!!!
Spoke to my compounding pharmacist guy about my crappy mood and I am going to quit the supplement I was taking for testosterone. The thinking is that the adreno supp I was taking (which works on some HPG axis??...or something like that) may have helped improve the low testosterone situation and now the testo-supp is not needed. He said my symptoms sounded like the "testy" symptoms that come with high testosterone. We'll see what happens!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-16T09:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-71016>

> **endless said**
>
> I'm curious to see what my next set of labs show...I am often 'testy' as well. But I think in my case it's just because I'm easily irritated.
>
>
> I had a sore back after CT a few times....almost like my muscles were tight for the session and then sore afterwards like I'd had a heavy workout.

Krista, I was easily irritated for the better part of 10 years and it seemed that anything & everything could tick me off and I tried many things to resolve this part of, what I thought was my personality. Once I found out my hormones were basically non-existent and I had symptoms of estrogen dominance I started on supplements to address this (suggested by my compounding pharmacist). The difference in my mood was remarkable, it didnâ€™t happen overnight but in a month or two I felt like a different person! I should have left well enough alone but I thought maybe it was possible to feel even better so we decided to start the testosterone supp since my saliva showed that was low too (however, I had already been on the other supps for 4 months by that time). Iâ€™m grasping at straws, since my crystal ball isnâ€™t working, but in hindsight, given my change in moods, I donâ€™t think I needed to add the testo suppl. Weâ€™ll see what happens.

Thanks for the insight on CT and soreness. My back feels much better today.

### diane — 2013-01-16T12:41:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-71042>

Paula - I also had noticed mood changes over the past few years. Wasn't sure what it was -whether I was just getting grumpier, life was wearing on me, or (gasp) was I turning into my mother???

Changing my diet had a huge affect on my mood. A couple of years ago I went to a holistic allergist and she told me a list of things I was allergic to. Within 2 days of eliminating them (and believe me that list was LONG) I felt better. Things that would grate on my nerves didn't bother me as much. Then, changing the light at nights, eating earlier - that brought around even more changes in my mood. It's crazy the things that change that you least expect! And I haven't even started any hormone therapy yet!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-18T19:45:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-71502>

So much to catch up on here when you miss a day! I didn't get to the Q &A; 9:00 pm really isn't that late but it kind of is for me right now. I've really been concentrating on getting to bed earlier since I had my consult with JK. I'm sure I missed some great info but my head is hurting just trying to figure out the latest supps and dosages from the last webinar! I think I ordered everything except the L carnitine. Will work on finding that one.
My joints are hurting like never before. CT is going to help improve this right? This is new for me. I go for a walk and feel it for days, I've been limping from pain in my hip region and my wrist just hurt so much while cleaning the bathroom. Weird!
Finally got hubby to try Mg at night because his sleep is so bad. Tonight, after having it for the last five nights he tells me...not tonight...apparently he was strapped to the toilet most of the day...oops!
Time to go figure out how I am going do some earthing...walking in the snow isn't appealing.

I will be so happy to not take so many supplements every day..

Opps...I did add the d-ribose this morning but my understanding is it doesn't affect us like sugar because it's 6 carbon vs 5, or something like that. But can the "sweet taste" stimulate the craving/bingeing part of my brain??? Again, grasping at straws but why, oh why am I fasticizing about ice cream....urgh!!!!

PQQ if I remember rightly is an uncommon Vit B especially needed by older women and those who have really mucked up their health.

I too, got terrible cravings yesterday and didn't know why. I don't have the excuse of having d-ribose though. It was probably having a handful of grapes that were too sweet for where I'm at at the moment. And I seriously thought of having icecream yesterday too. But didn't.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-27T00:45:25+0200

Of course, now that I'm logged in I really have nothing to say and can't remember what I did say in the posts that appear to be missing...oh well, at least we're back to the old forum!
Went for a short walk today. I kept going as long as I could because it was freezing and I wanted the CT. First time I had pink skin was today. My legs were pink and numb by the time I got home! I can't imagine how you gals are able to sit in water cold enough to get pink skin...but...I will get there!!!! I haven't been able to warm up. After three hours I broke down and had to put on my fleece pjs and I'm still freezing!
Well...off to check out the new, old forum!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-28T19:00:43+0200

Monday...yuck! My body feels yucky too. I had a scratchy throat and an annoying cough yesterday and felt like I was fighting "something". Still feel crappy today. This must be the third time in a month I've felt like this. Is my body going to react this way everytime I CT??? (rheutorical!!!). Cranky, cranky, cranky! Maybe it's partly because I have a friend who is keeping me posted on her daily weight loss! A friend who struggles to lose weight like me. Well, she read "The Plan" by Lyn-Genet, or something like that and thinks I should try it too. She knows what I'm doing and she doesn't disagree or anything but she knows I've not lost weight...she think's she's found the answer because it's not "a diet" and is basically an elimination thing aimed to reduce inflammation, etc. So she's sending me things like "lost 0.8 lbs after my beef test with a porterhouse steak"! I should be happy for her, right?!! BUT given my cranky, irritable mood of late, I'm thinking, "Great, she'll look good this summer at the beach and I'll still be sportin' rolls & cellulite!"
Alrighty...pity party over (stealing words from Darleen)!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-31T19:31:12+0200

It can't be an addiction if I only watch once a week, LOL!

I'm feeling better and back to work today. Not 100% but I can breath clearly and my head is better. I didn't CT while sick. On a positive note, the scale moved....down....4lbs...unheard of for moi! I could understand it if I had the flu but even with my lack of appetite I still made myself have protein drinks made with 1/2cup of HWC, which I had 3 each day. So, sure I was around 1200-1300 calories, which even though that is like starvation mode for me  the scale still never moves. I'll take it...perhaps a short-lived drop but we'll see. It does have me wondering if I'm eating something my body doesn't want me to be eating, hence the "damn scale never moves"! Hmmm...will be giving this some thought

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-05T06:02:04+0200

I'm not sure if its my lack of scientific knowlege or whether he's a bit off base in the way he redefines things to mean something quite different from the ordinary interpretations. However I don't really care because its clinical stuff that matters to me not his theory that underpins it. Theory can be wrong but if the practice is right, it just doesn't matter. And if he is right about the theory and his practice is right then that's fine too.

I question a lot of things these days....BUT I am very positive about the magnets versus the grounding (unless if it's natural grounding like feet on grass/mud/snow/trees). Something about the magnets seems 'right'. Also I know Jack puts more emphasis on magnets under the bed versus the grounding wire.

I can understand how it is confusing with EMF things. It is something tricky whereby you can remove all YOUR EMF but still have to deal with the rest of the populations who have their high EMF polluting your space. It's a balance thing for sure and it's tricky sometimes.

I have some experimentations in the next 6 months that are being sprung upon me out of my control...so instead I decided to treat it like an 'experiment'. Not what I wanted (in some regards) but at least I am not completely ungaruded in terms of education. If it was in the past I would have been unprepared and not even knowing what I'm getting into. But this time for this situation (which it's a trade off and there are MANY good aspects to this situation) at least I DO have knowledge/education and a better understanding of myself/body based on previous trial and errors so I hope this 'run' of experiment goes better than it could under normal circumstances.

Wish me luck!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-06T23:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-74501>

"Are you truthful with yourself? Do you keep promises you make yourself? Are you quick to forgive yourself? Do you accept responsibility for your actions? Are you consistent with your personal goals? Do you genuinely care and respect the life you have been given?"

That really resonated with me today. Thanks Jack!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-10T23:54:02+0200

Not much of an appetite yesterday and today. I still had the typical BAB but other than that I didn't want much. I take that as a blessing because weekends like this used to be dangerous in eating department. I did re-read an old book, The Pulse Test, which is about identifying food sensitivites. Very interesting stuff. Oh, and the constant thinking about oxytocin therapy (I mentioned over on the thread) was short lived...thoughts are gone...fun while it lasted.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-15T21:58:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76011>

This week bordered on disastrous. Seemed like any negativity circulating in my mind all got together and turned me into a WTH kinda-thinking monster. I’ve resurfaced and am starting to feel somewhat “normal” again but not before eating a Tea Biscuit Tuesday morning and then French fries covered with cheddar for supper. So needless to say Wednesday was spend battling a whirlwind of food fantasies. Thankfully I made it through and yesterday wasn’t so bad.

I left eggs off the menu yesterday. I usually eat 4 eggs on weekdays, 2 with BAB and 2 for lunch. I’ve been wondering whether eggs & I don’t agree so they’ve been tossed for now. I’ve also ditched the dark chocolate a couple of weeks ago & haven’t had nuts in a long time. Thinking my precious New Zealand whey isolate needs to go too. Just feeling a bit of desperation today & want to see something change. Please, oh please, don’t let it be the HWC…or the coffee.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-15T22:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76013>

I feel your pain. Really you know I do. I tend to flop around in public and whine and cry and suck my thumb. Then it gets better.

Did you do 23andme yet? I'm laying off eggs for a while because of the genetic genie results. Also throttling back on the protein just a smidge.

I've given up on things changing. I just can't torture myself like that anymore. If I wander off in that direction I start having black thoughts, i.e., cutting calories big time to force the issue. I know it won't work but emotional desperation makes one toss logic out the window.

well that was cheerful, wasn't it.

sorry.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-16T00:11:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76021>

Darleen you can always get a chuckle out of me. I _know_ you feel my pain!!! I hope you enjoyed your cheesecake as much as I enjoyed my tea biscuit, at least you didn't eat the crust. I forgot just how bad that crap makes me feel, oh well, another lesson **re-learned**!! No worries about me ever cutting calories, I can't go back there, I cringe at the thought of my old weight watcher days!

I haven't done the 23andme yet, but I should get on that.

### diane — 2013-02-16T00:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76030>

For some reason, that's one test i'm not anxious to do - just feels like it's so much work to figure out what it means! Then if you have genetic defects, I would feel hopeless about changing anything. But that's just me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-16T00:42:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76031>

Diane it really isn't that much work. Genetic Genie will sort through the raw data and pull out anything related to methylation issues. the information that is included with your profile is relatively straight forward and easy to read/understand. The info I have so far directly from 23andme is interesting. The only slightly elevated risks I show are for coronary disease (ain't gonna happen as long as I stick with epi=paleo) and (this made me laugh) hypothyroidism. Everything else is average to low. I'll blame that on my Viking ancestors, as well.

ETA you can then show this to your physician (operative word being SHOW and not let him/her add it to your permanent medical records) and work with them to deal with any issues.

### diane — 2013-02-16T00:47:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76033>

Good to know Darleen!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-76130>

I think a lot of us are having struggles with cravings and mood lately....maybe it's just being ready for longer light cycles to start. I'd love to embrace being cold and dark but I'm craving sun, darn it! And somehow in my brain that translates to carbs.

I did the 23 and Me....I am a bit more confused about my results but maybe I have more SNPs to decipher, or maybe me brain fog is preventing me from figuring it out. Either way not much has changed because of it yet!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-03-02T17:50:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78613>

Geez, it's been 2 weeks?!!! Could have sworn it was only a week. No wonder I'm having trouble catching up with everyone!!!! I went off the rails a bit and it ended with an ice cream binge 7 days ago. Then frustration took over and I read a new book a friend of mine has been bugging me to read. The Plan. Basically an anti-inflammatory WOE combined with an elimination diet. So, I decided to give it a try because I was really starting to think there's gotta be someting I'm eating that is reacting with my body and preventing any weight loss. I haven't left the JK camp and I'm still eating paleo, but still not epi-paleo. I haven't tried adding eggs or bacon yet, both of which are favourites. I suspect eggs were a big problem for me since I lost 4.9lbs this week. I know the journey isn't about the weight loss but I can't lie, it feels good to feel the pants getting looser! And the increase in energy is a welcome change. Anyway, it's a journey and mine continues...

### diane — 2013-03-02T18:10:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78622>

Hi Paulalynn! Yep, everyone has their own path for sure.

I have to say, though, that after reading the last few blogs, I'm not sure I can read any of those books and believe them.  While I may not be able to understand everything in there, I'm understanding the enormity of it all and the implications.

### endless — 2013-03-02T18:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78624>

Woohoo on the looser pants! Good idea to check into what you are reacting to.....I am considering going back to an AI protocol, despite my labs not really suggesting I need it, because I felt better on it.

I can relate to the ice cream binge....I did a similar thing a few days ago with sugar free gummy bears. I ate the entire bag, and then sat on the toilet in distress most of the night...if you've ever overindulged in Maltitol you'll understand why.

Good to see you posting! What is the book you've been reading?

### PaulaRichards — 2013-03-02T18:48:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78640>

Krista, the book is "The Plan". For a quick explaination, here's the website:
[http://lyngenet.com/](http://lyngenet.com/)

But like Diane said, can we beleive this stuff??? I don't know. I do feel better after a week and if it was eggs or bacon, for example that was, in some way, preventing weigh loss or positive change, than I'm more than happy to have discovered that fact and lost a few pounds while doing so!

I can honestly say I have not been able to connect all the dots from everything I've read on the blogs but I'm trying! I know there's enormous implications and hoping each small change I make will come together to create more positive changes!

Oh, and Krista, I feel your pain on the maltitol issue! It was years ago when I was low carb that I ate things with sugar alcohols, it was a hard lesson but I avoid the stuff like crazy now!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-02T20:17:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78660>

EWW on the sugar alcohols. Man I learned the hard way my body does not tolerate them AT ALL. Plus I gain weight on them the same as I do sugar so what's the point. Easy to give them up when you figure THAT out.

I got back on the dark chocolate train about 10 days ago. I'd had a stash sitting in the "bread" box for months and now it's all gone. I have some 100% sitting in there and I think I may try a nibble since I no longer like anything below 90%+ and even that's a bit much. Weird how tastes change.

GLAD YOU'RE BACK!

### caroline — 2013-03-03T00:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-78683>

Glad you are back Paula - great news about the weight loss! I am long suspected eggs and bacon for me isn't good.....

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-24T17:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93610>

6 months! Has it really that long?!?! Someday I'm going to want to come back and read about my obstacles and successes so it's time for a success update!!!

After so, so, so long of doing all the things suggested I hesitate to say it, BUT, I think I've figured out the Puzzle of ME! I'm down 25lbs. 25lbs!!!! And most days am feeling great. I knew there was 'something' preventing me from dropping the weight. It took a bit of effort figuring it out but it was food. The effort was beyond worth it!!!

I'm still eating 2000+ calories most days! Sometimes not because I'm simply not hungry. Cravings are gone. I haven't binged in months and don't even think about a binge! I feel free!! I must admit I haven't bid farewell to popcorn entirely but when I do indulge it doesn't drive me over the deep end.

I shed a quick tear when I realized pork was a no-no but I don't miss it too much. It was harder to bid farewell to chocolate but I've adjusted. I also discovered that I can eat eggs but not everyday.

So if anyone is still struggling and scratching their heads in dismay don't give up!!!!

### caroline — 2013-08-25T02:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93656>

Hi Paula! I am so happy to see your post.....congrats on the successes.

I can't eat eggs every day either - don't understand that - but that's okay .....and I really don't do well if I eat chocolate.
So you don't eat bacon anymore either? I don't do well with bacon either.

It is amazing when you get control back .....I love it! Being craving free is wonderful.

Do you find that you just need to eat more simply? That really works for me.

Please pop back in and tell us more .....it helps other women who are really struggling here and can't seem to get a handle on it .....

Life is good?

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-25T21:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93730>

Hi Caroline! Life is good!

I do eat quite simply now. Still high fat and very low carb but it's so easy when I'm not battling cravings. I haven't officially 'tested' any seafood yet so I can't say I'm epi-paleo yet. I'm still not exercising and that's not a complaint! At the beginning of the summer I got some CT in when the ocean was 60 degrees but it's up to 72 now so my CT is over until I get my butt in the bathtub but I've never been able to get disciplined enough to do that on a regular basis. I drink more red wine than perhaps I should but other then that the weight loss became almost effortless once I figured out the pork and chocolate. And believe weight loss was never easy.

I'm still not exercising and that's not a complaint! ### caroline — 2013-08-26T01:44:47+0300

### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-26T04:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93788>

I'm very low carb, high fat and moderate protein. I did struggle with not being able to lose weight for years but once i figured out what i was reacting to (pork and chocolate) everything seemed to fall into place. I thankfully stumbled upon the book, The Plan and it definitely helped me eliminate my problem foods.

> I'm very low carb, high fat and moderate protein. I did struggle with not being able to lose weight for years but once i figured out what i was reacting to (pork and chocolate) everything seemed to fall into place. ### PaulaRichards — 2013-08-27T21:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93937>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=93871)
>
> What is this book you speak of? sounds really interesting.....

"The Plan" by Lyn-Genet Recitas. I felt like she wrote it for me. The basic premise is that we're not all the same and our chemistry is different. Just because bacon works for one person doesn't mean it's going to work for me. It was very interesting. It was a lot of work during the first few weeks but it was SO worth it!

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-93968>

### PaulaRichards — 2014-12-26T15:29:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151015>

Wow...over a year! Is it even appropriate to post now?!?
Seems I've stayed 'On the Wagon' for over a year now. Down 40+lbs and have never felt better in my life, physically and or emotionally! Carpel tunnel went away...haven't worn a wrist support in about a year, arthritis-type feeling in a few fingers disappeared, no more hot flashes, regular periods, the list goes on.....
Thank you Jack Kruse!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-26T17:24:05+0200

Great to hear Paula........love it.

### caroline — 2014-12-27T02:53:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151028>

Hi Paula .....merry Christmas! So happy to hear how well you are doing!

What kind of a winter are you having?

### PaulaRichards — 2014-12-27T16:47:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151050>

### PaulaRichards — 2014-12-27T16:50:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151051>

Merry Christmas to you too Caroline! We're not having much of a winter here yet. All kinds of rain. Unusual for Nova Scotia.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-27T17:28:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151052>

### caroline — 2014-12-28T00:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151073>

Good morning from Oz Paula! looking forward to more details about what has been working for you. Lots of newbies here who would love to hear your story - when you have time!

### PaulaRichards — 2015-01-02T00:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-wagon-off-the-wagon-no-more-this-time-optimal.411/post-151329>

Well, winter temps have finally hit Nova Scotia. Ski hill opens Saturday, yay!!!
Caroline, I will definitely work on a summary of my story. It certainly took a few years but it really all came together this past year and. I've never felt better. I'll never forget JK's words during my consult....'you're not a total train wreck but you're definitely off the rails', he's the best!
In short, I was feeling good following what I was learning here but just couldn't figure out why the weight was barely budging. I never did well on carbs and that was a given. Even squash triggered binges. Anyway, Long story short, I determined that I couldn't eat pork. That was a biggie. After years, the weight starting coming off...effortlessly...even surpassing my goal. I could no longer go 'shopping in my own closet'...which is fun...but it was more fun when that was no longer an option.
Happy New Year!

### caroline — 2015-01-02T00:50:15+0200

# Thread: HmmÃ¢â‚¬Â¦I seem to be losing muscle and fat. Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### codys182 — 2012-03-25T02:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5635>

So I started CT about 2 weeks ago. I did the face dunks, ice packs, and just did my first bath yesterday. The weight is falling off. However, while working out today I felt and looked much weaker, and my performance had decreased. I am pretty strict keto and following the leptin reset protocol. I know my calories had dropped quite a bit during the past few weeks as I have lost most of my hunger. They are not too low, thoughÃ¢â‚¬Â¦and probably close to 2,000 cals a day.

My workouts are pretty low intensity - lift heavy for about 30 minutes, using mostly compound lifts. I have only been doing this 2-3 days a week. I am about 5'10, 185 lbs, and about 15% body fat.

Does anyone have any recommendations or run into this issue? Should I possibly add in some carbs? Or just make an effort to eat more?

### Glamorama — 2012-03-25T02:37:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5639>

I would focus on upping protein. I lost strength after a week of CT but it returned massively with increases in week 3! I haven't lost hunger though, I eat well... Lots of fat and protein. Try that and see how you go this week.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-25T02:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5641>

Ps! Fat loss releases estrogen into your system which often leads to lack of strength and libido in men. It's part of detox. Get B12 and B6.

### codys182 — 2012-03-25T02:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5646>

Thanks! Will do…definitely feeling as though my testosterone is taking a major hit.

### Cody — 2012-03-25T05:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5670>

Make sure you are getting plenty of magnesium and zinc too!!!

I've only had muscle gain with CT and the Leptin Reset. Gained 8 freaking pounds! But my waist is getting smaller.

Lift heavier, that tells your body to hold onto your muscle. 5 reps, last one should be FREAKING HARD!

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-25T17:01:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5787>

Another thing to consider is that it's just from being keto. I don't know if you were previously and didn't have issues, but if not you may just be noticing that. I haven't been doing any exercise while doing my reset and ct for the last 8 wks. BUT, before this I did go keto at various times and I noticed a MASSIVE difference in being able to do any exercise. I know that many lifters do as well. Dr. K says that you will get weaker at first and then have massive gains, but that it can take 3 months - 2 yrs! This is why most paleo lifters seem to prefer more carbs. But, I believe Dr. K that it isn't the best plan and will shorten telemeres, but I don't care if I have weakness for even a couple years as I don't compete or anything like that. My experience though has been added strength since doing CT, but I still can tell a difference in endurance. My muscles literally feel weaker and I can't do even half of what I normally can in endurance when I'm keto. But, it has been getting better with the CT for me.

### Cody — 2012-03-25T17:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5791>

Great point Lovelife!!!

More strength for me doing CT as well.

Keto will definitely make you feel weaker short term.

I've been keto off and on for over five years, so I didn't experience any strength loss from the Leptin Reset.

### Adria — 2012-03-25T17:07:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-5792>

> **Lovelife;5821 wrote:** Dr. K says that you will get weaker at first and then have massive gains

Yes this is what happened to me, at first I was weaker but as the months of keto progressed I got stronger and now lift more than ever before. I've been keto since 2009 and weightlifting 20+yrs. Then I started CT last month and noticed I'm a little weaker with my weights but expect greater results in the long run.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-04-11T04:43:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-13601>

> **Cody;5704 wrote:** Make sure you are getting plenty of magnesium and zinc too!!!
>
>
>
> I've only had muscle gain with CT and the Leptin Reset. Gained 8 freaking pounds! But my waist is getting smaller.
>
>
>
> Lift heavier, that tells your body to hold onto your muscle. 5 reps, last one should be FREAKING HARD!

How do you know if you need zinc? I know if it's really bad then you get a metallic taste in your mouth, which I don't have. Do you know if it's OK to take a zinc or just leave it?

### Glamorama — 2012-04-11T05:27:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-13627>

> **Gladina;13670 wrote:** How do you know if you need zinc? I know if it's really bad then you get a metallic taste in your mouth, which I don't have. Do you know if it's OK to take a zinc or just leave it?

I did a taste-test with my naturopath. I was very deficient, the solution tasted like water to me. I supplemented daily for 14 weeks, and byt that point I started feeling sick after taking zinc, so the doc told me I probably had my storage full, and could stop taking it altogether. Now I take it every 2nd night again, without any side-effects.

### chocolate — 2012-04-19T02:07:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hmm%C3%83%C2%A2%C3%A2%E2%80%9A%C2%AC%C3%82%C2%A6i-seem-to-be-losing-muscle-and-fat-help.412/post-16392>

this guys says it takes two weeks. after you are keto, the protein gets left alone, its a target when you are running on carbs

# Thread: Set the standard and they will follow!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-the-standard-and-they-will-follow.413/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-25T02:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-the-standard-and-they-will-follow.413/post-5642>

After thinking mommy was quite crazy these last few weeks, my children did their own versions of CT tonight! My 7-year old son sat on an ice block for a good 30 minutes while my 11-year old daughter decided on her own to wet and freeze a towel and then lay on it. I'm thrilled and hoping they continue! They also both asked me to buy them a pair of the blue light blocking glasses. I jumped and ordered some on Amazon right away!

They already do fairly well with diet. I would say they are 90% paleo/primal with just the occasional slip when my daughter is at a friend's house or my mom gives my son gluten free pizza - still trying to work on some of those issues. My DD was at a friend's house last night and had regular pizza. She told me today when she got home that she'd rather not "eat that stuff," but she doesn't want to be rude, so we had a talk about how to handle those situations. I love that she's thinking about things on her own and CHOOSING to do what she feels is best for her. My son even said he knows he feels better when he eats clean - that's HUGE for a 7 year old! I know they would have never had the same reaction if I forced all of this on them.

Just wanted to post to encourage all of you to continue being the example and shining the light for your children. They are watching and absorbing every little thing they see. My kids knew I had been very sick for awhile to the point of not even being able to take walks with them or play at times. The last week or so I've finally started to get some energy back and they're noticing.

Now if only I could get my parents and some others in my life on board . . .

# Thread: CT and ear ringing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2012-03-25T04:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5664>

Has anyone had any experience with CT and improving ringing in the ears?

I've been doing CT for about a week - started with face dunking (and didn't mind the cold, I'm Canadian LOL, but didn't like holding my breath), have been doing spot icing with ice packs around my middle (and melting them within about 40 minutes), and one cold bath so far, as well as cold showers in the morning. I've been feeling like I have a bit more energy, and have noticed that I'm a bit smaller around the middle so far.

I've had ringing in my ears for over 6 years. It's constant ringing, sometimes louder than others. I also have constant congestion in my head and runny eyes, many food allergies (probably more like intolerances) and think that the ringing is related. Have also been tested and have adrenal fatigue and leaky gut.

I'm hoping that this will help, because I haven't been able to even reduce it yet!

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-25T04:49:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5665>

Yes, my tinnitus has improved. some days it\`s quite dull and I forget I have it but if I am back under stress it returns. I had a fever this week and noticed today the tinnitus is back. I\`m back on CT Baths tomorrow and hope it will calm down again

### Melinda — 2012-03-25T05:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5672>

Just the opposite for me: my tinnitus had improved with singing and choir practice and "listening hard" to try and retrain the damaged hearing loss areas that are ghost adding the ring. But since I started the full body cold soaks a few days ago, it has increased again intermittently. I'm thinking it might just be a symptom of detoxing and will hush again in a few weeks. I'm calm about it b/c I have been able to shush it before. I remember Its the woo said it is louder with high glucose/insulin. Some ppl report elevated glucose numbers as they CT, maybe that's the tie in.

### Mikey7 — 2012-03-25T06:05:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-5689>

I have mild case of tinnitus, and I haven't notice anything. I know it still there but it doesn't bother me at all.

### Tita — 2012-04-11T21:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-13860>

Ha, funny... I thought you were writing about ringing in ears after CT.

I noticed after my swims in cold ocean, I got ringing in my ears and wondered why....(othervise I have no problems with sinuses&...)

### nonchalant — 2012-04-11T23:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-13884>

I had improvement in tinnitus when I increased my dose of magnesium. I rarely notice it any more. I also don't react as badly to sudden loud noises, like a slamming screen door. I seem to be more tolerant, for some reason. And I **think** my hearing might be a little better.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-04-11T23:42:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-13920>

I had tinnitus that came on after I started CT, it lasted for about 2.5 days and then I finally got the drainage going and it went away. My subsequent soaks didn't cause this, but I still get lots of sinus drainage because of all the garbage I'm detoxing. So I go slow and only get in when I am feeling good... which will NOT be for the next couple of weeks when the pollen count is out of this world here!

### Kirstin — 2012-04-12T04:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-ear-ringing.414/post-14050>

I noticed that my tinnitus improved dramatically by cutting out the grais, sugars, etc and eating strict paleo. Haven't been too consistent with CT, so haven't noticed too many changes yet.

# Thread: Question regarding sleep...I would appreciate your input
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2012-03-25T05:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5673>

I had a quick question I was hoping someone could help me with regarding my sleep. I have not been able to sleep well for the past 5 years or so and have been taking some kind of over the counter sleeping aid or melatonin tabs 3 mg every single night. I literally could not sleep without them. I decided to stop taking them about 2 months ago and now just sleep in pitch black every night without any aids. I lay down around 10 pm, fall asleep around 11 pm or so, and wake up every morning around 4:30. I cannot get back to sleep no matter what I do. So I go make and eat breakfast and lay back down around 5:30 a.m. and within a half hour I get very sleepy immediately and I fall back asleep from 6:00 to 9:00 a.m. before getting up for the day. A few questions...

1\. What is causing this? I find myself VERY sleepy after my breakfast, lunch, and dinner each day as well.

2\. Is this a problem and/or unhealthy for me?

3\. Should I stop laying back down after my breakfast and just remain up for the day from 4:30 a.m. on even though I am still very tired and my body is obviously still seeking the sleep?

Thanks for any light anyone could shed on this topic for me. I greatly appreciate it

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-25T05:38:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5678>

Not sure about on the nighttime issue, but sleepy after meals says insulin resistance (or diabetes) to me. Do you get sleepy if you don't eat any carbs at mealtime? I would recommend buying a cheap blood glucose meter at walmart & testing yourself. It should read less than 100 when you wake up in the morning; 101 to 130 is considered "pre-diabetic/insulin resistant"; over 130 is diabetic.

PS testing yourself in the morning is considered a "Fasting Blood Glucose" test; ie, don't eat anything 12 hours before testing.

### janagram — 2012-03-25T08:42:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5731>

Five and a half hours- you're almost there! six hours is good....I know 7 is better, but who gets 7?

Maybe don't eat brkfst, just read or something until you get sleepy again. (easy to say...I know)

I have a similar problem....so keep reporting....

### bennyha — 2012-03-25T15:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5755>

> **Chrisgraff;5712 wrote:** Not sure about on the nighttime issue, but sleepy after meals says insulin resistance (or diabetes) to me. Do you get sleepy if you don't eat any carbs at mealtime? I would recommend buying a cheap blood glucose meter at walmart & testing yourself. It should read less than 100 when you wake up in the morning; 101 to 130 is considered "pre-diabetic/insulin resistant"; over 130 is diabetic.
>
>
>
> PS testing yourself in the morning is considered a "Fasting Blood Glucose" test; ie, don't eat anything 12 hours before testing.

I haven't really eaten many carbs for the past few years. I use to carb cycle for a few years on workout days. So on Mon, Wed, Fri I would lift heavy weights then eat around 150 grams of carbs (usually 2 big sweet potatos with cinnamon post-workout). Then the other 4 days of the week I would only eat green veggies and carbs were around 30 grams or so. But I've been eating very low carb the past 3 or 4 weeks trying to get into ketosis along with cold therapy. I had a horrible binge eating problem for 2 years despite being very slim at 5'10 155 lbs and single digit bodyfat. I could literally eat 15,000 calories in 3 or 4 hours and not even befull. But I finally got rid of that after doing the leptin reset protocol. Back to regular hunger and have lost about 10 lbs I didn't want or need to lose. Maybe it's just taking my body some time to adjust? I'm sure the 2 years of binging on nuts and ice cream did some damage to me

### bennyha — 2012-03-25T16:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5756>

Also, I did notice the last time I ate some sweet potatoes a month or so ago, I did feel even more tired and experience much more brain fog than when I ate just fat and protein

### Adria — 2012-03-25T16:28:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5770>

The components to good sleep are diet, light, cold. Stick with the keto diet and be sure not to allow lights when the sun is setting, including computers etc. You might want to get some blue blocking glasses to use at night. Can you give a menu of what you eat? Seems strange you are sleepy after eating keto-paleo, post a typical menu. Are you Cting, if so give more detail.

### bennyha — 2012-03-25T18:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-5836>

> **Adria;5804 wrote:** The components to good sleep are diet, light, cold. Stick with the keto diet and be sure not to allow lights when the sun is setting, including computers etc. You might want to get some blue blocking glasses to use at night. Can you give a menu of what you eat? Seems strange you are sleepy after eating keto-paleo, post a typical menu. Are you Cting, if so give more detail.

Thanks for your reply. A typical breakfast is eggs and bacon cooked in ghee and some salmon and a little spinach or broccoli. Lunch is usually mackeral or salmon with some ghee and a little spinach or broccoli. Dinner is usually beef or some more fish. Also supplement with high dose EPA/DHA fish oil at every meal. Maybe I'm eating too much protein? I'm 150 lbs and eat around 200 grams of protein per day, 50+ grams at each meal. Carbs are really low as you can tell and fat is moderate to high.

As for CT, I sit in a tub of 50 degree water everyday for 35-40 minutes. It's a jacuzzi-style tub, so I am able to lay down fully with the water coming up to my chin the entire time, so my entire body is covered. At first I would be miserably cold after getting out of the tub for up to 2 hours. Now I find that I'm only uncomfortable for a half hour or so after getting out.

It's not falling asleep that's bothering me, it's the extreme brain fog/fatigue I feel after each meal

### L8F — 2012-03-26T03:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-6167>

Wondering about cortisol levels, have you done them? How about absorption issues with the intense prior cravings? Using Betaine HCl or enzymes? (Just listened to a Robb Wolff podcast on this digestion stuff..a lot can happen in the gut as we all know)

### bennyha — 2012-03-26T04:42:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-6204>

> **L8F;6202 wrote:** Wondering about cortisol levels, have you done them? How about absorption issues with the intense prior cravings? Using Betaine HCl or enzymes? (Just listened to a Robb Wolff podcast on this digestion stuff..a lot can happen in the gut as we all know)

I actually started taking digestive enzymes about a month ago and noticed significant improvement in the reduction of bloating after a meal. After I would eat a big meal for the past year or so it would just sit in my stomach and look like I was pregnant for like 3 hours. I also would have a bunch of bumps after I ate for an hour or two. All that has gone away since I started taking the digestive enzymes before meals. I highly recommend them to anyone who thinks they may have any digestion issues as they helped me significantly

### Adria — 2012-03-26T15:09:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-regarding-sleep-i-would-appreciate-your-input.415/post-6294>

With the brain fog and bloating you describe, I think the key for you is diet. You could be allergic or sensitive to the foods you're eating. I would experiment with it. Cut out all vegs and go with no ghee, basically zero carb. Just try it for a week and see how you feel and if your sleep and brain fog improves. If not try removing the seafood. Test everything and take your time, let your body adjust to each change (several days).

# Thread: But Sherpa's have a high starch diet !???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoMom — 2012-03-25T07:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-5720>

In CT 4 Jack talks about the Sherpas eating a more keto diet:

"They also ate a very different diet than the rest of India. It was one that was heavily skewed to animal protein and fats. No carbs really grow that high up or in those temperatures with any regularity."

[http://www.sherpatrek.com/culture/sherpa-food.php](http://http://www.sherpatrek.com/culture/sherpa-food.php)

[http://www.everyculture.com/wc/Mauritania-to-Nigeria/Sherpas.html](http://http://www.everyculture.com/wc/Mauritania-to-Nigeria/Sherpas.html)

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxqoGYW36So\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;GxqoGYW36So\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxqoGYW36So\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;GxqoGYW36So)

I don't see any evidence to back that up. Wim Hof also does not eat a keto diet and he certainly is cold adapted. I'm not saying Wim is optimal, but he is getting tons of benefit from the cold still and is very lean.

### janagram — 2012-03-25T23:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-5997>

well as I recall from reading, the Tibetan diet was a lot of meat, dried cheese, butter, and some barley....not much grows up there....of course in recent times, change comes to all original diets....in my understanding, potatoes are from the new world....so sure they get around...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-25T23:58:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6024>

What is common to the Inuit and the Sherpa's? They are the only humans who constantly live in the cold the last 25K yrs.

What is not common to them? One has access to the sea and the other does not.

So that means that one has access to the ideal source of food and one has to put something together to make up for it.

Following yet.

The Sherpa are good.......but not as good as the Inuit were in 1900. go read the account that was left on CT 6 by dr.bass or Dr. Schweitzer original papers on the Inuit..........what he said about them was hard to believe.

And remains that way because since their diet was polluted by the SAD in 1950 we have NO HUMANS who live in the perfect environment and who eat the perfect diet..........

So modern humans remain completely numb to the reality of the Ancient Pathway..........All biochem textbooks were written AFTER the Inuit diets were destroyed........so their adaptations were never studied.

Until NASA went to the moon in 1969, until Mark Spitz, Armstrong, Phelps, Sherpas........were rediscovered doing some amazing things.

got it?

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T01:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6092>

I'm not arguing if cold is good or not. I'm also not arguing whether the Inuit are amazing specimens or not. What I am wondering is why you stated the Sherpas eat a keto diet (no cites related to Sherpas on that post) when I could only find information stating the traditional diet of the Sherpas to be starch heavy.

It is fine to say that the Sherpas are healthy in spite of their diet because of the cold, because the cold is like a compensating factor for the lack of marine foods the Inuit have. However, stating they are healthy and live long lives because of the cold + keto (without a source to prove that) makes me question the how true this may be. The evidence that I see seems to be showing a culture that has cold plus starch. I understand that this is probably a diet that wasn't with them thousands of years ago, but how are they doing now healthwise on starch and do we have evidence of their health, endurance etc. from before starches were brought up the mountain?

Please, also understand that I am NOT trying to argue for the sake of arguing. I very much hate rocking the boat. I do want to fully understand why I should be eating keto vs. lower carb (say under 100) when I feel terrible at that carb level in spite of being paleo for about 6 years and being fat adapted. I also have great energy, sleep well and don't have any hormonal troubles. I have read the blog and have heard all the science behind the theory, however I don't see real life examples of people thriving with an active lifestyle on a keto diet in the cold.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-26T01:58:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6105>

I understand your questions. I, too, have questions in regard to the necessity of keto if you are long-term paleo, no leptin problems, no weight issues and eat an occasional good starch. Is it going to take away all benefits? I just feel better with occasional good starch, ie sweet potatoes one night a week and rice or quinoa two nights a week? Should I not delve into CT if I don't slide into complete keto?

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T02:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6114>

> **chewingthefat;6140 wrote:** I understand your questions. I, too, have questions in regard to the necessity of keto if you are long-term paleo, no leptin problems, no weight issues and eat an occasional good starch. Is it going to take away all benefits? I just feel better with occasional good starch, ie sweet potatoes one night a week and rice or quinoa two nights a week? Should I not delve into CT if I don't slide into complete keto?

Wim Hof has had lots of scientific tests done on himself that prove amazing things happening to him, yet he eats a regular diet, drinks alcohol etc. It _may_ be true that eating keto + CT is better, but there is certainly evidence in Wim that doing CT with any diet is better than not doing CT. I really enjoyed his book "Becoming the Iceman". Very peaceful, happy guy that just wants to share the gift of health from CT with the world.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-26T02:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6141>

I saw him on youtube. Amazing.

I understand the circadian aspect of CT and the need for the body to have balance in seasonal and light cues. I've been tuned into this for a long time.

I also know how my body feels and the fact that I was horribly fatigued after several months of a strict keto and no starch diet. Lost too much weight. And I got a serious sinus infection. Cycle stopped. Made me stop and think about what I was doing wrong. Started eating starches and I felt better. ?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-26T02:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6146>

> **PaleoMom;6149 wrote:** Wim Hof has had lots of scientific tests done on himself that prove amazing things happening to him, yet he eats a regular diet, drinks alcohol etc. It _may_ be true that eating keto + CT is better, but there is certainly evidence in Wim that doing CT with any diet is better than not doing CT. I really enjoyed his book "Becoming the Iceman". Very peaceful, happy guy that just wants to share the gift of health from CT with the world.

There are two phases I think - one is "becoming adapted", and then the "adapted". Dr. K said he eats seasonally now (when not n-1 experimenting) but that to assist in our transition to being cold adapted our cells need keto. That was my take-away on it.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T02:48:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6152>

Many years ago I read a book called "The Three Season Diet" by John Douillard. I don't back the non-paleoness of it, but the seasonal part I think is great. It is about the same as Dr. K is saying. With my paleo slanted view of the book, the advice was eat lots of fruit and veggies in late summer/early fall, in winter eat lots of fat and meat, in spring eat low fat (animals have leaned out considerably) lots of greens and generally less calories (not much plant matter is available in spring/ early summer.

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-26T04:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6190>

Yes, I read that book, too. It made a lot of sense to me at the time. As a gardener, I noted the availability of various foods at different times and season. Potatoes of all sorts, onions, winter squash, and root vegetables are available for "forage-ing" long after the snow flies. These used to be my go-to foods for late fall and winter. And it felt right eating them.

I know we are striving for earlier than the agricultural era, but I'm afraid I am running out of food to eat. No safe starches, no nightshades, no eggs (Hashimotos), no dairy (another sensitivity)...none of the obvious... no wheat (celiac), no corn, no rice, no grains, no quinoa, no berries, no nuts (mild sensitivity). What am I supposed to eat? I know...meat, fat, greens and other veggies. But no greens or veggies grow naturally during the winter, at least here? The strange thing is, I'm not hungry. But I don't need to lose any more weight.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-26T05:30:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6224>

> **chewingthefat;6225 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I know we are striving for earlier than the agricultural era, but I'm afraid I am running out of food to eat. No safe starches, no nightshades, no eggs (Hashimotos), no dairy (another sensitivity)...none of the obvious... no wheat (celiac), no corn, no rice, no grains, no quinoa, no berries, no nuts (mild sensitivity). What am I supposed to eat? I know...meat, fat, greens and other veggies. But no greens or veggies grow naturally during the winter, at least here? The strange thing is, I'm not hungry. But I don't need to lose any more weight.

Yes, exactly. Root veggies and squash are actually much more seasonal in the winter than greens and other veggies. And while they are certainly not something we would have had in the winter before agriculture (or very, very little of at least), I just can't see eating nothing but meat and fat and organs all winter long. Perhaps it will give me a bit longer life, however there are many thriving people that eat these things and worse in the winter and I would be plenty happy to grow old as healthy as they are.

Dr. K talks about the Monks in the "The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari" book. I'm currently reading it, yes they live up high in the mountains, but they also eat a vegetarian diet. I'm not finished with it yet, but so far the only mention of cold has been that as a punishment they will walk to an icy cold waterfall and stand under it until they can't stand it anymore. They do this to themselves if they have allowed a negative thought into their mind.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-26T06:24:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/but-sherpas-have-a-high-starch-diet.421/post-6238>

There are actually plenty of greens that grow in winter. I've harvested swiss chard and other similar greens from under snow in my garden. Some herbs, too. I do think, however, that in the dead of winter you drop to nothing but meat and fat you'd be fine. But if you want seasonal veggies, they do exist.

# Thread: Hey..from the other side of the world...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gemstar — 2012-03-25T08:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-5727>

I'm located in Israel. Been playing with low carb for many years. I have picture of me with my daughter who is now 28 looking great from being on the good ole' Atkins plan. I did CALP (carbo addicts life plan) and lost weight. But of course the yo-yo effect was always a part of life. I now am low carb and some days almost paleo. I still have issues, and eventually will try to do LR.

I 'envy' you Americans and your grass fed local beef/lamb/chicken. The closest thing we have here in Israel is frozen beef imported from South America. There is alot of local beef that some even enjoy free range. But we do not have green pastures all year round, in fact we only have green pastures for 4 months a year in our arid climate. Beef cattle is then fed hay and fermented vegetables and chicken manure. Our lamb is not much better, most of it is NOT even free range, and so is fed grain feed and some hay. Goat meat is probably the most natural meat we have here, but is extremely hard to find.

But even though our meat is toxic, I find a paleo style diet much much healthier than the SID (standard Israeli diet) that mimics the SAD, although most Israelis do eat a lot more vegies and fruits than SAD America. Unfortunately, Israel follows the modern approach to healthy eating, and people speak about eating cholesterol foods with that 'heart attack on a plate' attitude. Mainstream Israeli medicine and populace has totally bought into the cholesterol myth. That is even more annoying sometimes than not being able to find pastured animal produce.

Love and light and SHALOM

Myra in Israel

### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2012-03-25T11:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-5742>

Hi Myra,

JOIN THE CLUB!!!

I live in South Africa and share the same frustrations, can't even find anything "low carb" but managed as best I can, my friends all think I have lost my mind....but I will show them LOL

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-26T14:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-6288>

Welcome to the forum, Myra!

### gemstar — 2012-03-27T08:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-from-the-other-side-of-the-world.423/post-6879>

Thanks for welcome......Is there a recipe thread here?? I've been specializing in alternative sweets and baked goods for years. I just made up something AMAZING...that I would like to share with y'all. I'm going to look for the recipe forum.

### ste colombe — 2012-03-25T13:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hong-kong.426/post-5745>

Hi all,

Im from Hong Kong, hot and humid so fun trying to CT - I have to add ice constantly to the bath to keep it at the right temperature! I've been doing the Leptin RX since the beginning of the year and started CT 2 weeks ago. Thus far, I've not encountered many problems as I've always loved cold showers, so it made total sense to me instinctively, but for 3 days now I now have the most awful headaches (havent had those since I was a teenager). I'm 43 yrs old and a mum of 4, I'm of French origin and trying to fix Adrenal death, hashimotos, get a pink liver and lose a few kgs.... Dr Kruse THANK YOU and thank you all for your input, it helps so very much just reading what everyone is going through - we live in a small world...

I modified this recipe from MDA: [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/tomato-garlic-mussels/#axzz1q8snfAce](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/tomato-garlic-mussels/#axzz1q8snfAce)

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-25T17:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-porn-post-your-pics.427/post-5814>

Grassfed/finished beef short ribs

Prepare like a regular pot roast using the following ingredients:

2 Tbs ghee/butter/coconut oil - your choicd

2 large onios

garlic

1 can of fire roasted tomatoes - or 2 large tomatoes

paprika

bayleaf

1-2 c white wine

1-2 c homemade bone broth

thyime

cinnamon

2lbs boneless short ribs (ours had bones - so we saved them and made more bone broth!)

in dutch oven cook onions and garlic til translucent - add tomatoes, and seasoning - then add beef short ribs

throw all together in a 300deg oven

You don't need to sear the ribs before hand - if you leave the top off of the dutch oven

Attached files [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/5848=42-ImageUploadedByTapatalk1332686541.274347.jpg)

# Thread: Lab question for the geeks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T16:34:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5771>

I want to check my Leptin sensitivity.

which main tests do I need and what ranges equal Leptin sensitivity or which numbers equal leptin resistance? I want the biggest bang for the buck without ordering every test under the sun?

I am sure it has been posted already, I just don't have time to comb the threads. Thanks!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-25T17:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5805>

VAP or NMR lipid profile - checks your Leptin Sensitivity at the Liver

Free T3, Reverse T3, TSH, Free T4, TSH, Thyroid Antibodies: TPO & AB - checks Leptin Sensitivity at the Brain (HPA - Thyroid Axis)

Salivary cortisol - verifies - your Leptin sensitivity at the Brain -(what is the HPA -Thyroid axis doing)

Sex hormones (DHEA-S, Pregnenolone, Prolactin, Testosterone, Estrogen, Progesterone, Alderstrone) - identifies if you have pregnenolone steal - If any of the above are out of alignment - you'll have problems w/your sex hormones

VO2Max - will test your leptin sensitivity at the muscles - most ppl wont get this test... but there are ways out there to determine it w/intense exercise - and blood spot tests after each intense repetition - I remember when training at the national level for swimming we'd test VO2Max - by doing 3x200m all out swims w/1min rest btwn each 200m. we'd do blood spot tests at the completion of each 200m - some measurement was done - and by the end we could see where our VO2Max was...

Quelsen has a good list of labs + the labcorp codes...

### Owl — 2012-03-25T18:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5841>

Dr. K said this about determining if you're LR, "you can get a Leptin level or a T3 and rev T3 and then use the conversion calculator at the STTM.com to see where your ratio is. We like it above 20. Other signs......high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone. high ferritin, haptoglobin, HS CRP, low Vit D and low DHEA levels.......all things that walk hand and hand with LR."

And from his Leptin FAQ blog:

"How do I determine if I am leptin resistant? Remember, you can be LR (leptin resistant) if youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re fat or skinny. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re overweight by more than 30lbs, it is a lock you have some degree of LR. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re underweight by 20 lbs, you are likely LR, too. If you had an eating disorder, youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re likely suffering from a serious leptin issue.

The easiest test is to look in the mirror. The mirror does not lie and it is really cheap. For those people who still canÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t be sure after peeking in the mirror, you can order some blood tests. My favorite is the HS CRP (highly sensitive C-Reactive protein) and the reverse T3 tests (but there are others). They are accurate in over 90 % of cases.

My next favorite test is the salivary cortisol test, along with at least two of the following: HDL level, serum ferritin, homocysteine, fasting triglycerides, fasting insulin, or post-prandial glucose readings done every 15 minutes for one hour after eating. (Read my Hormone 101 blog for details.)"

### Owl — 2012-03-25T18:44:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5860>

As far as ranges, he has said he wants hs-CRP under .5, and preferably way lower than that. STTM site he mentions with the calculator is: [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/)

You want your FreeT3/RT3 ratio over 20, or your total T3/RT3 ratio over 10.

The adrenal saliva test should show all readings in the normal range.

I think he wants insulin under 2. Fast BG should be in the low 80's.

I'm not sure on the other levels.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-03-25T19:31:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5891>

We like it above 20. Other signs......high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone. high ferritin, haptoglobin, HS CRP, low Vit D and low DHEA levels.......all things that walk hand and hand with LR."

If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re overweight by more than 30lbs, it is a lock you have some degree of LR. If youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re underweight by 20 lbs, you are likely LR, too. If you had an eating disorder, youÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re likely suffering from a serious leptin issue.
>
>
>
> The easiest test is to look in the mirror. The mirror does not lie and it is really cheap. For those people who still canÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t be sure after peeking in the mirror, you can order some blood tests. My favorite is the HS CRP (highly sensitive C-Reactive protein) and the reverse T3 tests (but there are others). They are accurate in over 90 % of cases.
>
