> My next favorite test is the salivary cortisol test, along with at least two of the following: HDL level, serum ferritin, homocysteine, fasting triglycerides, fasting insulin, or post-prandial glucose readings done every 15 minutes for one hour after eating. (Read my Hormone 101 blog for details.)"

Ok, well, here's a question... Are we talking CM LDL levels or is there an alternative level I should know about. I got my LDL back and it was high (figured, not measured). Out of curiosity, I used that "Iranian Formula" that I had seen on another site that claims it is a better estimation of LDL for low carbers with low trigs (which mine were, with a very high HDL) and it lowered my LDL to within acceptable range.......

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T20:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-5926>

Thanks owl, I'll refer back to here when do my blood work!

### Owl — 2012-03-26T01:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-question-for-the-geeks.428/post-6100>

Out of curiosity, I used that "Iranian Formula" that I had seen on another site that claims it is a better estimation of LDL for low carbers with low trigs (which mine were, with a very high HDL) and it lowered my LDL to within acceptable range.......

I am not very knowledgeable about lipids, but he did say, "high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone." So you could test your pregnenolone. If it's low, you may have that problem.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all-from-ny.429/post-5781>

Hello all, Dex brought me over here, I've been out of the monster thread for a few months and missing everyone. I've been doing a lot of reading and research about Hashi's, adrenal fatigue, hypoT, etc. and needed to focus my efforts there. I feel like I have a handle on it now though and am ready to jump back into the world of leptin. I did the leptin reset last June and followed it until Dec. religiously. I did great on it until I had a Hashi flare up and things went a bit haywire. I'm getting that under control now. I've still been following the reset loosely, and looking forward to getting back to it completely. I haven't even read up on the CT stuff yet, I have a lot of catching up to do!

# Thread: PlaeoDentist's Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T17:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-5802>

INTRO:

42 y/o male, 5'9 medium build.

I have been on lowish carb Paleo Robb Wolf/ Sission for 14 months 225lb->184lb . I may have 5 lbs of body fat to lose if that much. (my wife thinks I look good already and thinks any more fat loss will make me look bad, but I want better body composition. ). ALthough according tho the charts I need to be 164, which will make me look like an anorexic. I do 30 min Heavy weights once per week using Body by Science protocols, but add in a few more exercises. I have done them fasted, but now will follow Dr K's protocol. The only thing I have is major sleep issues. I wake up 3-4am and can't go back to sleep. and feel very rested on 5-6 hrs sleep. But I know I need to sleep more. I bet my cortisone is messed up. may have it tested. I ate only dinner following my hunger cues.

I began Leptin Reset 6 days ago and lost 2 lbs. I eat a BAB although not hungry. I began CT yesterday using cold tap water (not measured temp but it was chilly) did 6 - 20 sec face dunks. and spot iced one problem fat area for 30 min. skin was white not pink. I plan on following your protocol to help me shed the remaining fat, especially spot cold the one problem area and to improve my sleep- which is the main driving factor.

Attached files [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/5836=116-Slide1-1.jpg)

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-25T17:27:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-5803>

Following my BAB ( eggs, bacon, smoked salmon wrapped in nori.) I took one spoon full of Raw organic Coconut oil (Trader Joe's) 16 oz ice cold water and did face dunks.

Sink with ice got it down to 45F tested with a digital meat thermometer. The eater was very uncomfortable as compared to yesterday. I managed 6 20 sec sessions with a 10-15 sec rest in between. my face burned and it did not feel good. no shivering. face was red. spot icing 30 min on lower abdomen with icepack.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-27T03:49:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-6736>

well, as per Dr K's observation that I am not Leptin resistant, I switched to the maintenance protocol. I am continuing to iet a protein rich breakfast, just not going for the 50 gm protein. I'm at about 35 gm. I skip lunch and eat a good dinner. tonight was a big ass salad with 1 lb GF ground beef with mexican spices. folioed by face dunking in ice water that was 50F. what I noticed today is that I became used to the cold a little bit better.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-28T03:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-7469>

continued with face dunking today. used lots of ice. temp down to 43F water. did 5 dunks working my way form 45 sec to 70 sec. I noticed that after about 20 sec my face began to feel warm. if I moved my head I felt a lot colder.. I wonder if its just the water around my skin warming up. didn't feel bad a t all. maybe this is what it feels like during baths.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T03:33:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-7477>

Just saying "hi" and to say best of luck with continuing the CT. Your progress is very inspirational!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-01T23:26:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-9956>

Today I tried a bath. I sat in my bathtub and started the cold water. It was 55F but then warmed up to 63F. it was up to my waist. I kept legs fully immersed. sat there for 25 min. not too bad. no shivering. I was fitting with the drain , which is leaking, so the water level started to go down after I turned the faucet off. I think I will add ice next time to get to 55-60F.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-07T15:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12434>

I only missed 1 day of bath since starting 4/1. the water is 58F and I stay in 30 min immersed up top mid stomach which is basically a full bath tub. I only missed one day, but did use an ice pack on lower abdomen for 40 min on that day. so far no major improvements. but I also realize it has only been 6 days! I have a long journey ahead of me. I plan to do 30 min sessions for 2-3 weeks then up it to 45 min and then 1 hour daily at each month increment. I think 1 hour bath is going to be my limit due to the constraints. I spend my time listening to Robb Wolfs earlier podcasts. There are plenty on the Jimmy Moore site to keep me entertained for a while!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-07T16:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12442>

I just posted a pic on FB......CTing at sunrise. I need optimal.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-07T17:50:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12462>

Man you folks just diving into the ice tub are braver than I am.

and I do NOT think that 164 will make you look anorexic. If you're packing extra muscle you might wind up at 170-175 and still look trim and great. The problem, I believe, is that we have all gotten so used to everyone around us being at a minimum 20-30 pounds overweight with a majority being obese, that we have lost sight of what a healthy, fit human being looks like.

Go look through photo archives from the 1800's and the early to mid 1900's. You'll see what I mean. I'll bet you'll think most of those people look like they're one meal away from starvation. But EVERYone looked like that.

My unasked for advice is to quit worrying about the scale and about body fat percentages and concentrate on how you feel. It's what I am trying whole-heartedly to do, myself. Not easy but much saner than the other way.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-07T22:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12523>

Thanks for visiting!!!!!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-07T22:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12524>

I'm off to Jamaica, only had time for a cold shower today. will CT there as much as possible.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-09T00:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12802>

OK first day in Jamaica. Its HOT! after breakfast I attempted a cold bath. now I understand the frustration of all the southerners with the cold tap water, which is actually warm! I do not have a thermometer, but its warm. I needed 3 waste baskets full of ice to get it down to a chilly level. not sure the exact temp, but it did make my skin cold, but all the ice melted. I guesstimate it was in to low 60'sF.

did a 30 min soak. the cold shower water is barely cold. I just turned down the room thermostat to 65F from the set 72F. Hopefully the wifey won't object (much). now off to the gym...

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-09T05:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-12900>

Well, i made a discovery at the hotel today. they have a58F cold plunge pool. so after today's full body workout I took advantage of it! considering that my cold bath attempt was pretty lame, the cold plunge pool more than made up for it. I soaked up to my neck for about 15 min. it was pretty cold! I will try to do 30 min after breakfast tomorrow.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-11T04:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-13597>

> **PaleoDentist;12969 wrote:** Well, i made a discovery at the hotel today. they have a58F cold plunge pool. so after today's full body workout I took advantage of it! considering that my cold bath attempt was pretty lame, the cold plunge pool more than made up for it. I soaked up to my neck for about 15 min. it was pretty cold! I will try to do 30 min after breakfast tomorrow.

YAY! That's great. although I'm so jealous you're in Jamaica and we're in cold old Wyoming. sigh. But our tap water is better ... about 43 straight outta the pipe.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-11T23:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-13899>

I've been going to the cold plunge bath every day so far, sometimes twice (although for a brief splash the second time as I do not want to over do it)

Today was a relatively comfortable 30 min soak. I'm going to miss it for sure. leaving tomorrow.

will continue the bathtub at home.

I am seriously considering going in the ocean soon. It occurred to me when Jack mentioned that he wished he lived next to an ocean for free CT. I live 5 min form the Pacific ocean! which I have taken advantage of for year round kayaking, but now may just jump in!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-11T23:25:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-13900>

I've been eating lots of lox for breakfast every morning. I think I will add more seafood to may weekly meals.

Will start curing my own lox form wild salmon when I get home.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-14T00:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-14891>

first day back. I did not have time for breakfast, as I overslept and rushed to work. got back and filled up my late tub. it was at 59F I added a bowl of ice and got it down to 58F. sat in it for 35 min submerged to chest. as the ice cube sulfated around me and melted I began to shiver in last 5 min. drank my ice water and now shivering in my robe. I never shivered before. this is my first CT without a high fat meal. i wonder if there is a relationship?

### nonchalant — 2012-04-14T00:39:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-14894>

Hi Paleodentist, yes you need to eat high fat to power all that thermogenesis.

But also, each CT session is different.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-14T00:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-14898>

I was hoping my fat reserves would do that! still shivering. so weird.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-15T19:27:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-15333>

Well, my reserves are diminishing, even with all the protein and fat for breakfast. Love it!!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-05-06T17:58:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-21771>

I have been averaging 5 days a week CT in 58F for 30 min so far. Diet has been slipping of late due to night time snacking and getting into chocolate and fruits. (my wife and son are not following my eating plan , so there are many temptations around the house and I am weak!). My pants have been feeling a bit snug lately, so I and clamping down as renewing my comment to being strict.

Its been 5 weeks of CT batch for me. wow seems longer!

### donkjellberg — 2012-05-06T19:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plaeodentists-journey.431/post-21785>

Are you getting enough sleep?

# Thread: will  sweat like crazy when its hot
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cowboy — 2012-03-25T17:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-5808>

Been doing the CT since Dr. K blogged it a few weeks back. Face dunkings graduated to one or two gel packs once a day to five gel packs three times a day, and luke warm showers graduating to up to three minutes of 50 degree water. Next is a large stock tank for the back yard for the full immersions.

I assume that my internal flame willing I will achieve cold adaptation.

My BIG QUESTION/WORRY is that one this occurs and the summer temps hit 95 degrees I will be sweating like a pig.

I've lost 60 pounds with vlc/paloe eating over the last year and a half. Started the LR in November and to be honest haven't had great results. Loosing 60 pounds is like taking off a warm jacket. Heat doesn't bother me nearly as much, but I am concerned about cold adaptation and then always feeling warm and always seating.

Is anyone far enough along to know what happens with cold adaptation and hot weather.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-25T18:11:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-5834>

There have been a few who have asked about this and the response is that once your body is cold adapted it will also function better in warmer temps and you will not have to worry about overheating.

the biggest problem with looking for guidance regarding summer temps is that few people outside of Dr K and his patients have gone through the process over a full year. the rest of us are just catching up

### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T06:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-6242>

In summer, you should be better at regulating your body-temp thanks to CT. So you should be feeling comfortable rather than insanely hot.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-26T22:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-6556>

I think I have most of the signals for LS with the exception of not sweating. How does your sweating change when you become LS? All my life I have never sweat much. Of course, I never was a big water drinker either, now I drink at least two quarts a day of iced water along with my morning coffee and afternoon mint tea and water to take my supplements with.

### Vicster — 2012-03-26T23:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-sweat-like-crazy-when-its-hot.432/post-6577>

> **Glamorama;6277 wrote:** In summer, you should be better at regulating your body-temp thanks to CT. So you should be feeling comfortable rather than insanely hot.

I do hope this is true! I'm in South Texas and I usually sweat profusely in the heat and humidity of our summers.

# Thread: CT while having a cold?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdBren — 2012-03-25T18:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/post-5843>

Is it okay to take cold baths with a cold?

### Adria — 2012-03-25T18:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/post-5848>

For a mild cold ok but don't overdo it. If you're have fever, coughing, etc I would skip for a few days, maybe just do ice packs instead. I seemed to get a slight cold a few wks into ct and just kept going, the cold went away and didn't get any worse.

### agatha — 2012-03-26T18:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-while-having-a-cold.433/post-6427>

I got a streaming cold in the second week and I backed off a little - only did the baths if i felt like it. I wasn't sure if it was a real infection or just a detox but either way I figured my body was struggling to cope and I should probably listen to my instincts. I think it was probably detox as I felt well otherwise.

# Thread: Some people must already be adapted...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BenG — 2012-03-25T19:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/post-5905>

Funny story, I’m in the tub on Friday doing a CT session. My wife (after giving me dumb look about CT for over a week), says,"I’ll get in, too" (no real adaptation/training). I tried to warn her she wasn’t ready;--she should gradually adapt.. she didn’t care. So she plops in the tub and gives me a dumb look — this is easy. I kept asking her, if she was cold, etc as the tub was filling up…"no. it’s fine"…45 minutes later...she acted as if she did this forever. Of course she decides she needs bubbles so she fills the 50-55 degree tub with soap and bubble bath.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-25T20:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/post-5908>

My DH is this way. Went straight to full cold showers, can roll around in the snow and will jump in lakes/pools and break the ice.

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-22T22:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-people-must-already-be-adapted.435/post-17863>

I got in the tub without a lot of hullabaloo either. I wonder if I need to up the antiin order to get benefit from it. I lost about 50lbs just eating low carb Paleo year. Been plateuaued ever since doing LR and CT as of a week ago to see if I can push through it. I always took cold showers and slept with it really cold/no covers though. Especially when I was pregnant last year. Hmmm...

# Thread: CT for Athletes - Results / Experiences
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-25T20:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-5931>

Is there anyone out there who started CT while they were a competitive / very active athlete? I'm curious to find out what you did, what improvements you realized, and how long it took to experience the improvements. I'm contemplating the idea of buying a rubbermaid stock tank, but would like see some more experiential evidence, or studies (yes, I've searched google scholar, etc.).

For most everyone else out there ... I think it's great that you're making progress with the protocols. I've read where working out can be bad and I accept this. I'm all for the non athlete to be eating primal and CT their way to lots of weight loss and an occasional pullup. For perspective, I'm interested in responses regarding people who can do things like deadlift over 400# and can run a 5K in less than 24 minutes.

Thanks,

Snuffy

### anonymouse — 2012-03-26T16:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-6366>

I'm not quite where you want me to be, but the 5k is easy (~22 minutes) and here are my stats:

BW: 165

DL: 325x6

Sq: 315x2

B: 230x1

OHP: 155x1

I've been paleo for about 3 months and been CT'ing for about 2 weeks. Haven't seen anything significant from CT yet, but it probably hasn't kicked in. Low carb has brought me down from 176lb while maintaining strength (added on OHP and DL, lost a bit on squat and bench).

I'll keep you posted. Will probably be a month before you see me on here again.

### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-27T00:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-6640>

anon...,

Thanks for the reply ... I started the nightly ice bath yesterday ... will see what happens!

### Bill Strahan — 2012-03-27T19:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7134>

44 year old male. 5'10" 195 pounds. 435 deadlift. My last 5k run was 24:17 so I guess I don't qualify, but I'm awful at running. I can row it on a Concept II in 20:10. Does that count?  90 pound weighted pullups, bench 275, strict press 185, squat 345.

In any case, I used to do ice baths after workouts. Fell out of the practice until about 10 days ago, and I read CT-6. Hmmm. Got me thinking to previous experiences. So I got a 100 gallon rubbermaid livestock trough, and have worked up to a non-stop 45 minutes submerged from ankles to my neck. I put a block under one end of the trough to hold it an angle, so my feet are just out of the water on the end that is propped up but the water comes all the way up to my neck on the other side. Usually keep my arms in, and sometimes my hands. Finish with the last two minutes holding my breath totally underwater.

I haven't been doing single rep max lifts recently, but I've tried to get dead-hang muscleups for a long time. Suddenly yesterday I noticed I could pull myself several inches higher on the rings. Was THIS (imagine my fingers held apart about 1/4") close to getting on top of the rings without a kip.

That is a sudden and recent change. I'll keep doing this and see what happens. I've been doing CrossFit since 2008, and the PRs are harder to come by. I track everything pretty detailed, so I'll notice if I suddenly start being capable of things I previously wasn't. FWIW in late 2007 I was over 240 pounds, couldn't to 1 pullup, could get about 3 pushups, and I was a tub of lard. Started bodyweight work, then found CrossFit, then started Zone diet, then switched to Paleo...that took about 1 year to progress through all that. I've gotten stronger and faster more or less continuously (barring a weird non-CF injury) for the last 4 years.

### SnuffySmith — 2012-03-27T22:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7349>

Bill,

That's good stuff! Thanks for sharing. I was looking at the rubbermaid troughs and wasn't sure if I'd need a 100 or 150 gallon tub! Guess I'll go with the 100 when I get to it!

Can't wait to hear more about your results.

BTW, what's your skin temp at the end? The lowest I've been so far is 68.

Thanks!

Snuffy

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-28T02:24:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7449>

I am going to look into the 100 gal trough for home and my gym. I have been doing daily cold baths in the evenings, abt 3 weeks now. About 45-60 min. I powerlift, and before I started CT my squat was 220, paused bench was 130, deadlift was 260. I am now up to 230 squat, 140 bench, 285 DL. That's pretty damn good! Considering it took about 6 weeks to go from 215 to 220 on the squat. I am 40 yo female.

I have a meet in 3 weeks, so now I am just soaking in the cold. After the meet, I will start doing some spot icing during training.

Actually, this past Friday, I did a little experimenting. I pulled 2 singles 245, 265, this was after heavy squat singles. My upper back felt weary, I know this feeling, and I had 1 more lift, 285. I absolutely know that if I didn't do what I did next, I wouldn't have been able to make that 285. So my upper back felt weary, and hot. I took 2 icy cold water bottles out of the little gym fridge, put them on the floor side by side and place my upper back on them so that they were on either side of my spine, in the mid thoracic area, where I tend to round when fatigued. I stayed on them for 4-5 minutes, the area was numb. I got up, chalked up, approached the bar and boom, flat back easy pull. Amazing. Deadlift has always been my "iffy" lift, so this was pretty exciting.

### Bill Strahan — 2012-03-28T03:10:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-athletes-results-experiences.436/post-7466>

No idea on skin temp. I think you'll waste a lot of ice if you get the 150 gallon tub. I got the 100, and tilt it as I described to minimize the total volume of water I have to get down to 45-50 degrees.

I'm contemplating making a wood wedge from plywood, partially filling it with sand (so it wont' float), sealing it, and putting it on the end my legs are on to displace water that isn't doing any good. I'm probably using 60 of the 100 gallon capacity, but most of that 60 gallons isn't touching my skin. Anything I could do to get the tub shape closer to the shape of my body when immersed would reduce the amount of ice I have to make.

I got some weird looks at the tractor supply laying down inside the tubs to see which to get.

I bought some plastic containers that are a little bigger than shoe boxes at Target. Each one will hold about 1.25 gallons with room to expand when it turns into ice, so I use the freezer in my hanger to freeze four of those at a time. that gets me about 40 pounds of ice. If I think I'm benefitting from this stuff, I'll look at making an refrigerated tub. Make a custom tub from foam and fiberglass, and run a coil of tubing into a small refrigerator to circulate the water...we'll see

# Thread: Youthfuliz's joyful life as a cavewoman :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 90
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T02:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6151>

Well, welcome all. I'm going into a cold shower so I'll keep this short.

I'm in my 30's, hoping to stay young and look like a 25 year old for as long as possible. I started to eat Paleo in the middle of 2011. Went strict Paleo in January of 2012, and have stayed in ketosis most of the time. I'm eating meat/seafood/leafy vegetables/99 percent chocolate/coconut oil/manna/butter. Lots of green tea and some coffee.

Not exercising much, but have started walking to school and back somedays now that the Spring is here. I'll pick up more sculpt/hiit in a month or so. I started leptin reset on January 26th. Now I go to sleep at 11pm, sometimes a bit earlier than that, and usually sleep to 6:30am, and I sleep very well. I dim the lights in the evening and enjoy a cold shower/bath at least every other day. My skin is becoming softer, clearer, and more elastic/youthful looking. Hair is smoother, healthier and feel silky through my fingers. I can't stop touching it!  I love touching myself all over my body too. haha. Love the cold shower. And I have been wearing less clothes and turning the room temperature down to 65F during the day and 62F at night. I cover myself with thinner blanket at night too and feel warm when I wake up.

Today's weight is 151. Started in January at 153. I have lost more inches than pounds and my legs are getting firmer. My goal weight is 138 by end of May, and maybe 132 by end of August. Or whenever I have a 26" waist. I don't really care about the weight, I just want to be lean and strong. Right now my waist is 29".

OK now I go to my cold shower.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T04:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6184>

32 minutes of cold shower. Started at 72F, down to 61F. Cold feels good! I realized that I sing louder when it gets colder.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T18:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6430>

150 pounds this morning.  Forgot to mention, I'm 5'5", medium frame.

### youthfuliz — 2012-03-26T19:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-6449>

A little more history. I have always been a curvy gal - hourglass shape, about 20~30 pounds above my ideal weight. I was in the lower 130s in my 20's and felt and looked great. I'd like to go back to that body.  My highest weight was in the 160's. I tried to lose weight by eating less and exercising more. In 2007 I dropped about 20 pounds and got to 138. When I stopped exercising I slowly gained back. In 2011 I started to try again, by eating lowcarb and exercising. I found the Paleo community and started to live the Paleo way. I gave in to carb cravings from time to time, and got back to ketogenic Paleo each time within shorter time. And I really don't enjoy the "mistakes" as much as I did before. They are just the old comforts that don't really work any more, but still flash a quick flirt sometimes. After reading the CT series, and especially the part on no safe starches in the winter (or CT process) it become easier to say no to junk food. I make my own legal dessert now  home made chocolate from 2 parts of coconut manna plus 1 part of 99 percent chocolate. I have three pieces before breakfast, and two pieces before dinner.

In 2011 my weight fluctuated between 150 to 162. I have been below 153 for almost all the days in 2012, below 150 for more than half of the time. I think I have been eating too much coconut manna/chocolate in Feb and March - almost forgot that they've got calories too!  Now I eat them slowly, and try not to exceed 6 pieces a day - 10g per piece.

And no snacking has been almost natural as soon as I started leptin reset. Not really feeling hunger any more. I almost always skip lunch, but usually still eat dinner. Although I think I won't feel bad even if I don't. So I usually have a light dinner. CT about 5 times a week. Not using very cold water yet, but pretty cold for me.  One day I'll just use the cold tap water which is 48F now.

OK, this is about it. My goal for the coming month is to stay in ketosis, eat meat/seafood/my home made coconut chocolate/butter/leafy greens that're in season. And keep doing CT. I'll do more walking, but won't add in HIIT/weights until later.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-01T20:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-9883>

April's plan:

1\. CT at least 4 times a week.

2\. Food: BAB, Dinner: eggs or 3 pieces of my homemade dessert (coconut manna + 99 percent chocolate)

3\. Walk to school whenever the weather allows

Todays's CT:

10 minutes of cool cleaning shower, followed by 30 minutes of cold bath (70F down to 63F), followed by a quick cold shower.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-01T20:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-9886>

**April's weight loss goal:**

Down to 142 pounds. No more ups and downs, it will be down down down all the way.

Eat 100% ketogenic paleo, light dinner. I don't really feel hungery any more. Eating is more of a fun activity. haha. I'll find other ways to keep myself entertained.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-03T17:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-10790>

Today's CT:

35 minutes total. Cold bath started at 68F, down to 66F. Followed by a quick cold shower. Played Barry Manilow's Summer of 78. It eneded exactly when I finished... 38 minutes total. I'll use it to time my CT for now.

I was feeling cold before CT, sneezed quite a few times - so I had to talk myself into the cold bath and kept the water not too cold for me, although it still felt cold and I even shivered a bit, which hardly happened before - but I didn't sneeze in the bath, and only started to sneeze again after I put the bathrobe on!  Kinda funny.

Next CT is Thursday. After breakfast.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-04T06:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-11150>

Will travel out of town this Thursday so probably no CT until Saturday. I'll do cool showers daily though, just not cold. Cool showers do wake me up.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-08T16:24:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-12645>

Finally back and can continue with my cold bath.

40 minutes today. 66F bath followed by a quick cold shower. Feel good. Next one will be Tuesday. I'll keep the temperature at 64F - 66F this week, 40 minutes each time, 4 times (S, T, Th, S) and drop the water temperature to 62F - 64F next week.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-09T03:16:06+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-10T19:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-13413>

Sure thing Doc. Thanks for providing us with this "cave home"!

Today's CT:

35 minutes bath. Started with 66F water, down to 63F. Spend half time immersed up to my shoulder, the other half sitting up with water up to my stomach. Finished up with a quick cold shower. I loved it when the cold shower fell on my face. Almost like a "Finally!" moment - anticipated peak moment realized.

My skin was pink when I got out of the bath. I followed what others have done - I warmed up naturally by staying naked for a little while  and it worked! Within minutes my skin returned to normal color, my body didn't feel cold without clothes, only my hands and feet still feel cold. So this is what I will do in the future. Warm up naturally.

Next one will be on Thursday.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-13T02:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-14507>

Today's CT:

After dinner. 35 mintues total, started with 65F bath, down to 62F. Finished with a quick cold shower... 57F! I didn't realized that it was so cold. It got me shiver a bit. My body warmed up quickly, within 10 minutes after I got out. My feet and hands are still cold and stiff, while I type this now, 15 minutes later.

But I loved it. 65F was easy to start with and although it felt cold when I got in, I felt fine within minutes. I had a full body immersion, up to my neck for more than half of the time.

I may do CT again on Friday. See if I want do it every day during the weekend. I think it's getting easier.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-14T20:09:31+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-19T14:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-16546>

Today's CT:

40 minutes of cold shower, started at 72F, down to 65F. Took just a few minutes to get comfortable in the cold, then I turned down the temperature to 70F, 68F, then 65F. At the end 65F wasn't too cold and I felt glad to have the cold water hitting on my "warm" body. I thought that there must be oxytocin (or some other happy hormone) being released, and I see why cold showers can be addictive.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-20T05:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-16907>

Wearing my blueblocking sunglasses now... and almost feel it's dumb to be up at this moment. I see myself going to sleep at 10pm soon.  I felt so hot today. It was only 65F but felt like summer. I was in t-shirt and my feet were on fire the whole day. I wished I could walk barefoot in office. I had to take a quick cold shower to cool myself down when I got home. Tomorrow I'll be in skirt and sandals. I start to wonder how hot it will get this summer.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T03:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-17620>

Just finished today's CT:

52 minutes total. Cold bath followed by a quick cold shower. Started at 65F. Down to 59F. I was quite comfortable at 62F. Then 60F was fine too. It was the last 5 minutes at 59F got me shivering. But I wanted to feel "cold" so I did. After I towel dried myself and sat my naked butt on my leather chair, the chair felt warm! I walked barefoot and the floor felt warm!  Mind you, the room temperature is just 68F. Now I need to do some exercise so my feet warm up enough for me to fall asleep tonight!

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T04:00:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-17623>

Hello Doc. Right now, after 7 minutes out of the bath, I feel fine. My body feels warm, hands and feet still cold, but I have stopped shivering... or almost stopped.  I'm glad that I tried 59F tonight, that means next time 60F is gonna be considered "easy".

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-22T15:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-17695>

I did some leg lifts with ankle weights last night and got my feet "not cold" in a matter of minutes. They were not warm, but normal. Went bed at 11pm and although my legs/feet still felt cool to touch (my hands warmed up obviously) I had no problem feeling cozy and fell asleep. Woke up around 7:15am. I like to have 8 hours' sleep. Maybe I'll go to bed around 10:30pm and see if I wake up earlier.

I think my appetite is decreased lately. A few days ago I was in a hurry so just ate three extra large eggs cooked in co for breakfast. I didn't feel hungry until 4pm. I eat my dinner early, like 5pm, often and don't feel hungry before bed. I think I'll try to eat less - I'll keep the BAB, but eat less for dinner. Maybe just eggs instead of steak. Will see how this goes.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-23T17:32:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-18075>

Today's CT:

47 minutes cold bath followed by cold shower. Started at 64F, finished at 62F. Thought of going to 60F but somehow 62F was cold enough for me today so left it like that. Washed my hair in 62F shower... that was pretty cool.  To keep the shower the same temperature as the bath was a bit challenging but I guess it's better to end on a high note(or low temperature).

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-24T05:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-18272>

Thinking of doing CT tomorrow after breakfast.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-24T13:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-18316>

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-25T04:39:00+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-27T02:18:57+0300

Well, it feels much colder when I got out of the bath. Haven't cursed so loudly for a while.  Now about 15 minutes later, my body has warmed up a bit, but my hands and feets still are cold... so I guess I need to workout a bit to warm them up. That is, as soon as I stop shaking.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-27T22:54:32+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-28T19:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-19596>

Today's CT:

46 minutes cold bath started at 62F, down to 59F. It was pretty easy (and warm!) until I dropped the water temperature to 59F. My skin was not even "dark" until then. I was moving my arms and legs to get the water circulating so that I can feel the coldness.  Shivering just a little bit at the end.

I'm planning to do CT eveyday. And may do a little weight training at night two hours before bed. My muscles are already larger without me doing anything. So why not test them out.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-29T03:18:39+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-29T18:00:55+0300

65 minutes total. Cold bath started at 61F, down to 56F. When it got to 58F I started to feel "cold and good", so I tried 56F for a second time. It didn't feel much different from 58F. And my body has already warmed up, except for my hands and feet.

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-30T19:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-20117>

I asked about the increased hunger here:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1248-CT-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1248-CT-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass)

For now I think it should be due to estrogen, since I have seen smoother, more elastic skin, smoother hair, even fuller and firmer "girls"... which should all came from the same estrogen that made me hungry in the last few days. So here is the plan:

I'll eat 65g protein for breakfast. Continue to CT daily, but do not push for colder temperature this week. Let it stay in the range of 62F - 56F. Try 60F - 54F next week. Prepare to eat lunch and dinner. If I still feel hungry at night, the only thing allowed after 7pm is fried eggs in coconut oil.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-03T00:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-20847>

Posted this in another thread. [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?524-How-was-your-CT-today&p=20920#post20920](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?524-How-was-your-CT-today&p=20920#post20920)

> **Pamela;20865 wrote:** I've stepped up my next gradient to go for 1 hour soaks 4 times a week in about 55 degree water. Getting in and adapted has been much easier this past week, which is week 5 for me. I really feel ready for my soak when the day comes, I can feel it in my legs. They feel kind of swollen and then I do the soak and they feel good again. I'm working on getting my hormones fixed, will be doing blood this week for Dzugan program. Weight is staying steady while I eat a good keto paleolithic diet and enjoy all my fats. My next plan of action is to get the water a little colder, will probably have to go buy a bag of ice each day for that.

My heart beats faster when I prepare my cold bath in the morning.  I would fill the tub when I finish eating my BAB, check the water temperature, walk in the tub and let my feet feel it, then go away for 10 minutes - surf the internet, take my vitamins, fish oil, etc, drink my iced water... then wash my face in cold water, then go in the tub. This "ritual" is kinda like foreplay  that prepares me to greet the cold water with grace.

I don't multitask in the tub, I focus on the bath and make the most out of it. My arms and legs are always moving to circulate the water - it feels great when water moves against my body, almost feel like being massaged. Sometimes I use a washcloth to bring water onto my chest which doesn't always get under the water, or use the washcloth to massage my arms and legs - I figure as long as I'm in the tub let's do some scrubbing  And I bring water to my face too. It feels great to cool down my hot face with cold water. At the end I take a quick cold shower to wash off and let water drop on my face. Wonderful.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-03T18:25:57+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-14T21:47:57+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-15T00:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-24043>

This TOM is almost over. I feel great. Minimal cramps, no tiredness, excellent mood  I wish I had known CT since my teenage years, so many petty fights could have been spared!  My mood does not go on a rollercoaster any more... Now I understand how men can be so consistent! Haha I can be that way too!

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-26T19:48:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27676>

Having a 24 hour fast today - dinner to dinner. plan to do 2 ct sessions in the summer - one right after getting up (or after breakfast) since the tap water is colder then, one after dinner. today's morning one: about 30 minutes, 55F. Hardly shivered... i miss the shivering, haha.  it's the first time i ct on an empty stomach... it wasn't difficult at all, i was so eager to get in that i forgot to time myself, or play the cd  and i ended it early because i kinda want to keep myself wanting for more... the evening one.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-26T22:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27713>

Just curious...why CTing 2X a day?

Is your tap water 55F? Lucky you!

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-26T23:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27715>

> **indigogirl;27811 wrote:** Just curious...why CTing 2X a day?
>
> Is your tap water 55F? Lucky you!

I feel unsatisfied since the tap water is warming up. It was 52F forever in the last three months and I have been slowly lowering the temperature of my bath... now I'm very comfortable with 55F, and can't get it colder since it's warming up. I really crave colder water.  I don't want to use ice since it's summer so I guess I can at least increase the time. I almost always only took cold bath in the morning since I need my feet to be warm to fall asleep. My recovery is very fast lately so I can take a cold bath in the evening and still have no problem falling asleep. I also hope the longer ct per day may speed up my weight loss a little bit.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-27T05:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27796>

2nd cold bath today: 25 minutes, 56F. It was cool and nice...

### indigogirl — 2012-05-27T06:53:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27803>

Thanks for the answer about the 2 CT baths a day.

My tap water is 83F!

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-27T18:29:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27857>

> **indigogirl;27901 wrote:** Thanks for the answer about the 2 CT baths a day.
>
> My tap water is 83F!

Oh that's bad... I'm moving to PA this summer and it's about 3 hours' driving to the south... I hope the tap water doesn't get too warm in the summer there! Sure loved the beautiful flowers they had when I visited in April!

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-28T04:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-27967>

today's ct: 28 minutes in 55.5F cold bath. It felt better than 56F and my body can tell the difference!

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-29T01:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-28091>

Having a lazy day here... took a nap in the afternoon, reading, making my coconut-chocolate, thinking of skipping dinner since I'm not hungry at all. I did CT in the evening during the last two days... although the warmup was quick, my feet were cold when I got to bed. I don't think I like that... so I'll probably stick with ct in the morning. That means no ct today, but I look forward to my morning one tomorrow.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-29T22:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-28229>

Had a short ct session this morning - 20 minutes in 56F bath. I don't feel cold in 56F any more. Pouring water on my chin, neck, chest while lying in the tub was my new trick, it felt wondeful! Will go back for another session this evening.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-30T04:46:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-28313>

Had my 2nd CT bath today... 20 minutes, 56F. Feeling good.

I notice hair loss lately... not very serious but quite obvious. Not sure why, but I hope it's just old hair falling out to provide opportunity for new hair to grow.  I have really thick hair anyway.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-31T04:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-28487>

today's ct: 30 minutes cold bath at 58F... well let's still call it CT... although it was just cool, not cold. Followed with a cold shower... loved it.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-01T05:06:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-28807>

Just a quick cold shower after dinner to cool myself down. It's getting too warm here. I'm gonna take lots of cold showers this summer...

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-02T05:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-29001>

Well I spot iced today... using ziplock bags filled with ice cubes and tap water  It was cold!

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-04T03:29:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-29298>

today's ct: 30 minutes in 58F cold bath, followed by a cold shower. I don't shiver any more, walking into the cold bath is the easiest thing in the world... it was cool, but not cold. I enjoyed the cold shower afterward... I didn't shiver under the shower either. Showering with just cold tap water... such a healthy thing to do. And I do it naturally now.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-05T23:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-29613>

Started to add in exercise today: 40 minutes walking with intermittent sprints. Plan to do CT before bed tonight.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-06T00:32:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-29623>

Did my ct right after the exercise instead: 20 minutes, 58F bath.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-07T00:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-29794>

TOM is here. Feeling completely fine... energetic even, and in a good mood.

Walked 3 miles, had a cold shower after dinner. Have a lot to do lately so I probably won't do CT bath every day, but will at least take cold showers. Appetite is under control... the full signal still comes about 10 minutes after I start eating... so I only eat about 2/3 of what I prepared. Will add in a cold bath whenever I have the time.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-25T00:47:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-33367>

Back to CT bath after two weeks' break. Tap water comes out at 62F. 20 minutes of bath, followed by a shower. Feeling completely normal in the bath and shower.

I'll take a cold shower first thing in the morning, and soak in cold bath 3 times a week in the next a few weeks.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-25T01:47:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-33375>

**OK, I'm going to challenge myself. I have a goal weight for 12/30/12... 132 pounds.**

I actually don't remember when I was 132 before, this may be too thin for me... well then if I get into all my skinny jeans (size 4 being the smallest) I will be satisfied too. I guess that means I should have a waist of 27"? Or if I see my abs...that'll be nice too. 6 months from now, to lose 20+ pounds. I have plenty of time. I'll make sure that I make progress every month. I'll reach my goal by doing the following:

1\. Cold shower first thing every morning

2\. Cold bath at least 3 times a week

3\. Eat ketogenic paleo

4\. Eat seafood at least 5 times a week

5\. Go to bed at 10pm ready to have the best recovering sleep

6\. Weigh-in and take measurements once a month and always make further plans at the end of the month

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-27T01:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-33795>

Today's CT:

50 minutes in 62F bath. I used ice today, for the first time. About 11 pounds of ice I made with bottles of water frozen in freezer. Tap water came out at 63F, I got in when the water became 62F with the ice, and it stayed at 62F for 50 minutes. I poured iced water on my face, neck, back the whole time. Very nice. When I was done I wanted to take a cold shower as I usually do... the tap water felt warm!!! I let it run for a little bit and finally could take a cool shower. Loved it.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-30T17:06:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-34592>

Today's CT:

50 minutes, bath + shower. Water was 62F, down to 60F with 12 pounds of ice. I was glad to feel a bit chilly with the help of the ice.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-06T00:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-35723>

Today's CT:

50 minutes, cold bath + shower. 62F to 59F with the help of 15 pounds of ice. I miss winter.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-07T14:43:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-36070>

today's CT:

40 minutes, shower+bath. Used about 15 pounds of ice, water temperature 62F - 57F. My tummy was pink afterward.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-21T19:07:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-40016>

I've been very busy this month. Dealing with graduation, moving from NY to PA, and starting a new job. Good thing is I have tons of energy. Well I need to thank iodine for that.

Long story short, I found a lump on my neck on July 4th. Went to the school health center on the 5th, did blood test and ultrasound. Before getting the results I did a lot of online search - I read "most thyroid problems are due to iodine deficiency" and watched video presentation by Dr. Brownstein and other iodine doctors - and started to supplement iodine right away. Seaweeds, brazil nuts, oysters, potassium iodide (in liquid form I found in local store) and I ordered iodoral, selenium and other cofactors online. I supplement about 50mg iodine+iodide starting from July 7th. The lump shrunk within the first day of iodine supplementation. When I saw my thyroid reacted so quickly to iodine I knew I was doing the right thing. On July 10th the results came back, normal thyroid hormones, ultrasound shows two nodules most likely benign. The doctor said no need for any treatment and he doesn't think iodine may help.  My lump had shrunk by 50% by then but that didn't interest the doctor. I asked him to give me an iodine deficiency test, he sadi there was no need. Iodine deficiency is history in the America. I asked him to give me another ultrasound, I can show him that my nodules have shrunk in a week due to iodine supplementation... he didn't want to do it. Most CW doctors are not trained to understand how iodine deficiency can lead to problems. I just need to find me an iodine literate doctor next time. I continue taking 50mg iodoral daily.

So it has been two weeks. I stopped checking the lump first thing in the morning... it's getting smaller but the change is not as obvious as when I first increased the iodine level in my body. I think my breast like the additional iodine too... they look and feel happy.  I have tons of energy as I mentioned earlier, have taken care of so many things this week, have been very resourceful and have good judgment... just super productive. I like it.

The rest of July and August will be very busy too. But I look forward to starting a new chapter of my life. Will continue to take 50mg of iodoral with the cofactors.

I talked about the thyroid issue here:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2417-Thyroid-Nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2417-Thyroid-Nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in)!

Here is our iodine thread:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread)!!!

There is also a MDA iodine thread that I gathered lots of information from.

### youthfuliz — 2012-11-11T21:13:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-63675>

OK I'm back after a really busy three months... Graduated, Moved, Started a new job, finally my life goes back to normal (kinda).

I have been sleeping early (11pm the latest, and often 10pm) a lot, and have been avoiding artificial lights at night. Although I'm not perfect, I'm so much better than 1 year ago. Feel sleepy every night around 10pm and sleep well through the night to 7am.

I've been eating lots of fish/shellfish lately too. I think it's helping my hormones (observation from my last mentrual cycle). Lots of mushrooms and cilantro. Haven't been able to lose weight consistently which I blame on the high-carb food I had once awhile and having very little physical activities lately - I've been tied to my desk with work. Now that work is pretty much under control I'll have time for more walking, maybe some HIIT and I'll focus on my eating - I'll eat a big breakfast (which I have been doing), and a light/late lunch. Will not eat anything after 6pm. Go to bed at 10pm most nights.

### youthfuliz — 2012-11-11T21:54:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-63680>

Forgot to update on the thyroid/iodine front: I've been taking 100mg/day in the last month or so - I take twice a day, 50mg each, and sometimes I forget my 2nd portion, sometimes I forget for the whole day.... but overall I think I take 500mg/week on average.

Thyroid nodule is much smaller though not completely gone. I haven't been to a doctor again since I'm too busy at work and I'm glad that iodine/and lots of seafood are working at reducing it. Good energy level although it can be better. Sleep well, and also my constipation problem is solved all by itself... so things are going toward the right direction.

### Destiny — 2012-11-14T15:12:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-64233>

> **youthfuliz;65579 wrote:** OK I'm back after a really busy three months... Graduated, Moved, Started a new job, finally my life goes back to normal (kinda).
>
>
>
> I have been sleeping early (11pm the latest, and often 10pm) a lot, and have been avoiding artificial lights at night. Although I'm not perfect, I'm so much better than 1 year ago. Feel sleepy every night around 10pm and sleep well through the night to 7am.
>
>
>
> I've been eating lots of fish/shellfish lately too. I think it's helping my hormones (observation from my last mentrual cycle). Lots of mushrooms and cilantro. Haven't been able to lose weight consistently which I blame on the high-carb food I had once awhile and having very little physical activities lately - I've been tied to my desk with work. Now that work is pretty much under control I'll have time for more walking, maybe some HIIT and I'll focus on my eating - I'll eat a big breakfast (which I have been doing), and a light/late lunch. Will not eat anything after 6pm. Go to bed at 10pm most nights.

You'll get there! Just keep going. I started with 152 lbs. I experienced a lot of aggravation in regards to losing weight. It was two step forward and one back for almost 9 months. I was stuck in the fat losing range of 7-11 lbs. I the past 4 months, things started to progress in the right direction weight wise. I am at 135 lbs now and I am aiming lower. Good luck.

### youthfuliz — 2012-11-18T16:17:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youthfulizs-joyful-life-as-a-cavewoman.440/post-65129>

It was two step forward and one back for almost 9 months. I was stuck in the fat losing range of 7-11 lbs. I the past 4 months, things started to progress in the right direction weight wise. # Thread: Are there any Ice princesses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-26T02:50:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6155>

Was thinking about the people who are doing the amazing human tricks are all men. Any leads on women that have gone full frost?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-26T03:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6178>

Dara Torres. She puts less time in the pools& weights etc now that she's older & she keeps getting faster we'll see her at the Olympics as the oldest female athlete & the only women's swimmer who's been to 6 Olympic games.

CT has to have played some role w/her comeback in 2008. I read she spent a max of 1:30 min/day in the pool. Normal training time for elite swimmers is 2hrs 2x/day

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-26T03:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6179>

There is a trainer on my FB list who is doing some big things but she wants to see how it goes longer term

### Birdy — 2012-03-26T04:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6191>

I probably was when I was younger. I am old enough to remember when pools weren't heated, and I was in them as much as I could, even in October if I could find one that was open. I didn't do super-human feats, but I was very thin and could run farther than any women I knew. When I started competitive swimming in a college heated pool, it probably wasn't much good, they keep it hot like a bathtub. I am working my way back towards ice princess. My goal is ocean swimming.

### agatha — 2012-03-26T18:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6424>

Oh yes, Birdy, ocean swimming was one of my big goals too - and I've done it twice in the last week after 3 weeks of cold baths at home. It's just wonderful. Wearing a hat and neoprene gloves really helped though i looked entirely bonkers.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-26T20:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6482>

This reminds me that my mother was unknowingly raising my sibs and me to be ice princesses. When we were toddlers, she would always have us take our daily naps outdoors, in a bassinet or playpen. All year round! In the winter, she would layer blankets on us and she swore that we were all very healthy, no ear infections, colds. She did this on recommendation from her Irish grandmother. ALso same g-ma recommended cold dips in the ocean, as a cure-all for anything.

I did this with my daughter, my husband thought I was crazy, she would fall asleep in her stroller when I would take her for a walk, I would leave it in the back yard, under the kitchen window, and let her sleep in the cold. I did that til she was 4. She had 1 ear infection. No colds.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-26T23:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6576>

> **Teenie Leek;6518 wrote:** This reminds me that my mother was unknowingly raising my sibs and me to be ice princesses. When we were toddlers, she would always have us take our daily naps outdoors, in a bassinet or playpen. All year round! In the winter, she would layer blankets on us and she swore that we were all very healthy, no ear infections, colds. She did this on recommendation from her Irish grandmother. ALso same g-ma recommended cold dips in the ocean, as a cure-all for anything.
>
>
>
> I did this with my daughter, my husband thought I was crazy, she would fall asleep in her stroller when I would take her for a walk, I would leave it in the back yard, under the kitchen window, and let her sleep in the cold. I did that til she was 4. She had 1 ear infection. No colds.

I love this!!

I did have a lot of ear infections (probably due to being gluten intolerant) but I remember spending a lot of time as a kid playing in the snow in wet jeans and not being bothered by it or swimming in cold lakes or the river next to my house. I really didn't get sick much.- esp. compared to my friends' kids who really don't eat junk food, yet get sick every 6 weeks. I hardly had colds or coughs. I asked my mom about my siblings and I and she said "You kids rarely got sick."

### Birdy — 2012-03-27T04:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-any-ice-princesses.441/post-6743>

My mom was ALWAYS trying to get me out of the cold. I remember playing in the snow for hours, and would only come in when I was soaked to the core and turning blue. Then I would get a change of clothes and go out again. I just loved it, and spent most of that time alone because none of my friends liked to be outdoors that much.

# Thread: Question about optimal ranges?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-optimal-ranges.442/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-03-26T02:57:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-optimal-ranges.442/post-6160>

So, I'm wondering about my labs...

I'm trying to drill down to what precisely is going on so I have a leg to stand on when I go see my naturopath. For my salivary cortisol and DHEAs (salivary) ranges, is it OK if I'm within range or should I be aiming at a certain part of the range? Also, can someone explain to me why I have high nighttime cortisol (everything else is "within range" although noon and evening are at the lower end of "normal"--see question above, LOL) and I have never ever ever had the tiniest problem falling asleep or staying asleep. In fact, often I fall asleep on the couch-sometimes with half a mug of coffee in hand!

Really curious here and I want to make sure that the lab result is not in error. Its hard when the lab says your nighttime cortisol is way out of range but you have no symptoms that would back that up.

### Owl — 2012-03-26T05:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-optimal-ranges.442/post-6227>

Here's a good site for optimal lab ranges: [http://www.drkaslow.com/html/lab\_findings.html](http://http://www.drkaslow.com/html/lab_findings.html)

I don't know if he has DHEA, but you want to be at the top of the range on that.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-03-27T20:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-optimal-ranges.442/post-7259>

I have to say--I just got my hand on "Stop the Thyroid Madness". What an amazingly helpful book, including optimal ranges as well as really helping me to see what is going on. Adrenals, thyroid, hormones. So much clearer now. And I'm ready to make an appt with my naturopath, now that I feel better educated about my own (admittedly f-ed up) physiology!

### JanSz — 2012-03-30T17:49:46+0300

Estradiol (saliva) 0.6 pg/ml 1.3-3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)

cjco;3263 wrote:** Hormone Test 03/05/2012 Units Range

Estradiol (saliva) 0.6 pg/ml 1.3-3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Progesterone (saliva) 8 pg/ml 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)

Ratio: Pg/E2 (saliva) 13 Optimal: 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3 pg/ml

Testosterone (saliva) 31 pg/ml 16-55 (Age Dependent)

DHEAS (saliva) 6.8 ng/ml 2-23 (Age Dependent)

Cortisol Morning (saliva) 6.1 ng/ml 3.7-9.5

Cortisol Noon (saliva) 1.8 ng/ml 1.2-3.0

Cortisol Evening (saliva) 0.8 ng/ml 0.6-1.9

Cortisol Night (saliva) 2.4 ng/ml 0.4-1.0

Free T4 (blood spot) 1.6 ng/dL 0.7-2.5

Free T3 (blood spot) 2.5 pg/ml 2.5-6.5

TSH (blood spot) 0.6 uU/ml 0.5-3.0

TPO (blood spot) \* 15 IU/ml 0-150 (70-150 borderline)

Vitamin D, 25-OH, D2 < 4.0 ng/ml

# Thread: Cold Thermogenesis for dummies -
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-for-dummies.443/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### aug19 — 2012-03-26T03:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-for-dummies.443/post-6171>

Can anyone give me the break down of CT?

I want to add this to my ongoing LeptinRX/LeakyGut/AIPaleo protocol.

Plan on using my very deep garden tub.

I need to get a digital meat thermometer for temperature.

What temp water? (50's)

What temp skin?

Why wear Champion shirt and running pants?

I need to dunk my face in some too?

Any dangers?

Anything I need to watch out for?

Okay to do this daily?

I sit for 2-4 hours for work daily - I need to lose most in my butt so should I be sitting on ice packs?

Thanks for the advice.

Love this forum! I could read and read all day long. It's all I want to talk about with others but most aren't interested in learning. Want so bad to be successful to 1. be healthy and 2. encourage others to do some research

### marthabob2u — 2012-03-26T04:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-for-dummies.443/post-6199>

Start by face dunkng in cold water for as long as you can, then don't wait for the digital meat thermometer, just do it!

By all means if you are outside in a garden tub wear light clothing or the buzzards may circle!

What I have been doing is adding ice blocks to my 'cool' Mexican water and just soaking for as long as possible. I add ice packs to my lardy tummy and you could sit on a couple for sure. It is OK to get red skin but don't go beyond that. I often start with a washcloth between me and the ice block, then do skin contact for a while, keeping an eye on skin colour.

Do it as often as you can- I think every day is preferable. At first I didn't see much progress and then suddenly my waist dropped a couple of inches. It WORKS!

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-27T05:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vegan-men-better-in-bed.444/post-6777>

Here's her rebuttal. Don't read it if stuff like this gets your cortisol up, lol.

> They are obviously not very informed on hormones in their diets if they are worried about the phytoestrogens in soy and not all the excess hormones, including those produced by the lactating cows, in the animal flesh and bodily secretions they are consuming.
>
>
>
> According to wikipedia: Males
>
> A 2010 meta-analysis of fifteen placebo-controlled studies said that "neither soy foods nor isoflavone supplements alter measures of bioavailable testosterone concentrations in men."\[28\] Furthermore, Isoflavones supplementation has no effect on sperm concentration, count or motility, and show no changes in testicular or ejaculate volume.\[29\]\[30\]
>
>
>
> While this article from the Harvard archives talks about the dangerous levels of ANIMAL estrogens in dairy:
>
> [http://news.harvard.edu/gazette/2006/12.07/11-dairy.html](http://http://news.harvard.edu/gazette/2006/12.07/11-dairy.html)
>
>
>
> I just did a check on what, exactly, the paleo diet is. Seems like it omits dairy, which is great. Dairy is poison. That being said, when you consume animal flesh you are still consuming the hormones they produce, including estrogen. So if these paleo men think their diets are free from estrogens, they are sadly mistaken.
>
>
>
> In addition, not all vegans eat a lot of tofu. I eat almost none!
>
>
>
> Also, what I have found that affects my libido the most is the amount of vitamin D I am taking in. I'm not talking through food supplementation, but through actually being out in the sun. I have noticed a direct correlation between spending time in the sun without sunscreen below my face and my sex drive.
>
>
>
> As an aside, vegan gas isn't anywhere close to being rank like that of a meat eater!! =)
>
>
>
> They should check out the movie 'Forks Over Knives.' It explains the benefits of a plant based diet better than I ever could.
>
>
>
> Oh. Just to remind you. My point about vegans sexuality wasn't about libido, it was about impotence and how vegans, having healthier hearts and circulation due to the lack of cholesterol in our diets, don't have the issues with impotence that men who eat animal products do.
>
>
>
> Leave it to propaganda put out by the dairy industry to muddy the waters, though!!!

### GathererGatherer — 2012-04-02T21:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vegan-men-better-in-bed.444/post-10432>

Hi Souldanzer,

As an athletic vegan man for over half my life (I'm 31) with a high sex drive and bountiful libio, (and who often places first during burpees) I have to agree that science is on the side of your roommate and all the peanut-gallery here sound like a bunch of sexist bullies. Fructarians are vegan by default, as would be someone who eats air, but do not think that they represent any significant portion of those who consider themselves vegan. I am not at all spiritual and don't associate with Buddhists or anyone who aims to "transcend" this plane of existence (sex and all), and I believe that critics of vegan diets often associate and mistake what is popularly considered feminine (compassion, caring) with weakness. It is pretty offensive and sexist to say that empathy and compassion are weak. Protectors, defenders and heroes for those that are weaker than ourselves requires incredible strength both physical and that of character. Eating meat is simply a performance of power against the powerless.

It has been my experience, which of course is anecdotal, that most Paleo guys I've met are homophobic bullies who subscribe to romanticized stone-age gender roles - and it makes sense that vilifying anything involving estrogen is popular. How does one attempt to discredit veganism? By saying it causes men to have breasts and become women, which is a load of chauvinist BS. The mythology that if you eat muscle you become muscle is as silly as believe that eating tiger penis increases virility or eating brain increases intelligence. These are mythologies that flatter male identity but are not scientifically accurate, and there are many cultural rewards for reinforcing traditional male-dominance so these claims are often supported by groups who socially benefit from their percieved truthfulness.

As opportunistic omnivores, we can choose where we derive our nutrition. Of course you can be a healthy meat eater, but that doesn't mean that's the only way to be healthy. We are not carnivores.

The scientific truth is that "Dairy accounts for 60 percent to 80 percent of estrogens consumed"(1) and A 2010 meta-analysis of 15 placebo controlled studies showed that neither soy foods nor isoflavone supplements alter measures of bioavailable testosterone or estrogen concentrations in men.(2)

Check these vegans out:

# Thread: Hello from Sweden
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sweden.445/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FF_BC — 2012-03-26T12:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sweden.445/post-6282>

Hello everyone!

I'm a young man from Sweden, almost 22 years old

Height: 190cm

Starting weight (summer 2011): 109 kg

Present weight: 97 kg

Goal weight: Do not know

I've been low carb since mid-summer 2011, went from 109 kg to 102 kg in 3 months. Since about Januari I've been IF:ing (intermittent fasting) leangainsstyle while eating paleo/low carb, lost about another 7 kg in 3 months. The last month or so I've actually gained 1-2 kg partly because eating like a moron, though I suspect some muscle gain from squating and deadlifting.

Came across Dr Jack Kruse and CT while reading some blogs from paleopeople for about two weeks ago, read some of Dr JK:s blogpost and just could not stop thinking about CT for about a week. Ended up taking colder showers and dunking my face in cold water for a couple of days then thought: "wtf I'm Swedish, cold shouldn't be a proplem for a Viking like myself!". Did my first cold bath (not using ice, just the water from the tap at coldest setting) three days ago, the first 5 minutes I shivered like crazy and then it became oddly nice  Sat for 15 minutes my first session, 30 minutes the second and 35 minutes yesterday (third session).

Now I'm looking forward to my next cold bath this evening, aiming for 40 minutes! Also I'm very curious at the combination of CT and IF, someone else doing both IF and CT?

Have a nice day!

hejhej! fun to see more swedes

i started eating LCHF a year ago, and after a few months i hit a plateau so i started looking into ways of beaking it. cue Leptin, cue Kruse  and now, since that worked so nicely, i am testing CT as well. it's so exciting to be here and see him unveil everything live!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-03T17:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-sweden.445/post-10780>

Welcome to the road to optimal!

### FF_BC — 2012-04-03T19:48:45+0300
