I think her idea is a good one........for sure. And some CT too. In fact a lot of CT. Its cheap and there is nothing better for the human brain

### Ertarox — 2012-03-27T06:34:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-6832>

THANK YOU!

I love neurofeedback. It gives me hope. I don't know what kind she was doing but my psychologist was very trauma-competent, and learned neurofeedback when she did BECAUSE it does the trick. I cannot afford it is the problem. And my only hesitation also is if there is an oxygen-supply problem in the brain.....I feel that should be addressed before I invest in rewiring my brain. Because it's like sweeping the deck during a tornado. Why bother? Lack of oxygen to the brain is forever going to affect it. Fortunately, I should be able to rule that out soon. The GOAL is to move to Eugene Oregon shortly, where cost of living is cheap and healthy living is easier, and there is a neurofeedback center there that focuses in PTSD even. I love the idea of Brain State Conditioning and consulting with Gerdes.

When I know a BIT more neurologically and obviously have the money, I'll go for it. According to the 'psychic' that helped me immensely, whatever is going on with me is going to be studied profusely by neuroscientists. I think that's interesting if it happens, but I was more concerned with getting answers, and that is what she primarily helped me with. I know I'll be following a heavy neurotransmitter protocol, to say the least. And anti-candida.

And Dr. Kruse, thank you! What is CT?

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-28T17:36:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-7733>

Ertarox,

[EMDR therapy](http://http://www.emdr.com/general-information/what-is-emdr.html) is another treatment to look in to for PTSD.

"**Eye **Movement D**esensitization R**eprocessing" - recreates the processes that occur in the brain during REM sleep.

Theory is that the subconscious supresses memories that are too traumatic to deal with. This might explain your horrible sleep.

How often do you dream?

### ineedanLR — 2012-04-06T18:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-12175>

I'm curious regarding significance of inflammatory responses relative to your health.......clearly they are underrated with respect to their importance to our overall health.

Have you stopped sugar (all sugar especially high fructose), gluten/grains/rice, milk protein (whey), nightshade family foods (potato, cayenne etc)? Whether you're overweight or not, I would think that eliminating the foods that cause gross inflammatory responses in the body would be beneficial to you (and to at least 99 percent of the population).

In the coming years, I think the diet and the treatment of inflammatory related medical issues that are currently at epidemic proportions will undergo serious rewriting and that a revolution is in the making.

Regards,

Art

### Pamela — 2012-04-06T21:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-an-important-question-for-dr-kruse-a-friend-said-he-is-a-good-person-to-ask.457/post-12235>

> **Ertarox;6788 wrote:** Hi,
>
>
>
> This part fascinates me, too:
>
> Last September, I discovered that the xanax I was taking for sleep for the past 8 months (I finally resorted to scripts after averaging 4 hours sleep every 3 or 4 days, total), that I switched to due to the work injury, had accidentally become an addiction. The symptoms were disrupting my work injury symptoms, which is bad. I apparently was going through interdose withdrawal for months, not knowing it. I knew the risks but just cold-turkeyed it. I learned it was xanax addiction after accidentally withdrawing for about 48 hours once or twice before, then taking it. I could not believe alllll of that was xanax, I thought I was dying and you can die. I had paid attention, and I figured out fast that I needed to wear a neckbrace interestingly enough. Then the w/d went okay (as long as I stayed upright).
>
>
>
> Why this is INTERESTING is that(I since learned that) in xanax withdrawal, it blows out (or something) all your calcium chloride. I am not a doctor, but I observed, then learned then researched it, that any tiny injury will be blown up to grotesque proportions when going under xanax w/d for this reason. My meniscus tear was sensitive, but I needed a brace and was limping profusely at night, versus could walk damn near fine during the day, when not going through withdrawal. This is key to me because the TWO sources of major dysfunction were my knee, where there was a known meniscus tear, and my neck. I felt it at the top of my neck (brainstem) and I could not hold my head up. I took a cab to the E.R where a friend is a doctor just to get a neck brace. I wore it for a week. It disabled my knee and my neck, grossly blowing the problem out of proportion. But my brainstem has been a source of frustration my whole life. So I am oddly grateful for the experience because I KNOW there's an issue. There IS an injury. And my vestibular-ocular (sp?) pathway is affected as

Xanax and any psychiatric drugs are deadly Don't take them. This forum gives you the answers and there are other self help methods than meds. Glad you are off them!

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T07:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-6853>

My lipids have gone through the roof according this comprehensive health diagnostic labs reports:

total cholesterol- **318** ref ALA-**0.2% ref-0.1-0.4

DPA-**2.6% ref-0.6-4.1

EPA- **1.2% ref-0.1-2.5

DHA- **7.3% ref-0.1-8.4

omega 6 total-**27.3% ref 28.6-44.5

AA- **12.6%** ref 10.5-23.3

Linoleic- 10.9%** ref 4.6-21.3

cis monounsaturated total- 17.7%** ref 11.5-20.5

Saturated Total- 43.1** ref 36.6-42

Trans total-0.6%** ref 0.1-1.8

Hemoglobin A1c- 5.1** (drawn just prior to the above labs)

Fasting Blood Glucose- 98

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T16:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-6976>

Anyone? Sure hoping for some input!

### L8F — 2012-03-27T16:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-6980>

How long ago did you make the switch over to Paleo eating? There is an adjustment period while your liver makes the change over, and cholesterol goes up before settling in. That being said, your trigs are high. THis was fasting right? Also how much percent paleo are you? Are you low carb as well? Don't want to assume...

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T17:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7013>

It did not convert to Paleo recently. Let me give you a little background.........

I've been lowcarb since discovering metabolic syndrome in my early 30's, so about 20 years. Numbers were pretty well controlled until the past few years. They just seem to keep creeping higher. The trigs averaged around 5,000 in my thirties before I started lowcarb, shot as high as 7,000 when they put me on a no-fat program. I was a vegetarian from about age 18 through my twenties. It no doubt created metabolic syndrome. I also developed PCOS, IBS and scleroderma in my late 20's. The scleroderma went into remission about 5 years after I gave up vegetarianism and went lowcarb. IBS vanished immediatly after eliminating grains. I battled with PCOS unitl I was in my early 40's.

I have been very regimented with carbs for the past 20 year to keep hgbA1c in check, generally 20 net/day. Sometimes will cycle 50-75/net day if doing more activity. I track in cronometer. I have eaten mainly organic and grassfed raised animal products for the past 20 years, my husband and I were certified organic growers through the 80's. We grow a lot of our own veggies too. I try to eat both local and seasonal. When the term 'paleo' came along, it was easy to see we fit that description. I have not had grains in 20 years.

Several things have occurred that may have played a role in the lipids rising so dramatically. I stopped bodybuilding competitively a few years ago. Also, another player is age. I see the two biggies influencing my lipids as ApoE genotype and the hyperabsorption of fats. What worked to control lipids in my late 30's and through my 40's now seems to be backfiring. I also retired as a personal trainer about a year ago and have tried to change my workout fanaticism. I worked out with weights for an hour and a half plus at least an hour of hardcore biking/cardio per day. The leptin reset helped me recover adrenal function, leptin sensitivity and basal body temps (it freaked me out not working out at all for the first month). Since reducing exercise levels, I am battling lipids. I still lift 5 days per week and take the dogs for a walk every day. Trying to do exercise more in moderation. I am oc cousre tempted to begin using exercise again as a method to control numbers, because it does seem to keep lipids & blood pressure in check.

Most think of familial hypercholesterolemia when they see numbers like this, but Apo E4 E4 genotype will create a similar panel.....in spite of low carb. These however....are the worst my numbers have looked since my thirties. The sdLDL and particle size specifically are frightening. Very sobering.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T17:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7043>

**healthnut;6889 wrote: My lipids have gone through the roof according this comprehensive health diagnostic labs reports:

total cholesterol- 318** ref11.3% ref-0.1-14.1

ALA-**0.2% ref-0.1-0.4

DPA-**2.6% ref-0.6-4.1

EPA- **1.2% ref-0.1-2.5

DHA- **7.3% ref-0.1-8.4

omega 6 total-**27.3% ref 28.6-44.5

AA- **12.6%** ref 10.5-23.3

Linoleic- 10.9%** ref 4.6-21.3

cis monounsaturated total- 17.7%** ref 11.5-20.5

Saturated Total- 43.1** ref 36.6-42

Trans total-0.6%** ref 0.1-1.8

Hemoglobin A1c- 5.1** (drawn just prior to the above labs)

Fasting Blood Glucose- 98

Ok I'm seeing 3 things here the first is Inflammation your HbA1C, homocystine, hsCRP are at the high end of normal. Dr K likes to see HbA1C below 4.5 and hsCRP as close .1 and below as possible. W/your history of autoimmune I'd avoid all nightshades, eggs, and dairy b/c you still have inflammation - CT will help w/the inflammation - push this as a tool!

2nd: your Fasting BG is High - given Dr K's and Dr Davis's views anything above 90 for fasting = diabetic. Get your self a cheap bg meter and check your bg before each meal, and for every 15mins until it returns to your pre-meal levels. you don't want to see more than a 10-15point spike during the 1hr or so after eating. Dairy can spike your bg

3rd: trigs and sdLDL & the APOE gene: How long have you been grain free? I didn't see anything in your elaboration that explained how long? my understanding is dairy (especially the casein protein) can stimulate trigs the same way in some ppl - based upon my conversations w/my pcp. ARe you dairy free? If not try going dairy free. .

you're having some oxidization going on... so that tells me you're body's not converting the LDL its making for the sex hormones... your hormones are probably off as well. Have you had a full hormone panel done recently.. you mention adrenal fatigue - I'm guessing you're still having issues w/conversion of LDL to pregnenolone - and instead you're still got some Hypothalmus- pitutary -Adrenal axis mismatch occuring... which is also impacting your Thyroid... but can't be sure unless you have more labs...

### BenG — 2012-03-27T19:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7118>

I recall Dr. Kruse saying no dairy till FBG is < 85. You might want to throw some anti-inflammatories in there too like tumeric, green tea (EGCG)(PQQ), etc. Read his top ten paleo supplements: [http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/) Homocystine..I'd probably add B-Complex supps. too. CT helps too. You're also lifting 5 days a week...Exercise is anti-inflammatory in moderation. Over training is pro-inflammatory though. How's your sleep? Dr. Kruse is anti-cardio...he'll tell you how it depletes your stem cells and causes early death. He only advocates Lifting and HIIT. (low intensity like walking is ok). If you have adrenal problems which Shijin mentioned (lousy sleep, low DHEA-S, High Cortisol \[overtraining causes that), you may have adrenal fatigue and should consider his Adrenal Rx, but a hormone panel would tell if you have pregnenolone steal syndrome.

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T19:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7144>

> **Shijin13;7080 wrote:** Ok I'm seeing 3 things here the first is Inflammation your HbA1C, homocystine, hsCRP are at the high end of normal. Dr K likes to see HbA1C below 4.5 and hsCRP as close .1 and below as possible. W/your history of autoimmune I'd avoid all nightshades, eggs, and dairy b/c you still have inflammation - CT will help w/the inflammation - push this as a tool!
>
>
>
> 2nd: your Fasting BG is High - given Dr K's and Dr Davis's views anything above 90 for fasting = diabetic. Get your self a cheap bg meter and check your bg before each meal, and for every 15mins until it returns to your pre-meal levels. you don't want to see more than a 10-15point spike during the 1hr or so after eating. Dairy can spike your bg
>

> 3rd: trigs and sdLDL & the APOE gene: How long have you been grain free? I didn't see anything in your elaboration that explained how long? my understanding is dairy (especially the casein protein) can stimulate trigs the same way in some ppl - based upon my conversations w/my pcp. ARe you dairy free? If not try going dairy free. .
>
> you're having some oxidization going on... so that tells me you're body's not converting the LDL its making for the sex hormones... your hormones are probably off as well. Have you had a full hormone panel done recently.. you mention adrenal fatigue - I'm guessing you're still having issues w/conversion of LDL to pregnenolone - and instead you're still got some Hypothalmus- pitutary -Adrenal axis mismatch occuring... which is also impacting your Thyroid... but can't be sure unless you have more labs...

Thanks for your in depth and thoughtful reply I agree, hemoglobin A1c is not where it should be. I have officially had diabetes since my 30's, is getting harder to control w/age.(I am 53). When I was bodybuilding & overexercising it stayed around 4.7-4.9...that's the lowest I ever was able to get it.

Unfortunately, the homocystiene and hsCRP have also risen in the last few years with the lipids, though not as dramatically.

I am not a big nightshade fan....but will steer clear. Peppers on occasion.

About the eggs and dairy. We raise chickens and goats. They are free range...they roam the woodland paths. The only concentrates they are fed are flax, mineral salt and kelp. I think the sulfur and choline (among many other nutrients) might be important in the eggs? Do you think eggs/dairy from clean, home raised, pastured animals would also cause inflammation?

I've been rationalizing having a cup of homemade goats milk Greek style yogurt every day. Goat milk has a very different composition than cow's milk. It contain A2 beta casein, the one that does not problematic as is A1 beta casein from cows milk. Goat milk provides medium chain triglycerides like coconut does, it is high in caprylic acid (this fatty acid is named after 'caprine' AKA-goat). I also feel the abundance of different strains of beneficial bacteria in home cultured, raw kefir provides large numbers of gut flora that lasts longer in the digestive system than anything that could be bought commercially. I culture the yogurt 24 hours, then strain it into a cheesecloth until it is very thick. It drains off most of the whey (lactose) and is similar to greek yogurt in carb count. What do you think? Still no good? I know Dr Davis speaks of the insuniogenic properties of milk. I don't see a post prandial rise, but maybe I am getting insulin to spike....maybe that is why I often see a post prandial drop instead of rise? Prolly thinking, won't render optimal results!

I am officially diabetic, and have been using a glucometer for maybe 8 years. I have controlled HgbA1c w/diet pretty well, but it is getting more difficult. The fasting blood glucose goes higher on keto levels of carbs. (dawn phenomenon). If on keto levels... my breakfast 1 hour post prandial drops, often 20-30 points. It's not really reactive hypoglycemia, since the drop doesn't normally go below 70's & 80's. But FBG can be as high as 120. If I get carbs up around 50grams/day, BG reacts more normally and fasting BG is lower.....but A1c rises. so, I try to look at a combination of A1c, FBG and 1 hour post-prandials. My 1 hour post prandials are rarely over 100.

Realy enjoying CT, I feel so relaxed afterwards.

I went off grains when I went lowcarb in my 30's, so about twenty years. I did re-introduce them for a brief period when I was bodybuilding, but it brought back IBS....so I cut them back out.

I am due for a complete hormone panel, I see my PCP next week to go over these results. I will post results. Last November, they were looking good and cortisol levels were near normal compared to where they were during the stage II adrenal failure. Pregnenolone was way higher too, but not too optimal. Still on some 7keto for that though.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-27T19:25:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7153>

I'm in a similar boat as you. I'm sorting my lipids out, too. We might learn from each other.

Dr. Kruse seems nonplussed about lipids in general.

My CRP isn't too high, but it's not optimal. Right now, I feel like I have the lit match (not optimal CRP) and the case of dynamite (high TC, high LDL, high LDLp, and high small LDLp). My lipids were okay prior to paleo/leptin/CT.

Mine really frightened me, too. I'm still formulating my plan and checking into a few more labs, but I'll post my ideas when I'm ready.

I'm sure Apo E4 is playing a big role in your case. Probably mine, too. (My test is still at the lab.)

### BenG — 2012-03-27T19:37:20+0300

If you have an autoimmune disease - which I believe you mentioned in your earlier write up - you need to avoid dairy, nightshades and eggs, follow the leptin reset and leaky gut rx at the same time... your inflammation is most likely in your gut - and transferring across everything else.

I'm off diary right now...at the request of my pcp - based upon a CIMT - which showed my arterial age of 51 - I'm 38! I go back to see her at the end of april beginning of may - and will have another set of labs run - if things are still high - out goes the eggs. I'm already night shade free - b/c I get wicked inflammation when I eat them - my joints swell, and turn red...

I'd skip the eggs and diary - even the A2 goats milk...

### L8F — 2012-03-27T20:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7230>

Robb Wolf's podcasts (my version of paleo university) have a lot about why AI folks should avoid dairy and eggs. He does not care if it is goat or cow's milk, it is highly insulinogenic and pro growth and inflammation in his book. As a diabetic this is going to be tough tough on your system. The eggs explanation is on the podcast from 12/21/09. It is not about quality of the eggs, it is more about the fact that they protect their reproductive material much like a seed or a grain does--->bad for AI especially. You might notice a dramatic difference if you worked on gut as suggested above along with _strict_ AI diet protocol. You have serious AI history with scleroderma, so don't mess around. Leaky gut stuff, hit it hard.

I find it hard psychologically to marry the nutritive values of certain foods with the potential downsides for certain populations, but it is what it is. Robb W knows his autoimmunity for sure though.

### healthnut — 2012-03-27T21:17:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7281>

I take it in the evening with regular L-tryptophan. I take 100mg 5HTP and 500mg L-tryptophan, plus 400-600mg mag glycinate & 200mg phosphatidyl serine. When I stopped the L-tryprophan and 5HTP, sleep went back to waking around 2-3am and headaches returned. I think I may be low in seratonin.

I stopped it because I take too many supps.....and my sleep was great even if I forgot to take it. The sleeplessness didn't kick back in until after I'd been off for a few weeks.

### BenG — 2012-03-27T22:00:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7315>

> **healthnut;7215 wrote:** _"Consider HIIT/Sprints. When I do them my FBG goes from 80-84 to 68-75. They can really bring it down. 5-6 bursts of maximum effort 2-3x a week is all you need."_
>
>
>
> I tried this a few times this winter, was fearfull it may elevate cortisol? I did the peak 8 sprints on cycle. 30 second sprint, 90 sec slow pace....8 cycles. I'll give it a go.

Your poor sleep suggests a cortisol problem. You probably should do salivary testing (day, evening, night etc) over 24 hours.

Studies have shown you can maintain your level of fitness by scaling back volume and intensity by 2/3rds as long as intensity stays the same. That said, if you have a cortisol/overtraining problem, I'd say workout no more than 1-2x a week. Often overtrained people, who cut volume back to 1-2x a week all the sudden see gains because it gives their hormones a chance to recover from chronically elevated cortisol/fatigue. Maybe 1 day of heavy weights and 1 day of sprints. The CT should improve recovery

See [http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/](http://http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/)

Adrenal Rx: [http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/)

On Leaky gut: [http://jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription/)

and see if any apply to you, although it's hard to be sure without testing. Still, the bad sleep suggests a cortisol problem and low DHEA-S, sex steroids, Vitamin D and/or pregnenolone steal syndrome, so the links should help you fix. Going to and keeping artifical light to a minimum after dark is a huge help.

Yep, had overtraining adrenal/cortisol issues. I had tested 8 months prior to beginning LR. I was in stage two adrenal failure (did the salivary testing ZRT). Retested two months after beginning LR and cortisol levels were almost normal.

I have scaled back workouts a lot and am no longer a cardio addict. It's a hard habbit to break! Still hesitant to go whole hog on HIIT or sprint intervals. Especially since one knee has no miniscus left on either side. Yeh, maybe once or twice a week would work. The weights aren't stressfull now, stopped training like a bodybuilder prepping from competition. Much more relaxed. I still lift heavy, but not crazy heavy. I was squatting over 450# before my last knee surgery a few years ago. Now bench 175# for sets of 6. Used to rep 225#. We now work out at the local HS gym so I am not tempted to get competitive again.

Thanks for the links, I need to catch up on all the JK blogs, I am a greenhorn when it comes to much of the hormone talk. I overproduced hormones through my 30' & 40's. Just started to fall back last year. I take 10,000IU's of D and levels are hard to get/stay optimal. I had it up to 72 in the fall......now it fell back this spring. We live North of the 45th parallel.....not too much natural D production here in the deep woods. I have the glasses and the screen dimmer and turn lights down/off when it gets dark. The cold therapy helps w/sleep a lot too.

### healthnut — 2012-03-28T18:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worrisome-lipids.459/post-7760>

I will attempt to post older labs today.

There are a few slamming me for not being on statins....or at least zetia.... or a bile sequestriant....

I thought you guys might find this analysis of my labs interesting. It is from a doctor who is on the track your plaque forum:

_"I want to point out all of the BEAUTIFUL parts of this test.

NO inflammation! Your myeloperoxidase (surrogate marker for dysfunctional HDL) is georgous. Your LPPLA2 has only a trivial elevation (marker for oxidized plaque) Your hscrp is excellent. Then, your omega 3 index is optimal,,,,so, maybe you do have a "build up" of plaque in an artery wall. It is not inflamed, (lppla2 almost normal), your hdl will work to help clear it out (myeloperoxidase normal), the fibrous protective "cap" over it is solid (omega 3 index optimal), Now even IF you were to have a plaque rupture, would the stuff just blow downstream and silently disburse, or would it "clot", form a "thrombus", cause an" event"? That depends on the characteristics of the blood rushing by....So your fibrinogen is nice and low! Fibrinogen is part of the clotting cascade so great! You don't have Factor V Leiden or the prothrombin gene (genetic tendencies of blood to clot more readily) You don't have Lp(a) which resembles plasminogen, another clot former. Your homocysteine is optimal despite C/T MTHFR genetics. Homocysteine elevation promotes clot formation. MTHFR (C/T)? This means that you can optimize probably only 70 % of the folate you eat. But then look at your RBC folate. (RBC folate is a 3 month folate, much better than a serum "3 minute" folate) Your dietary intake of folate is very high,AND 70% OF VERY HIGH is still excellent. Then what about your platelets? Are they ready to clump together and clot? No....your aspirinworks (Platelet clumping test) is very low.

So...high lipids, no oxidized HDL or LDL, no fibrous cap erosion, no increase in clotting factors, no abnormal platelet clumping.

Also BP parameters excellent (NTPRoBNP, Gallectin 3, urine for microalbumin)

Insulin, c-peptide excellent (no diabetes here)

Elevated nonesterified FFA? Thats your MCT oil.

F2 isoprostanes so low that they can't be measured? This says that you are super, super fit!!!_ "

Thanks for your input! I know my numbers are far from optimal. I was bewildered after getting these results! I will keep working on things.

Please tell me more about your CIMT, no radiation involved? I listened to a podcast recently that talked about the differences in diagnosing mens heart disease and womens. Diagnostic and therapeutic strategies \[for heart disease\], which had been developed in men, by men, for men weren't working so well for women. They said women tend to get more IM thickening as an early warning, so diagnosis methods typically used for men, do not detect a problem in women. I had an echo and a heart ultrasound last month, it did show some plaque. But I think by my age everyone has 'some' plaque. I plan to get CIMT and a heart scan to see exactly what's going on.

Here is the talk about the differences between men's and women's heart disease: [http://www.ted.com/talks/noel\_bairey\_merz\_the\_single\_biggest\_health\_threat\_women\_face.html](http://http://www.ted.com/talks/noel_bairey_merz_the_single_biggest_health_threat_women_face.html)

> To reach the total cholesterol to HDL optimal ratio of under 3, I have a ways to go. Currently at 4.29.

The ones that say "no-flush" seem to cause a flush rather badly. Slo Niacin avail at Wal-Mart for around $13 for 100 tablets of 500mg each.

# Thread: DELICIOUS desert...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gemstar — 2012-03-27T08:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-6882>

My sweetener of choice is date syrup. Since I live in Israel this is readily available and unlike honey, even the strictest paleo person will accept it as a sweetener. I created these cookie balls last week, and they are fantastic.

Eyeballed recipe...btw. But I will try to make it something that I can pass on to others.

About 6 cups maybe 7 cups of dry stuff. Since this is a 'ball' cookie, you can play with this until you get it right. My dry ingredients were made up of what I have around.

1 cup hazelnuts, almost ground to a flour

1 cup almonds " " "

1 cup freshly ground flax seeds

1 cup freshly ground whole seseme seeds

1 cup coarsely ground pumpkin seeds

1 cup coarsely ground sunflower seeds

1/2 - 1 cup 85% chocolate (optional but heaven)

1 cup whole grain tahini sauce

1/2 cup date syrup.

Mix wet and dry ingredients well until it forms a dough that will stick together. If too dry add some more tehini sauce. If too wet, add some more of the ground to flour nuts or flax. Form into small balls the size of walnuts and roll in white seseme seeds. Refrigerate on large tray then when hard transfer to a container. Keep refrigerated.

I deliberately did not use coconut in this, just to work on a non coconut recipe. But I make the same thing with coconut too. I also then add some coconut oil along with the tehini. I also usually add dried fruit to the coconut balls, usually organic dried cranberries. As you can probably tell, this is the kind of thing that begs for experimentation. Since this is not a baked item you basically cannot go wrong, and you can constantly taste it to know what to add or cut back on.

ENJOY!!!

I hope it's ok to post a recipe in this section.

### Guest — 2012-03-27T14:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-6920>

In any place of earth we will get a lot of ideas and always feel a perfect observation as well as freshness

and breeze so we will be happy and enjoy atmospheric n any stage.

### gemstar — 2012-03-28T08:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-7618>

Thanks Jolly John.....this is soooo true!

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-28T09:52:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-7633>

That sounds delicious! Sending to wife . . .

### Snowyky — 2012-04-05T21:14:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/delicious-desert.461/post-11837>

> **donkjellberg;7672 wrote:** That sounds delicious! Sending to wife . . .

Why.. so she can print it out for you to make later?

I have a question regarding sleep and cold. You never mentioned anything regarding how to sleep in the CT series or elsewhere. Yet, I see discussions regarding sleeping with window open and such. Cold hibernation time.

I can not figure this out. I can sleep in a very cold room but I need to have heavy covers. Light covers prevent the deep sleep. Not well rested feeling is what I get if covers are light or if I get cold when asleep.

Could you please elaborate on this so we know where to sleep? Under or over the covers?

Cooling

Jack Says: (comments CT-7)

March 24th, 2012 at 8:38 am

@Cooling if you want to sleep like a rockstar make it cold and darkâ€¦â€¦â€¦on top of the covers naked. it takes practice but I do this all the timeâ€¦â€¦..it took me about 6 months to get to that point in 2007.

Cooling

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-28T01:04:01+0300

### Glamorama — 2012-03-28T03:41:12+0300

### Marsha — 2012-03-28T04:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-on-top-of-the-covers-naked-anyone.463/post-7510>

My husband and I both sleep in the nude.

For winter, we each have our own 'dyne' - Norwegian for duvet or eiderdown comforter...otherwise you get too hot with two bodies under one!

Since starting this whole new way of living (Leptin reset and CT) with windows open, I'm shoving the whole thing off of me by 3AM and reaching for it after I cool off. Then maybe just my feet out till I'm convinced it's getting light out (been wearing a sleep mask and ear plugs) and get up to start my day.

Soon it will be so hot in Colorado, that I'll only need a sheet and/or AC, but that won't last long if I keep up with the CT!!!

### LinD — 2012-03-28T04:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-on-top-of-the-covers-naked-anyone.463/post-7521>

### Glamorama — 2012-03-28T04:29:34+0300

I'm very familiar with the dyne, having grown up with them (and still have them, but in Oz they call them doona).

I always sleep in the nude, I feel suffocated if I wear Pjs to bed. Since CT I leave the windows open in our bedroom, so it is quite fresh.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-28T04:34:47+0300

# Thread: CT is Fasted State Training
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-is-fasted-state-training.464/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-03-27T14:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-is-fasted-state-training.464/post-6918>

To continue my "Fasted State" training adaptations thread, there is something else which I have noticed that correlates with CT.

Doing ultra long distance, fasted state and minimal fueling exercise puts the body in a significant calorie deficit. As mentioned I have been doing this for years, yet my body weight has remained relatively stable, my LMM has increased and my BF % has reduced. Again, non-controlled, N=1, so no hard data to go on.

To put things into perspective, here's an example of one of my days

Wake: 7am

Breakfast : 2 x freshly ground coffee's

8am: pre race warm up run (1hr), easy jog over mountainous terrain

9.30 - 2pm: 52Km mountain ultra race, tough race over mountainous terrain, ran harder (i.e. at a higher threshold) than normal longer races

Result: 4th

Intake During: approx 500kcal

Post Race Intake: approx 2500kcal

Conservative Estimate of a 5.5hr mountain run EE = 3000kcal

Add BMR/PRAL of say 2000kcal and you have a total EE of ~5000kcal... and I still think thats conservative.

So intake for the day was roughly 3000kcal, expenditure was approx 2000kcal greater.... do the math !!

There has got to be some serious adaptation mechanisms possibly related to T3/T4 andd leptin going on ?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Pacing Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T15:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-6948>

How do you know you are ready to add on another 5-10 minutes to the cold immersion bath?

I want to keep advancing, but I also don't want to push it too far.

### vwaggs — 2012-03-27T15:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-6964>

I just forced it. Mind over matter in my case. I play podcasts to keep my mind busy so I don't keep complaining in my head LOL. Makes the time fly by now! How long are you going now? How cold?

### indigogirl — 2012-03-27T16:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-6986>

Are you both gradually cold adapting, or did you just take plunge?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T18:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7084>

I'm not sure if this is gradual or not. But I did the face dunking, then the icepack to 60 minutes. Then started with 10 minute 50F baths.

Now I am up to 35 minutes at 50 F. But I'm just not sure when to push it to 40 minutes and beyond.

### shilohman — 2012-03-27T18:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7096>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;7121 wrote:** I'm not sure if this is gradual or not. But I did the face dunking, then the icepack to 60 minutes. Then started with 10 minute 50F baths.
>
> Now I am up to 35 minutes at 50 F. But I'm just not sure when to push it to 40 minutes and beyond.

Just let your body tell you. If you are shivering really bad after the 35 minute bath, then stick to the 35 minutes and allow your body to adapt. The shivering won't go away, but it should subside as you adapt. If I eat right before the bath, then I find that I shiver the least or very little. Make sure you are supplementing or eating bitter melon.

### indigogirl — 2012-03-27T18:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7111>

How long to face dunk before doing CT in a bath?

I assume I can do ice packs on specific body parts at any time during the adaptation?

Do I really need to measure temp of water and my skin?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T18:58:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacing-question.465/post-7113>

Initially, I shivered the first immersion bath I did.

But since then, nada.

I don't shiver at all. But after 35 minutes, I am darn cold!!! And it takes a long time to warm up - couple of hours.

# Thread: Starting Wk4 of LRx first CT bath yesterday.  Fibro flares..big time.  Any input????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2012-03-27T17:04:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-7006>

Hi all long time reader 1st time poster.

I have a long history of chronic illness that came to head in 2009 with a lyme, babesia, bartonella diagnosis. 18+ months of antibiotics/anti malarials (gut killers) and countless supplements later I am like a new person. All cognitive functions are back, joint pain gone, migraines gone, fevers gone, symptoms of LUPUS gone, CFS gone, no more shingles flare ups. All labs show massive improvement all around.

That said, my fibromyalgia was gone as well until now. Any reason why after 3 full weeks of Leptin Rx and first CT bath my fibro would flare? Its not debilitating just this searing burn in the back and neck I remember all to well. While in the cold bath (59 degrees...my tap doesnt get any colder) my back really seized up on me. I lasted 20 mins but the back pain got me out...not the cold.

I am still waging war against systemic candida, low progesterone, low estrogen, hypothyroid.

Trying to figure out what spiked the back pain and how I can get control of that?

Thanks for any input

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-27T19:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-7129>

I'd take the CT slower & strictly follow the protocol as outlined in CT4:

1\. Face dunks

2\. ice with compression shirt

3\. ice directly on skin

4\. baths (wear a hat at first)

5\. baths with ice on torso

There's a temptation to heal ourselves more quickly than our bodies will allow. Considering your history I would proceed _very_ slowly & back off if you have adverse effects (as you mentioned).

Dr. K has also mentioned two supplements to aid the transition of cold adaptation:

1\. 500mg of Krill oil 3x daily

2\. bitter melon extract 2hrs prior to CT

Good luck!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-27T19:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-7137>

I stumbled on this today.

The short version: Ovarian/Adrenal/Thyroid problems are very much connected. Fix the Adrenal and the others will fall in line.

I was hypo (not hashi) and was able to fix it with adrenal supplements alone. I might suggest seeing a naturopath and having a cortisol salivary test. I do also think it is common for some symptoms as the toxins. I do hope you can get some relief!

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-03-30T21:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-8928>

> **cantweight;7043 wrote:** Any reason why after 3 full weeks of Leptin Rx and first CT bath my fibro would flare?

Can't say that I know the reason, but I have been having a similar experience. I have RA and I have been having all sorts of weird flare ups since starting CT baths. I'm low on all steroids except Estrogen. I think I have some level of adrenal fatigue. My backgound is chronic ENT infections as a kid that had to be treated with antibiotics ... 20+ years of that ended in abcessed tonsils that were removed as an adult. Don't think the gut ever recovered from all those treatments of antibiotics. It is certainly in a dismal state right now. I believe this was the first stepping stone that lead to my joint issues.

FWIW - I had to back off the CT baths (because my core temp was dropping post bath) and just do the icing with a compression shirt. I just got back into the bath this last week. Currently I do the bath while icing the tum. Core temp is not dropping any more. My tap water ranges between 48 - 50 degrees right now. I started at 20 minutes and added 5 minutes each time. I'm now shooting for 45 minutes 1x day. I plan on giving it 6 weeks before making a judgement. My poor hubby is freaking out on my flare ups - latest conversation was about how maybe I should go to the Mayo Clinic because they have more experts there. Sigh. Yes, they have more experts who will want me to take toxic drugs and will tell me the rest of my issues are just "to be expected ... you are getting older ...". I tried to explain to him that they don't truely care because they dont expect people with RA (or fibro or MS, etc) to have a good quality of life and they certainly don't expect us to have a long life.

Also - have you tried an autoimmune paleo diet (no eggs, dairy, grains, legumes, nightshades, nuts, seeds)? You might find that could help minimize your flare ups. It can be tough but it is worth it. Ohh and (if you are not already) double down on food based probiotics.

If you keep up with the CT baths, will you let me know how it goes for you?

### cantweight — 2012-04-04T18:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-wk4-of-lrx-first-ct-bath-yesterday-fibro-flares-big-time-any-input.467/post-11322>

Thanks so much for all the input. I attempted baths again and really focused on keeping my upper body relaxed. I was so tense with my arms out of the tub for 20 mins I think that was my problem. Handling much better now.

I have been on adrenal support supplements for a few years now. I work very hard to keep stress under control....but right now I am selling my house, in college, and have 3 kids so stress is at an all time high....possibly the cause of flare? lol

Wk 5 of leptinrx....awesome. Wondering now if any of the fat loss could be the cause of newer autoimmune symptoms flaring.

This stuff is wild!

# Thread: High evening cortisol levels

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-27T17:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-evening-cortisol-levels.468/post-7015>

What ideas do you all have if someone has low morning, and high afternoon/evening cortisol levels?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T17:26:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-evening-cortisol-levels.468/post-7022>

Circadian mismatch.

did they ever work shift work? are they "night owls"

I did several years of swings/graves in the military ~5yrs worth of either working 4-midnight or midnight -8am. result - even 15yrs later was a backwards circadian rhythm...low in the am (when I'd be getting ready to go to sleep after shift work) and High at night (When I'd be getting ready to go to work)

I focused on adpatocrines, and getting light on my eyes first thing in the am, and turning off all lights etc at night...

### JustinB — 2012-04-04T13:39:32+0300

# Thread: Being LS and Recovery Time/Sweating?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-ls-and-recovery-time-sweating.469/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-27T17:50:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-ls-and-recovery-time-sweating.469/post-7039>

Hey guys. So, I'm trying to decide if I can move onto the post leptin diet part and one of the things to look at is recovery time.

Here's my question. What do you guys think constitutes as "better recovery time" from exercise?

Here's my before.. If I'm out of shape and haven't exercised in months or years and I do heavy lifting or a hard bodyrock workout (basically overtrain) I will be sore 3-4 days. If I have been working out even like once a wk and I do the same then I would be sore for 1-2 days. I think that being sore for at least a day is normal as your muscles are recovering and rebuilding.

So, any of you who are LS what is your experience like in recovery time? What do you think "proves" you are LS?

Also, the part about sweating. Dr. Kruse lists " You will notice a change in your sweating pattern."

What kind of change? What is good sweating vs bad sweating?

Thanks for your help guys. Btw I'm a 28 yr old female if that matters in all of this.

# Thread: Cold adaptation protocol using air per Dr. K - how do i do it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tulipwood — 2012-03-27T18:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7057>

Hi

per Dr. K's faq on Cold Thermogenesis

"The physics of heat loss following cold water immersion dictate a more rapid drop in surface and core temperature than from exposure to a cold air environment.Ã‚ So most people will choose to use water because it works a lot faster"

I am curious how does one go about doing cold adaptation using air?

also would it be advantageous to dress lightly and be a bit chilly in my office, movie theaters, etc this summer where they run the a/c super cool vs wearing a sweater?

tx

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-27T19:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7170>

Seems like the easiest way to do it would be while you sleep. Dr. K says he keeps the thermostat @ 60ÂºF at night.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-27T20:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7253>

A lot depends on where you live. If you live in the North and have cold winter temps - you can just spend time outdoors and start wearing less and less layers. Eventually walking outside when it is freezing cold in short and a T - shirt.

If you live wear the temps are warmer, then you gotta us AC or work in a cooler.

That is why ice and water are easier - better access and they conduct more energy than air as well.

### kalibex — 2012-03-28T15:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-protocol-using-air-per-dr-k-how-do-i-do-it.470/post-7689>

I've been supplementing the spot icing and cold baths by wearing fewer layers when outside, especially on the random cold mornings/evenings here in New England. I'll sometimes take a jacket or sweater along in my bag as a security blanket, but ultimately there's this feeling of security in knowing that even if it's chilling and uncomfortable, that as long as it's not too close to actually being freezing outside, it's okay; your body can handle it. Also been leaving my heat off and my window cracked open, including at night. (Though felt like a wimp the other night when I had to close it during an over-night hard freeze...  )

# Thread: How are you getting your Selenium?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-your-selenium.471/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-27T19:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-your-selenium.471/post-7117>

Dr. Kruse says:

> Selenium is needed for the proper functioning of the immune system cells. It is a cofactor in the activation of neutrophils, macrophages, and Natural killer cells and T lymphocytes and many other immunoregulatory functions as laid out in FerencikÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s paper in 2003. Selenium seems to act by two modes to protect us from cancer. One is that is catalyzes a many immune reactions to increase our immune function in the GALT. Secondly, Se seems to hinder oncogenesis by protecting the p53 gene in the gut and does not allow oncogenesis to occur. In fact, it appears to favor low level inflammation seen in autoimmune conditions and favor apoptosis of defective cells instead. It appears that low Selenium levels reduce the production of plasma glutathione peroxidase. Patients with low selenium levels are known to have higher rates of polyps and autoimmunity. (Fernandez-Banares et al., 2002) Naturally occurring Selenium is found in plants like garlic and broccoli as a compound called Se-methylselenocysteine (Se-MSC). Se-MSc produces 33% reduction in cancerous lesions (colon) than selenite (synthetic). It also produces a 50% decrease in new tumor development locally in the gut. It also induces cells to undergo apoptosis and not oncogenesis. It has been shown to reduce angiogenesis to make the cellular terroir less hospitable to cancer. Se- MSC also directly down regulates vascular endothelial growth factor that is commonly found in most GI cancers. So the story seems to show that selenium is pretty critical to gut health and disease prevention.

How are you getting yours?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-27T19:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-your-selenium.471/post-7132>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T19:24:30+0300

I take 200 & 400 mcg alternating of Se supplement every other day. I have Hashimotos and have taken this for years.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-27T23:19:02+0300

# Thread: Zeltiq?

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12

### ChimpChick — 2012-03-27T22:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7320>

If CT is only effective while one is ketogenic, and doing CT in a non keto state causes a mismatch (inflamation, weight gain, bad things) Does this mean that if one were to get the Zeltiq procedure done in the summer while eating seasonal paleo-approved carbs it would be ineffective? Or worse could cause the opposite of the intent?

Thanks!

### Owl — 2012-03-27T22:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7324>

It doesn't sound like Wim Hof is ketogenic and he's doing ok. Maybe not absolutely optimal, but he's certainly cold adapted and healthier for it. Zeltiq is being used by the public eating the SAD. If it didn't work during a certain time of year, that would have come out by now.

### ChimpChick — 2012-03-27T22:35:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7343>

Thanks Owl! Good point.. The seasons are throwing me for a loop.. I have an unheated pool that will be open soon and will still be quite chilly until August, so I want to make the most of it.. But I would also like to eat a blueberry now and then during the short period of time they are allowed. So I guess the answer is to plan my fruit around my swimming and never mix the two..

### caroline — 2012-03-28T01:59:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7432>

> **ChimpChick;7381 wrote:** Thanks Owl! Good point.. The seasons are throwing me for a loop.. I have an unheated pool that will be open soon and will still be quite chilly until August, so I want to make the most of it.. But I would also like to eat a blueberry now and then during the short period of time they are allowed. So I guess the answer is to plan my fruit around my swimming and never mix the two..

I asked about zeltiq before. When I read their web site it says results take 2 to 4 months. I was wondering if you would detox after the procedure like we are doing?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-28T16:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7702>

I didn't realize that Zeltiq took that long. That is good to know. I was getting a little worried when my weight loss plateaued a bit.

And if Zeltiqu takes 2-4 months, then I would expect a slower gradual process, like the CT here, would take even longer.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-28T17:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7723>

CT works for \*anyone\* at any time to increase metabolism and increase BAT (= spot fat burning). It works for far more (adrenal, thyroid, etc., etc.) if you pair it with the ketogenic diet, and far more quickly if you use water (especially immersion).

### hazyjane — 2012-03-28T21:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7873>

> **ChimpChick;7358 wrote:** If CT is only effective while one is ketogenic, and doing CT in a non keto state causes a mismatch (inflamation, weight gain, bad things) Does this mean that if one were to get the Zeltiq procedure done in the summer while eating seasonal paleo-approved carbs it would be ineffective? Or worse could cause the opposite of the intent?
>
>
>
> Thanks!

I know a lady who was part of a Zeltiq clinical trial and she didn't get results from it. I have a feeling that diet affects the results of Zeltiq. Some people get great results and others, none. Next time I see her, I'll ask her what she was eating at that time.

### JC2K — 2012-03-28T21:56:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7886>

I would expect some detox from Zeltiq, but over a longer amount of time, since the cells are dieing off at a slow rate.

### LinD — 2012-03-28T22:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7906>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;7743 wrote:** I didn't realize that Zeltiq took that long. That is good to know. I was getting a little worried when my weight loss plateaued a bit.
>
> And if Zeltiqu takes 2-4 months, then I would expect a slower gradual process, like the CT here, would take even longer.

That IS interesting.

Makes me love and appreciate CT even more!

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-28T22:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7908>

> **MamaGrok;7764 wrote:** CT works for \*anyone\* at any time to increase metabolism and increase BAT (= spot fat burning). It works for far more (adrenal, thyroid, etc., etc.) if you pair it with the ketogenic diet, and far more quickly if you use water (especially immersion).

I agree with this. Also, think of it like in the wild. Animals/humans went into water plenty.. whether the ocean, lake, or river.. the water would be cold or at least not heated much of the time. Have you ever been in the Pacific Ocean? That bitch is cold even in 100 degree weather! LOL. The pool you're going into isn't heated which is exactly what natural sources of water would be like. This is what makes sense to me on "CT" in the summer. It's more like just natural behavior to take a cool/cold shower as water isn't heated in the wild.

### ShuffleUp — 2012-03-28T23:26:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7944>

If you read some about Zeltiq they mention that you should tell your doc if you have any liver problems. I read something that said they anticipated some detox issues but have not largely seen problems with it. I have also read that it is not for the obese, and should be used generally for the last few pounds and is fda approved for the love handle area. They say it kills about 20% of the fat in the treated area. I was also suprised it does take 2-4 months to see the full effects which to me seems to indicate that those looking for results in our CT experimentation (which for most of us hasn't been longer than 6 weeks) are probably expecting tangible weight loss results too soon. Although I'm unclear as to whether or not Zeltiq was more of an anaology than the exact function that CT is having on us. i.e. Zeltiq probably does not exhibit much BAT activity increase which is a big part of what I believe CT, as laid out by Kruse, is meant to do.

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/article-1299505/Instead-melting-away-flab-laser-hottest-craze-America-freeze-death.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/article-1299505/Instead-melting-away-flab-laser-hottest-craze-America-freeze-death.html)

### caroline — 2012-03-29T00:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zeltiq.475/post-7980>

I think you are right shuffleup. We are expecting too much too soon. I just want this blubber gone!

# Thread: cold therapy and the nervous system
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-27T23:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/post-7381>

I'm partway through an old book called Dexterity and It's Development. It's fascinating and a must read for anyone interested in evolutionary biology.

The point is that part of the reason we are so dextrous is that we are warm blooded. Our nerves require a certain baseline temperature to transmit signals properly. This is why you lose coordination when you are cold. Think of your hands when you are skiing/snowboarding trying to do something on your coat or bindings that requires coordination.

Anyway, it is interesting for me to note that during my cold adaptation period I am finding myself fairly uncoordinated for long periods after the cold exposure. I just spent an hour outside, lightly dressed, in ~50f weather with windchill unaccounted for. I've been inside now ~45 minutes and i can still feel a little difficulty typing this.

My assumption is that this is a transitionary phase and that after adaptation my body temp will be within the optimal range for nervous system communication regardless of ambient temperatures.

So at least that can serve as a marker for me. There is no way I'm cold adapted as long as my dexterity is compromised.

jesse

### Melinda — 2012-03-28T01:21:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/post-7413>

I find my mental dexterity is hampered as well. I'm doing 30 min in my salt water pool, swimming and playing today-no more being a statue, and toward the end of the secession and a while afterwards I can't find the words I'm looking for with the usual speed. It's like my mind is so busy processing other stuff that it doesn't have the bandwidth to help me be eloquent.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-29T19:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-therapy-and-the-nervous-system.477/post-8350>

Interesting feedback Melinda. Thanks. What temperature is your pool?

# Thread: Pork Spare ribs in a Crock pot - let me have your best Recipes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-03-28T01:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7415>

Ok, this entire week in my house hold has been about breaking out of the \*rut\* we've been stuck in. so for tomorrow I'm planning on making some pork spare ribs in the crock pot... but I need some Ideas...

I've found two so far - but I'm kinda meh about them...

[http://kaitsprimalkitchen.blogspot.com/2011/08/paleo-bbq-marinated-crock-pot-spare.html](http://http://kaitsprimalkitchen.blogspot.com/2011/08/paleo-bbq-marinated-crock-pot-spare.html)

and

[http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/02/23/5-spice-slow-cooker-pork-ribs/](http://http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/02/23/5-spice-slow-cooker-pork-ribs/)

so ladies and gents....give me your best recipes!!! I want something that's both OPTIMAL And RockStar!

### tweety — 2012-03-28T22:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7921>

I'll be watching. I had an AMAZING recipe that I used last year. It cooked all day in this beautiful Italian tomato sauce. The meat just fell off the bones. And now, I can't remember what cookbook it was in! I had a phase where I tried a whole bunch of cookbooks from the library and I can't remember which one it was. My husband said it was exactly like what his little Italian Grandma used to cook when he was a kid. Off to cearch again... fingers crossed!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-28T22:55:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7930>

We make lots of meats in the crock. 2-3 times a week. I had to give up on cooking them overnight for morning consumption though, the yummyness kept waking me up.

Here is my best advice.

1\. Consider cooking in a big enameled iron pot. You can brown it to develop fond, then put it in over on 175-200 degrees for hrs just like crock.

I would be happy to offer more info on that. . .

but since you asked about crock pot, I'm going to assume you don't want the hassle of browning in one pan, moving it etc. Who has time for that.

1\. Lay ribs on bed of onion.

2\. Salt all ribs excessively.

3\. handful of cumin seeds

4\. a little spoon of finely ground coffee

5\. a heap of chocolate power (valrohna is my fav, make sure it's nothing but chocolate)

5\. a cup or so of broth (I've used whatever is on hand, chicken, beef, veal)

6\. a cup or so of malbec

7\. excessive amounts of garlic.

8\. Can of chopped tomatoes.

Once done, strain all solids and put juice in a sauce pan. If more liquid is needed add either broth or wine or both. Might need to salt again. Take off heat, add several little pieces of ice cold butter and wisk it in. This will make a glistening and to-die-for sauce.

### tweety — 2012-03-29T00:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7972>

Yum, Cavemam. That sounds awesome!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T01:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-7992>

I know you are looking for a crock pot recipe but this is my favorite all time recipe for ribs:

[http://www.oprah.com/food/Quincy-Jones-Thriller-Ribs](http://http://www.oprah.com/food/Quincy-Jones-Thriller-Ribs)

There are two steps involved which may be too much work cuz I know you have a busy life. They have always been the most tender most flavorful ribs! YUM!!

Cavemam that recipe sounds fabulous! Thanks.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-29T02:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8015>

I just took some paleo BBQ sauce, mustard, balsamic vinegar, liquid smoke & hickory seasoning. Tossed it all together and put it on low. Fraking amazing. Dd turned down asparagus for a 2nd helping of ribs!!! I thought we'd have enough for left overs tomorrow but I doubt it!!!

### Slone07 — 2012-03-29T02:33:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8019>

I like mine cooked under sauerkraut that's been sautÃ©ed with bacon, onions, and garlic. And creole seasoning...my usual seasoning.

Yours sound fantastic, though!

### skupe — 2012-03-29T03:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8056>

In case you are unaware, Accent is MSG and very unhealthy. I would find something else to use in its place like some organic smoked salt.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8242>

Shinjin, I just had a big grok party with spare ribs. I just salt and peppered them, put them on low (250F) in the oven for 3-4 hours and finished them off on the grill for the last 10-15 minutes. Even the SAD people in attendance didn't add bbq sauce. The ratio of pork to fat makes spare ribs the perfect cut without too much fuss. So tasty!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8243>

For sweet, I would second cavemam's suggestion for cocoa, but also cinnamon and sea salt and CO is amazing with pork! YUM!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-30T01:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-8505>

Ok, made beef roast with my standard method mentioned before. I encourage everyone to brown before crockpotting on beef roast. Pork it's not as necessary (due to the way the proteins loosen, slow low for whole thing is good).

For beef, I don't add the cocoa and cinnamon, but use Onion, red wine, cumin seeds and an obscene amount of salt. I brown the beef in CO. Kitchen fills with smoke.

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8553=68-photo \(2\).JPG>)

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8553=69-photo \(3\).JPG>)

Again, I caution about leaving overnight (although this is an awesome breakfast!) because you can't sleep with all the yummy smells!

### mafixit — 2012-09-22T03:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-spare-ribs-in-a-crock-pot-let-me-have-your-best-recipes.478/post-53348>

I take a pork shoulder, soak it overnight in liquid smoke, then the next morning add worstershire sauce and garlic if desired and cook on low for 24 hours.

Hubby likes to add BBQ sauce to his. I like it just like it comes out of the cooker.

# Thread: Chrystin's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-03-28T02:09:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-7437>

Ok, here goes...

It's hard to know where to start, isn't it? After a year and a half of hopelessly battling my 10 lb weight gain (on the heels of 35 years of being very fit) I stumbled upon the leptin rx. Not two weeks in, I had gained about 6 pounds! When I commented about my weight gain on the MDA forums, dr. K said that if you gain weight on the rx, your hormones are out of whack. My thought was "whatever. My hormones are fine.". And I kept going.

Then I decided to get some testing done-just to prove to myself that I really was healthy and that my weight gain and binge eating was because I was crazy, lazy and self indulgent. I was floored when my testing came back... Cortisol- low during the day, high at night. Estrogen and progesterone-low. T3 (and prob t4) - low. Dheas- low. Vit d-low.

After doing some research, I've finally realized that all of my symptoms over the past year or so are consistent with my messed up adrenals and thyroid. I've had minor sensitivity to noise, light, temperature, some hair loss, weak nails, dry skin. Those didn't grab my attention. The symptoms that have been most bothersome to me have been (obviously) the weight gain and the emotional ones.

I picked up the book "stop the thyroid madness". Wow. What an education. It was here that I was able to finally see my psychological issues in adrenal dysfunction and hypothyroid. Every symptom- rage, intolerance, complete lack of ability to deal with even the slightest stress, needing to be alone, ups and downs, depression, lack of joy (a few years ago I was the happiest, bubbliest, most energetic person you'd ever meet) completely characterized my every day existence. I just though I was an awful mom and wife. My mom was really demanding, perfectionistic, stressed and high strung and I thought I was just carrying on in her example. To think that all this has been a chemical imbalance.....

Well, I'll see my naturopath next week. I expect her to do some further testing- iron, electrolytes, rt3, maybe b12, iodine, gluten, hormones. Then we'll see where to go from there...

That's why I decided to start this journal. I'm not sure where this journey will take me. I've been unable to stay on the leptin rx for longer than a couple of weeks before I'm overtaken by bingeing. Restrictive programs always set me off... But I keep on keepin on and I'm religiously doing my cold baths every day.

### Birdy — 2012-03-28T02:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-7458>

Good luck, and I am looking forward to reading more.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-03-31T13:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-9166>

Haven't posted in few days. I've been extremely moody. Rage, tears, depression. And cravings. I think I looked like one of those movie schizophrenics yesterday after lunch, fighting my craving for sugar. Ugh. This is how I'm wasting the precious seconds and minutes and hours of my life? It is shameful. Really really shameful.

Started B complex, C complex, E and Magnesium supplementation yesterday (from Wilson's "Adrenal Fatigue") ahead of my naturopath appt on Tuesday. I have to say that I think the Magnesium helped. I had to take it after dinner (he says to take it near food, although after 8 is best- those two obviously do not coincide on the Rx). I slept much better than the past few nights. I think I may have woken up during the night, but I don't recall.

I will return to CT today. Baths, that is. I've iced the past two days because I just couldn't make myself get in the tub (TOM-2 weeks late and very heavy). The icing is just too easy to give any results, IMHO, LOL. I also plan to lift some weights this afternoon.

I was reading on another forum (about paleo/primals with sugar cravings)...This one girl got rid of her sugar cravings by drinking a can of coco milk. Got me thinking again about bulletproof drinks... My plan is to have one after each meal. (coffee at breakfast, until naturopath I imagine will tell me to stop coffee) and green tea at lunchtime and dinner. I had coco oil in green tea immediately following dinner last night and it \*may\* have helped. I'll continue that experiment.

...and I continue to track my meals on a spreadsheet. Oh, and I'm waking up very thirsty every morning. I don't think I'm getting enough water. Maybe that will help me flush all of these moody crappy feelings out better...

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-04-04T14:08:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-11210>

Quick check in while i make my kids' breakfast... Saw naturopath last night. Not \*quite\* as knowledgeable (read:in agreement with me, LOL) as I had hoped. She asked if the "diet" I was on had more fat. Long sigh...... Anyway, got a full thyroid panel ordered, dhea-s at least and will be doing a whole month of saliva testing and another draw on day 21 for hormones.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-04T14:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-11219>

Welcome to the road to optimal.....

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-04-04T15:55:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-11240>

Love from the master...nice.

So, to expand for anyone who's listening or interested or, hell, just for posterity...

ME: I think my problem is my thyroid- possibly hashimoto's (kids have sub clinical gluten allergy by IgG and IgE-I suspect I may have it too, but I haven't been tested yet), screwing up everything else down the line (adrenals and hormones).

HER: She thinks my problem is solely hormonal-specifically low progesterone. She's undecided about the thyroid, wants more testing. She didn't really see too much of a cortisol issue.

ME: What I saw were cortisol numbers "in range" for morning noon and evening, but in the completely wrong part of the range (lower than optimal) and then high out of range for the night levels. Thyroid similar with T3 at the rock bottom of the range. BUT, I totally agree with her that one day's salivary cortisol testing and blood spot thyroid test is insufficient to move forward with.

HER: She thinks I may be oversupplementing with Vit D (I take 8000 IU/day after my level came back at 50).

ME: I would like my VitD levels to be higher.

I did not give her details about the Leptin Rx. I didn't feel she would be receptive and I sure as hell didn't show her my lipid results. I really didn't think she would feel warm and fuzzy about them, even though I think they're ok. She offered to put me on a "shake detox" and offered me access to the infrared sauna (to "burn extra calories", even bigger sigh), but I must have had quite a look on my face because she didn't bring it up again.

SO, had my thyroid drawn last night and will very impatiently wait for next Wednesday, when we have a phone appt to discuss the results. What i'm hoping for, honestly, is for my suspicions about my thyroid to be borne out in my lab results. To me, that would best explain my symptoms and the gradual decline of my emotional and physical health and well being over the past 3 or so years.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-04-11T20:13:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-13823>

Just shoot me. Full thyroid panel. totally normal. HS crp 0.6. Me-honest to god the fattest I've ever been outside of pregnancy. Totally out of control. Doing month long salivary hormone testing now. Testosterone was accidentally added to my last lab req and was low.

I feel totally, utterly helpless and hopeless. My obsession with my weight and food continues to grow... I was so hoping that my emotional issues were caused by an underfunctioning thyroid.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-13T22:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-44759>

Im baaaack...four months later. I am tempted to default to the phrase"off the wagon" but I prefer to think that this has been the path that I've needed to take. I've been working with my naturopath, reading (hormones, adrenals, thyroid, as well as almost everything martha beck has written. After much testing that revealed not much (to my naturopath, anyway) and talks with her about my desire to treat my significant hormonal deficiency symptoms, rather than my numbers and her attempts to treat me homeopathically, supplementally and pretty much every other manner _except_ BIHT...it would seem that I've gotten nowhere. But, again, I don't wish to frame it that way. Its more like when we tell laboring moms (after hours of active labor) that they are still at the same dilation they were hours before...a LOT has been happening, changing and progressing. The baby has been turning, preparing, secreting hormones, readying itself for the journey. Measuring progress by dilation alone is a mistake, albeit the only one we are able to accomplish.

I have tried the following over the course of the last few months...

T3, vitex, maca, adrenal support, sepia (homeopathic), pregnenolone, inaddition to supplementing with mag citrate, b complex, c, krill oil, vit E, a women's multi...

My naturopath is hesitant to pull the trigger on hormones...despite my low progesterone and absent testosterone and encouragement that I AM NOT AFRAID, CONCERNED OR WORRIED ABOUT GOING ON BIHT FOR THE REST OF MY DAYS!!!!

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-13T22:37:47+0300

Today!

BAB. 7:30 AM. Not within 30 minutes of waking. Need to get up b4 my kids for that! 4 eggs w greens and onions in CO, 3.5 slices niman ranch pepper bacon. 1 slice cantaloupe, 1 cherry. cup coffee w half and half (until i get cream in the house)
