### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-13T23:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-44771>

Im baaaack...four months later. I am tempted to default to the phrase"off the wagon" but I prefer to think that this has been the path that I've needed to take. I've been working with my naturopath, reading (hormones, adrenals, thyroid, as well as almost everything martha beck has written. After much testing that revealed not much (to my naturopath, anyway) and talks with her about my desire to treat my significant hormonal deficiency symptoms, rather than my numbers and her attempts to treat me homeopathically, supplementally and pretty much every other manner _except BIHT...it would seem that I've gotten nowhere. But, again, I don't wish to frame it that way. Its more like when we tell laboring moms (after hours of active labor) that they are still at the same dilation they were hours before...a LOT has been happening, changing and progressing. The baby has been turning, preparing, secreting hormones, readying itself for the journey. Measuring progress by dilation alone is a mistake, albeit the only one we are able to accomplish.

I have tried the following over the course of the last few months...

T3, vitex, maca, adrenal support, sepia (homeopathic), pregnenolone, inaddition to supplementing with mag citrate, b complex, c, krill oil, vit E, a women's multi...

My naturopath is hesitant to pull the trigger on hormones...despite my low progesterone and absent testosterone and encouragement that I AM NOT AFRAID, CONCERNED OR WORRIED ABOUT GOING ON BIHT FOR THE REST OF MY DAYS!!!!

Im baaaack...four months later. I am tempted to default to the phrase "off the wagon" but I prefer to think that this has been the path that I've needed to take. I've been working with my naturopath, reading (hormones, adrenals, thyroid, as well as almost everything martha beck has written. After much testing that revealed not much (to my naturopath, anyway) and talks with her about my desire to treat my significant hormonal deficiency symptoms, rather than my numbers and her attempts to treat me homeopathically, supplementally and pretty much every other manner except_ BIHT...it would seem that I've gotten nowhere. But, again, I don't wish to frame it that way. Its more like when we tell laboring moms (after hours of active labor) that they are still at the same dilation they were hours before...a LOT has been happening, changing and progressing. The baby has been turning, preparing, secreting hormones, readying itself for the journey. Measuring progress by dilation alone is a mistake, albeit the only one we are able to accomplish.

I have tried the following over the course of the last few months...

T3, vitex, maca, adrenal support, sepia (homeopathic), pregnenolone, in addition to supplementing with mag citrate, b complex, c, krill oil, vit E, a women's multi...

My naturopath is hesitant to pull the trigger on hormones...despite my low progesterone and absent testosterone and encouragement that I AM NOT AFRAID, CONCERNED OR WORRIED ABOUT GOING ON BIHT FOR THE REST OF MY DAYS!!!!

So, my next step (after I read Wiley's book and Ageless-I've already read both Uzzi Reiss books) is to try out this local doc who is part of the "anti aging" crew, if you will. Its in Woburn, MA---called BodyLogic. If anyone has any experience with these guys, please let me know. It is about 2 hours away and I am done wasting my time and, of course, money!! OMG!! I just looked this guy up and, guess what????? He is paleo!!!!!!

Moving on, I am back in the tub. And the whole seafood/seaweed thing intrigues me. SO I'm back in. And I've read up on all of the blogs I've missed.

As someone with disordered eating issues (as much as I detest labelling myself that way, I'm not sure how else to say it), I was wary of participating in a program that was so limiting, being that I felt that was how my issues began. But, now I'm thinking that those issues began along with and as part of my hormonal cluster \*\*\*\* and were not caused directly by my food limiting.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-15T20:18:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-45046>

Crap, thought I posted, but it isn't here! Oh well. Body Logic graciously let me back out and is refunding my money....Now just waiting for the OBGYN listed on the Wiley site to call me back. Says she's certified antiaging. I'm all for that.

If anyone in New England wants to contribute any paleo/primal/Kruse friendly docs/professionals/businesses, I'd like to create a resource for newbies--less legwork and trial and error for those just getting on board. Let's face it, they have enough to occupy their time and energy...CT, epi-paleo, reading the blogs, reading the forum, sleeping, lights out.....

Back to all important me, LOL...

I did very well after lunch yesterday. Loaded up for lunch, expecting to skip dinner because I was working. Total success, didn't even think about food and went right to bed when I got home at 9:45ish.

This morning 5:30 BAB. Scrambled eggs with leftover pork chops and greens that were cooked in butter and CO and coated with [sea shakes](http://http://www.injoynow.com/seashakes/index.html). Love that stuff. Sea salt and seaweed seasoning. Also coffee, a ramekin of watermelon cubes.

12:30 lunchish....I made a blueberry (we picked em this morning-doesn't get more seasonal than that!) coconut flour "cake" for my kids. Really simple, coco flour, salt, baking soda, blueberries, vanilla, eggs and maple syrup (naughty) Had a few spoons of that with . Not total compliance, there, but I thought I'd take that opportunity to have another dose of magnesium and pregnenolone and some kombucha maybe. Moving on...

Dinner will be sockeye salmon ceviche! I'm going to top it with a caponata-style salad- red onion, garlic, tomato, green and black olives. Really excited 'bout that.

I've been dumping tons of water the past two days. Amazing how fast the water leaves once clean eating commences, especially after a week of vacation filled with crap. We made a lot of awesome food, too, but it was accompanied by lots of crap.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-15T20:40:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-45052>

> **kathylu;45292 wrote:** Darlene, do you have a period with the Wiley protocol? Why did you decide to do this rather than other BHRT methods? Thanks!

No periods because I don't have any parts. And I chose this because after I read Wiley's book I realized that conventional BHRT would NOT be of much help. It just makes so much sense to me that if you want the protection your hormones provide you HAVE to have them at the levels where they gave you the most protection, i.e., in your glorious 20's when you were at your childbearing peak years.

I swear I was already showing symptoms of early dementia before I got on the WP. I could not remember ANYthing and my reading comprehension was down to about nothing. I was walking through each day in a total mental fog. It's much better now but I don't know if I will ever get back to full mental functioning. It's extremely frustrating. So when I say they can have my BHRT/WP when they can pry it from my cold, dead hands I am NOT kidding.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-19T04:28:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-45656>

BAB about 7:20 just eggs! Wanted something simple this am. 6 scrambled in butter with a splash of cream, fresh thyme, salt and pepper. A slice of cantaloupe, a slice of watermelon, small handful of blueberries, coffee with cream and supplements.

Lunch @ 1PM: full fat greek yogurt with a few frozen dark cherries and kombucha and an extra dose of magnesium for kicks. I know, dairy is not suggested, but its what I felt like, so...

Dinner@ 6. Went out to great local place...italian...guy grows his own produce, local meats and fish. Great stuff, low key but amazing food and we love to sit outside on the deck. I made my first official foray into non-crustacean shellfish. Frutta di mare. clams, calamari, mussels, shrimp in a red sauce. LOVED it!! Yay. So much so that we stopped for mussels at the store on the way home to make tomorrow! Then I had a giant braised pork shank- brought home a little and the giant bone it came on! Glass of Malbec. And then,,, cup of coffee and slice of amaretto chocolate cheesecake.

1\. I regret getting the cheesecake. Mostly because it wasn't FANTASTIC. Also because nothing was really striking me from the dessert menu, but I ordered it anyway. I ordered it because I thought it would be the least damaging of the choices. Not as much as ordering no dessert, of course Oh well, still did great...italian restaurant with amazing bread in front of me..not tempted.

I also did 30 minuted of CT, although the water coming from the tap was a couple of degrees warmer than the past few days....about 55\*

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-19T17:53:16+0300

Thanks, ABM. Haven't scheduled the consult yet. Want to get my labs from my naturopath. But I just finished writing up my healh hx, so, 1 step closer.

Something else I realized when i was writing up my hx... Since starting reset again, my desire for coffee has gone waaaay down. Like 1 cup a day is enough. I was never a really massive coffee drinker and I never drank it because i felt I "needed" it to wake up. I just enjoyed it. Max 4 cups/day. But now, not so much....interesting...

Also, may I say, that EVERYONE should read Wiley's book "Sex, lies and menopause". Male, female, old, young, whatever. Amazing. Such a great explanation of circadian biology, how we've moved away from how we were optimally designed to live and why we need hormones if we want to live a long, healthy life. To sum up...you cannot alter natural, biologic processes without consequences. We have f\*\*\*\*d with mother nature. Thanks so much to those of you who suggested reading it.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-27T17:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-47485>

In the tub this am...first time I've been back to update journal since consult. Super busy weekend and soooooo much to think about. First off, consult was mind blowing. Glad I recorded it as some suggested. If you do a consult you HAVE to find a way to record it. Even if you have to buy something to do it! I just used garage band. Thanks to dr k, I can no longer live in denial. My problems are fixable, but if I don't fix them I will be one sick puppy! My main issues are 1. Light and 2. Hormones. I was told the the only thing probably keeping me from being worse off than I am is my diet. Scary.

So, morning light in my eyes 3-5 minutes (I'm apparently wicked sensitive to light), Bab or hab, 4-5 hrs btwn last meal and bed. Orgasm morning and nite. Need to get my estrogenrogesterone flipped. Start taking d again. One of the most interesting parts was when he said (paraphrasing). " you think that your diet is what's making you fat...it's really the light issues that are making you fat."

He also said if I did a FBg, it would be over 88.

It was 89.

Totally totally totally worth it.

Issues that remain...

I work at nite often (like 6-9:30) in an brightly lit retail store. I have no problem eating b4 work and leaving sufficient time between meal and bed. But what about the light?

I am holding out hope that this new doc will give me everything I need, accept some of the recommendations I received from dr k and help me get back to optimal. I am waiting for the lab reqs to come. Had new est, prog, dhea, testosterone drawn on Saturday for the naturopath. Maybe I'll take the consult to her first...... I just don't know

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-08-30T20:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-48412>

Ugh. Back and forth, back and forth. I'd like this relationship with my naturopath to continue. She is a very caring practitioner, she just needs some more knowledge. In attempts to school her, I paid about ten bucks to have my consult transcribed online and it is a disaster. SO much for that. Will have to take another approach, I guess! I don't want to waste anymore time. The Wiley doc wants me to do a 24 hour urine and a thyroid panel that she wants sent to a special lab. The 24 hour urine cant be done at TOM (currently) and must be eight days away from any supps. So I'm looking at a week b4 I can do that. They thyroid draw must be sent spun and frozen, which the lab by her will do for me. It is 2 hours away. Just for a blood draw. I really would like my naturopath to get on board. My next step is to just send her the recording....And the Suzanne Somers book.

Interesting update....

So, I've had this gnawing sensation (almost like hunger-goes away if I eat) that flares up from time to time. Been to the ER, talked to the naturopath. Everyone's \*\*\*\*ing clueless about it. Listening to my consult again, Dr. K mentioned that, with my cravings he wondered if I might have a passenger in my gut-namely candida or H pylori. How come no one else ever thought that with my flare ups!! Jeez! Its not like H pylori is uncommon...at all! I'm going to demand that my naturopath test me for it. I'm having a flare up now. And I'm eating ice cream (where's the smiley for shame?).

Waiting for naturopath to call me back as my labs were in yesterday. My approach is going to be that I really want to continue being treated by her, but in order to do so, I need her to start me on BHRT...stat! She's been trying everything else since April. That's 6 months _just since i've been seeking her treatment. I was suffering for years before I went to see her_.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-09-07T17:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-50012>

Omg. Only partially thru BG11. So important!

I wanted to hop on and mention some interesting changes that have been happening since I 1. upped my intake of seafood from like once-twice a month to 6 days/wk, 2. changed my supps- added K2, a little DHEA, a good proB, started D again, melatonin 2 hrs b4 bed (already taking Mag) and 3. started doing my absolute best at avoiding light after dark (work is a pain, but my blublockers go on as soon as i get in the car-still looking for a tinted pair of "fashion glasses") and daylight in my eyes in the am (without contacts- very difficult for me as my glasses are broken right now and I am majorly near sighted). I get early morning sun in my eyes, too, but my contacts are in by then-still working on that.

Anywho, been taking mag for a while now. never gave me loose stools even when taken 800 mg with loads of c. Since I started the other supps, whoa...loose stools often. That is such a deviation from my normal patterns, I'm rather enjoying the idea that SOMETHING is happening. Mrs. Libido has been making unexpected appearances, also I honestly thought she was 6 feet under.

Still fighting the good fight hormonally. I have a phone appt w my naturopath for the 17th to go over recent labs. Oh boy is she going to get an earful. Also have a meet and greet with a local GYN practice the same day. That should be quick. I have one question for them. They will either give me the answer that I want or I am outta there. Will you put me on hormones based on my current labs and symptoms and will you support me in titrating them myself as I need to? Will you also support my thyroid, at least temporarily, with Armour? I need affirmatives all around. I've heard only good things about this practice, but I haven't heard of a specific situation like mine.... We will see....

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-09-19T17:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-52692>

Watching things inch down. Decided to take my fbg weekly. Had done it daily, ran out of strips, then not at all. THis Am was 84. not my lowest, but I believe a downward trend. Weight was 117- on the lighter side. That means after multiple weighings, I had 117s and 116.5. I have had this scale forever and I know that means that I'm on the "light side" of 117. Down half pound+ from last time I weighed (9/7). Used my body fat scale as well. We're not talking laser precision, here, but it is good for trending. For the first time it read my visceral fat as 4. Had been 5 ever since I first bought it.

So, really interesting changes. I've had a cold, so sleep is not fantastic and I continue to wake up pretty groggy in the AM.

I've been reading up on SAD. Although i don't suffer from noticably more depression with less light, I think I fall in to the "sub-syndromal" group. Something else I've been delving into is the major move we made December 31st 2007. We moved from lat 34 degrees to 43 degrees (SC to NH). In the middle of winter. I was pregnant. From the charts I'm looking at, it looks like about an hour of light difference at that time of year. Wondering aloud about what impact that may have had...

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-09-28T19:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrystins-journal.480/post-55045>

GYN consult....

I'll try to sum up. Ugh, such a "typical" MD. Rush rush. Your LDL is too high, your low libido is to be expected with three young kids, you weight is "normal", blah blah blah.

The good stuff...Although he denies I'm in any state of hormonal decline, he agrees that my Progesterone and T are in the toilet. He thinks my "diet and healthy lifestyle" is keeping me out of adrenal issues (I told him I avoid grains, eat lots of seafood, meat and veggies) he agrees that my naturopath is not well versed nor experienced with hormones and he prefers a cyclical oral bioidentical progesterone, 50 mg to start and T cream, 1 mg to start. He thinks labs are important but wants to treat me based on symptoms.

So, all in all, I'd say he's about 40% of where I'd like him to be. I told him I thought the progesterone was a big player in my weight gain, making me insulin resistant and ultimately skewing my gut flora. He was with me up until I mentioned gut flora, LOL. Just wanted to get a feel for his depth of understanding. I told him that although my weight was "normal", what was telling was my body comp-my body fat has nearly doubled in the past three to four years. Yuck. He said the nutrition plan they recommend sounds very similar to what I'm already doing. AT which point I wanted to scream "ITS NOT WHAT I'M EATING!". Anyway, I think he's good enough to get me where I need to go. He seemed irritated that I seemed to know anything at all. My bad. I forgot that its his job to know everything and my job to sit quietly in awe of his brilliance. Maybe he's not all that bad. We'll see. He is very supportive of my naturopath, a least and said he has sent many patient to her for naturopathy.

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-09-29T17:13:56+0300

This time, I just had to check the time. I felt around for my blublockers and looked---It was 5:45 AM. Decidedly not in the middle of the night. Totally awake. Nice. Decided last night I would weigh and take FBG this am. More good news there. FBG 79 (1st time below 80) and Weight 115.5! Down about 1 1/2 pounds over ten days. # Thread: Some weightlifting while on the Leptin Reset?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Xena — 2012-03-28T02:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-7442>

So, I know a cool trainer at a CrossFit place in Denver and he tells me that there are some studies show doing some weightlifting while one's adrenals are fatigued, help the adrenals get better. Has anyone else heard of this? I know Dr. K. doesn't say to do this until LS signs are coming in.

My stats are: I have badly fatigued adrenals and have for several years. My numbers are slowly getting better. Doc is great and said no exercise except light walking which I do a few times a day. Next cortisol test is in a few months.

I'm female, age 49, 6'1" tall and weigh 218. Sounds like a lot but I'm down to a size 14 tall which blows my mind. Still, I'd like to weigh less-trying to let that go. I've been on the reset for 7 weeks, haven't lost any weight (but lost 29.4 lbs just prior to going on the reset), but continue to lose inches. I'm doing the reset dairy free except a tiny bit of grass-fed butter in my coffee, mostly nut free (a few macadamias once a week) and nightshade free, although I don't have auto-immune disease. I eat organ meat a few times a week, stick to grass-fed, pastured everything and generally feel amazing every day. Optimal is coming, I can feel it.

I'm on week 3 or so of CT and spot icing 1-2x per day in addition to face dunking.

Appreciate any thoughts/hacking.

Xena

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-28T02:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-7448>

I would make sure the trainer/coach knows your goals, and that you are able to do strength training only: oly or powerlifting. Not metcons. And by lifting I mean 5 x 5s etc, not metcons with a barbell. They are still metcons LOL

Good luck, it is possible to do strength only programs at a crossfit gym. I do.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-30T18:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-8818>

I concur with Teenie Leek - if you can get your coach on board to customize the programming a bit for you to do this in a box - go for it. If not, figure out how to do the lifting on your own. Make sure though that you are adhering to the protocol. Most people take 6-8 weeks to get LS and you really shouldn't start lifting before that. That said, if you have the signs in your skin and your appetite that means you are heading the right direction. Since adrenals are very much part of the sleep equation, I wouldn't start weights until I had seen some improvement in that sign as well. And absolutely positively, if you do lift - don't do it more than 2-3x a week, see the value in bodyweight exercises as strength training as much as the barbells, and drop it like a 300# DL if you see any negative impact to doing it. Negatives would be rotten recovery - DOMS that is still there when you hit the second lift of the week, fatigue, or a down turn in your sleep.

### Xena — 2012-03-31T05:26:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-weightlifting-while-on-the-leptin-reset.481/post-9099>

Thanks, my sleep keeps improving and now I'm waking up right before my husband's alarm goes off at 5:15, sometimes an hour earlier. I'm not dragging around either. But I REALLY want optimal and am feeling more cautious about this today. I'll find out from my contact about the perimeters of doing this--I am only on week 8 of the reset and in some ways feeling like I'm still a beginner.
